column1: So You Want To Be A Hero  [score %d of 500]

column1: Suddenly, you hear voices, seeming to come from everywhere at once.  They all sound like Baba Yaga, and they all say:\n"Wheeeeeere's myyyyyy\nMaaaandraaaaake Rooooooooooooot????"
column2: 突然，你聽到各處傳來的聲音，全\n都像芭芭雅嘎，她們都喊著：\n「我的…曼德拉草根…呢？？？？」

column1: Because you failed to meet Baba Yaga's DEADline, her curse turns you into a frog on the spot, and you are forced to live out your years dodging Sauruses (Saurii?) with large feet.
column2: 因為你沒有趕上芭芭雅嘎的死亡期限,她的詛咒當場把你變成青蛙,你被迫用剩下的日子躲避有大腳掌的索魯斯(索魯西?)。

column2: 你吃了幾朵妖精環的蘑菇。美味極了。

column1: Wow!  Look at all the nice paisley horses!
column2: 哇!看看所有漂亮的西洋花紋馬!

column1: Not to mention the beautiful neon sky.
column2: 更別說那美麗的霓虹天空。

column1: That's funny.  I could have sworn I warned you about eating too many Magic Mushrooms.  Your mind permanently warped, you die a garishly polka-dotted death.
column2: 真搞笑。我發誓我警告過你別吃太多魔法蘑菇。你的心智永久變形,以花俏的圓點花紋死亡。

column1: Hmm, that was interesting.  But it would probably not be a good idea to repeat the experience.
column2: 嗯，那很有趣。但重複那種經驗可能不是個好主意。

column2: 捲軸已經不在這裡了。

column1: You see no scroll here.
column2: 你看不到捲軸。

column2: 當你讀著捲軸上的魔法符文時，捲軸化為虛無。你現在已經掌握了施放「鎮定術」的知識。

column2: 石頭上刻著「伊瑞娜的安寧之地」。也許你該配副眼鏡了。

column1: The stone has the words "Erana's Peace" carved upon the top.  There are some runes carved along the side.
column2: 石頭的頂端刻著「伊瑞娜的安寧之地」字樣。側面還刻著一些符文。

column1: It reads:  "If thy Will is Magic, so shall I share.  Open this Stone and claim what is there."
column2: 內容寫著：「若汝意志乃魔法者，吾願與爾分享。開啟此石，領取所有寶藏。」

column1: No amount of physical effort will be able to move this stone.
column2: 再怎麼費力氣也動不了這塊石頭。

column1: The meadow lies covered with a blanket of flowers, unusual for this early in the spring.  It is warm, even though surrounded by the late snows of winter.
column2: 春天才剛開始，草地上卻已經覆滿了一片花海——這很不尋常。儘管被冬天的殘雪包圍，這裡卻溫暖如春。

column1: The air has the fresh, clean scent of the mountains, accompanied by numerous perfume-like fragrances.
column2: 空氣中飄散著山區的清新氣息，混合著各式各樣香氛般的花香。

column1: A large, carved stone lies flat on the ground.  You feel as though someone gentle was watching over you.  You feel that you are safe here.
column2: 一塊巨大的石雕平放在地上。你感到彷彿有某個溫柔的人在守護著你。你感到自己在這裡很安全。

column1: All kinds of colorful and fragrant flowers and grasses cover the meadow.
column2: 各式各樣的繽紛花朵和香草覆蓋著整個草地。

column1: The sky is clear.
column2: 天空晴朗無雲。

column1: The stars shine.
column2: 繁星閃爍。

column1: The sun feels warm.
column2: 陽光暖洋洋的。

column1: The small tree is most amazing.  It bears blossoms and fruit at the same time, and the fruit on its boughs seems to shyly appear and disappear, shimmering.
column2: 這棵小樹最是不可思議。它同時開著花和結著果，枝頭上的果實似乎害羞地時隱時現、閃爍著。

column1: The tree's blossoms are soft, green and fragrant.
column2: 樹上的花朵柔軟、青翠又芬芳。

column1: The fruit glows and shimmers in the moonlight.  It produces a fragrance like a sun-ripened strawberry.
column2: 果實在月光下閃閃發亮、搖搖欲墜。散發出像熟透草莓的香氣。

column1: The fruit sparkles in the sunlight, yet it appears soft and fuzzy.
column2: 果實在陽光下閃閃發光，但看起來軟軟的、毛毛的。

column1: To the north, the high, snowy peaks of the Dragon Smoke mountains are clearly visible.
column2: 北方遠處，龍煙山脈高聳的雪峰清晰可見。

column1: The meadow is an oasis at the edge of the snowline.  The rocky hillside looks very steep and slippery.
column2: 這個草地是雪線邊緣的綠洲。岩石山坡看起來陡峭又滑溜。

column1: You are at the snowline.  All around you, except for the forest, the rocks and mountains glisten with slippery snow.
column2: 你已經來到雪線。除了森林以外的四周，岩石和山脈都被滑溜的積雪所覆蓋。

column1: To the south, you see the deep forest through which you came to this meadow.
column2: 往南看，你能看到那片你來時走過的深林。

column1: The stone has been moved.
column2: 石頭被挪開了。

column1: The large stone appears to be ancient and deliberately placed.
column2: 這塊大石頭看起來很古老，是故意擺在這裡的。

column1: You see no holes.
column2: 你看不到洞穴。

column1: The hole that was beneath the stone is empty.
column2: 原本在石頭下方的洞現在是空的。

column1: Go over to the tree and pick some.
column2: 去樹旁摘一些吧。

column1: The sweet, juicy fruit of the tree is amazingly satisfying and refreshing.
column2: 樹上香甜多汁的果實令人滿足又清爽。

column1: The lovely blossoms should stay on the tree.
column2: 這些漂亮的花應該留在樹上。

column1: The fruit is very soft and juicy.   It would be impossible to keep in your pack.
column2: 這水果軟軟的、很多汁，根本沒辦法帶在包裡。

column1: The grasses in the meadow are covered over with flowers.
column2: 草地上鋪滿了鮮花。

column1: You take another handful of the lovely, fragrant flowers.
column2: 你又摘了一把香氣迷人的花朵。

column1: As you pick a variety of the sweet-smelling flowers, they seem to glow in your hands.  You put them safely away.
column2: 當你摘下各式各樣香氣四溢的花朵時，它們在你手上似乎閃閃發光。你小心地把它們放好。

column1: The smell reminds you of laughter.
column2: 那香氣讓你想起了笑聲。

column1: There is no further purpose in casting the Open spell.
column2: 沒必要再施放「開啟術」了。

column1: There is an aura of magic throughout this meadow.  It seems to be benevolent and restorative.
column2: 整個草地籠罩在魔法的光輝中。這魔法似乎既善良又有療癒的力量。

column1: The rock faces are slippery with melted snow, and there is no need to climb the little tree.
column2: 岩石表面被融化的雪弄得溜滑，根本不需要爬那棵小樹。

column1: The atmosphere here is peaceful and calm.   There is no need to throw anything.
column2: 這裡的氣氛很平和寧靜。沒必要亂扔東西。

column1: You sleep comfortably among the fragrant flowers.
column2: 你舒舒服服地在香氣四溢的花叢中睡著了。

column1: You're kidding, right?
column2: 你開玩笑呢，對吧？

column1: You don't have a knife.
column2: 你沒有刀子。

column1: There are none here to search.
column2: 這裡沒有東西好搜。

column1: The Ogre's body must have been carried away during the night by some marauding beasties.
column2: 那隻食人妖的屍體應該是昨夜被掠奪的野獸拖走了。

column1: However, there is a dead Ogre here.
column2: 不過這裡有一隻死掉的食人妖。

column1: However, there is a very large Ogre here.
column2: 不過這裡有一隻非常巨大的食人妖。

column1: There is a cave entrance large enough to admit a man.
column2: 有一個足夠讓人進出的洞穴入口。

column1: There is a big, ugly, dead Ogre here.
column2: 這裡有一隻又大又醜、已經死掉的食人妖。

column1: This Ogre is in a bad mood.
column2: 這隻食人妖心情很差。

column1: And big. Very BIG.
column2: 而且超大隻。非常非常大。

column1: The dead Ogre's chest lies open and empty.
column2: 死掉的食人妖的寶箱打開著，但裡面空空如也。

column1: The dead Ogre's chest lies open, its contents glittering brightly.
column2: 死掉的食人妖的寶箱打開著，裡面的東西閃閃發光。

column1: The Ogre is carrying a chest in his massive arms.
column2: 食人妖的巨大臂膀裡抱著一個寶箱。

column1: The chest has a heavy lid with a strong lock.
column2: 寶箱有個沉重的蓋子和堅固的鎖。

column1: There is nobody here.
column2: 這裡沒人。

column1: This Ogre will never eat again.
column2: 這隻食人妖再也吃不了東西了。

column1: This Ogre looks like it would rather eat you.
column2: 這隻食人妖看起來寧願吃了你。

column1: There are no locks here.
column2: 這裡沒有鎖。

column1: It's already unlocked.
column2: 它已經開著了。

column1: It's locked -- you'll have to use another skill to open this.
column2: 它上了鎖——你得用另一種技能才能打開。

column1: You don't have the right tools -- you'll have to find another way to open it.
column2: 你沒有合適的工具——你得想想別的辦法打開它。

column1: The lock is beyond your current skill.
column2: 這把鎖超出你目前的技術。

column1: There's nothing like that here.
column2: 這裡沒有那種東西。

column1: A magical aura emanates from the cave's entrance.
column2: 洞穴入口散發著魔法的光輝。

column1: You've wasted a spell.
column2: 你浪費了一個法術。

column1: Ok, he'll be a dazzled cadaver.
column2: 好吧，他就成了個被眩暈的屍體囉。

column1: How much more calm can he get?
column2: 他還能更冷靜嗎？

column1: You're wasting a spell.
column2: 你在浪費法術。

column1: There's nothing here to open.
column2: 這裡沒有什麼可以打開的。

column1: The Ogre's chest is already unlocked.
column2: 食人妖的寶箱已經打開了。

column1: Your spell unlocks the Ogre's chest.
column2: 你的法術打開了食人妖的寶箱。

column1: Your spell is too weak to open the Ogre's chest.
column2: 你的法術力量太弱，打不開食人妖的寶箱。

column1: The chest is held shut by the Ogre's massive arms.
column2: 寶箱被食人妖巨大的臂膀夾住了。

column1: You don't seem to have a weapon to charge.
column2: 你好像沒有可以充能的武器。

column1: You want to fetch THAT?
column2: 你想去撿那個？

column1: The dead Ogre's huge club is much too heavy for you to lift.
column2: 死掉的食人妖的巨大狼牙棒重得你根本拿不起來。

column1: Hostile intent is evident.  You prepare for battle.
column2: 它明顯滿懷惡意。你做好了戰鬥的準備。

column1: You need to get closer to the dead Ogre.
column2: 你需要更靠近這隻死亡的食人妖。

column1: A search of the dead Ogre's body produces no treasure.
column2: 搜索死掉的食人妖的屍體沒有找到任何寶藏。

column1: However, the treasure chest he was carrying lies beside his body.
column2: 不過，他身旁還放著他原本抱著的寶箱。

column1: The lock on the Ogre's chest clicks open.
column2: 食人妖的箱子上的鎖咔地一聲打開了。

column1: The dead Ogre's chest contains 1 gold and 43 silver, which you take and put away.
column2: 死掉的食人妖的寶箱裡裝著一枚金幣和四十三枚銀幣，你把它們拿走了。

column1: You force the Ogre's chest open.
column2: 你強行打開了食人妖的寶箱。

column1: The chest is locked.
column2: 寶箱上了鎖。

column1: The dead Ogre's chest is empty.
column2: 死掉的食人妖的寶箱是空的。

column1: The Ogre appears to have recovered from your spell.
column2: 食人妖似乎已經從你的法術中恢復過來了。

column1: They're like stalagmites, but they go the other way.
column2: 它們像鐘乳石，但方向相反。

column1: They're like stalactites, but they go the other way.
column2: 它們像石筍，但方向相反。

column1: The cavern contains some impressive formations and is rather beautiful, as caves go.
column2: 洞穴裡有一些令人印象深刻的地形，就洞穴來說相當美麗。

column1: The stalactites, stalagmites, and cave walls glow from a phosphorescent fungus growing there.
column2: 鐘乳石、石筍和洞窟岩壁因為那裡生長的螢光菌而發光。

column1: The stalactites grow slowly.
column2: 鐘乳石生長得很慢。

column1: ...or are they stalagmites?
column2: …或者那是石筍？

column1: The stalagmites grow slowly.
column2: 石筍生長得很慢。

column1: ...or are they stalactites?
column2: …或者那是鐘乳石？

column1: The light from outside illuminates the cave opening.
column2: 外面的光照亮了洞窟的開口。

column1: Beyond the bear, the cave seems to continue.  The bear blocks the way.
column2: 熊的另一邊，洞穴似乎還要繼續。但熊擋住了路。

column1: The baron's in his castle.
column2: 男爵在他的城堡裡。

column1: There is no sign of the Baronet or the bear.
column2: 看不到小男爵或熊的蹤跡。

column1: You don't see any people -- just that bear.
column2: 你看不到人——只有那隻熊。

column1: The bear appears docile for the time being.
column2: 熊目前看起來很溫馴。

column1: Stunned, the bear is temporarily frozen.
column2: 熊被震懾住了，暫時動不了。

column1: Although looking somewhat scorched, the bear is still very dangerous.
column2: 雖然看起來有點焦黑，熊還是非常危險。

column1: On one side of this cavern is a creature which looks like a large bear.
column2: 這個洞窟的一側有一隻長得像大熊的生物。

column1: The bear looks menacing and angry.  There is something attached to its leg.
column2: 熊看起來很兇悍，很生氣。它的腿上綁著什麼東西。

column1: There is no sign of the bear or his chains.
column2: 看不到熊或它的鐵鏈。

column1: You can't see very well.
column2: 你看得不太清楚。

column1: There is a manacle and chain attached to the bear's leg.
column2: 熊的腿上纏著腳鐐和鐵鏈。

column1: I just don't know what to tell you.
column2: 我真不知道該怎樣跟你說。

column1: The fungus is slimy and stuck tight to the cave walls.
column2: 真菌又滑膩，緊緊粘著洞穴的牆壁。

column1: Better not.  You might make the bear angry.
column2: 還是別了。你會惹怒那隻熊。

column1: That won't help you.
column2: 那對你沒幫助。

column1: No food fights in here.  It already smells like a monkey cage.
column2: 在這裡別鬧食物戰爭。這地方已經聞起來像猴籠了。

column1: The walls are too slimy to climb.
column2: 牆壁太滑，爬不上去。

column1: There is no magic in the cave.
column2: 洞穴裡沒有魔法。

column1: There is an aura of magic throughout the cavern.  It seems to center on the bear.
column2: 整個洞窟都籠罩著魔法光暈。好像都集中在那隻熊身上。

column1: There is no point to that.
column2: 這沒意義。

column1: The magic about the bear's manacle is too great for your Open spell.
column2: 熊腳鐐上的魔法太強大，你的開啟術搞不了。

column1: You'd better not.  The bear isn't wearing a flea collar.
column2: 你最好別碰。那熊沒戴跳蚤項圈。

column1: Hi there!  I represent Ditto Realty.  We have some LOVELY properties that are PROVEN bargains.  We know that's true, because we've sold them so many times.
column2: 嗨，朋友！我代表迪圖房地產公司。我們有些超棒的房產都是保證划算的便宜貨。我們知道這是真的，因為我們賣過那麼多次。

column1: You know that bears can't talk!
column2: 你知道熊是不會說話的啦！

column1: The Flame Dart has spoiled the bear's appetite.
column2: 火焰鏢毀了那隻熊的食慾。

column1: Its hunger diminished, the bear takes a new attitude toward you.
column2: 肚子沒那麼餓了，那隻熊對你的態度改變了。

column1: You need a magical key.
column2: 你需要一把魔鑰匙。

column1: Sorry, this lock is enchanted.  You'll need a magical key.
column2: 抱歉，這把鎖被施了魔法。你得拿把魔鑰匙。

column1: As your eyes adjust from sunlight to darkness, you examine the interior of this eerie cavern.  You sense something moving off to your right.
column2: 當你的眼睛從陽光逐漸適應黑暗時，你打量著這個詭異洞穴的內部。你感覺到右邊有東西在動。

column1: A very large bear rears up as you approach.  It looks hungry and dangerous.
column2: 當你靠近時，一隻巨大的熊站了起來。它看起來既飢餓又危險。

column1: Boy, that smarts!  Your last thoughts, although trivial, gently nudge you toward infinity.  "I wonder if the bear's paws were clean.........."
column2: 哎呀，這下痛死了！你最後的思緒雖然微不足道，卻輕輕地把你推向無窮盡。「真想知道那隻熊的爪子乾不乾淨……」

column1: Boy, that smarts!  Your last thoughts, although trivial, gently loft you toward infinity.  "I wonder if the bear's paws were clean.........."
column2: 哎呀，這下痛死了！你最後的思緒雖然微不足道，卻輕輕地把你托向無窮盡。「真想知道那隻熊的爪子乾不乾淨……」

column1: The Kobold's key disappears as you turn it in the lock.
column2: 當你用地精的鑰匙轉動鎖時，鑰匙消失了。

column1: You seem to have bumped into an unseen object on the cave floor.
column2: 你好像撞上了洞窟地板上看不見的東西。

column1: You examine it by touch.  It feels like a wooden chest.
column2: 你摸著探索。感覺像是個木箱。

column1: It must have been made invisible by the Kobold.
column2: 一定是被地精變成隱形的。

column1: You've already made a mess of that flower.
column2: 你已經把那朵花搞得一團糟了。

column1: There's not much point in that, now that you've already done what you came here to do.
column2: 既然你已經做完了來這裡的目的，那樣做就沒什麼意義了。

column1: You don't need a rock for anything.
column2: 你沒有必要用石頭做什麼。

column1: Not now!
column2: 現在不行！

column1: That would not be appropriate here.
column2: 在這裡這樣做不太適合。

column1: To the north and west are steep cliffs.   There are some unusual plants growing out of the side of the cliffs.
column2: 北邊和西邊是陡峭的懸崖。懸崖側面長著一些奇異的植物。

column1: To the south and east is the forest from which you came.
column2: 南邊和東邊是你來時走過的森林。

column1: To the south and east is the forest from which you came. To the north and west are steep cliffs. There are some unusual plants growing out of the side of the cliffs.
column2: 南邊和東邊是你來時的森林。北邊和西邊是陡峭的懸崖。懸崖側面長著一些不尋常的植物。

column1: They are spitting what seems to be some kind of seed.
column2: 它們吐出似乎是某種種子的東西。

column1: The cliff walls are very steep, and they look difficult to climb.
column2: 懸崖壁非常陡峭，看起來很難爬。

column1: The meadow clearing in which you stand is grassy and littered with blown pine needles and small mossy rocks.
column2: 你站著的草地空地長滿了草，散落著被吹落的松針和長滿苔蘚的小石頭。

column1: The evening is clear and the stars are bright. Dark clouds pass over the moon.
column2: 夜晚天朗氣清，星光璀璨。烏雲飄過月亮。

column1: The sky is a piercing blue with scudding white clouds.
column2: 天空是刺眼的藍色，白雲飛快地掠過。

column1: You can see pines, cedars, birches and other trees frequently associated with mountain forests.
column2: 你能看到松樹、雪松、樺樹和其他常見於山區森林的樹木。

column1: You can't see it.
column2: 你看不到它。

column1: The large seed spins as it travels through the air.
column2: 大種子在空中飛行時旋轉著。

column1: The leaves look tough and fibrous.
column2: 葉子看起來又硬又有纖維。

column1: As you attempt to draw your weapon, you lose your balance momentarily.
column2: 你試圖拔出武器時，一時失去了平衡。

column1: If you had a sword, you might be able to do that.
column2: 要是你有一把劍，也許可以試試。

column1: You don't think you can climb any further up the cliff.
column2: 你覺得你爬不上去了。

column1: Wait a second!
column2: 等等！

column1: You're already on the ground.
column2: 你已經在地上了。

column1: There's not much point to that now.
column2: 現在那樣做沒什麼意義。

column1: Why do you think you're standing like this.  Trying to catch Flying Water?
column2: 你為什麼站成這樣。想接住飛水？

column1: You can't catch the seed from down on the ground.
column2: 你從地上抓不到種子。

column1: It's not that easy.
column2: 事情沒那麼簡單。

column1: You might have tried to throw a rock at the seed, but it's too late now.
column2: 你可能想過扔石頭打種子，但現在太晚了。

column1: You get in position for a good throw.
column2: 你擺好姿勢準備投擲。

column1: You need a good rock to throw.
column2: 你需要一塊好石頭來扔。

column1: Perhaps it would be better to find something else to throw.
column2: 也許找別的東西扔比較好。

column1: That's really not a good idea.
column2: 那真的不是個好主意。

column1: You lose your concentration.
column2: 你失去了專注。

column1: Your skill with the Open spell is not great enough to affect the plants.
column2: 你的開啟術技巧還不夠強，無法影響植物。

column1: You might have tried to cast a spell at the seed, but it's too late now.
column2: 你可能想過對種子施法，但現在太晚了。

column1: You get into a good position.
column2: 你擺好位置。

column1: That spell would damage the seed.  You don't want to do that.
column2: 那個法術會傷害種子。你不想那樣做。

column1: There is no need to be so destructive.
column2: 沒必要這麼有破壞性。

column1: Since the flowers have stopped spitting, the seed might be a little harder to get to.
column2: 既然花已經停止吐種子了，種子可能會有點難拿。

column1: You'd like to do that, wouldn't you?
column2: 你想那樣做，是吧？

column1: You have no need to take the flowers.
column2: 你沒有必要摘花。

column1: You see the seed floating above the shattered remains of the plant.
column2: 你看到種子浮在被摧毀的植物殘骸上方。

column1: You take the seed.
column2: 你拿起種子。

column1: You lose concentration, and the spell fades.  You will have to practice some more.
column2: 你失去了專注，咒語消散了。你還得多練習。

column1: You place the seed into your pack.
column2: 你把種子放進背包。

column1: You've never seen anything quite like them. They're pretty, in a grotesque way.
column2: 你從來沒見過這樣的東西。它們有種怪異的美感。

column1: At least, they're pretty when they're not dead.
column2: 至少，它們活著的時候還滿漂亮的。

column1: That's one mean Ogress!
column2: 那真是隻狠辣的女食人妖！

column1: There's no time for idle chatter.
column2: 沒時間閒聊。

column1: There is a bat, all folded up within his wings, hanging on the wall.
column2: 有隻蝙蝠用翅膀把自己包起來，掛在牆上。

column1: Is that a SMILE on that spider's face?
column2: 那隻蜘蛛的臉上有微笑嗎？

column1: You HATE houses with cobwebs in the corners, especially when there's an immense spider squatting cheerily in the middle of the cobweb.
column2: 你討厭角落裡長滿蜘蛛網的房子，尤其是當中間還蹲著一隻巨大的蜘蛛時。

column1: The glass seems to distort the view of the outside world.
column2: 玻璃好像扭曲了外面世界的景象。

column1: There's a lot of SOMETHING cooking away in the huge black cauldron.
column2: 巨大的黑色大鍋裡有什麼東西在煮。

column1: The smell is awful, the color is sickening, and you don't even want to know what's being prepared for dinner.
column2: 味道很難聞，顏色令人作嘔，你甚至不想知道在準備什麼晚餐。

column1: The fire burns hot under the kettle.
column2: 火在茶壺下面燒得很熱。

column1: The stone hearth and chimney radiates too much heat.
column2: 石頭壁爐和煙囪散發出太多熱度。

column1: It's a plain wooden kitchen cupboard.
column2: 這是個普通的木質廚房碗櫥。

column1: The kitchen knives look old but sharp.
column2: 廚房刀子看起來又舊又利。

column1: The pile of straw covered by a blanket must be the occupant's bed.
column2: 那堆被毛毯蓋住的稻草應該是主人的床。

column1: You furtively grasp the Magic Mirror.
column2: 你偷偷地抓住了魔鏡。

column1: You cannot see the witch anywhere.
column2: 你看不到女巫。

column1: You might as well be talking to yourself.
column2: 你根本就是在自言自語。

column1: Your magic is useless here.
column2: 你的魔法在這裡沒用。

column1: A quick look around shows that this is a creepy place, despite the pot (cauldron?) simmering briskly on the fire.  Look at the size of that spider!
column2: 快速掃一眼就知道這是個很陰森的地方，雖然火上的鍋子（大釜？）正在嗞嗞作響。看看那隻蜘蛛有多大！

column1:
Two witches had two watches.
Which witch would watch which watch?
Which watch would which witch watch?
(Is that too witchy watchy for you???)
column2:
兩個女巫有兩個錶。
哪個女巫會看哪個錶？
哪個錶會被哪個女巫看？
（太魔法錶了嗎？）

column1: The small hut has a beamed ceiling.
column2: 小屋有露樑的天花板。

column1: The floor is tidy, with a pile of straw in one corner.
column2: 地板很整潔，一角有堆稻草。

column1: Look pets...we have a visitor!
column2: 「看啊，寵物們……我們有訪客了！」

column1:
Powers of Night,
Shadows of Day
Heed now my Words!
Henceforth you STAY!
column2:
「夜晚的力量，
白晝的陰影
聽我此言！
從此你就待著吧！」

column1: Your body is frozen by the power of the witch's spell.
column2: 你的身體被女巫的咒語凍住了。

column1: Well Dearies!  What shall we have for supper today?
column2: 「好啊，親愛的們！今晚吃什麼？」

column1: Hero sandwiches?\nI had something more formal in mind.
column2: 「英雄三明治？我想要點更正式的。」

column1: Ah...that's it!\nFrog Legs Fricassee.
column2: 「啊…那就是了！炸青蛙腿。」

column1: Now how does that spell go.....?
column2: 「那個法術怎麼念來著……？」

column1:
Hear me, oh Powers
Of Klatha and Mana!
Turn now my guest
Into species called Rana!
column2: 「聽我說啊，克拉瑟和法力之力！把我的客人變成蛙族！」

column1: I learned that spell from Erasmus, kids. Doesn't it look delicious?
column2: 「我從伊拉斯謨那裡學的咒語，孩子們。看起來美味嗎？」

column1: Stubborn, aren't you?
column2: 「你很固執呢？」

column1: Well, you won't be so tough after you've simmered in a little wine sauce.
column2: 「好啦，你在紅酒醬裡燉一下就不會那麼硬了。」

column1: Bon appetit, Dearies!
column2: 「祝你胃口好，親愛的！」

column1: Next time you are asked a question by someone who has just turned you into a frog, perhaps you should be more polite.  It's better than being fricasseed!
column2: 下次有人把你變成青蛙然後問你問題時,也許你該更禮貌。比被燉了好多了!

column1: It's just as well.  I'd lose my dinner if you were brave.
column2: 「也好啦。你要是勇敢的話我就沒胃口了。」

column1: Lovies, it's about time we dine.
column2: 「親愛的們，該吃飯了。」

column1: Didn't anyone ever tell you that a hero is supposed to be brave even when he isn't?  Looks like you found the coward's way out...sauteed in wine sauce.
column2: 沒人告訴過你，英雄即使害怕也應該表現得勇敢嗎？看起來你找到了懦夫的出路……被葡萄酒醬燜著。

column1: Well, if you won't be a sweet, then you'll be my meat!
column2: 「好吧，如果你不乖，就變成我的肉！」

column1: Nothin' says lovin' like frog legs in the oven.
column2: 「什麼都比不上烤青蛙腿能表達愛意。」

column1: It seems a shame to have such a promising career go up in flames (assuming she decides to flambe you).   Wouldn't it have been better just to do a small favor for such a sweet little old Ogress?
column2: 看起來很可惜,前途似錦的事業就這樣付之一炬(假設她決定把你烤了)。就不能為了這個甜蜜的小食人妖老奶奶幫點小忙嗎?

column1: No mandrake for me, eh?  What a waste of spells you turned out to be.
column2: 「沒有曼陀羅給我？你這浪費法術的傢伙。」

column1: Actually, since it's my breakfast time, we'll have some amphibian omelette with bacon and legs.
column2: 「實際上，既然現在是我吃早餐的時間，我們就吃炒兩棲動物加培根和腿吧。」

column1: At least you could have said "Yes" before you croaked!
column2: 你至少可以在掛掉之前說聲「好」!

column1: You again?
column2: 「又是你？」

column1: Hello, Hero.
column2: 「你好，英雄。」

column1:
Powers that rule
Over regions soggy:
Change this creature
Back into a froggy!
column2: 「統治濕潤地域的力量：把這生物變回青蛙！」

column1: Ooooh...yummy!  I'm glad you returned.
column2: 「嗯……美味！我很高興你回來了。」

column1: Before you confront someone who is obviously more powerful than you are, give yourself a chance to reflect.
column2: 在你對抗明顯比你強大的人之前,先給自己反省的機會。

column1: The witch is hopping mad.
column2: 女巫氣得跳腳。

column1: What have you DONE to me?\nHow DARE you use my own spell against me?\nI'll, I'll....Oh oh!
column2: 「你對我做了什麼？\n你怎麼敢用我自己的法術\n對付我？我……喔喔！」

column1: Hear me, Oh Element of Air and Wind.\nGive me the power to save my own skin.
column2: 「聽我說，空氣與風的元素啊。\n賜予我拯救自己的力量。」

column1: Looking around, you get the feeling that this is not a very friendly place to be.
column2: 四周張望，你感覺這不是個很友善的地方。

column1: All the other skulls have glowing eyes. But me?....noooo!  I don't need lovely glowing eyes!  Just open and close the gate, that's all I'm good for.
column2: 「其他的頭骨都有發光的眼睛。但我?…不要!我不需要漂亮的發光眼睛!我只會開關大門,那就是我的全部用處。」

column1: But if you can give me a glowing gem for my eyes, then I'll let you in the gate.
column2: 「但如果你能給我個發光的寶石當眼睛，我就放你進門。」

column1: Well, is it a deal or isn't it?
column2: 「好吧，這是成交還是不成交？」

column1: You know the deal!  Get me a glowing gem for my eyes, and I'll let you in.
column2: 「你知道交易!給我一個發光寶石裝在我的眼睛裡,我就讓你進去。」

column1: Do you agree to the deal?
column2: 「你同意這個交易嗎?」

column1: Nothing happens.
column2: 什麼都沒發生。

column1: I'm moving as fast as I can!
column2: 「我已經盡快了！」

column1: The hut is a strange little house perched on chicken legs.
column2: 這是個奇怪的小房子，站在雞腳上。

column1: Those are the biggest chicken legs you've ever seen.
column2: 那是你見過最大的雞腿。

column1: The sharp ends of the fence are covered with some nasty-looking green slime.
column2: 柵欄的尖端被一些令人厭惡的綠色粘液覆蓋。

column1: The gate's gone.
column2: 門消失了。

column1: The large skull on the gate seems to stare vacantly at you.
column2: 門上的大骷髏似乎茫茫然地盯著你。

column1: It looks a lot like the slime that was eating away the barstool at the tavern.
column2: 看起來很像那次在酒館啃著凳子的黏液。

column1: You've never seen anything like it.  It looks quite unhealthy.
column2: 你從沒見過這種東西。看起來很不衛生。

column1: The skulls on top of the fence have eerily glowing eyes.
column2: 籬笆上的頭骨發著詭異的光芒。

column1: The eyes of the large skull on the gate flash merrily away at you.
column2: 門上大骷髏的眼睛朝你閃閃發光。

column1: You can see nothing through the windows of the hut.
column2: 你看不透小屋的窗戶。

column1: The door is open, but hardly inviting.
column2: 門是開著的，但不太吸引人。

column1: The door of the hut is ornately decorated.
column2: 小屋的門裝飾得很華麗。

column1: Your eyes are drawn to the incredible hut on chicken legs.
column2: 你的目光被那個不可思議的雞腿小屋吸引了。

column1: Now???
column2: 現在???

column1: The green slime dripping off of the fence top makes you realize that climbing is not a good idea.
column2: 從柵欄頂端滴下來的綠色粘液讓你意識到攀爬不是個好主意。

column1: What's to climb?
column2: 有什麼好爬的？

column1: You have second thoughts, and you decide not to attempt such a potentially dangerous excursion.
column2: 你改變了主意，決定不嘗試這樣可能很危險的遠征。

column1: You have to get there, first.
column2: 你必須先到達那裡。

column1: Too much work.
column2: 太費事了。

column1: Huh?
column2: 嗯？

column1: Ok, go ahead and say the rhyme.
column2: 好吧，你就說那個韻文吧。

column1: The deal is made, then.  A glowing gem in exchange for an opportunity to see Baba Yaga herself.
column2: 「那麼交易成立。一顆發光的寶石，換個見到芭芭雅嘎本人的機會。」

column1: Give me my gem!!
column2: 「把我的寶石還給我！」

column1: Hey, it's your life!
column2: 「嘿，這是你的人生！」

column1: No gem, no entry.  That's the deal.
column2: 「沒有寶石就不能進去。這是交易。」

column1: What kind of hero are you?  Can't even find a little glowing gem for me to see with!
column2: 「你算什麼英雄？連找個小寶石讓我看都做不到！」

column1: Just answer the question, will ya?
column2: 「就回答問題不行嗎？」

column1: Do you have my gem?
column2: 「你有我的寶石嗎？」

column1: Do you want back in?
column2: 「你想進去嗎？」

column1: Is it a deal or not?
column2: 「這是成交還是不成交？」

column1: Right!
column2: 「好的！」

column1: Not until you get my gem!
column2: 「在你拿到我的寶石之前，免談！」

column1: Hey!  Where's your manners?  Let me tell you something, here!
column2: 「嘿！你的禮貌呢？讓我告訴你一件事！」

column1: Wait a minute...I'll get around to that.
column2: 「等一下……我待會兒會說到。」

column1: You kept your end of the deal, all right!
column2: 「你確實信守了諾言！」

column1: What's to tell?  You've seen her for yourself.
column2: 「有什麼好說的？你已經親眼見過她了。」

column1: Baba Yaga is the most powerful Ogress around.  If you have any brains (and it looks like you don't), you'll stay away from her.
column2: 「芭芭雅嘎是周圍最強大的女食人妖。要是你還有腦子（看起來你沒有），就離她遠點。」

column1: Some hero YOU are!  Don't know what an Ogre is.  Just check out Baba Yaga!
column2: 「你真是個英雄！連食人妖是什麼都不知道。去看看芭芭雅嘎就知道了！」

column1: All I ask for is a little gem or jewel that glows in the dark.   You're the big hero, so go find one.
column2: 「我只要一個在黑暗中閃閃發光的寶石或珠寶。你是大英雄,所以去找一個吧。」

column1: It's just the right color!
column2: 「顏色剛剛好！」

column1: I wish I had some.
column2: 「我真希望我有一些。」

column1: I'm thrilled to have some!
column2: 「我很高興能有一些！」

column1: The bone brains on top of the fence are Baba Yaga's spies.  That's why THEY have glowing eyes.
column2: 「籬笆上的骨頭腦袋是芭芭雅嘎的間諜。這就是為什麼它們眼睛發光。」

column1: It's not much, but I call it home.
column2: 「這裡不怎麼樣，但我把它當成家。」

column1: You've seen one, you've seen them all, I say.
column2: 「你看過一隻就看過全部了，我這麼說。」

column1: Of course, most fences aren't poisoned on top!
column2: 「當然，大多數柵欄頂部都沒有毒！」

column1: Baba Yaga's hut will squat down if you say the rhyme.
column2: 「要是你唸出咒語，芭芭雅嘎的小屋就會蹲下去。」

column1: The rhyme is:  'Hut of brown, now sit down'.
column2: 「咒語是：『褐色小屋，現在坐下』。」

column1: My time is important, and I'm very busy, as you can well see.  Ask me about something important.
column2: 「我的時間很寶貴，我忙得不可開交，你看得出來。問我點重要的事。」

column1: You place the glowing gem inside the skull.
column2: 你把發光的寶石放進骷髏裡。

column1: I can see!  I have eyes again!!
column2: 「我能看見了！我又有眼睛了！」

column1: Yeccchhh!  Is THAT what you look like???
column2: 「哎呀！你看起來就是這樣？」

column1: Oh well!  Have fun visiting Baba Yaga.  And good luck....you'll need it!
column2: 「好吧！祝訪問芭芭雅嘎愉快。祝你好運…你會需要的！」

column1: Baba Yaga does not welcome strangers!  You have to deal with me before you can enter.
column2: 「芭芭雅嘎不歡迎陌生人!你必須先和我談條件才能進去。」

column1: Baba Yaga is one tough magic user.  If you're so stupid that you want to enter, perhaps we can make a deal.
column2: 「芭芭雅嘎是個厲害的法師。如果你傻得想進去,也許我們可以做筆交易。」

column1: So you're back!  Changed your mind about making a little deal, hero?
column2: 「所以你回來了!改變主意要做個小交易,英雄?」

column1: Did you get it?  Did you get my glowing gem???
column2: 「你得到了嗎？你拿到我的發光寶石了嗎？」

column1: So you made it back, did you?  She is expecting you.
column2: 「所以你回來了？她在等你。」

column1: You again??  Do you really want to go back in there?
column2: 「又是你??你真的想再進去嗎?」

column1: I hope you can remember the rhyme.
column2: 「我希望你能記得咒語。」

column1: You really brought down the house!  Too bad you were under it at the time.
column2: 你真的把房子給拆了！可惜你當時在底下。

column1: There goes Baba Yaga.  Now you've REALLY made her soar!
column2: 芭芭雅嘎走了。現在你真的讓她飆起來了！

column1: From a distance, you hear the witch intone:
column2: 從遠處,你聽到女巫低聲吟誦:

column1: Oops!  I very nearly forgot...
column2: 「哎呀!我幾乎忘了…」

column1:
Hear me, oh Powers
Of Mana and More:
Turn this fool back
As he was before.
column2:
「聽我說，偉大的力量
魔力與更多：
將這個傻瓜變回
原本的樣子。」

column1: As you contemplate the occurrences of the last few minutes, you conclude that you'd better find her mandrake root...and fast!
column2: 當你回顧最近幾分鐘內發生的事件，你得出結論，你最好趕快去找她的曼德拉草根！

column1: What an awful creature, to turn me into a FROG!, you say to yourself.  "Someday, I must return the favor."
column2: 「多麼噁心的生物，竟然把我變成青蛙！」你自言自語道。「有一天，我必定要報此仇。」

column1: You see snow and feel a cold wind blowing from the east.
column2: 你看到雪地，感受到從東方吹來的冷風。

column1: The air is crisp here and you can see your breath as you walk. There is a path leading up to a dark mountain, atop which is precariously perched a purple house.
column2: 這裡空氣清爽，走路時能看見自己的呼吸。有一條小徑通往黑色山峰，山頂上危險地立著一棟紫色房子。

column1: The path seems to be carved out of the rock.  It forms a steep trail up to the house on the mountain.
column2: 小徑似乎是從岩石上雕刻而成的。它形成了一條通往山上房子的陡峭小道。

column1: All of the mountains you can see are covered with snow except for the one to which the path leads.
column2: 你看得見的所有山峰都被白雪覆蓋，除了小徑通往的那座山。

column1: The drifts of snow look several feet high in some places.
column2: 某些地方的雪堆有好幾尺高。

column1: Looking north, you see the strange house nestled on its craggy peak.  It is hard to see much at this distance.  All you can tell is that the house is very large, very purple, and very strange.
column2: 向北看，你看到奇怪的房子窩在陡峭的山峰上。從這個距離很難看清楚。你只能看出房子非常大、非常紫、異常詭異。

column1: To the east is the forest.
column2: 東邊是森林。

column1: You see the surrounding mountains.
column2: 你看到周圍的山峰。

column1: The clouds skirt the mountain edges.
column2: 雲朵沿著山邊飄動。

column1: There is a rocky path through the snow.
column2: 雪地裡有一條石頭小道。

column1: There is nothing here to throw it at.
column2: 這裡沒有東西可以扔。

column1: You detect a strange, magical aura in this place.
column2: 你在這個地方察覺到奇異的魔法光環。

column1: There's nothing here to dazzle.
column2: 這裡沒有東西可以眩目。

column1: The magical aura around this place prevents you from casting a flame dart.
column2: 這個地方周圍的魔法氣息阻止了你施放火焰鏢。

column1: A sign appears.  It reads:
column2: 一塊牌子出現了。上面寫著:

column1: Willkommen auf Zauberberg!
column2: 「歡迎來到魔法山!」

column1: Welcome to Magic Mountain!
column2: 「歡迎來到魔法山!」

column1:
Bienvenu a Mont Magie!	「歡迎來到魔法山！
Another sign appears.  It reads:	另一塊牌子出現了。上面寫著:
Trespassers will be toad!"
column2: 「侵入者會變成癩蛤蟆!」

column1: Now go home!
column2: 「現在滾回家!」

column1: Proceed at your own risk!
column2: 「自行決定風險前進!」

column1: You see the purple home of someone who must enjoy its precarious perch upon the peak.
column2: 你看到了一座紫色的家，某人一定很喜歡它在峰頂上搖搖欲墜的位置。

column1: The stone creature looks like it's bored.  Wouldn't you be?
column2: 石造生物看起來很無聊。你不會嗎？

column1: The cliff walls are steep, and it's a long way to the snow fields below.
column2: 懸崖陡峭，下面的雪原距離很遠。

column1: You can see the mist flowing around the edges of the Wolf's Bane Peaks.
column2: 你可以看到霧氣在狼禍峰的邊緣流動。

column1: Below you, the snow glistens.
column2: 你下方的雪地閃閃發光。

column1: It is cloudless. It seems that you are above the clouds.
column2: 天氣晴朗。看起來你在雲層上方。

column1: Looking down and to the south, you see the tortuous path you climbed to get here. You'd hate to have to do that again.
column2: 向下看向南方，你看到你爬上來的曲折小道。你不想再走一遍。

column1: Looking below you to the west, you can see the Baron's Castle surrounded by the forest.
column2: 向下看向西方，你可以看到被森林環繞的男爵城堡。

column1: From outside, you can see that the interior of the house is curiously furnished.
column2: 從外面，你可以看到房子的內部擺設非常奇特。

column1: It's about time the gargoyle opened the door for you.
column2: 是時候讓石像鬼為你打開門了。

column1: You'd like to look at it more closely, if only the gargoyle would let you.
column2: 你想更仔細地看看它，只要石像鬼允許。

column1: The door is already open.
column2: 門已經開了。

column1: You feel as though you have just scaled the Matterhorn in full armor.  What a climb!
column2: 你覺得你剛剛穿著全套盔甲爬上了馬特洪峰。真是一次艱難的攀登！

column1: After you finally catch your breath, you see that you have reached the rather eccentric-looking house that you saw from below.
column2: 你終於喘過氣來後，發現你已經到達了那棟你從下面看到的相當怪異的房子。

column1: There is an ugly gargoyle above the entrance.
column2: 入口上方有一尊醜陋的石像鬼。

column1: Your body can't take too much more of this kind of mountaineering.  At this point, you envy the gargoyle his job.
column2: 你的身體無法承受太多這樣的登山。此時此刻，你羨慕石像鬼的工作。

column1: No matter how strong you feel at the bottom of the mountain, you're always more exhausted than the last time you reached the top.
column2: 無論你在山底感覺多麼強大，當你登上山頂時，你總是比上次更疲勞。

column1: The climb seems much longer this time, but you made it!
column2: 這次的攀登似乎更長，但你做到了！

column1: DO NOT DISTURB THE WIZARD AT REST!
column2: 「勿擾，巫師正在休息！」

column1: AND YOU WANT TO BE A HERO?!
column2: 「而且你還想成為英雄?!」

column1: YOU DON'T GIVE UP EASILY,\nDO YOU?
column2: 「你不輕易放棄，是吧？」

column1: OH!  YOU AGAIN!
column2: 「哦！又是你！」

column1: STAND FAST!
column2: 「站好!」

column1: CAN YOU HEAR ME?\nYOU HAVE THREE QUESTIONS.
column2: 「你能聽到我嗎？你有三個問題。」

column1: HERE COME YOUR QUESTIONS:
column2: 「問題來了:」

column1: HE WHO WOULD THE WIZARD SEE\nFIRST MUST ANSWER QUESTIONS THREE.
column2: 「想見巫師者\n必先答三題。」

column1: BEAT IT, BUB!
column2: 「滾蛋，老兄！」

column1: WRONG!
column2: 「錯誤！」

column1: ASKING QUESTIONS\nIS *MY* JOB, BUB!
column2: 提問\n是我的工作，老兄！

column1: NO THIEVES ALLOWED!
column2: 「禁止盜賊進入！」

column1: The Wizard is not running an inn.
column2: 巫師不經營客棧。

column1: THAT'S NOT NICE!
column2: 「那不太好！」

column1: WHAT IS YOUR NAME?
column2: 「你叫什麼名字?」

column1: WHAT IS YOUR FAVORITE COLOR?
column2: 「你最喜歡的顏色是什麼?」

column1: WHAT IS YOUR QUEST?
column2: 「你的任務是什麼?」

column1: WHO DO YOU SEEK HERE?
column2: 「你在這裡尋求誰?」

column1: WHAT IS THE MEAN AIRSPEED\nOF AN UNLADEN SWALLOW?
column2: 「無裝載的燕子\n平均時速是多少？」

column1: WHAT IS THE MEANING OF LIFE,\nTHE UNIVERSE, AND EVERYTHING?
column2: 「生命、宇宙和一切的意義是什麼？」

column1: IS IT FURTHER TO SHAPEIR,\nOR BY CAMEL?
column2: 「去夏皮爾距離更遠，\n還是騎駱駝？」

column1: IS THIS A YES OR NO QUESTION?
column2: 「這是不是一個是或否的問題？」

column1: WHAT IS THE BARON'S FIRST NAME?
column2: 「男爵的名字是什麼？」

column1: WHOSE SPELL PROTECTS THE TOWN?
column2: 「誰的法術保護了這個鎮？」

column1: WHAT IS THE THIEVES' PASSWORD?
column2: 「盜賊的密碼是什麼?」

column1: GOOD.  YOU'RE NOT A THIEF!
column2: 「很好。你不是盜賊！」

column1: THAT IS CORRECT.
column2: 那是正確的。

column1: THE WIZARD WILL NOW SEE YOU.
column2: 巫師現在會見你。

column1: GO DIRECTLY UP TO THE TOWER.\nDO NOT DALLY!
column2: 直接上塔。\n不要磨蹭！

column1: This room is full of odds, ends, and stuff.  The wizard must be a real character.
column2: 這個房間充滿了雜七雜八的東西。這位法師一定是個真正的人物。

column1: Through the portal to the east is a large formal dining room.
column2: 通往東邊的傳送門是一間寬敞的正式餐廳。

column1: Through the portal to the west is a small sitting room.
column2: 通往西邊的傳送門是一間小客廳。

column1: Against the east wall, you see a bookcase and a desk, above which is hung a dragon's head trophy.  These are on either side of a doorway, above which hangs a shield.
column2: 東牆上，你看到一個書架和一張書桌，上面掛著龍頭戰利品。這些位於一道門的兩側，上面掛著一面盾牌。

column1: Leaning against the west wall is a mummy case.  Above that hangs an ancient tapestry.
column2: 靠在西牆上的是一具木乃伊棺。上面掛著一幅古老的掛毯。

column1: The wall is plain.  You see nothing but the front door of the house.
column2: 牆很簡樸。你只看到房子的前門。

column1: Above the staircase hangs a portrait, flanked by decorative wall hangings.  In an alcove on the landing stands a suit of armor.
column2: 樓梯上方掛著一幅肖像，兩側是裝飾性的牆飾。著陸台的壁龕中擺著一套盔甲。

column1: There is a table up against the staircase wall, and on the wall hangs an onklunk.
column2: 樓梯牆邊有一張桌子，牆上掛著一個翁克倫克。

column1: It was a moderately large and fearful dragon at one time.
column2: 它曾經是一條中等大小且可怕的龍。

column1: The Ball of Light floats magically over the center of the room.
column2: 光球在房間中央上方魔法地漂浮著。

column1: You admire the plumage on the peacock.
column2: 你欣賞孔雀的羽毛。

column1: The poor stuffed peacock seems bedraggled, worn, and over-used.
column2: 可憐的填充孔雀看起來邋遢、破舊和過度使用。

column1: You can make out the runes imprinted on the\nstrange device:  "Acme Toy Co."
column2: 你可以看出刻在\n奇怪裝置上的符文：「美國精品玩具公司」。

column1: Oh my!  It's a Dunking Dragon!  You thought they were only to be found in myths and legends.
column2: 天哪！這是一條浸擊龍！你以為它們只存在於神話和傳說中。

column1: It is a standard roll-top desk.  There is nothing on it.
column2: 這是一張標準的捲簾式桌子。上面什麼都沒有。

column1: The suit of armor was made for someone much taller than you.   The plaque underneath it reads:\n  "Bequeathed by Colonel Gulden Dijon."
column2: 這套盔甲是為比你高得多的人製作的。下面的銘牌寫著：\n「金牛上校古爾登‧迪戎遺贈」。

column1: You have no idea what it is, but it has the words "Lytton P. D." inscribed upon it.
column2: 你不知道這是什麼，但它上面刻著「利頓警察局」。

column1: Dredging up your ancient Egyptian,\nyou translate the hieroglyphs:\n  "She of the golden hair."
column2: 挖掘你的古埃及知識，\n你翻譯了象形文字：\n「金髮女子」。

column1: This is an excerpt from the Rosella Stone!  There is a picture next to the message.
column2: 這是羅塞塔石碑的節錄！訊息旁邊有一幅圖片。

column1: By golly, it's a rare Peruvian Onklunk!
column2: 天哪，這是一個罕見的祕魯翁克倫克！

column1: You wonder if the portrait of Erasmus is really a painting, or something completely different.
column2: 你想知道伊拉斯謨的肖像是真的畫還是完全不同的東西。

column1: Your eyes are drawn to the eyes of the figure in the portrait.  His intense stare is unnerving.
column2: 你的眼睛被肖像中人物的眼睛吸引。他炯炯有神的凝視讓人不安。

column1: This would not be someone to trifle with, for he looks subtle and quick to anger.
column2: 這不是一個可以輕率對待的人，因為他看起來很敏銳，容易發怒。

column1: This item appears to be an ebony chest. It could contain anything.
column2: 這件物品似乎是一個烏木箱。它可能裝著任何東西。

column1: The table is bare, except for a stuffed bird which faces the door.
column2: 桌子是空的，除了一隻面向門的布娃娃鳥。

column1: The books on the shelves of the bookcase appear to be very old, very rare, and very magical.
column2: 書架上的書看起來非常古老、非常稀有和非常有魔力。

column1: You find titles like:  "Zen and the Art of Magical Maintenance",  "How to Be a Halfling",  "The Sensuous Succubus",  and "Tobin's Spirit Guide, Volume II:  Asmodeus to Casper".
column2: 你看到了一些書名，如：《禪與魔法維護的藝術》、《如何成為半身人》、《感官女魔鬼》和《托賓的精靈指南，第二卷：阿斯莫德到卡斯珀》。

column1: The shield looks like it belonged to one of those "Once and Future" kings.
column2: 盾牌看起來像是屬於某位「曾經與未來」國王的。

column1: There is a strange figure on a shelf above the stairs.  It looks like an overgrown rat wearing a wizard's hat.
column2: 樓梯上方架子上有一個奇怪的人物。看起來像一隻戴著巫師帽的巨大老鼠。

column1: An adumbrated wall hanging.  It's design evokes thoughts of ancient times and battles past.
column2: 一幅陰影掛毯。它的設計喚起了對古代和往日戰役的思想。

column1: There are two finely crafted chairs, one larger than the other.
column2: 有兩把精緻的椅子，其中一把比另一把大。

column1: Beneath the "Dunking Dragon" is an old steamer trunk.
column2: 「浸擊龍」下面是一個老式蒸汽行李箱。

column1: The only actual door visible is the door leading back outside.
column2: 唯一看得見的真正的門是通往外面的門。

column1: The staircase connects the upstairs to the downstairs.
column2: 樓梯連接樓上和樓下。

column1: The pattern is obscured by age.
column2: 圖案因年代而模糊。

column1: The high ceiling has ornate sconcing and scrollwork.
column2: 高天花板有華麗的壁燈和捲尾裝飾。

column1: Purple planking?
column2: 紫色木板？

column1: The wizard does not run an inn.
column2: 巫師不經營客棧。

column1: I wouldn't do that if I were you!
column2: 如果我是你，我就不這樣做！

column1: Those rooms are forbidden!
column2: 「那些房間是禁區！」

column1: Since you're leaving anyway...
column2: 「既然你還是要離開的話……」

column1: That'll learn ya!
column2: 「那樣就會教導你了！」

column1: Behind the wizard's stern and forbidding appearance you see a twinkle in his eye.  You suspect that he is not always as serious as he appears.
column2: 在巫師嚴肅而令人敬畏的外表背後，你看到他眼中的一絲閃光。你懷疑他並不總是看起來那麼認真。

column1: Fenrus looks back at you with a grin.
column2: 芬魯斯咧嘴回頭看著你。

column1: To the west, the Baron's castle can be seen, surrounded by forest.
column2: 向西可以看到男爵的城堡，被森林環繞。

column1: To the east, the peaks of the Wolf's Bane Mountains are clearly visible.
column2: 向東方向可以清楚看見狼禍山脈的山峰。

column1: The table is elaborately carved on the sides. Erasmus has a teacup on the table in front of him.
column2: 桌子側面雕刻精美。伊拉斯謨面前的桌上放著一個茶杯。

column1: The tea in the cup sitting on the table seems to refill itself as the wizard drinks.
column2: 坐在桌子上的杯子裡的茶似乎隨著法師的飲用而自動補充。

column1: The tea in the cup has a strange aroma.
column2: 杯子裡的茶有奇怪的香氣。

column1: The cheese looks awful, but Fenrus seems to enjoy it.
column2: 乳酪看起來很難吃，但芬魯斯似乎很喜歡。

column1: WOW!  A Real Hero!
column2: 哇！真正的英雄！

column1: The ceiling is domed and ornately decorated.
column2: 天花板是圓頂的，裝飾華麗。

column1: As you look down, you realize the steepness of the staircase you ascended to get here.  Either the wizard is a fitness fanatic, or he uses a lot of "teleport" spells.
column2: 當你向下看時，你意識到你上來的樓梯有多陡。要麼巫師是個健身狂，要麼他經常使用「傳送」法術。

column1: You're strange!  Erasmus hasn't told the punchline yet.
column2: 你很奇怪！伊拉斯謨還沒說笑點呢。

column1: Just answer the question:\nYes or No?
column2: 只要回答問題：\n是還是否？

column1: YUK-YUK-YUK-GUFFAW!
column2: 呵呵呵呵哈哈大笑！

column1: A-HYUK, A-HYUK, A-HYUK.  GAWRSH!
column2: 呀呵呵、呀呵呵、呀呵呵。天哪！

column1: Tee-Hee-Hee!
column2: 嘻嘻嘻！

column1: HO-HO-HO-HEE-HEE-HEE\n HA-HA-HA!
column2: 呵呵呵呵嘻嘻嘻\n 哈哈哈！

column1: (Snicker!)  (Chuckle!)  (Titter!)
column2: （竊竊私笑！）（輕聲笑！）（咯咯笑！）

column1: This is a serious game, Rosella!
column2: 這是一個嚴肅的遊戲，蘿莎！

column1: The chairs are rather large.  But then again, so is Erasmus.
column2: 椅子相當大。但說實話，伊拉斯謨也很大。

column1: You must first develop more skill in the magical arts.
column2: 你必須先在魔法藝術中磨練更多技能。

column1: It would be best to wait until Erasmus puts down his cup of tea before asking any more questions.
column2: 最好等伊拉斯謨放下他的茶杯後再問其他問題。

column1: The only thing I've seen the brigand warlock cast is sneezing powder.
column2: 我看過山賊術士施放的唯一東西是噴嚏粉。

column1: It's important for a magic user to know as many spells as possible.  You never know what might come in handy.
column2: 對於法師來說，掌握盡可能多的法術很重要。你永遠不知道什麼可能會派上用場。

column1: They have a supposed warlock working with them.  From what I've seen, he's more nincompoop than necromancer.
column2: 他們據說有一名術士與他們合作。從我看到的，他更多是傻瓜而不是死靈法師。

column1: I am a wizard of some reknown.
column2: 我是一位頗有名氣的巫師。

column1: Please!  The least you could do is ask me about something more interesting.
column2: 拜託！你至少可以問我一些更有趣的事情。

column1: I told you to be more interesting!  You may go now.
column2: 我告訴你要更有趣！你現在可以走了。

column1: You seem to know all of my punchlines, so...\nto SPELL with you!
column2: 你似乎知道我所有的笑點，所以…\n去拼法術吧你！

column1: Your thoughts give you away, but before you can DO anything...
column2: 你的想法洩露了你，但在你能做任何事情之前…

column1: Not in MY house you don't...
column2: 不在我的房子裡，你不行…

column1: Well, if you really must go...
column2: 好吧，如果你真的必須去…

column1: Too much for you?  I guess you should work on your magic.  Maybe next time you'll be more of a challenge.
column2: 「這對你來說太難了?我想你應該多練習魔法。也許下次會更有挑戰。」

column1: Guess I still have a knack for the old Maze game.
column2: 「我想我仍然對老迷宮遊戲有天賦。」

column1: Well, you seem to have beginner's luck.  It only took you %d game%s to win.  Hmph.  It usually takes at least %d tries for anyone less than a full Wizard to defeat me.
column2: 「嗯，你似乎有初學者的運氣。你只花了 %d 局就贏了。嗯哼。對於不是完全巫師的人來說，通常至少需要 %d 次才能打敗我。」

column1: As I promised, I shall now teach you the spell known as `Erasmus' Razzle Dazzle'.
column2: 「如我承諾的那樣，我現在將教你一個被稱為『伊拉斯謨的絢彩眩目』的法術。」

column1: Well!  You don't have to rub it in, you know!
column2: 「好吧!你不用這樣嘲笑我，你知道吧!」

column1: My feet are killing me.
column2: 我的腳疼死了。

column1: You feel the sound of dainty footsteps behind you.
column2: 你感受到身後細小腳步聲的聲音。

column1: Our hero looks really lost.
column2: 我們的英雄看起來真的很迷茫。

column1: Our hero looks a little lost.
column2: 我們的英雄看起來有點迷茫。

column1: Have I been here before?
column2: 我以前來過這裡嗎？

column1: Please step where I can see you.
column2: 「請走到我能看到的地方。」

column1: There is nobody to open the portcullis.
column2: 沒有人打開城堡大門。

column1: Leave the climbing to those who know how.
column2: 爬牆還是讓懂的人去吧。

column1: You're not in a good place to climb.
column2: 你現在的位置不適合爬。

column1: The guards won't allow you to do that.
column2: 衛兵不會讓你這樣做。

column1: The castle looms over the gatehouse and looks rather impressive.
column2: 城堡俯瞰著門房，看起來相當令人印象深刻。

column1: The gatehouse is a massive structure with a portcullis closing off access to the castle.
column2: 城門樓是堅實的建築，落下的鐵柵欄擋住了城堡的入口。

column1: The towers stand out above the castle walls.
column2: 塔樓在城牆上高聳入雲。

column1: The sky is clear except for a few clouds.
column2: 天空清澈，只有幾朵浮雲。

column1: The grass seems to grow well here.
column2: 這裡的草長得很茂盛。

column1: You see the road leading past the Healer's house.
column2: 你看到通過治療師房子的道路。

column1: You see the forest.
column2: 你看到森林。

column1: Come in.  Come in.  You have been expected.  Baron von Spielburg awaits you in the castle.
column2: 「進來。進來。我們一直在等你。馮·史畢柏格男爵在城堡裡等你。」

column1: Are you speaking to me?
column2: 「你在跟我說話嗎?」

column1: Who's there? Come out and show yourself!
column2: 「誰在那兒?出來讓我看看!」

column1: Well?  Enter the castle grounds!
column2: 「好吧？進入城堡場地！」

column1: Once more the guard raises the portcullis.
column2: 守衛再次升起城門。

column1: The guard, sensing something is awry, raises the portcullis.
column2: 守衛感覺到異常，升起了城門。

column1: You're not a quick learner.  Now you known why the grass grows so well around the portcullis.
column2: 你不是個快速學習者。現在你知道為什麼草在城堡大門周圍長得這麼好。

column1: That smarts.  Maybe you should move a little more quickly.
column2: 那很疼。也許你應該快一點。

column1: Had you been healthier, you probably could have survived that fall.  In your weakened condition, however, you succumbed to your injuries.
column2: 要是你身體更健康,也許能活下來。但在你體弱的狀況下,你死於傷口。

column1: Experience is the best teacher.
column2: 經驗是最好的老師。

column1: Practice makes perfect.
column2: 熟能生巧。

column1: Try, try again etc...
column2: 再接再厲…

column1: Take a break.  It's Mueller time.
column2: 休息一下。是時候放鬆了。

column1: The gatehouse is more difficult to climb than it first appeared.
column2: 城門樓比起初看起來還要難爬。

column1: Remember what happened to Humpty Dumpty.
column2: 記得蛋頭先生的下場嗎？

column1: After climbing down the wall you says to yourself, "Self", you says, "that wasn't too bad."
column2: 爬下牆後，你對自己說：「自己」，你說：「那不太糟糕。」

column1: You can't see much of the gatekeeper over the battlements.
column2: 你無法從城牆看到守門人的太多細節。

column1: As you can see, the gate is open.
column2: 「如你所見，大門是開著的。」

column1: I will not open for a faceless voice. Come out where I can see you.
column2: 「我不會為一個無臉的聲音打開。出來讓我看到你。」

column1: Very well.
column2: 「好吧。」

column1: If you wish.
column2: 「如果你願意。」

column1: As you can see, the gate is closed.
column2: 「如你所見，大門是關著的。」

column1: Don't keep everybody waiting with idle talk.
column2: 不要用閒談讓大家等待。

column1: Hay is for horses.
column2: 「乾草是給馬的。」

column1: I'm here!  What do you want?
column2: 「我在這裡！你要什麼？」

column1: What?
column2: 「什麼？」

column1: Then go, before I close the portcullis.
column2: 「那麼去吧，在我關上城門之前。」

column1: Sorry.  I wasn't listening.  Could you repeat youself?
column2: 「抱歉。我沒在聽。你可以重複一遍嗎？」

column1: OK.
column2: 「好的。」

column1: Go ahead.
column2: 前進吧。

column1: My name is Karl.
column2: 「我叫卡爾。」

column1: That's me. I guard the portcullis day after day.
column2: 「那是我。我日復一日地守著城門。」

column1:
The portcullis is the metal grate that bars the entrance to the castle yard.	「城門是金屬柵欄，阻擋著城堡院子的入口。
This is the Castle of the Baron Stefan von Spielburg, Lord of this Valley."
column2: 「這是史蒂芬·馮·史畢柏格男爵的城堡，此山谷的領主。」

column1: My Lord is busy organizing his troops.
column2: 「我的領主正忙著組織他的軍隊。」

column1: My Lord sees no one since the loss of his son and daughter.
column2: 「自從失去兒子和女兒後，我的主人就不見任何人。」

column1: Ten years ago the Baron's daughter, Elsa, was stolen away by magic.  Five years back, the Baronet Barnard von Spielburg went on a hunt from which he did not return.
column2: 「十年前，男爵的女兒艾爾莎被魔法偷走了。五年前，小男爵巴納德·馮·史畢柏格去狩獵，卻沒有回來。」

column1: This was all clearly part of Baba Yaga's curse.
column2: 「這顯然都是芭芭雅嘎詛咒的一部分。」

column1: The Baronet is busy hibernating.
column2: 「小男爵正在冬眠。」

column1: The Baronet Barnard von Spielburg was a proud young man with the dark hair and eyes of his father, the Baron.
column2: 「小男爵巴納德·馮·史畢柏格是個驕傲的年輕人，有著像他父親男爵一樣的黑髮和眼睛。」

column1: He rode off one morning and his horse returned without him.  The horse had been raked by the claws of some large animal.  There is still a reward offered for the safe return of the Baronet, but I fear he is dead.
column2: 「他一早騎馬出去，馬卻獨自回來了。馬被某隻大型動物的爪子抓傷了。仍有賞金懸賞小男爵的安全歸還，但我恐怕他已經死了。」

column1: Elsa von Spielburg was a lovely young child with braided blond hair and bright blue eyes, like her mother, the Baroness, used to have.
column2: 「艾爾莎·馮·史畢柏格是個可愛的小女孩，有著編成辮子的金髮和明亮的藍眼睛，就像她母親女男爵從前一樣。」

column1: A large winged creature flew over the castle walls, grabbed Elsa, and vanished before the guards could fire upon it.  We searched everywhere for her, but to no avail.
column2: 「一隻巨大的有翅膀的生物飛過城堡高牆，抓走艾爾莎，在守衛開火前消失了。我們到處尋找她，但徒勞無功。」

column1: Even the jester, Yorick, joined the search for her.  The Baron sent a troup of guards to storm Baba Yaga's hut, but I think their skulls sit atop her fence now.
column2: 「連弄臣尤瑞克都加入了尋找她的行列。男爵派一隊守衛去突襲芭芭雅嘎的小屋，但我想他們的頭顱現在就擺在她的柵欄上了。」

column1: Elsa von Spielburg would be about eighteen now.
column2: 「艾爾莎·馮·史畢柏格現在應該約十八歲了。」

column1: The stablekeeper could use a strong young person to help clean out the stable.  It does not pay much, but it is good, honest work.  If you would like to take the job, just ask me to open the portcullis.
column2: 「馬廄管理人需要一個強壯的年輕人幫忙清理馬廄。薪資不多，但這是誠實的好工作。如果你想做這份工作，就請我打開城門吧。」

column1: Cleaning up after the horses is a good way to build up your muscles.
column2: 「清理馬匹是鍛鍊肌肉的好辦法。」

column1: The Baron ordered Baba Yaga to leave and she first cursed the graveyard.  When he then sent the Baronial Guards after her, only the captain returned to the castle.
column2: 「男爵命令芭芭雅嘎離開，她先詛咒了墓地。當他派男爵衛隊追捕她時，只有隊長回到了城堡。」

column1:
He spoke the words,
   'Hear me, oh mighty Baron.
Thou hast angered me, and thus you are cursed.
All that you value you shall lose;
all that you treasure shall be taken from you.'
The captain then dissolved into a pool of sticky blackness.
column2:
「他說出了這些話，
「聽著，偉大的男爵啊。
汝激怒了我，故汝被詛咒。
汝所珍視者汝將失去；
汝所寶愛者將被奪走。」
隊長隨即化作了一池黏稠的黑色物質。」

column1: He was a very jolly fool who loved to laugh.  He was devoted to Elsa and swore he would not return until Elsa was safe.
column2: 「他是個非常活潑愛笑的傻瓜。他對艾爾莎很執著，發誓在艾爾莎安全之前不會回來。」

column1: This powerful Ogress is the cause of all the Baron's misfortune.  She is a vile creature who knows much magic.
column2: 「這個強大的女食人妖是男爵所有不幸的根源。她是個邪惡的生物，懂得很多魔法。」

column1: There is still a reward of 50 gold offered for the safe return of the Baronet and Elsa von Spielburg.
column2: 「仍有 50 金幣的賞金懸賞小男爵與艾爾莎·馮·史畢柏格的安全歸還。」

column1: For the capture or death of the brigand Leader there is the reward of 60 gold and the title of 'Hero of the Realm of Spielburg'.
column2: 「捕捉或擊殺山賊首領可獲得 60 金幣與『史畢柏格領地之英雄』的稱號。」

column1: The brigands are protected by a magic user.  Not much is known about him, but he seems to use more magic potions and powders than spells.
column2: 「山賊由一位法師保護。關於他知之甚少，但他似乎更常使用魔法藥水和粉末而非法術。」

column1: The leader of the brigands must be a good strategist.  He has organized and led many raids which result in much treasure and little loss on the part of the brigands.  Without their leader, the brigands would soon flee.
column2: 「山賊首領必定是個傑出的策士。他組織並領導了許多掠奪，帶來大量戰利品卻傷亡甚少。沒有他們的首領，山賊很快就會逃散。」

column1: There are now too few guards to take out the brigands, who still rob our people and drive away the traders.  They hide somewhere near the edge of this valley.
column2: 「現在守衛太少了，無法對付仍在搶劫人民、驅趕商人的山賊。他們躲在山谷邊緣某處。」

column1: This was once a very wealthy valley.  Many merchants have been robbed by the brigands.  There is a merchant in town who could tell you about his recent loss.
column2: 「這曾是個非常富裕的山谷。許多商人都被山賊搶劫過。鎮上有個商人可以告訴你他最近的損失。」

column1: Baba Yaga cursed the graveyard such that the dead can not rest at night.  One cannot go near the graveyard at dark without risking one's own death unless one uses protection.
column2: 「芭芭雅嘎詛咒了墓地，使死者在夜間無法安息。夜間無法靠近墓地而不冒生命危險，除非使用保護。」

column1: There is an unguent you can purchase from the Healer which protects against the dead that walk at night.
column2: 「治療師那裡有可以購買的藥膏，可以保護你免受夜間行走的死者傷害。」

column1: 'Hero of the Realm of Spielburg' is a title of respect and honor.  A true hero can release this land from its curse by using intelligence and courage.
column2: 「『史畢柏格領地之英雄』是尊敬與榮譽的稱號。真正的英雄可以用智慧與勇氣將這片土地從詛咒中解放出來。」

column1: There are many foul creatures stalking the forest.  The Baron has too few guards to protect this valley from them.
column2: 「許多邪惡的生物在森林中遊蕩。男爵守衛太少，無法保護此山谷免受它們的侵害。」

column1: Spielburg Valley was once a prosperous and peaceful place before the Baron lost his family.  Now the Baron will not leave his castle and has no idea of the ruin this land has come to.
column2: 「在男爵失去家人之前，史畢柏格山谷曾是個繁榮而平和的地方。現在男爵不願離開他的城堡，也不知道這片土地的衰落。」

column1: Baba Yaga has her hut to the northwest.  It is a very dangerous place and many Baronial Guards were lost there.
column2: 「芭芭雅嘎的小屋在西北方。這是個非常危險的地方，許多男爵衛隊在那裡喪生。」

column1: The Baroness died soon after the birth of her daughter.  The Baron still mourns for her.
column2: 「女男爵在生下女兒後不久就去世了。男爵仍在為她哀悼。」

column1: The Baron von Spielburg lost most of his guards trying to defeat Baba Yaga.
column2: 「馮·史畢柏格男爵在試圖擊敗芭芭雅嘎時失去了大部分衛兵。」

column1: The gatehouse is a shelter and resting place for the guards.
column2: 「城門樓是守衛的棲身所和休息地。」

column1: Everybody talks about the weather, but nobody does anything about it.
column2: 「每個人都談論天氣，但沒人對此做什麼。」

column1: The Weapons Master often practices in the castle courtyard.  He gives lessons in the art of swordplay.  The lessons are expensive, but well worth the price.
column2: 「武器大師經常在城堡院子裡練習。他教授劍術的課程。課程很貴，但物有所值。」

column1: I wouldn't know about that, I don't get around much these days.
column2: 「我不清楚，我最近沒什麼走動。」

column1: The castle is securely locked for the night.
column2: 城堡在夜間被牢牢鎖上。

column1: The portcullis has been drawn, and the way to the castle is clear.
column2: 城門已放下，通往城堡的道路暢通無阻。

column1: The portcullis presents a severe obstacle for entry to the castle.
column2: 城門對進入城堡造成了嚴重的阻擋。

column1: The castle of the Baron Stefan von Spielburg, Lord of the valley.
column2: 史蒂芬·馮·史畢柏格男爵的城堡，山谷的領主。

column1: The gatehouse is a massive structure which houses the guards.
column2: 城門樓是座巨大的結構體，裡面住著守衛。

column1: The gatehouse is a massive structure.
column2: 城門樓是座巨大的結構體。

column1: The mountains in the distance ring the valley of Spielburg, creating a formidable barrier to travelers.
column2: 遠處的山脈環繞著史畢柏格山谷，對旅人構成了可怕的障礙。

column1: This is the barracks area.
column2: 這是兵營區。

column1: The castle is northeast of here
column2: 城堡在此地東北方

column1: The building's appearance is deceptive; it evidently extends well into the castle wall.  From your present vantage point, the barracks looks like it could house some sixty guards.
column2: 這棟建築的外觀很會騙人；它明顯深入城牆內部。從你目前的角度看，兵營似乎可以容納六十多名衛兵。

column1: You see the castle and the guards.
column2: 你看到城堡和守衛。

column1: You see the main courtyard of the castle and the stables beyond.
column2: 你看到城堡的主院子和遠處的馬廄。

column1: Along the wall to the southeast is the gatehouse.  A line of bushes partially obscure a defensive pit along the wall.
column2: 沿著東南牆邊是城門樓。一排灌木部分遮擋了牆邊的防禦溝渠。

column1: You can see the barracks and a man sitting in front of them.
column2: 你可以看到兵營和坐在前面的一個人。

column1: The guard would object.
column2: 衛兵會反對。

column1: You barely get to sleep when the guard on night patrol kicks you out.
column2: 你才剛要睡著,巡邏的守衛就把你踢出去了。

column1: The guard looks like he is either very tired or very lazy.
column2: 這衛兵看起來要不是累死了，要不就是懶死了。

column1: You're not close enough.  He's hard of hearing.
column2: 你靠得不夠近。他聽力很差。

column1: If you want to fight, go talk to the Weapons Master.
column2: 「如果你想打鬥，去跟武器大師談話。」

column1: As you approach the guard with your weapon, he jumps up and opens the barracks door as he yells for help.  Four guards playing cards inside the barracks quickly grab their weapons and overwhelm you.  As you are dragged into the castle and down into the dungeon, you realize that was probably not quite the right thing to do.
column2: 當你拿著武器靠近守衛時，他跳起來打開兵營的門，同時大聲求救。兵營裡玩牌的四名守衛迅速拿起武器將你制服。當你被拖進城堡下到地牢時，你意識到那可能不是個好主意。

column1: I'm not here to answer your stupid questions.  Go talk to Karl the gatekeeper.  He talks to anyone about almost anything.
column2: 「我不是來回答你笨問題的。去找城門守衛卡爾。他什麼都跟人談。」

column1: Just a fence.
column2: 只是個柵欄。

column1: These are the mountains that ring the valley of Spielburg.
column2: 這些是環繞史畢柏格山谷的群山。

column1: This is the forest that surrounds the castle.
column2: 這是環繞城堡的森林。

column1: The guards patrol from here.
column2: 衛兵在這裡巡邏。

column1: Go away.  This is just the barracks for us guards.
column2: 「走開。這只是我們衛兵的兵營。」

column1: You are in the courtyard of Castle Spielburg.
column2: 你在史畢柏格城堡的院子裡。

column1: Nobody there.
column2: 沒人在那裡。

column1: There are two guards near the castle door.
column2: 城門附近有兩名衛兵。

column1: He isn't here at the moment.
column2: 他現在不在這裡。

column1: What you see, along with what you smell, gives the impression that the building on the east side of the courtyard contains the stables.
column2: 根據你所看所聞，東側院子的建築物似乎是馬廄。

column1: On the west side of the courtyard is a building that looks like soldier's barracks.
column2: 院子西側有座看起來像是士兵兵營的建築。

column1: As a matter of fact, there's a soldier sleeping in front of it now.
column2: 說實話，現在有個士兵睡在它前面。

column1: The door to the Baron's castle is well guarded.
column2: 男爵城堡的門警衛森嚴。

column1: As you look back at the castle gate, you see a line of bushes along the south wall and the gatehouse and portcullis.
column2: 當你回頭看向城堡大門，你看到南牆邊一排灌木、城門樓和城門。

column1: You barely get to sleep when you hear the guards searching the castle grounds for bums.  You decide it's time to make like a tree and leave.
column2: 你才剛要睡著，就聽到衛兵在城堡院地搜查流浪漢。你決定該溜之大吉了。

column1: There are too many guards around.
column2: 周圍衛兵太多。

column1: Haven't you got anything better to do than hang around here?  Come back some other time you want to get rich.
column2: 「你沒有比在這裡閒逛更好的事做嗎？等你想發財時再回來。」

column1: The stable looks much better since you cleaned it.
column2: 自從你清理過後,馬廄看起來好多了。

column1: It doesn't look like the stableman spends much of his time cleaning.
column2: 看起來馬夫沒有花太多時間在清理上。

column1: The castle's stable looks like it holds about six horses.  It could use some cleaning up.
column2: 城堡的馬廄看起來可以容納約六匹馬。它需要清理一下。

column1: The Baron was once known for his many fine and noble steeds.  Only a few aging animals remain in his stable.
column2: 男爵曾以他許多優良高貴的馬匹聞名。他的馬廄裡只剩下幾匹老馬。

column1: The moon lights the Baron's courtyard.
column2: 月光照亮男爵的院子。

column1: It is a beautiful day.
column2: 這是個美好的一天。

column1: You have raked the manure and covered the stable floor with new straw.
column2: 你把糞便耙乾淨,用新的稻草覆蓋馬廄地面。

column1: In the stable, the manure is beginning to pile up.
column2: 馬廄裡的糞便開始堆積起來。

column1: The castle is across the courtyard to the northeast.
column2: 城堡在院子的東北側對面。

column1: The stable marks the eastern boundary of the Baron's courtyard.
column2: 馬廄標示了男爵院子的東邊界。

column1: Across the main courtyard to the west are the soldiers' barracks.
column2: 跨過主院子西側是士兵的兵營。

column1: There is a fenced corral next to the stable building.
column2: 馬廄建築旁邊有個圍欄馬圈。

column1: A low-looking lout.  HE could use some cleaning up.
column2: 一個長相粗陋的傢伙。他倒是需要打理一下。

column1: He's not standing where you can see him.
column2: 他不在你能看到的地方。

column1: Don't tire me out with a bunch of questions.  Can't you see what a hard life I have?
column2: 「別用一堆問題煩我。你看不出我有多辛苦嗎?」

column1: The stableman can't hear you.
column2: 馬夫聽不到你說話。

column1: It's too early in the day; come back later.
column2: 現在太早了,改天再來。

column1: It would be more comfortable to sleep on clean hay in the stable.
column2: 在馬廄的乾淨乾草上睡覺會更舒服。

column1: Hey you!  It's bad enough you hang around here all day, but I sure ain't gonna let you sleep here before you've worked enough to earn it!
column2: 「喂,你!你整天在這裡閒晃就已經夠討厭的了,但我絕不會讓你在沒有好好工作前在這裡睡覺!」

column1: Wait a minute!
column2: 等等！

column1: If you put in a day's work, and you wanna take a nap, it's OK with me.
column2: 「如果你做了一天的工作，想睡個午覺，我沒意見。」

column1: These horses, they act just like animals or somethin'.
column2: 「這些馬，牠們就像動物或什麼的。」

column1: Just get out and wander around a little.  You'll find out.
column2: 「出去走一走,到處逛逛。你自己會發現的。」

column1: Don't ask me about anything to do with magic.  I don't wanna know.
column2: 「別問我關於魔法的事。我不想知道。」

column1: Those things scare me silly!
column2: 「那些東西把我嚇得無法思考!」

column1: Ha-ha-ha-ha-ha!  Hee-hee-hee-ho-ho!
column2: 「哈哈哈哈哈!嘻嘻嘻嚯嚯!」

column1: Sure!  C'mon in and grab a rake.
column2: 「當然！進來拿把耙子吧。」

column1: Don't tire me out with a bunch of questions.  I don't need your comments, either.  Can't you see what a hard life I have?
column2: 「別拿一堆問題煩我。我也不需要你的評論。難道你看不出我的生活有多辛苦嗎？」

column1: Sorry, the Baron's horses aren't for sale.
column2: 「抱歉，男爵的馬不出售。」

column1: Neigh!  says the horse.
column2: 「嘶！」馬叫道。

column1: OK.  C'mon over here.
column2: 「好吧,來這邊。」

column1: The stableman hands you some coins and says,  "Now you're five silvers richer."
column2: 馬夫遞給你一些銀幣,說:「現在你多了五個銀幣。」

column1: You curl up on some clean hay and get some rest.
column2: 你蜷縮在乾淨的乾草上休息。

column1: It IS a rude awakening, however.
column2: 不過那是粗暴的驚醒。

column1: WHAT DO YOU THINK THIS IS, A REST HOME???  GET TO WORK!  NOW!!!
column2: 「你以為這是什麼地方,養老院嗎???去工作!現在就去!!!」

column1: Well, if you don't want to answer...
column2: 「好吧，如果你不想回答…」

column1: Good!  Come in here and take a rake.
column2: 「好!進來這裡拿耙子。」

column1: Too bad!  I could use some help."
column2: 可惜了！我需要一些幫助。」

column1: That's what I'm asking YOU about, nimrod.
column2: 「我正是在問你這個呆瓜。」

column1: I see you're back.  Need some work?
column2: 「我看你回來了,需要工作嗎?」

column1: Someone approaches you.
column2: 有人走了過來。

column1: You lookin' for some work?
column2: 「你在找工作嗎?」

column1: Quit fidgeting and answer me...yes or no!
column2: 「別亂動，回答我…是或否！」

column1: So you says to yourself, "Self", you says...
column2: 所以你對自己說，「自己」，你說…

column1: You face the main doors of the Baron's keep.
column2: 你面對男爵城堡的大門。

column1: The castle grounds look like no one has taken care of them.  The castle is still impressive close up, but it looks rather dirty
column2: 城堡周圍看起來沒人打理。城堡近距離看起來仍然令人印象深刻，但看起來相當髒亂

column1: You see the castle and the guards
column2: 你看到城堡和守衛

column1: Along the wall to the southeast, you can see the stables.
column2: 沿著東南牆邊，你可以看到馬廄。

column1: You see the main courtyard of the castle.
column2: 你看到城堡的主院子。

column1: Along the wall to the southwest, you can see the barracks.
column2: 沿著西南牆邊，你可以看到兵營。

column1: The doors are securely locked for the night.
column2: 大門已經牢牢上鎖過夜了。

column1: The doors are guarded by Frederick and Pierre.
column2: 大門由腓特烈和皮埃爾守衛。

column1: The castle walls are very high.  You judge that you would not be able to climb them.
column2: 城堡的牆壁非常高。你判斷你爬不上去。

column1: There is no response.
column2: 沒有人回應。

column1: The doors are securely locked and barred from the inside.
column2: 大門已經用鎖和門栓從裡面牢牢封住了。

column1: Plays a mean horn.  A member in good standing of the musicians guild.
column2: 會吹響亮的號角。音樂家公會的尊敬會員。

column1: You don't think the guards would allow you to do so.
column2: 你認為守衛不會允許你這樣做。

column1: No sooner do you draw your weapon then the guards have their weapons out and quickly disarm you.  It seems that they practice with the Baron's Weapons Master.  They promptly drag you into the castle and down into the dungeon, which is not a very likely spot for the attainment of hero status.
column2: 你才剛拔出武器，守衛就已經拔出武器並迅速繳械你。看來他們經常與男爵的武器大師練習。他們立即把你拖進城堡下到地牢，那不是成為英雄的好地方。

column1: The horns are tarnished and starting to corrode.
column2: 號角變得暗淡，開始腐蝕。

column1: The guards look to be middle-aged, but in good physical condition.  Their clothing is faded and patched.  Even the horns look dull.
column2: 守衛看起來中年紀,但身體狀況良好。他們的衣服褪色且打過補丁。連角都看起來黯淡無光。

column1: The guards are ignoring you.
column2: 守衛忽視了你。

column1: He's very proud of his moustache, but don't mention his bald spot
column2: 他對自己的鬍子感到自豪，但別提他的禿頂

column1: Doorway to castle great hall.
column2: 通往城堡大廳的門口。

column1: The torch lights the way to the Baron's Hall.
column2: 火把照亮了通往男爵大廳的路。

column1: You can't reach the windows from these trees.
column2: 你從這些樹上無法到達窗戶。

column1: Weeds grow profusely, but nobody notices.
column2: 雜草茂密生長，但沒人注意。

column1: This is the castle of the Baron Stefan von Spielburg.
column2: 這是史蒂芬·馮·史畢柏格男爵的城堡。

column1: These flagstones are from a rock quarry in eastern Germany.
column2: 這些鋪路石來自東德的石採石場。

column1: Well...maybe the flagstones are from western Germany.
column2: 嗯…也許鋪路石來自西德。

column1: Flagstones from Europe?
column2: 來自歐洲的鋪路石？

column1: Granite from our very own mountains?
column2: 來自我們自己山脈的花崗岩？

column1: I don't know!
column2: 我不知道！

column1: The Baron von Spielburg and Baronet await you in the great hall.
column2: 馮·史畢柏格男爵和小男爵在大廳裡等著你。

column1: No one enters the castle without the Baron's permission.
column2: 「沒有男爵的許可,任何人都進不了城堡。」

column1: Says you.
column2: 「你說得好。」

column1: This is the castle of the Baron Stefan von Spielburg.
column2: 「這是史蒂芬·馮·史畢柏格男爵的城堡。」

column1: The Baron's daughter has been gone for years.
column2: 「男爵的女兒已經失蹤多年了。」

column1: I'm Frederick.
column2: 「我是腓特烈。」

column1: The barracks are off to my right, but you have no business going there.
column2: 「軍營在我右邊,但你沒有理由去那裡。」

column1: I told you not to mention it.  Now you've hurt his feelings, and he won't talk until he gets over it.
column2: 我告訴過你別提它。現在你傷害了他的感情，他要等恢復好心情才會說話。

column1: I can't help you on that.
column2: 「我在那方面幫不了你。」

column1: Looks like meathead is sulking.  If I talk to you anymore he'll get even with me.
column2: 「看起來呆瓜在生悶氣。如果我再跟你說話，他會找我算帳。」

column1: Karl is the gatekeeper.  He is a good one to answer questions.
column2: 「卡爾是門衛。他很樂意回答問題。」

column1: The Baron sees no one.
column2: 「男爵不見任何人。」

column1: We cannot leave our post.
column2: 「我們不能離開崗位。」

column1: You will have to see the Baron to get his permission.
column2: 「你得去見男爵才能得到他的許可。」

column1: The Baron is busy and will see no one.
column2: 男爵忙碌中，不見任何人。

column1: The Baronet is in the castle recovering from his ordeal and will see no one.
column2: 小男爵在城堡裡恢復，不見任何人。

column1: The Baronet Barnard von Spielburg is missing.
column2: 「小男爵巴納德·馮·史畢柏格失蹤了。」

column1: The stable is off to my left.
column2: 「馬廄在我左邊。」

column1: My name is Pierre.  I'm one of the Baron's personal guards.
column2: 「我叫皮埃爾。我是男爵的私人衛士之一。」

column1: It is not our place to answer questions.
column2: 「回答問題不是我們的職責。」

column1: Spot?  What spot?
column2: 「斑點？什麼斑點？」

column1: Why don't you ask Karl?
column2: 「你怎麼不去問卡爾?」

column1: Why thank you.  Your manners have improved.
column2: 「哦謝謝你。你的禮儀進步了。」

column1: Don't get me mad.
column2: 「別惹我生氣。」

column1: After a wonderful meal with the Baron and his son, a peaceful night's sleep in a featherdown bed, and a filling breakfast in bed, you are ready to go adventuring once more.
column2: 與男爵和他的兒子共享美食,在羽絨床上安穩入眠,還有在床上享用豐盛的早餐,你已經準備好再次踏上冒險之旅。

column1: You seem to see movement, but you are not certain what is over there.
column2: 你似乎看到了動作，但不確定那裡是什麼。

column1: The rocks look as if they were deliberately piled.
column2: 這些岩石看起來像是刻意堆積的。

column1: You see forest.
column2: 你看到森林。

column1: You see a Goblin, a small but dangerous humanoid.
column2: 你看到一隻哥布林，一個小但危險的人形生物。

column1: You see a small cave concealed in the rocks.  The cave is not big enough for you to enter.  This is where the Goblins hide.
column2: 你看到岩石中隱藏著一個小洞穴。洞穴不夠大供你進入。這是哥布林躲藏的地方。

column1: You can not see a cave.
column2: 你看不到洞穴。

column1: Something about the bushes here gives you a very bad feeling.
column2: 這裡的灌木給你一種非常不好的感覺。

column1: You're not close to anything matching that description.
column2: 你沒有靠近任何符合該描述的東西。

column1: You find a small cave concealed in the rocks, just big enough for the Goblins to come through.
column2: 你發現了岩石中隱藏著一個小洞穴，剛好夠哥布林通過。

column1: Nothing here, but you're HOT!
column2: 這裡什麼都沒有，但你很接近了！

column1: Nothing here, but you're warm.
column2: 這裡沒什麼，但你很接近了。

column1: Nothing here, you're warm.
column2: 這裡沒什麼，你接近了。

column1: Nothing here, you're cold.
column2: 這裡沒什麼，你很遠。

column1: Nothing here, you're frigid.
column2: 這裡沒什麼，你冷透了。

column1: The cave opening is too small and the rocks are too hard for you to be able to enter.
column2: 山洞入口太小，岩石太堅硬，你進不去。

column1: The rocks are too large and heavy to move.
column2: 岩石太大太重，無法移動。

column1: You can't do that.  Maybe in the next game.  We're getting behind schedule.
column2: 你做不到。也許下一部遊戲可以。我們進度落後了。

column1: Looks like no one has the stomach to fight you.
column2: 看起來沒人敢跟你打。

column1: The rock are too large and heavy to move.
column2: 岩石太大太重，無法移動。

column1: It's R2D4!
column2: 是R2D4!

column1: It's Hollywood, a virtual Goblin!
column2: 是好萊塢,一個虛擬哥布林!

column1: It's Droopy!
column2: 是垂頭喪氣!

column1: It's Sneaky!
column2: 是鬼鬼祟祟!

column1: It's Nickaroo, a real Goblin!
column2: 是尼卡魯,一個真正的哥布林!

column1: It's JohnnyFive!
column2: 是約翰尼五號!

column1: One strange bush!
column2: 真奇怪的灌木叢!

column1: A Goblin steps out of the bush.
column2: 一個哥布林從灌木叢裡走出來。

column1: The only good Goblin is a dead Goblin!
column2: 只有死亡的哥布林才是好哥布林!

column1: You find 5 silvers concealed in a pouch.  You take the silvers.
column2: 你在小袋子裡找到了5個銀幣。你把銀幣拿了。

column1: You've already taken his money.
column2: 你已經拿過他的錢了。

column1: You've seen one dead Goblin, you've seen 'em all.
column2: 你看過一具死哥布林,就等於看過他們全部。

column1: What's he staring at?
column2: 他在盯著什麼?

column1: You find 4 silvers tucked in his tunic.  He has no use for the silvers.  Into your pocket they go.
column2: 你在他的外衣裡找到 4 枚銀幣。他用不著。錢進你的口袋。

column1: Strange place to take a nap.
column2: 在奇怪的地方打盹。

column1: The poor slob was the big loser at the poker game.  He didn't even have lunch money.
column2: 那傢伙在撲克遊戲中大輸。他連午餐費都沒有。

column1: He didn't have anything.
column2: 他什麼都沒有。

column1: He's getting a sun tan?
column2: 他在曬太陽?

column1: It's your lucky day, this was the big winner at last night's poker game.  You take his 35 silvers.
column2: 你的幸運日子，昨晚撲克遊戲的大贏家。你拿了他 35 枚銀幣。

column1: A good Goblin!
column2: 一個好哥布林!

column1: In this Goblin's left shoe you find 8 silvers. You look around and slip the silvers into your pocket.
column2: 在這個哥布林的左鞋裡你找到了8個銀幣。你四下張望,把銀幣偷偷放進口袋。

column1: Does anyone know CPR?
column2: 有人會人工呼吸嗎?

column1: Clutched in his hand are 4 silvers.  You take the money.
column2: 他的手裡緊握著4個銀幣。你拿走了這些錢。

column1: Limp as a dishrag.
column2: 軟得像抹布。

column1: This guy lost all his dough to a loan shark.
column2: 這傢伙把所有的錢都輸給了高利貸。

column1: You've know he's broke.
column2: 你知道他沒錢了。

column1: He lost.
column2: 他輸了。

column1: The Goblin government lost his paycheck.
column2: 哥布林政府弄丟了他的薪水。

column1: He never had any money.
column2: 他從來沒有錢。

column1: You'll fight all right.
column2: 「你一定會打的。」

column1: Wow!  You threw that dead goblin a long way!
column2: 哇!你把那具死哥布林扔得很遠!

column1: Looking at the Centaur's muscles, his hoe, and his four gleaming hooves, you get the feeling that hostility is not the best of ideas.
column2: 看著半人馬的肌肉、他的鋤頭和四隻閃閃發光的蹄子，你覺得和他對抗不是個好主意。

column1: The majestic Centaur watches you carefully.
column2: 莊嚴的半人馬小心地注視著你。

column1: The handsome Centaur has a look of pride and dignity as he rakes his field.
column2: 英俊的半人馬邊整地邊展現出尊嚴和驕傲的神情。

column1: There's a world of difference between a horse and a Centaur!
column2: 馬和半人馬是天壤之別！

column1: The Centaur is half man, half horse.
column2: 半人馬是半人半馬。

column1: It is a sturdy iron rake with a wooden handle.
column2: 這是一把結實的鐵耙，有著木製的柄。

column1: You must go see my daughter, Hilde, who sells our fruits and vegetables on Market Street.
column2: 「你一定要去看我的女兒希爾德，她在市集街賣我們的水果和蔬菜。」

column1: Go ahead.  Ask him something.
column2: 去吧。問他點什麼。

column1: I am Heinrich Pferdefedern.  I live in town with my daughter Hilde, who sells our produce on Market Street.
column2: 「我是海因里希·費德費德恩。我和我女兒希爾德住在鎮上，她在市場街賣我們的農產品。」

column1: I'm sorry.  I know little about the Baron and his family.
column2: 對不起。我不太了解男爵和他的家族。

column1: Hilde is a bright little filly.  I'm very proud of her.
column2: 「希爾德是個聰慧的小馬駒。我為她感到驕傲。」

column1: We Centaurs are a very ancient and proud people.
column2: 「我們半人馬是古老而驕傲的民族。」

column1: It is truly a sight to see.
column2: 「那真是美景一樁。」

column1: There are a few carrots and potatoes left from last season, but I'm just starting to plant this year's crops.
column2: 「去年剩下一些胡蘿蔔和馬鈴薯，但我才剛開始種今年的莊稼。」

column1: I can grow enough on this little plot to feed my family and the village.  You should see this field at harvest time!
column2: 「我在這小塊地上能種出足夠養活我的家族和村子的糧食。你應該看看收穫時的景象！」

column1: The brigands attacked me a few months ago.  I am a strong fighter and my hooves are deadly, but there were too many of them.
column2: 「山賊幾個月前襲擊了我。我是個強悍的戰士，我的蹄子很致命，但他們人太多了。」

column1: They nearly killed me.  My right leg was broken, and there is no pride for a Centaur who cannot run.
column2: 「他們差點殺死我。我的右腿被打斷，對半人馬來說，不能奔跑就沒有尊嚴。」

column1: If it had not been for the unlikely intervention of their leader, I would be dead.
column2: 「如果不是他們的首領意外出手，我早就死了。」

column1: The leader came up while the brigands attacked me and forced them to stop, ordering them not to hurt people from the town.
column2: 「首領在山賊襲擊我時現身，強行讓他們停手，命令他們不要傷害鎮上的人。」

column1: Then, to my amazement, the leader gave me a Healing Potion and had me carried to the Healer's house.
column2: 「然後，令我驚訝的是，首領給了我一瓶治療藥水，還讓人把我送到治療師的屋子。」

column1: It was hard to judge the leader, whose face was obscured by a large helm, but there was compassion unlike a brigand.
column2: 「很難判斷首領的真面目，大頭盔遮住了臉，但那份同情心一點都不像山賊。」

column1: The Healer lives just east of here.  She is very skilled with potions.
column2: 「治療師住在這兒的東邊。她很擅長製作藥水。」

column1: The late snow has made the mountains very pretty.  Fortunately, our valley is sheltered, so the frost has not come to my fields.
column2: 「遲來的雪讓山脈變得很美。幸運的是，我們的山谷被保護著，所以霜凍沒有來到我的田地。」

column1: Fields must be carefully tended to produce the best crops.
column2: 「田地要細心照料才能長出最好的莊稼。」

column1: Our stand is in the northeast corner of Spielburg.  The produce is not of the finest this time of year, but you will not find better than Pferdefedern fruit come harvest time.
column2: 「我們的攤位在史畢柏格的東北角。這季節的農產品不是最好的，但到了收穫時，你找不到比費德費德恩果實更好的。」

column1: We grow fine fruit in our little orchard, and sell it at our stand in town.  There you will find fresh cherries in the spring, peaches in summer, and apples in the fall."
column2: 「我們在小果園裡種優質水果，在鎮上的攤位賣。春天有新鮮櫻桃，夏天有桃子，秋天有蘋果。」

column1: I'm afraid I can't tell you much about that.
column2: 「我恐怕幫不了你。」

column1: Climbing this wall is too difficult for your level of skill.  Keep practicing.
column2: 爬這面牆對你目前的技能等級來說太難了。繼續練習吧。

column1: You're not in a good spot for climbing the wall.
column2: 你現在的位置不適合爬牆。

column1: The town gate is wide open during the day.  You would look silly climbing the wall.
column2: 城門在白天敞開著。爬牆會讓你看起來很傻。

column1: The field is hoed and fertilized.
column2: 田地已經被耙過並施過肥。

column1: The garden's dark dirt awaits a new day's sunshine.
column2: 花園的黑色泥土等著新一天的陽光。

column1: You see a nicely manicured field.  A Centaur rakes the rows.
column2: 你看到一片經過精心整理的田地。一位半人馬正在耙著田壟。

column1: The trees have tiny fruit forming.
column2: 樹上長著小果子。

column1: The forest surrounds the farmer's small fields and orchard.
column2: 森林圍繞著農民的小田地和果園。

column1: The crops have yet to be planted.  There are only a few weeds and some grasses at edge of the forest.
column2: 莊稼還沒種。森林邊緣只有幾株雜草和野草。

column1: You can see the roofs of Spielburg beyond the town wall.
column2: 你能看到城牆外史畢柏格的屋頂。

column1: You can see a small house.
column2: 你能看到一棟小屋。

column1: Beyond the farmer's orchard, you see the forest.
column2: 越過農民的果園，你看到森林。

column1: Noticing that you are addressing him, the Centaur stops and gives you his attention.
column2: 注意到你在跟他說話，半人馬停了下來，把注意力轉向你。

column1: The Centaur turns toward you.
column2: 半人馬轉身面向你。

column1: The Centaur stops raking as you approach.
column2: 當你靠近時，半人馬停下鋤地。

column1: You can't tell what it is from down on the ground.
column2: 從地面上你看不出是什麼。

column1: It looks like a golden ring, flashing in the sunlight.
column2: 看起來像一枚金戒指，在陽光下閃爍。

column1: The ring appears to be made of fine gold.
column2: 這枚戒指看起來是精製的黃金。

column1: You retrieve the ring you dropped.
column2: 你撿起掉下的戒指。

column1: You'll think of something.
column2: 你會想到辦法。

column1: Go check out the nest.
column2: 去看看那個巢。

column1: How can you get something you can't see?.
column2: 你看不見的東西怎麼拿？

column1: Climb the tree first.
column2: 先爬樹。

column1: There's nothing out on the limb to risk your neck over.
column2: 樹枝上沒什麼值得冒險的。

column1: The creature looks more like a small flying lizard than a bird.
column2: 這生物看起來更像一隻小飛蜥蜴，而不是鳥。

column1: It'll be tough.
column2: 會很難。

column1: It is made of twigs, leaves, and herbs, probably stolen from the healer's garden.
column2: 它是由樹枝、葉子和草藥做成的，可能是從治療師的花園偷來的。

column1: There is a birdlike creature sitting in the nest.
column2: 一隻類似鳥的生物坐在巢裡。

column1: The nest sits on the branch.
column2: 巢坐在樹枝上。

column1: In the nest is an object which appears to be a ring, glinting in the sunlight.
column2: 巢裡有一個物體看起來像是一枚戒指，在陽光下閃閃發光。

column1: It is empty now.
column2: 它現在是空的。

column1: The remnants of the nest are scattered on the ground.
column2: 巢的殘骸散落在地面上。

column1: What's left of the nest is charred beyond recognition.
column2: 巢剩下的部分已經被燒得面目全非。

column1: It would be too messy to stow in your pack, and it doesn't appear to be good for anything.
column2: 放進背包裡會太亂，而且看起來也沒什麼用處。

column1: Perhaps you can do that.
column2: 也許你可以試試。

column1: It's a mess, and you don't need it, anyway.
column2: 這是一團亂，反正你也用不著。

column1: There's not enough left to bother with.
column2: 沒有足夠的東西值得麻煩。

column1: Ooooops!
column2: 哎呀！

column1: You think about it, but you decide to keep it in your pack.
column2: 你想了想，決定把它放在背包裡。

column1: As you try to cast a spell from your high perch on the tree, you lose your concentration, and...
column2: 當你試著從樹上的高處施法時，你失去了專注力，然後…

column1: You don't have a clear shot.
column2: 你沒有清楚的射擊角度。

column1: Move to a better position.
column2: 移動到更好的位置。

column1: That spell is no longer useful here.
column2: 那個法術在這裡沒用了。

column1: The door seems to be barred on the inside.  Perhaps you should knock.
column2: 門似乎從裡面被閂上了。也許你該敲一下。

column1: Your lock-picking skill will do you no good here.  The door is barred from the inside.
column2: 你的開鎖技能在這裡幫不上忙。門是從裡面被閂上的。

column1: The door appears to be barred on the inside.
column2: 門似乎從裡面被閂上了。

column1: Your skill in lock picking is insufficient to open a sardine can -- with a key!
column2: 你的開鎖技能還不夠強，甚至連有鑰匙的沙丁魚罐頭都打不開！

column1: There is no answer.
column2: 沒有人回應。

column1: You hear the voice of the healer saying:  "The last time you were here, I found I was missing some potions."
column2: 你聽到治療師的聲音說：「你上次來的時候，我發現我少了一些藥水。」

column1: I don't like thievery.  Go away!
column2: 「我不喜歡小偷。滾開！」

column1: You hear the inside bolt slide open.
column2: 你聽到裡面的門栓滑開。

column1: Come on in.
column2: 「進來吧。」

column1: You don't have a rock to throw.
column2: 你沒有石頭可扔。

column1: You throw a rock into the air...
column2: 你把一塊石頭扔向空中……

column1: Wait to see if you hit something.
column2: 等著看你是否擊中了什麼。

column1: That won't accomplish anything.
column2: 那樣什麼也做不了。

column1: You don't see any good hand or foot holds on this side of the tree.  Maybe it's better on the other side.
column2: 你看不到樹的這一側有好的手足著力點。也許另一邊更好。

column1: You're already up the tree.
column2: 你已經爬上樹了。

column1: You're not in a position to climb down.
column2: 你現在的位置不適合往下爬。

column1: Climb what?
column2: 爬什麼？

column1: You don't want to climb that.
column2: 你不想爬那個。

column1: The healer might be able to use them, but you can't.
column2: 治療師也許用得上，但你不行。

column1: It's gone.
column2: 它不見了。
