column1: It's been destroyed.
column2: 它被毀了。

column1: On either side of the road are a large oak and a hut.
column2: 路的兩邊各有一棵大橡樹和一間小屋。

column1: The road itself leads north to a castle in the distance and south to the crossroads.
column2: 這條路本身向北通往遠處的城堡，向南通往十字路口。

column1: The house looks cheery and well-kept.  It has the sign of the Healer above the entrance.
column2: 房子看起來明亮整潔。入口上方掛著治療師的招牌。

column1: The apothecary bottle indicates that the occupant is skilled with potions.
column2: 藥瓶表明住在這裡的人懂得配製藥水。

column1: The tree is a large oak.
column2: 這是一棵大橡樹。

column1: There is a nest on a single limb that seems to reach out toward the hut.
column2: 樹的一條樹枝上有一個巢，似乎伸向小屋。

column1: The oak tree has one dead limb.
column2: 橡樹有一條枯死的樹枝。

column1: The hut's roof is made of thatched straw.
column2: 小屋的屋頂是茅草做的。

column1: In front of the hut is a small garden in which grow the kinds of plants and herbs that are used for making potions.
column2: 小屋前有個小花園，裡面種著用來製作藥水的各種植物和草藥。

column1: You can see nothing in the dark.
column2: 你在黑暗中什麼也看不到。

column1: The curtains obscure your vision.  You can only make out a clutter of bottles, jars, and pots on the sill.
column2: 窗簾遮住了你的視線。你只能看到窗台上雜亂的瓶子、罐子和壺。

column1: There is a forest to the east.
column2: 東邊有一片森林。

column1: There is a plowed field to the west.
column2: 西邊有一片犁過的田地。

column1: The road winds northward toward a walled castle.
column2: 道路向北蜿蜒至一座城牆環繞的城堡。

column1: To the south is the crossroads in front of the town gate.
column2: 南邊是城鎮大門前的十字路口。

column1: You see the glinting object in the remains of the nest.
column2: 你看到巢的殘骸裡有閃閃發光的東西。

column1: You see nothing in the remains of the nest.
column2: 你在巢的殘骸裡看不到任何東西。

column1: You pick up a shiny gold ring.
column2: 你撿起了一枚閃亮的金戒指。

column1: You place the nest on the ground. It is of no use to you.
column2: 你把巢放在地上。它對你沒有用處。

column1: You take a lovely gold ring from the nest.
column2: 你從巢裡取出一枚漂亮的金戒指。

column1: You place the ring into your pack and the nest on the ground.
column2: 你把戒指放進背包，把巢放在地上。

column1: You see nothing in the nest.
column2: 你在巢裡什麼也看不到。

column1: Had you been healthier, you probably could have survived that fall. In your weakened condition, however, you succumbed to your injuries.
column2: 要是你身體更健康,也許能活下來。但在你體弱的狀況下,你死於傷口。

column1: That's what happens sometimes when you go out on a limb.
column2: 有時候走上樹枝時就會發生這種事。

column1:
I cannot make you another Dispel Potion unless you can convince the Dryad to give you another magic acorn.  I do not think that is likely.	「除非你能說服樹精給你另一顆魔法橡實，否則我無法給你配製驅咒藥水。我覺得這不太可能。
Please leave me to make the potion and when you return I shall have it for you.	請讓我配製藥水，等你回來時我就給你。
If you know the ingredients for a Dispel Potion and bring them here, I can make you such a potion."
column2: 「如果你知道解除魔法藥水的材料並帶來，我可以為你製作這種藥水。」

column1: Let's see, to make the Dispel Potion I still need: %s %s %s %s %s
column2: 讓我想想，要做解除魔法藥水我還需要：%s %s %s %s %s

column1: You pay the Healer for the potion and put it away.
column2: 你為藥水付錢給治療師，把它放起來。

column1: I hope this will help you.
column2: 「我希望這對你有幫助。」

column1: You find that you have less money than you thought.  You can't afford to buy the potion.
column2: 你發現你的錢比想的要少。你買不起這瓶藥水。

column1: Oh, by the way, if you happen to find a ring on your adventures, I lost my favorite gold ring.  I will give a reward to the one who returns it.
column2: 「哦，順便說一下，如果你在冒險中碰巧找到戒指，我弄丟了我最喜歡的金戒指。我會給找到它的人獎勵。」

column1: You can buy potions, get information, or sell ingredients here.
column2: 你可以在這裡買藥水、打聽消息，或賣材料。

column1: You see a clean, but very cluttered little hut filled with bottles and potions.  The Healer is busy puttering around with her potions.
column2: 你看到一間整潔但凌亂的小屋，裡面擺滿瓶瓶罐罐和藥水。治療師正忙著擺弄她的藥水。

column1: There is a small flying creature preening itself .  A ladder leads to the loft above.  A pot over the fire simmers with herbs.  It is pleasantly warm in here.
column2: 有一隻小飛行生物在梳理羽毛。一架梯子通向上面的閣樓。火上的鍋裡燉著草藥。這裡溫暖舒適。

column1: You see a middle-aged woman with rosy cheeks and smile lines around her eyes.
column2: 你看到一位中年婦女，紅潤的雙頰和眼角帶著笑紋。

column1: You see a winged lizard much like the one you saw outside.
column2: 你看到一隻翼龍，很像你在外面看到的那隻。

column1: You see bottles and flasks of a wide variety of different potions.
column2: 你看見各式各樣的瓶子和燒瓶裡裝著五花八門的藥水。

column1: Whatever is boiling there is either soup or some sort of potion.
column2: 不管那鍋裡煮的是什麼，要麼是湯，要麼是某種藥水。

column1: Soup is good for you.  Mmmm Mmmm good.
column2: 湯對你很好。嗯嗯嗯真好。

column1: It looks like a ladder.
column2: 看起來像一架梯子。

column1: It's not your soup.
column2: 這不是你的湯。

column1: Come back in the morning, please.
column2: 「請早上再來。」

column1: Don't be nosy.
column2: 別多管閒事。

column1: You can look but don't touch.
column2: 你可以看，但別摸。

column1: A rather motherly-looking woman with a look of vagueness that belies her shrewdness.  She wears an apron over her dress and a scarf wrapped around her head.
column2: 一位看起來相當慈祥的女性，眼神飄遠，但掩蓋不住她的精明。她穿著圍裙，頭上纏著頭巾。

column1: Those fairies are cute, but they play rough!
column2: 「那些妖精很可愛，但調皮得很！」

column1: It is shaped in gold like a braid of the herb Althelas with entwined leaves.
column2: 「它是用黃金製成的，形狀像柄草植物的辮子，葉子交纏在一起。」

column1: I don't know how I lost it.  I hardly ever take it off.
column2: 「我不知道怎麼丟的。我幾乎從不摘下來。」

column1: You already have your reward.
column2: 「你已經得到你的獎勵了。」

column1: I will give six golds to the person who returns my ring.  It was a gift when I graduated from the college of Healing.
column2: 「誰把我的戒指還給我，我就給誰六枚金幣。這是我從治療學院畢業時的禮物。」

column1: I will pay you for Cheetaur claws, Troll beard, magic mushrooms, and flowers from Erana's Peace.
column2: 「我會買獵豹人的爪子、巨魔的鬍鬚、魔法蘑菇和伊瑞娜的安寧之地的花。」

column1: I use them to make my potions.
column2: 「我用它們配製藥水。」

column1: This potion is used to heal damage and keep wounds from getting worse.  It has gotten pretty dangerous around here, and a Healing Potion can be quite useful in a serious situation.
column2: 「這種藥水用來治療傷害並防止傷口惡化。這裡變得很危險，治療藥水在危急情況下能派上大用場。」

column1: Vigor Potion is used to revitalize yourself after vigorous exercise.   It helps to restore stamina.
column2: 「活力藥水用來在劇烈運動後恢復活力。它有助於恢復耐力。」

column1: Magic Potions restore the energy needed to cast spells."
column2: 「法力藥水能恢復施法所需的能量。」

column1: I make up potions that heal injuries.  I will be happy to sell you a Healing Potion if you like.
column2: 「我配製治療藥水。如果你需要，我很樂意賣你治療藥水。」

column1: I make and sell Healing Potions, Magic Potions, Vigor Potions and Undead Unguent.
column2: 「我配製和販賣治療藥水、法力藥水、活力藥水和不死藥膏。」

column1:
The Potions will cost you: 
            Healing Potion 40 silvers 
            Magic Potion 60 silvers 
            Vigor Potion 20 silvers 
            Undead Unguent 100 silvers.
column2:
「藥水的價格如下：
            治療藥水 40 銀幣
            法力藥水 60 銀幣
            活力藥水 20 銀幣
            不死藥膏 100 銀幣。」

column1: Undead Unguent is used to drive off the minor undead such as zombies or floating spirits.  It doesn't last long, so you should use it only when you are anticipating an encounter with such things.
column2: 「不死藥膏用來驅趕小型不死生物，比如殭屍或浮靈。效果不會維持太久，所以只在預計會遇到這類東西時使用。」

column1: There are not too many zombies in this land... too damp, I suppose.  There are some floating spirits, I have heard.
column2: 「這片土地上殭屍不太多……太潮濕了，我想。不過我聽說有浮靈。」

column1: Floating Spirits are the ghosts which usually hang around graveyards at night.  They are dangerous, so don't go near them unless you use Undead Unguent first.
column2: 「漂浮靈魂是通常在夜間在墳墓周圍徘徊的幽靈。它們很危險，所以除非你先使用不死藥膏，否則不要靠近它們。」

column1: Stamina is a measure of the energy the body uses as you work or play.
column2: 「耐力是衡量你工作或玩樂時身體使用的能量。」

column1: I've never heard of a monster around here with green fur.  Rare things are sometimes by their nature magical.
column2: 「我從未聽說過這裡有綠色毛皮的怪物。稀有物品有時生來就具有魔法性質。」

column1: Fairy Dust obviously comes from fairies.  They dance around mushroom rings when it is night.  Fairies are magical beings, so be careful around them.
column2: 「妖精粉顯然來自妖精。當夜幕降臨時，她們在蘑菇圈周圍舞蹈。妖精是魔法生物，所以要小心她們。」

column1: I use flowers from Erana's Peace to the north in nearly all my potions.  They have some of the magic of the place even when they are dried.
column2: 「我在幾乎所有的藥水裡都用北邊伊瑞娜的安寧之地的花。即使乾燥了，它們仍然保有那個地方的魔力。」

column1: I will pay you five silvers for a flask full of flowers.
column2: 「我會給你五枚銀幣買一瓶花。」

column1: I use the mushrooms from a fairy ring to make Magic Potions.
column2: 「我用妖精圈裡的蘑菇來配製法力藥水。」

column1: I will pay you one gold for some.
column2: 「我會給你一枚金幣買一些。」

column1: The Cheetaur looks like a cross between a panther and a man.  It is vicious and tough.  Unless you are a very tough fighter, you had best try to get away from it.  If you do manage to kill it, then I will pay you five silvers for each claw.
column2: 「獵豹人看起來像豹子和人類的混合體。它凶悍又強悍。除非你是個非常厲害的戰士，否則最好逃跑。如果你真的殺死了它，我會給你每根爪子五枚銀幣。」

column1: The Trolls around here are tough monsters that cannot stand the light of day and so are found at night or in caves.  They are very difficult to kill, but I will pay two healing potions for the beard of a Troll.
column2: 「這裡的巨魔是強悍的怪物，無法忍受日光，只在夜間或洞穴裡活動。它們很難殺死，但我會給你兩瓶治療藥水買一根巨魔鬍鬚。」

column1: I have heard that the Dryad of the woods knows a Dispel Potion to disenchant people with spells upon them.
column2: 「我聽說森林的樹精知道怎麼配驅咒藥水，用來解除人身上的咒語。」

column1: I'm not sure what kind of water that is.
column2: 「我不確定那是什麼水。」

column1: The only place you can get a magic acorn from is a Dryad's oak tree.
column2: 「魔力橡果只能從樹精的橡樹上取得。」

column1: I will pay you one silver for each empty flask you bring me so I can use it for more potions.  Waste not, want not, I always say.
column2: 「你每帶來一個空瓶子，我就給你一枚銀幣，這樣我可以用來裝新藥水。我總是說，勿浪費，勿短缺。」

column1: Well, my name is Amelia Appleberry, but mostly I am just known as the Healer around here.
column2: 「好吧，我叫阿蜜莉亞·蘋果莓，但大家都叫我治療師。」

column1: Oh, that's my pet, Pterry, the pterosaur.  He has a girl friend, Pteresa, who has a nest in the oak outside my door.  Pterry keeps me company and listens to my chatter as I work.
column2: 「哦，那是我的寵物翼龍彼得。他有個女朋友彼得薩，在我門外的橡樹上築巢。彼得陪著我，還聽我工作時嘮叨。」

column1: They are a species of flying lizard.  I understand they can grow quite large in the south.
column2: 「它們是飛行蜥蜴的一種。我聽說它們在南方能長得相當大。」

column1: Poor man, he hasn't been the same since his son and daughter were taken from him.
column2: 「可憐的人，自從他的兒子和女兒被帶走後，他就再也不像以前那樣了。」

column1: He was just a young dashing man when he last rode off and never returned.
column2: 「他上次騎馬離開時還是個年輕英俊的小伙子，但再也沒有回來。」

column1: The dear child, I can still picture her with her beautiful blond hair done up in braids.  She was so sweet.  It's hard to believe she's gone.
column2: 「親愛的孩子，我還能看到她那漂亮的金色長髮編成辮子。她真是可愛。難以相信她已經離我們而去。」

column1: Those brutes!  I'm always having to heal someone they've beaten up and robbed.  I hate thieves and brigands.
column2: 「那些野蠻人！我經常得治療被他們打劫的人。我恨小偷和山賊。」

column1: Would you believe the brigands almost killed him a while back?  Fortunately their leader made them bring Heinrich here where I could heal him.
column2: 「你能相信山賊曾幾乎殺死他嗎？幸好他們的首領命令他們把海因里希帶到我這裡，我才能救他。」

column1: Almost due north of here is the meadow called Erana's Peace.  It is a very magical area and it is a place of safety.  It is beautiful all year, for the flowers are always in bloom.
column2: 「幾乎正北方有一個叫伊瑞娜的安寧之地的草地。那是一個很有魔力的地方，也是安全的地方。它一年四季都很漂亮，因為花總是開著的。」

column1: I have the skill to use my magic to create potions.  I am a bit proud about it.  Not everyone can do that, you know.
column2: 「我有用魔法配製藥水的技能。我對此感到驕傲。並不是每個人都能做到，你知道。」

column1: Mandrake root is used in a variety of spells, mostly for evil purposes.  Mandrake must be pulled from a deadman's grave at midnight.  The root is particularly powerful.
column2: 「曼德拉草根用在各種法術裡，大多是邪惡目的。曼德拉草必須在午夜時從死人的墳墓裡拔出。根部特別強大。」

column1: I'm sorry, I just don't know much about that.  Perhaps you should ask someone else.
column2: 「對不起，我不太了解那個。也許你應該問別人。」

column1: Oh, thank you for finding my ring.  How I've missed this.  Here are six golds and two Healing Potions for your reward!
column2: 「哦，謝謝你找到我的戒指。我多麼想念它。這是六枚金幣和兩瓶治療藥水作為你的獎勵！」

column1: Thank you, but I have all the flowers I need.
column2: 謝謝，但我已經有夠多的花了。

column1: Thank you.  I often use flowers from Erana's Peace in potion making.  Here are your silvers.\nYou put them away.
column2: 「謝謝。我經常在配製藥水時用伊瑞娜的安寧之地的花。給你銀幣。」\n你把它們放起來。

column1: Thank you, but I have enough mushrooms.
column2: 謝謝，但我已經有夠多的蘑菇了。

column1: You have ruined the mushrooms with toadstools.  I can not buy your mushrooms.
column2: 你把蘑菇搞壞了，有毒菇混在裡面。我買不了你的蘑菇。

column1: These are very nice.  I'll dry them and grind them into a powder.  Let me think...oh yes! I said these are worth a gold.  Here you are.\nYou put it away.
column2: 「這些很好。我會把它們曬乾磨成粉。讓我想想……哦對！我說過這些值一枚金幣。給你。」\n你把它放起來。

column1: Do you mean to tell me that you actually managed to kill a Cheetaur?  You're quite a hero, aren't you?  Here is your money.\nYou might think about purchasing a Healing Potion in case you have to fight such monsters again.
column2: 「你是說你真的殺死了獵豹人？你真是個英雄呢。給你錢。\n你應該考慮買瓶治療藥水，以備再次跟這樣的怪物戰鬥。」

column1: Don't tell me you actually killed a Troll?  Why, you are really amazing.  I haven't had any Troll's Beard for a long time.  Here are two Healing Potions for the beard.\nYou put them away.
column2: 「別告訴我你真的殺死了巨魔？天哪，你真了不起。我好久沒見過巨魔鬍鬚了。給你兩瓶治療藥水。」\n你把它們放起來。

column1: So, you say this is one of the ingredients for the Dispel Potion.  Well, I had better get started making it.
column2: 「所以，你說這是解除魔法藥水的一種材料。好吧，我最好開始製作它。」

column1: Thank you.  I'm sure that I'll find a good use for Fairy Dust.
column2: 「謝謝。我相信我會找到妖精粉的好用途。」

column1: I don't need any more Flying Water.  Thank you anyway.
column2: 「我不需要更多飛水了。不過還是謝謝。」

column1: Thank you for the offer, but I really don't need any water.
column2: 「謝謝你的提議，但我真的不需要任何水。」

column1: The Healer makes some sort of arcane gesture over the flask of water, then looks surprised.
column2: 治療師對著水瓶做出某種奧祕的手勢，然後看起來很驚訝。

column1: I'm sorry, but this water does not seem to have any magical potential at all.  This can't be the `Flying Water' you described.
column2: 「抱歉，但這水似乎完全沒有任何魔法潛力。這不可能是你描述的『飛水』。」

column1: The Healer makes some sort of arcane gesture over each flask of water, then smiles.
column2: 治療師對著每個水瓶做出某種神秘的手勢，然後微笑。

column1: The Healer makes some sort of arcane gesture over the flask of water, then smiles.
column2: 治療師對著水瓶做出某種奧祕的手勢，然後微笑。

column1: Flying Water. how clever.
column2: 「飛水。真聰明。」

column1: Those Meeps sound so interesting.  I'd like to meet them sometime.  I'll get to work on that potion of yours.
column2: 「那些咪普聽起來很有趣。我想有機會見見它們。我來開始製作你的藥水吧。」

column1: Thank you.  I'll save this for a later use.
column2: 「謝謝。我會存起來以後用。」

column1: So you helped the Dryad, that's nice.  She does keep the forest around here healthy.  So that's how to make a Dispel Potion, is it?  Thanks for letting me know.
column2: 「所以你幫了樹精，很好。她確實保持著這裡森林的健康。所以這就是配製驅咒藥水的方法？謝謝你告訴我。」

column1: Thank you, I always need flasks.
column2: 「謝謝，我總是需要藥瓶。」

column1: I don't have any use for that.
column2: 我用不著那個。

column1: You'll have to be more specific.
column2: 「你得說得更具體一點。」

column1: I need all my flasks for potions.  You'll have to get one somewhere else.
column2: 我所有的瓶子都要用來裝藥水。你得從別的地方找。

column1: I don't have any.
column2: 我沒有。

column1: You're getting greedy.  She'll notice if you take any more.
column2: 你貪心了。你再拿她就會發現的。

column1: You carefully grab a couple of Healing Potions and conceal them under your cape.
column2: 你小心翼翼地抓起幾瓶治療藥水，藏在你的披風下。

column1: If you're going to steal, at least wait until the Healer is not looking.
column2: 你要偷的話，至少等治療師不看的時候。

column1: You don't really think that is the correct way to actually win this game, do you?
column2: 你不會真的以為那是贏這個遊戲的正確方法吧？

column1: Please, show some respect.
column2: 拜託了，給點尊重。

column1: There is an aura of magic throughout this small cabin.
column2: 整間小屋籠罩著魔法氣息。

column1: Small on a prehistoric scale, but still a pterosaur.
column2: 按史前標準來說很小，但仍然是翼龍。

column1: Cheesecloth.
column2: 起司布。

column1: Ladder up to the loft where the Healer has her personal quarters.  No, you can't go up the ladder.
column2: 通往閣樓的梯子，那是治療師的私人房間。不行，你爬不上去。

column1: The makings of various potions.
column2: 各式藥水的材料。

column1: The pterosaur's litter box.
column2: 翼龍的貓砂盆。

column1: Iron holder fit for a lit torch.
column2: 適合放點燃火把的鐵架。

column1: The view from the window has been obscured by soot from the fire.
column2: 窗戶外的景色被爐火的煙灰遮住了。

column1: This and that.
column2: 七七八八的東西。

column1: Ingredients.
column2: 材料。

column1: Petrified icicles and greens.
column2: 石化的冰柱和綠色植物。

column1: The fragrance of the herbs mingle with some other rather pungent odors as you step into the Healer's house.
column2: 踏進治療師的家，草藥香與其他相當刺鼻的氣味混在一起。

column1: Well, what can I do for you this time?
column2: 「嗯，這次我能為你做什麼？」

column1: Lovely day, isn't it?  My, you look very healthy for an adventurer.  You must be new.
column2: 「美好的一天，不是嗎？哇，你作為一個冒險家看起來很健康。你一定是新手。」

column1: What can I do for you?  I can sell you potions if you like.  I also buy spell components if you are interested in gathering some for me.
column2: 「有什麼我能幫你的嗎？想要的話我可以賣你藥水。如果你有興趣幫我收集咒語成分，我也會買。」

column1: Don't mind me, I always have so much to do around here.
column2: 「別在乎我，我這裡總是忙得不可開交。」

column1: You say to the Healer "I have been to visit the Dryad of the Woods.  She gave me a Magic Acorn and told me the formula for a potion to dispel enchantments!"
column2: 你對治療師說「我去拜訪了樹林的樹精。她給了我一個魔法橡果，還告訴我解除魔法的藥水公式！」

column1: Here is the Potion of Dispel!  Use it by splashing it on the victim of a magic spell.  Mind you, this won't work if the spell is caused by a magic item rather than a cast spell.
column2: 「這是解咒藥水！把它潑在魔法咒語的受害者身上就行。注意，如果咒語是由魔法物品引起的而不是施放的咒語，這招就不管用。」

column1: Thank you so much.  Oh, I'm so happy to have my ring back!
column2: 「非常感謝。哦，我好高興能拿回我的戒指！」

column1: You put the reward away and tell her she is welcome as you leave to avoid being kissed again.
column2: 你把獎勵收起來，趁著離開前告訴她不用客氣，免得再被親一次。

column1: The Dispel Potion will take a while to make.  Why don't you come back later?
column2: 「解除魔法藥水需要一些時間製作。你何不稍後回來？」

column1:
Brauggi I be, to boast of my boldness,
\nStrong as the storm that sends forth the snow.
\nFiercer in fighting than foes in their fury,
\nFear now this Frost Giant fighter and flee!
column2:
Brauggi I be, to boast of my boldness,
\nStrong as the storm that sends forth the snow.
\nFiercer in fighting than foes in their fury,
\nFear now this Frost Giant fighter and flee!

column1:
Find me some fruit for to mellow my mead horn,
\nGift I will give of a gem that now glows --
\nJewel from Jotunheim, flare of the frost flame,
\nFetch to me fruit that will fill up my fists!
column2:
Find me some fruit for to mellow my mead horn,
\nGift I will give of a gem that now glows --
\nJewel from Jotunheim, flare of the frost flame,
\nFetch to me fruit that will fill up my fists!

column1: (It looks like it will take a lot of fruit to fill up THOSE fists!)
column2: （要填滿那樣的拳頭看來得花不少水果啊！）

column1: From the blue color of his skin and his huge height, you can tell this is a Frost Giant.
column2: 從他皮膚的藍色和巨大的身軀，你能看出這是個暴風巨人。

column1: The Giant (who does not seem pleased at your casting a spell in his presence) radiates a magical aura.  The strongest magic centers on the glowing gem at his belt.
column2: 巨人（看起來對你在他面前施法不太高興）散發著魔法氣息。最強的魔法聚集在他腰上那顆會發光的寶石上。

column1: You can't offer what you don't have.
column2: 你沒有的東西無法提供。

column1: The Giant looks at you in disgust.  Apparently, you did not offer what he desires.
column2: 巨人厭惡地看著你。看來你沒給他想要的東西。

column1:
Near now is North, for the winter has wandered,
\nBringing this brave one to barter so bold.
\nI journey from Jotunheim, home of the giants,
\nSource of all snowstorms, the sender of sleet.
column2:
Near now is North, for the winter has wandered,
\nBringing this brave one to barter so bold.
\nI journey from Jotunheim, home of the giants,
\nSource of all snowstorms, the sender of sleet.

column1:
Hollow my food house,
\n        for my hunt has been hindered,
\nBy having to hurry here from my home.
column2: 「我的食物倉空了啊，因為狩獵受阻，得匆匆從家趕來這裡。」

column1:
Highpoint of harvest, the finest of flavor,
\nApples or oranges, peaches or pears.
column2: 「豐收的高峰，最上乘的滋味，蘋果或柳橙，桃子或梨子。」

column1:
Mead is the mother's milk, mighty yet mellow,
\nThat brings joy to Giants and madness to men.
column2:
Mead is the mother's milk, mighty yet mellow,
\nThat brings joy to Giants and madness to men.

column1:
Glow of the frost flame that fills up the night field,
\nA jewel that is flawless, the finest of gems.
column2: 「充滿夜空的冰焰光芒，無瑕的寶石，最精美的珍珠。」

column1:
Bored I become when the banter so blindly
\nSpeaks of the subjects I care not about.
column2:
Bored I become when the banter so blindly
\nSpeaks of the subjects I care not about.

column1: Annoy me not over matters so minor.
column2: 「別為小事煩我。」

column1:
Waste not my wits, for I weary of words;
\nMy axe it is asking for action and more.
column2:
Waste not my wits, for I weary of words;
\nMy axe it is asking for action and more.

column1: The mountains rise quickly from this point and are covered with snow.
column2: 從這裡往上，群山急速上升，佈滿白雪。

column1: The snow has melted in most of the forest, but here it is still heavy.
column2: 雪在大部分森林都融化了，但這裡積得還很厚。

column1: The gem glows like an aurora in the night sky.
column2: 那顆寶石像夜空的極光一樣閃耀。

column1: You don't see a gem here.
column2: 這裡沒有寶石。

column1:
Far from the frost field, fares forth this fighter,
\nHunger has hurled me hither from home.
\nMy name it is known in the Northlands as Brauggi,
\nBarter with blade's clash, or bargain with me.
column2:
Far from the frost field, fares forth this fighter,
\nHunger has hurled me hither from home.
\nMy name it is known in the Northlands as Brauggi,
\nBarter with blade's clash, or bargain with me.

column1:
Fruit you have found, to fill all my food stores,
Thus filled the bargain, my gem you have bought.
Brauggi has bartered, and all has been answered;
The mead it may mellow, and now I head home.
column2:
你尋得的果實填滿了糧倉，
交易已成，寶石已買。
布勞基交易成，一切已說；
蜂蜜醇厚，我將歸家去。

column1:
Fiercer than frost's bite, my fury is fearless,
\nMy axe it is edged as is the ice.
\nThis warrior is willing to wield now the war drum,
\nThe battleaxe blazes and bellows for blood.
column2:
Fiercer than frost's bite, my fury is fearless,
\nMy axe it is edged as is the ice.
\nThis warrior is willing to wield now the war drum,
\nThe battleaxe blazes and bellows for blood.

column1: Next time, pick on someone your own size.
column2: 下次找個你體格相當的對手。

column1:
Courage you carry, but skill it is scanty;
\nFace not this foe before fiercer you fight.
column2: 「你有膽量啊，但技巧還差得遠；別跟我這敵手交手，除非你變得更兇悍。」

column1: Your fruit definitely makes a generous pile in the Giant's cupped hands, but they're not full yet.
column2: 你的水果在巨人的雙手窩裡堆成不錯的一堆，但還沒滿。

column1: Oops!  you say.  "I guess I don't have any fruit for you.  Sorry!"
column2: 「唉呀！」你說。「看來我沒有水果給你。抱歉！」

column1: Your meagre selection of fruit makes a paltry pile at the bottom of the Giant's cupped hands.  You'll need to get quite a few more apples to fill THOSE hands!
column2: 你那可憐兮兮的水果在巨人雙手窩底堆成一小堆。你得多拿好幾個蘋果才能填滿那樣的手！

column1: As you start to throw...
column2: 當你開始投擲時…

column1: As you begin to cast your spell...
column2: 當你開始施法時…

column1: In matters of magic, I am your master; Seek not to slay me with sendings so small.
column2: 「在魔法的事上，我才是高手；別想用那點小把戲來對付我。」

column1: Running on the ice is apparently not a very good idea.
column2: 在冰上跑看起來不是什麼好主意。

column1: This large rock covers a Meep hole.
column2: 這塊大石頭蓋住了一個咪普洞穴。

column1: A furry blue Meep
column2: 一隻毛茸茸的藍色咪普

column1: A furry purple Meep
column2: 一隻毛茸茸的紫色咪普

column1: A furry green Meep
column2: 一隻毛茸茸的綠色咪普

column1: No way!  Those Meeps are too fast and the rocks too hard for you to want to try that again!
column2: 沒門！那些咪普太快、石頭太硬了，你不會想再試一次！

column1: Try going over to where the Meeps are if you really want to hack them into little furry bits.
column2: 真想把他們砍成碎片的話，就去咪普們那邊吧。

column1: You detect no other magic here.
column2: 你在這裡沒有偵測到其他魔法。

column1: The Open spell only works on locks and small closed things.
column2: 「開啟術」只對鎖和小型密閉物品有效。

column1: You don't have a rock
column2: 你沒有石頭

column1: You can't get that from where you're standing.
column2: 從你現在站的地方拿不到那個。

column1: The Meeps would not appreciate it if you tried to take their fur -- the weather here is still very cold.
column2: 咪普們不會感激你拔它們的毛——這裡天氣還很冷。

column1: You don't see a Spell Scroll here.
column2: 這裡沒有法術捲軸。

column1: The baby Meep is just too quick for you.  Besides, they eat too much.
column2: 小咪普快得你根本追不上。而且它們食量太大了。

column1: You don't see any baby Meeps at the moment.
column2: 現在看不到小咪普。

column1: You look at the apple cores, and quickly decide they are of no use to you.  Besides, they look disgusting.
column2: 你看著蘋果核，迅速決定它們對你沒用。而且它們看起來噁心得要死。

column1: You are in the Meeps' Peep.  The colorful, furry Meeps timidly pop out of their holes from time to time.
column2: 你在咪普的窩裡。色彩繽紛、毛茸茸的咪普們不時膽小地冒出洞口。

column1: The baby Meep sure is cute.
column2: 小咪普真的很可愛。

column1: All of the Meeps are hiding deep within their holes.  Your vicious attack has them terrified.
column2: 所有咪普都躲在洞裡深處。你的凶悍攻擊嚇得它們要死。

column1: You realize that you have never seen more than one green Meep at a time.  Perhaps there is only one.
column2: 你意識到你從沒同時見過一隻以上的綠咪普。或許只有一隻。

column1: There seems to be an abundant supply of blue and purple Meeps.  If only they'd stay still for a moment, perhaps you could count them.
column2: 看起來有不少藍色和紫色的咪普。要是它們能靜一下，也許你就能數出來有幾隻。

column1: You see blue Meeps, purple Meeps, and occasionally a green Meep.  They seem to be very shy; whenever you approach one, it hides under its rock.
column2: 你看見藍咪普、紫咪普，偶爾還有綠咪普。它們看起來超害羞；你一靠近，它就躲到石頭底下。

column1: Large, heavy-looking rocks cover the many holes.  The Meeps must be stronger than they look to lift these rocks so easily.
column2: 大塊沉重的石頭蓋著許多洞。咪普們要夠強悍才能這麼輕鬆搬起這些石頭。

column1: You keep seeing differently-colored Meeps come out of each hole.  They either lead to large underground caverns, or they are all connected under the earth.
column2: 你不斷看見不同顏色的咪普從每個洞冒出來。它們要嘛通向大型地下洞穴，要嘛全部在地底連通。

column1: The trees and bushes look much like those you've seen elsewhere in the valley.
column2: 樹木和灌木看起來跟你在山谷其他地方見過的很像。

column1: The mountains are very steep here.
column2: 這邊的山峰陡得要命。

column1: The meadow is covered with a light carpet of grass, broken up by the Meep holes.
column2: 草地被薄薄的草皮覆蓋著，中間散佈著咪普的洞。

column1: There is a wad of bright green fur on the ground.
column2: 地上有一團亮綠色的毛皮。

column1: The Meeps have brightly-colored, soft-looking fur.
column2: 咪普長著色彩繽紛、軟呼呼的毛皮。

column1: It looks like a Spell Scroll.
column2: 看起來像一卷魔法捲軸。

column1: The scroll is blank now, but once contained the Detect Magic spell.
column2: 捲軸現在是空白的，但曾經含有偵測魔法法術。

column1: You can't talk to any of the Meeps.  They are all hiding deep within their holes, terrified after your vicious attack.
column2: 你沒辦法跟任何咪普說話。它們都躲在洞裡深處，被你的凶悍攻擊嚇得要死。

column1: Hey, just ask me about anything you want to know.
column2: 「嘿，有什麼你想知道的儘管問我。」

column1: You don't see any Meeps to talk to at the moment.
column2: 現在看不到能說話的咪普。

column1: We use rocks for doors.  They keep us dry and warm.
column2: 「我們用石頭做門。它們讓我們保持乾燥溫暖。」

column1: We are happy Meeps, living in our happy holes.  Don't worry.  Be happy!
column2: 「我們是快樂的咪普，住在我們快樂的洞裡。別擔心。要開心！」

column1: Hey, go ahead, take it. It's yours.
column2: 「嘿，去吧，拿去。它是你的。」

column1: Hey, like fur is good stuff, keeps us warm.  Mine's the best -- it's like green, you know?
column2: 「嘿，咦，毛皮是好東西啊，能讓我們暖和。我的最讚──是綠色的，你知道吧？」

column1: Gee, boss, I really don't know much about that at all.  Hey, sorry.
column2: 「嘿，老闆，我真的對那一竅不通。嘿，抱歉。」

column1: Why, that's me!
column2: 嘿，那是我！

column1: Man, those rocks are tough!
column2: 天啊，那些石頭可硬了！

column1: You hear squeaky muttering from beneath the ground.  It seems the Meeps are having quite a discussion about you.
column2: 你聽見地底傳來吱吱嘎嘎的碎碎念。看來咪普們正在熱烈討論你呢。

column1: Hiya, hiya.  Nice to seeya 'gain.
column2: 嘿呀，嘿呀。很高興又見到你了。

column1: Hiya, hiya.  Really pleased to meetcha.
column2: 嘿呀，嘿呀。很高興認識你。

column1: Hey, there ya go.  Hope it helps ya.
column2: 「嘿，給你了。希望能幫到你。」

column1: Hey, sorry I couldn't help ya.
column2: 「嘿，抱歉我幫不了你。」

column1: Oh, you want %s?  I think I have %s somewhere around here.
column2: 哦，你要 %s？我想我這邊某個地方有 %s。

column1: Gosh, I don't seem to have anything like that left.  Really sorry 'bout that.
column2: 天啊，我好像沒有那種東西了。真的很抱歉。

column1: You pick up the pile of soft green fur and carefully place it in your pack.
column2: 你拿起那堆柔軟的綠色毛皮，小心地放進背包。

column1: You pick up the Spell Scroll.  As the magical runes fade, you find you now know how to cast the "Detect Magic" spell.
column2: 你撿起魔法捲軸。當符文褪去時，你發現自己現在知道怎麼施展「偵測魔法」法術了。

column1: The magical runes fade from the paper before your eye can focus on them.
column2: 魔法符文在你眼睛聚焦前，就從紙上消褪了。

column1: As the ghosts suck the life out of your body, and you crumple into the dust of the old graveyard, you think unspeakable thoughts of torture and torment directed at programmers, game designers, and vicious artists named Kenn.
column2: 當鬼魂吸乾你的生命，你倒在古老墓地的塵埃裡，你想著要對程式設計師、遊戲設計師，和一位叫肯恩的兇狠美術設計進行難以名狀的折磨。

column1: The wall is too difficult for your level of skill, but keep practicing.
column2: 這道牆對你的技能等級來說太困難了，但要繼續練習。

column1: You would have trouble convincing people you are a Hero if you climbed the wall into town during the day.
column2: 如果你在白天爬牆進城，你將難以說服人們你是英雄。

column1: The large tombstone seems to have a barely legible epitaph:
column2: 那塊大墓碑上的墓誌銘看起來幾乎無法辨認：

column1: Here lies an atheist...
column2: 「這裡躺著一個無神論者…」

column1: All dressed up\nand nowhere to go.
column2: 「衣著華麗卻無處可去。」

column1: Growing up out of the grave is an evil-looking plant with a slimy, poisonous sheen.
column2: 從墳墓中長出一株邪惡的植物，散發著黏稠、有毒的光澤。

column1: The large stone you are facing has been engraved with this message:
column2: 你面前的這塊大石頭上刻著這樣的訊息：

column1: Here lies Lester Moore\nGored six times by a wild boar.
column2: 「此墓：萊斯特·摩爾，被野豬戳傷六次。」

column1: No Les, no Moore.
column2: 「沒有萊斯，沒有摩爾。」

column1: The carving on the gravestones, for the most part, seems to have been intentionally defaced and worn with time.
column2: 墓碑上的刻文大多數看起來是故意被破壞、經年累月磨損的。

column1: You sense what nobody needed to tell you:  SOMETHING is making those spirits fly around, and it isn't the wind.
column2: 你感覺到無人需要告訴你的東西：有什麼東西在讓那些靈魂四處飛舞，絕不是風。

column1: You sense a spiritual coldness.
column2: 你感受到一股精神上的寒冷。

column1: The only things that can be opened here are graves, and you don't want to open THOSE!
column2: 這裡唯一能打開的就是墳墓，而你不想打開它們！

column1: Your magical abilities are to no avail against the undead.
column2: 你的魔法能力對不死生物毫無作用。

column1: You can't see the bottom of the hole before you.
column2: 你看不到眼前洞穴的底部。

column1: Nothing grows in the dirt of the old graveyard.  Some of the graves appear to have been disturbed.
column2: 古老的墓地裡沒有任何東西生長。有些墳墓似乎被擾動過。

column1: You see transparent, decaying, writhing, slimy, undead fragments of undulating ectoplasm!
column2: 你看到透明、腐爛、扭動、黏稠的不死幽靈在波動著！

column1: I DO believe in spooks!  I DO believe in spooks!  I DO, I DO, I DO believe in spooks!!
column2: 我相信幽靈！我相信幽靈！我真的相信幽靈！！

column1: You recall from somewhere that the most likely time to find ghosts is at night.
column2: 你從某處想起，最有可能找到鬼怪的時間是在夜晚。

column1: The graves seemed to be placed haphazardly in the tiny graveyard.  All appear to be old and poorly maintained.
column2: 這些墳墓似乎在這個小墓地中隨意放置。所有的都看起來又舊又維護不良。

column1: The town wall looks more massive from this side.
column2: 從這一邊看，城鎮的牆看起來更龐大。

column1: There is a very large and very dead tree which hovers over the graveyard.
column2: 有一棵非常大、已經死透的樹懸在墓地上方。

column1: The forest surrounds the graveyard on three sides.
column2: 森林從三面包圍著墓地。

column1: You see the rotted remains of a picket fence.
column2: 你看到了腐爛的柵欄殘骸。

column1: What at first appears to be a ladder turns out to be the rotted remains of a picket fence.
column2: 起初看起來像梯子的東西，其實是腐爛的柵欄殘骸。

column1: Let's see...root..root...\nI know I put that root SOMEwhere!
column2: 讓我看看…根…根…\n我知道我把那個根放在某個地方！

column1: You don't see it.
column2: 你看不到它。

column1: It appears that the spirits frolic here during the dark hours.
column2: 看起來靈魂在黑暗時刻在這裡嬉戲。

column1: It's certainly creepy here even during the daylight hours.
column2: 即使在白天，這裡也確實令人毛骨悚然。

column1: Everything here is already dead.
column2: 這裡的一切都已死亡。

column1: You don't get ghosts.  Ghosts get YOU!
column2: 你不是在遇見鬼怪，鬼怪在遇見你！

column1: You haven't found it yet.
column2: 你還沒找到。

column1: It appears that you have company in the graveyard tonight.
column2: 看起來你今晚在墓地裡有同伴。

column1: This must be the town graveyard.  What a run-down place!
column2: 這一定是鎮上的墓地。多麼破敗的地方！

column1: You stepped into a hole whose depth is unfathomable.  As you fall ever downward, you feel icy fingers clutching at you, and you think: "How could I be so careless?"
column2: 你踩進了深不可測的洞裡。當你不斷往下墜，你感到冰冷的手指抓住你，你想：「我怎麼會這麼不小心？」

column1: You wrench the mandrake plant free from the tombstone on which it has grown.  You hear a scream like that of a dying child as you yank the root from the ground.
column2: 你用力從墓碑上掰下曼德拉草。當你從地面拉起根莖時，你聽到像瀕死的孩子一樣的尖叫。

column1: You wrench the mandrake plant free from the tombstone on which it has grown.  Somehow you thought this would be a lot harder.  You wonder if you did this right.
column2: 你用力從墓碑上掰下曼德拉草。不知怎地，你以為這會困難得多。你想知道你是否做對了。

column1: The root shrivels and twists in your hands until nothing is left.  Astonished, you look at your empty hands.
column2: 根莖在你手中枯縮扭曲，直到什麼都沒有了。驚訝地，你看著你的空手。

column1: If ya want a good time, try the 'Dragon's Breath' at the bar.
column2: 「如果你想玩得愉快，試試酒吧裡的『龍息』。」

column1: The town gate is locked for the night.
column2: 城鎮大門已上鎖過夜。

column1: The gate is open.
column2: 大門是開著的。

column1: The town gate is closed.
column2: 城鎮大門是關著的。

column1: The gate is kept open during the day.
column2: 大門在白天保持開放。

column1: After making sure nobody is watching, you climb over the town's wall.
column2: 確保沒有人在看著之後，你爬過城鎮的牆。

column1: Climbing the wall proves too difficult for your current level of skill, but keep practicing.
column2: 攀爬牆證明對你現在的技能等級來說太困難了，但要繼續練習。

column1: The town gate is wide open.  You would look silly climbing the wall.
column2: 城鎮大門敞開著。你爬牆會看起來很愚蠢。

column1: The road from town branches here.  The eastern fork is the road which brought you into this valley.
column2: 從城鎮出來的路在這裡分岔。東邊的叉路是帶你進入山谷的那條路。

column1: The town wall is made of very sturdy carved rock.  The sign has been carved from wood.
column2: 城鎮圍牆用堅固的雕刻石塊做成。標誌是用木頭雕刻的。

column1: The full moon portends dangerous times.
column2: 滿月預示著危險的時刻。

column1: What can be seen of the sky through the trees is bright and clear.
column2: 透過樹木可以看到的天空是明亮而清晰的。

column1: Its a road.
column2: 這是一條路。

column1: Heavy forest looms to the south.
column2: 南邊籠罩著濃密的森林。

column1: The main road by which you entered this valley winds its way through the dense forest.
column2: 你進入這個山谷的主要路線蜿蜒穿過濃密的森林。

column1: The north road winds its way through the woods.
column2: 北邊的路蜿蜒穿過樹林。

column1: The breeze is cool, but you feel a shiver deeper than just the cold.  You are on your own in a very dangerous place.
column2: 微風很涼爽，但你感到的寒意遠超於溫度。你獨自一人在一個非常危險的地方。

column1: Bruno is big and a tough fighter.  He's also very adept at throwing daggers.
column2: 布魯諾身型壯碩，是個硬漢格鬥家。他也很擅長投擲匕首。

column1: Next to the town's wall, you notice a man who appears to be waiting for someone.
column2: 在城牆旁邊，你注意到一個看起來在等人的男人。

column1: Bruno hangs around the town gates.  He will sell information.
column2: 布魯諾常在城門口閒晃。他會賣情報。

column1: It looks like a particularly deadly weapon, and the thief handles it with great skill.
column2: 看起來像是特別致命的武器，那個盜賊用高超的手法操控著。

column1: Bruno nods and makes the countersign.  "Ya need ta talk to Sneak about the password.  Ya can find him in the alley at night."
column2: 布魯諾點頭做出對暗號。「你需要和斯尼克談密碼。你可以在晚上在小巷裡找到他。」

column1: Get outta my face.
column2: 「離我遠點。」

column1: Give me dough- ya know.
column2: 「給我錢——你懂的。」

column1: A little silver might loosen my lip.
column2: 「一點銀幣可能會讓我多嘴。」

column1: You had better talk with him and find out how much he wants for his information.
column2: 你最好和他聊聊，瞭解他的情報要多少錢。

column1: What's in it for me?  If ya give me a silver, I might have a bit of info you can use.
column2: 「我能得到什麼？如果你給我一個銀幣，我可能會告訴你一些有用的信息。」

column1: For another silver, I can tell ya about where to go to find the Baron, Baba Yaga, or some goblins who have a lot of treasure. So if ya want some information, hand me some cash.
column2: 「再給一枚銀幣，我可以告訴你去哪裡找男爵、芭芭雅嘎，或有很多寶藏的哥布林。所以要是你想要情報，就給我錢吧。」

column1: Don't mess around with Bruno.  You don't have a silver.
column2: 別招惹布魯諾。你沒有銀幣。

column1: (Specify how many gold coins to give him.)
column2: （請指定要給他幾個金幣。）

column1: Okay, whaddaya wanna know
column2: 「好吧，你想知道什麼」

column1: Go south and you'll see a hollow log.  Then head west until ya can go south again.  Then go east until you can go south again.  From there go west. Them brigands just love vistors.
column2: 「向南走，你會看到一根空心原木。然後向西走直到你可以再向南走。然後向東走直到你可以再向南走。從那裡向西走。那些山賊就喜歡訪客。」

column1: What's this for?
column2: 「這是幹什麼的？」

column1: Don't get me wrong.  I'll take it!
column2: 「別誤會。我拿著了！」

column1: You don't have enough money.
column2: 你沒有足夠的錢。

column1: I seen her one time yelling out to the house, 'Hut of brown, now sit down.'  That's one spooky dame, let me tell you.
column2: 「我有一次看她對著房子大喊『棕色小屋，現在坐下。』我跟你說，那個女人真恐怖。」

column1: For ten golds, I'll tell ya where to find some of the robbers what have been robbing people coming into the valley.
column2: 「用十個金幣，我會告訴你在哪裡找到一些搶劫進入山谷的人的盜賊。」

column1: They hide it in the graveyard.  It's under one of the tombstones.  You can see the thieves if you go there at night.
column2: 「牠們把它藏在墓地裡。在某個墓碑下面。如果你在夜晚去那裡，你可以看到盜賊。」

column1: Okay, whaddaya wanna know?
column2: 「好吧，你想知道什麼？」

column1: For ten golds, I'll even tell ya where to find some of the robbers what have been robbing people coming into the valley.
column2: 「十個金幣，我甚至可以告訴你去哪裡找那些搶劫進山谷的人的盜賊。」

column1: Go south and you'll see a hollow log.  Then head west until ya can go south again.  Then go east until you can go south again.  From there, go west.  Them brigands just love visitors.
column2: 「往南走，你會看到一個空心木頭。然後往西走，直到你能再往南走。然後往東走，直到你能再往南走。從那裡往西走。那些山賊最喜歡招待訪客。」

column1: For two gold, I'll tell ya how to get into old Baba Yaga's hut.
column2: 「用兩個金幣，我會告訴你如何進入芭芭雅嘎的小屋。」

column1: Nobody knows much about the leader.
column2: 「沒人太了解那個領導者。」

column1: For ten gold, I'll tell ya where some of those robbers are that're robbin' folks as they come inta the valley.
column2: 「十個金幣，我可以告訴你那些搶劫進山谷的人的山賊在哪裡。」

column1: Just call me Bruno.  That was an easy silver.
column2: 「叫我布魯諾就好。那是筆容易的銀幣。」

column1: She's an ugly hag who knows some magic.  She lives in a weird house.
column2: 「她是個懂點魔法的醜老女巫。她住在奇怪的房子裡。」

column1: Her place is due west of the Baron's place.  Can't miss it.  And for two gold, I'll tell ya how to get into her hut.
column2: 「她的地方在男爵的正西邊。肯定能找到。再加兩個金幣，我可以告訴你怎麼進她小屋。」

column1: Ya just take the road north from here past the Healer's and go till ya get to his castle.
column2: 「你只需從這裡往北走，經過治療師那邊，一直走到他的城堡。」

column1: The little pests are just nothwest of the cemetery.  They're a bunch of wimps.
column2: 「那些小蟲子就在墓地西北方。他們都是些軟腳蝦。」

column1: It's due west of town.  Nice place to visit, but I wouldn't want to stay, if ya know what I mean.
column2: 「在城鎮的正西邊。是個值得一遊的好地方，但我不想待著，你懂我的意思吧。」

column1: Everyone loves Dragon's Breath.
column2: 「每個人都喜歡龍息。」

column1: The Sheriff and the goon are pals.   Otto even lives with the Sheriff and his wife.
column2: 「治安官和那個傢伙是朋友。奧圖甚至和治安官以及他的妻子住在一起。」

column1: He's a pal of mine.  He usually hangs out in the bar.  A real sweet guy.  Give him a slap on the back for me.
column2: 「他是我的朋友。他通常在酒吧裡閒晃。一個真正好的傢伙。替我在他背上拍一下。」

column1: The thieves' password?  Sorry, I can't risk losing my thief license.  Chief wouldn't like it if I blabbed that to the likes'a you.
column2: 「盜賊的密碼？抱歉，我不能冒著失去我的盜賊執照的風險。首領如果我對你這樣的人透露會不高興。」

column1: He's the only one in the valley who can beat me at daggers.
column2: 「他是山谷裡唯一能在匕首上打敗我的人。」

column1: She's got some good potions.  Her hut is just to the north of here.
column2: 「她有些不錯的藥水。她的小屋就在這裡的正北面。」

column1: He's some kinda magic user.  And he laughs alot.  That's all I know.
column2: 「他是某種魔法使用者。而且他經常笑。我只知道這些。」

column1: Hahahaha!  Yeh, the antwerp is a good monster for you to fight.  It loves to play.
column2: 「哈哈哈！是的，安特衛普是一個適合你戰鬥的好怪物。牠喜歡玩。」

column1: Lots'a monsters in this valley.  Most of 'em are wimps, though.
column2: 「這個山谷裡有很多怪物。但大多數都是懦弱的傢伙。」

column1: Hey, I just know what I know.  If you want other info, ask someone else.
column2: 「嘿，我就是知道我知道的。要是你想要其他情報，去問別人吧。」

column1: hi
column2: 嗨

column1: For a gold, well, I can tell where the Thieves' Guild is hidden.
column2: 「一個金幣的話，我可以告訴你盜賊公會隱藏在哪裡。」

column1: You talk'n ta me?
column2: 「你在跟我說話？」

column1: The town gates are closed and barred at night.
column2: 城鎮的大門在夜間關閉並上了門閂。

column1: The carved wooden sign reads "SPIELBURG."
column2: 雕刻的木牌上寫著「史畢柏格」。

column1: He saw you casting a spell, and he already had a dagger in his hand.  It doesn't pay to try to fight someone who uses poisoned daggers.
column2: 他看到你施法，已經拔出匕首。試著和用毒匕首的人對戰不值得。

column1: It takes too long to draw your weapon against someone with a dagger in his hand.  It doesn't pay to try to fight someone who uses poisoned daggers.
column2: 對著拿著匕首的人拔武器太慢了。想和用毒匕首的人打鬥根本不划算。

column1: Why dontcha leave me alone?  I ain't done nothin'.
column2: 「你幹嘛不放過我？我沒幹什麼。」

column1: Hey, I'm just trying to make a buck.  No need to give me a hard time.
column2: 「嘿，我只是想賺點錢。不必給我難堪。」

column1: You look nervous.  Why dontcha take a walk and cool off.
column2: 「你看起來很緊張。你幹嘛不出去走走，冷靜一下。」

column1: Bug off.
column2: 「滾開。」

column1: I think I hear your momma calling you.
column2: 「我好像聽到你媽媽在叫你。」

column1: Your boot's untied.
column2: 「你的靴子沒綁好。」

column1: There seems to be a fox north of the road.
column2: 北邊道路有隻狐狸。

column1: The forest deepens north and south of you.  To the east, the road twists it's way up the mountain.  To the west, the road is hidden by trees.
column2: 南北方的森林深入你的位置。向東，道路蜿蜒上山。向西，道路被樹木隱沒。

column1: For a moment there, you thought you saw something. You can't see anything now.
column2: 有一瞬間，你以為看到了什麼。現在什麼也看不到。

column1: It seems to be a simple spring trap and you can easily open it.
column2: 看起來是簡單的彈簧陷阱，你可以輕易打開它。

column1: The trap seems to be caught on something.
column2: 陷阱好像被什麼東西卡住了。

column1: You can see no sign of a trap here.
column2: 你看不到這裡有任何陷阱的跡象。

column1: The fox seems to have a foot caught in a trap.
column2: 狐狸好像有一隻腳卡在陷阱裡。

column1: The fox is gone.
column2: 狐狸消失了。

column1: You must free the fox first.
column2: 你必須先救出狐狸。

column1: Walk over to the trap.
column2: 走過去找陷阱。

column1: You don't see the trap now.  It seems to have vanished.
column2: 你現在看不到陷阱。看起來它消失了。

column1: My foot is caught in this cruel trap and I am in great pain.  Surely you could take a minute and set me free.
column2: 「我的腳被這個殘忍的陷阱卡住了，我感到很大的痛苦。你肯定能抽出一分鐘時間讓我自由。」

column1: I am in such pain and agony I cannot think.   Help me, good adventurer, please.
column2: 我痛得要死，根本想不了。好心的冒險者，拜託幫幫我。

column1: Walk around to the other side of the fox, where the trap is.
column2: 走到狐狸的另一邊，陷阱在那裡。

column1: There's no one here to fight.
column2: 這裡沒有人可以戰鬥。

column1: You would need a sword.
column2: 你需要一把劍。

column1: There is an aura of magic around the fox.
column2: 狐狸周圍有股魔法的氣息。

column1: There is no magic present.
column2: 這裡沒有魔法。

column1: The fox is in such pain that it's already dazzled.
column2: 狐狸痛得已經被眩暈了。

column1: You don't have to put me out of my misery, you just have to get me out of this trap.
column2: 你不用讓我痛快地死去，只要把我從這個陷阱裡救出來就行。

column1: You realize, of course, I really find it hard to relax with this thing on my leg.
column2: 當然，你明白吧，我實在很難在腿上戴著這東西的情況下放鬆。

column1: Help me, Brave and Kind Hero.
column2: 幫幫我，勇敢善良的英雄。

column1: Please help me, Brave and Kind Hero.
column2: 拜託幫幫我，勇敢善良的英雄。

column1: Please have mercy on a creature who is in great pain.
column2: 求求你，對這隻深陷痛苦的生物發發慈悲。

column1: The fox does not seem to want to fight.
column2: 狐狸好像不想打架。

column1: That was strange, no sooner did you kill the fox than it vanishes.
column2: 那很奇怪，你才殺死狐狸，它就消失了。

column1: Hmmm.  Perhaps it needed a stronger spell.
column2: 嗯…也許得用更強的法術。

column1: Yep.  It's open, all right.
column2: 是的。它確實是開著的。

column1: You spring the trap.
column2: 你啟動了陷阱。

column1: In exchange for your kindness, I will give you some advice and a bit of information.  First of all, it sometimes pays off to be polite, even to rude people.
column2: 為了回報你的善心，我會給你一些建議和資訊。首先呢，禮貌待人有時是值得的，就算對方很粗魯也不例外。

column1: As for the amusing tidbit - Baba Yaga put an enchantment on the Baron's Daughter some years back.  To break the spell you need to talk to the Dryad.  Au Revoir, Ta-ta, see you sometime.
column2: 「至於那個有趣的小故事——幾年前芭芭雅嘎對男爵之女施下了魔法。要破除法術，你需要和樹精談談。再見，拜拜，等著見。」

column1: The trap seems to be caught on something.  You can't pull it loose.
column2: 陷阱好像卡在什麼東西上。你沒辦法把它拉鬆。

column1: That's not worth any game points, and will not make the fox feel any better.
column2: 那沒有任何遊戲點數，也不會讓狐狸好受點。

column1: My foot is caught in this trap.  If I had hands like you, instead of paws, I could free myself.
column2: 「我的腳被這個陷阱卡住了。如果我有像你一樣的手，而不是爪子，我可以自己解救。」

column1: You are in a clearing at the edge of the forest. The road you have been following is completely blocked by a very recent avalanche.
column2: 你在森林邊緣的空地上。你一直跟著的路被最近發生的雪崩完全堵住了。

column1: It appears that a very recent avalanche has sealed off the road leading out of the valley.
column2: 看起來最近發生的雪崩已經堵住了通往山谷外的路。

column1: The meadow clearing in which you stand is grassy; the road is densely packed and slightly muddy.
column2: 你站著的草地空地是草地；道路密集且略微泥濘。

column1: The low-lying bushes form tight tangles of shrubbery between the trees.
column2: 低矮的灌木在樹之間形成緊密的灌木叢。

column1: A tumbled pattern of rocks can be seen at the forest's edge.
column2: 在森林邊緣可以看到一堆堆疊的岩石。

column1: The ragged peaks of the snow-covered mountains can be seen clearly. The pass, however, is blocked by very recent avalanches.
column2: 雪山的參差不齊的山峰可以清楚看到。但是通道被最近的雪崩堵住了。

column1: There are no caves here.
column2: 這裡沒有洞穴。

column1: Climbing would serve no purpose right now.
column2: 現在爬山沒有任何用處。

column1: Hi, Kris!
column2: 嗨，克里斯！

column1: Hi, little kid!
column2: 嗨，小鬼！

column1: Hi, Max!
column2: 嗨，馬克斯！

column1: Hi, Bridgette!
column2: 嗨，布麗琪特！

column1: Your senses gradually return to normal.
column2: 你的感官逐漸恢復正常。

column1: Don't you want to dance?
column2: 你不想跳舞嗎？

column1: What for?  There's no one to dance with.
column2: 為什麼？沒人跟我跳舞啊。

column1: What for?
column2: 為什麼？

column1: They seem to be ignoring you.
column2: 他們似乎在忽視你。

column1: Who are you talking to?
column2: 你在跟誰說話?

column1: You know all about it, now.
column2: 現在你全知道了。

column1: There is much magic surrounding the fairies and their ring of mushrooms.
column2: 環繞著妖精和她們的蘑菇圈有很多魔法。

column1: You detect faint emanations of magic near the ring of mushrooms.
column2: 你在蘑菇圓環附近偵測到微弱的魔法氣息。

column1: You don't have a dagger.
column2: 你沒有短劍。

column1: The fairies can avoid your grasp easily.
column2: 妖精能輕易躲開你的掌握。

column1: Don't be greedy.  You already have some.
column2: 別貪心。你已經有一些了。

column1: Perhaps you should be better prepared to get some fairy dust next time.
column2: 也許下次你應該準備得更好才能取得妖精塵。

column1: Where could you possibly get that?
column2: 你怎麼可能得到那個?

column1: Maybe you should ask the Fairies for some.  That would be the polite thing to do.
column2: 也許你應該問妖精要一些。那是禮貌的做法。

column1: The trees look more vibrant than most of the forest.
column2: 這些樹看起來比森林裡大多數的樹更充滿生氣。

column1: There is a ring of mushrooms on the northwest side of the clearing.
column2: 空地的西北邊有個蘑菇圈。

column1: The fairy ring is luminous at night.
column2: 妖精圈在夜晚會發光。

column1: The ring of mushrooms contains mushrooms slightly larger than the ones you are used to.
column2: 蘑菇圈裡有比你慣見的蘑菇稍大的蘑菇。

column1: The fairies look like little dancing lights, but you get a strong sense that they are female with butterfly wings.
column2: 妖精看起來像小小的舞動光芒，但你強烈感覺她們是有蝴蝶翅膀的女性。

column1: There aren't any of those around.
column2: 這附近沒有那種東西。

column1: The trees seem more dense and lush than the rest of the woods.
column2: 這些樹看起來比森林其他地方更密集、更翠綠。

column1: You see trees and brush.
column2: 你看到樹木和灌木。

column1: The trees here look thicker and healthier than in other parts of the forest.
column2: 這裡的樹看起來比森林其他地方更粗壯、更健康。

column1: I'll bet you're sorry you said that.
column2: 我打賭你現在後悔說那句話了。

column1: For such wimpy-looking creatures, those fairies sure can play rough!  You never danced so hard in your entire life, which is now over.
column2: 這些看起來很弱不禁風的傢伙，妖精們竟然那麼兇悍！你一生從未跳過那麼猛烈的舞蹈，現在已經結束了。

column1: You eat a few of the lovely mushrooms.
column2: 你吃了幾個美妙的蘑菇。

column1: You pick a handful of the smaller mushrooms and carefully put them away in your backpack.
column2: 你摘下一把小蘑菇，小心翼翼地放進背包裡。

column1: You hold out your hand...what else could you do?
column2: 你伸出手…你還能怎樣?

column1: You place the dust carefully away in an empty flask.
column2: 你小心翼翼地把粉末放進空瓶子裡。

column1: You realize that you need something such as an empty flask to put this fairy dust into.
column2: 你體會到你需要像空瓶子這樣的容器來裝這些妖精塵。

column1: As the fairy dust sifts through your fingers, you realize that you needed something to put it into.
column2: 仙粉穿過你的手指時，你意識到需要某個東西來裝它。

column1: Wow!  That was pretty wild!  It's probably not a good idea to eat too many more of these mushrooms, though.
column2: 哇!那相當狂野!不過吃更多這些蘑菇可能不是個好主意。

column1: The objects on the ground are a brigand's shield and spear.
column2: 地上的物品是山賊的盾牌和長槍。

column1: The dead brigand lies very still.
column2: 死山賊靜靜地躺著。

column1: You see a hard-looking character who appears to be a thief.  He must be one of the Brigands!
column2: 你看到一個看起來很硬漢的傢伙，看起來像個盜賊。他一定是其中一個山賊！

column1: The dead brigand's shield is not worth your while.
column2: 死山賊的盾牌不值得你動手。

column1: Get closer to him.
column2: 靠近他。

column1: This man looks very tough.  From his clothing, you guess him to be a member of the Thieves' Guild.
column2: 這個人看起來非常硬漢。從他的衣著,你猜測他是盜賊公會的一員。

column1: You are using the old archery target for dagger practice.
column2: 你用舊弓箭靶來練習匕首。

column1: The old archery target looks as though it has not been used in quite some time.
column2: 那個老舊的射箭靶子看起來已經好久沒用過了。

column1: The only daggers around here are the ones you've thrown at the target.
column2: 這裡唯一的匕首就是你扔在靶上的那些。

column1: There are no useable spears near you.
column2: 你附近沒有可用的長槍。

column1: These bushes are full of thorns. You decide not to risk it.
column2: 這些灌木全是荊棘。你決定不冒這個險。

column1: Some marauding beast must have taken the brigand's body away.
column2: 某隻掠食野獸一定是把山賊的屍體帶走了。

column1: You see nothing like that here.
column2: 你這裡沒看到這樣的東西。

column1: You detect no magic here.
column2: 你在這裡沒偵測到魔法。

column1: You waste a spell.
column2: 你浪費了一個法術。

column1: The brigand is too angry to calm.
column2: 山賊太生氣了，無法冷靜。

column1: You waste a spell. Fetch is only good for fetching small, visible objects.
column2: 你浪費了一個法術。取物術只能用來取小的、看得見的物體。

column1: Just wait. The nice brigand will throw you his knife.
column2: 只要等著。那個好心的山賊會把匕首扔給你。

column1: The forest is very overgrown near here.
column2: 這附近的森林長得特別茂盛。

column1: The wall and buildings of Spielburg can be seen over the heavy brush.  An old target leans against the town wall.
column2: 隔著濃密的灌木可以看到史畢柏格的城牆和建築。一個舊靶子靠在城牆上。

column1: You see a couple of hard-looking characters talking.  One looks like a thief; the other appears to be a fighter of some sort.
column2: 你看到幾個看起來兇悍的傢伙在談話。其中一個看起來像盜賊，另一個似乎是某種格鬥者。

column1: So what's this about, anyway?
column2: 這到底是怎麼回事？

column1: Her Nibs is starting to get suspicious about us.
column2: 那位高高在上的傢伙開始懷疑我們了。

column1: What's the bee in her bonnet?
column2: 她怎麼了？

column1: Seems the hero" wandering around here has her leery.  She thinks he's going to go for the gold on her head."
column2: 「那個『英雄』在這裡到處遊蕩，讓她很警惕。她以為他會去奪她頭上的懸賞。」

column1: What's it ta do with us?
column2: 這跟我們有什麼關係？

column1: She's been asking too many questions 'bout us.
column2: 她一直在問太多關於我們的問題。

column1: And the laughing jackass' eyeing me.  I had to sneak out.
column2: 「那個愛笑的傢伙還盯著我。我只好偷偷溜了出來。」

column1: Then we got to avoid the ambush and use the back for a bit, 'til the heat is off.
column2: 那麼我們得避開伏擊，用後門一陣子，等風吹過去。

column1: Maybe we should just make our move now.
column2: 也許我們該現在就採取行動。

column1: Naw, let's wait for the creep to go first.
column2: 不，我們先等那傢伙離開。

column1: While she's busy with him, we take over.
column2: 趁她忙著應付他的時候，我們接管。

column1: She'll take him out easy, then we take her out.
column2: 她會輕鬆解決他，然後我們解決她。

column1: Where's the back door, then?
column2: 那後門在哪裡？

column1: Where the bouncer hops around.  Ya got your key still?
column2: 保鑣蹦蹦跳跳的地方。你還有鑰匙嗎？

column1: Yeah.
column2: 是的。

column1: Don't lose it.  I got the only other one.  Yull haveta search the rock for the keyhole.  It's hidden good.  And remember the 'word'.
column2: 「別丟了。我有唯一的另一把。你得在石頭裡搜尋鑰匙孔。它藏得很好。還要記住那個『暗語』。」

column1: What Word?
column2: 「什麼暗語？」

column1: The 'word' what lets ya in so that Fred goes away.
column2: 那個『密碼』讓你進去，這樣佛瑞德就會消失。

column1: Oh yeah, sure.
column2: 「噢，是的，當然。」

column1: What is it?
column2: 「是什麼？」

column1: Hiden Goseke.  Ya better learn it.
column2: 希登·格塞克。你最好學起來。

column1: You think I'm a dummy or somethin'?
column2: 你以為我是個傻瓜或什麼的？

column1: Say the 'word' before ya open the door or ya might make Fred mad.
column2: 「在你打開門之前要說那個『暗語』，不然可能會惹 Fred 生氣。」

column1: Ya don't want Fred ta get mad.
column2: 你不想讓佛瑞德生氣。

column1: Hey, no problem.  What's the 'word' again?
column2: 嘿，沒問題。那個『密碼』是什麼來著？

column1: Hiden Goseke.
column2: 希登·格塞克。

column1: Hiden Goseke.  Got it.
column2: 希登·格塞克。明白了。

column1: I gotta get back before the Chief misses me.  Be back in a bit.
column2: 我得在頭兒發現我之前回去。等一下就回來。

column1: Hiden Goseke.  See ya.
column2: 希登·格塞克。回見。

column1: Done in by poisoned daggers and desperate desperadoes!  Try to avoid annoying that deadly duo next time.
column2: 被毒匕首和孤注一擲的亡命漢幹掉!下次別惹那對致命組合。

column1: Hey, watchu doin' here? Spyin', I'll wager!
column2: 嘿，你在這裡幹嘛？偵查，我敢打賭！

column1: Done in by daggers and a desperate desperado!  Try to avoid annoying that deadly duo next time.
column2: 被匕首和孤注一擲的亡命漢幹掉!下次別惹那對致命組合。

column1: There are too many bushes between you and the target.
column2: 你和靶子之間有太多灌木。

column1: You're too close to the target for it to be a challange.
column2: 你離靶子太近，它成不了挑戰。

column1: You have no daggers to throw.
column2: 你沒有匕首可以扔。

column1: You can't get any daggers.
column2: 你拿不到任何匕首。

column1: You find nothing else on the brigand's body.
column2: 你在山賊的身上找不到別的東西。

column1: You find a single key on the brigand's body, and put it away.
column2: 你在山賊的身上找到一把鑰匙，並把它收起來。

column1: For a moment there, you thought you saw something.  But nothing is there now.
column2: 有那麼一刻，你以為看到什麼。但現在什麼都沒有。

column1: You pick up the Dryad's gift of a Magic Acorn.
column2: 你拿起樹精贈與的魔法橡實。

column1: You find an ordinary acorn on the ground.
column2: 你在地上找到一顆普通的橡實。

column1: That acorn tasted awfully bitter...\njust like an ordinary acorn.
column2: 那顆橡實嚐起來苦得要命…就像一顆普通的橡實。

column1: Until you have done so, you are only intruding on my concentration.
column2: 「在你完成之前,你只是在打擾我的冥想。」

column1: You need to become a friend of the forest.  Harm not the gentle creatures or plants. See with all your senses the magic of the trees and wild places.
column2: 「你需要成為森林的朋友。不要傷害溫和的生物或植物。用你所有的感官去感受樹木和野生地方的魔法。」

column1: Wander and learn.  Then you too will be in harmony with the forest.
column2: 「漫步其中吧,並學習。然後你也會與森林和諧相處。」

column1: The stag looks more surprised than hurt.
column2: 那隻雄鹿看起來驚訝多於受傷。

column1: The stag is startled by your action.
column2: 白鹿被你的行動嚇到了。

column1: Give the seed to me.
column2: 「把種子給我。」

column1: You are forced to confess that you lied to the Dryad.  You don't have the magic seed.
column2: 你被迫承認你對樹精撒謊了。你沒有魔法種子。

column1: She tells you:  "It's not nice to fool Mother Nature."
column2: 她告訴你：「戲弄大自然母親是不禮貌的。」

column1: Then you shall aid me, and I shall aid you in your quest.
column2: 那麼你要幫助我，我也會在你的任務中幫助你。

column1: Bring me a seed from the Spore Spitting Spirea of the North that I may plant it elsewhere in order to preserve these rare and magical plants.
column2: 給我帶來一粒來自北邊的孢子噴發繡線菊的種子，這樣我可以在別處種植它，以保護這些稀有的魔法植物。

column1: Thus will you become a true friend of the forest.
column2: 「這樣你就會成為森林的真正朋友。」

column1: I detect that you have in your possession such a seed.  Are you willing to give me the seed?
column2: 「我發現你擁有這樣一顆種子。你願意把種子給我嗎?」

column1: Please.  Just answer my question.
column2: 「拜託。只要回答我的問題。」

column1: The Dryad radiates magic.
column2: 樹精散發出魔法。

column1: The oak tree radiates magic.
column2: 橡樹散發出魔法。

column1: He's beyond your reach.
column2: 他超出你的範圍。

column1: Something about the old oak tree makes you reluctant to climb upon it.
column2: 那棵老橡樹有什麼東西讓你不太想爬上去。

column1: The rocks to the north look too steep.
column2: 北邊的岩石看起來太陡峭。

column1: Perhaps the farmer has dates in his field.
column2: 「也許農夫在他的田裡有棗子。」

column1: I beg your pardon?
column2: 「你說什麼？」

column1: Does a Dryad bark?
column2: 樹精會叫嗎？

column1: No, but she's a real birch.
column2: 不會，但她是個真正的難相處傢伙。

column1: Don't make an ash of yourself.
column2: 別讓自己出醜。

column1: As you begin to form thoughts of violence...
column2: 當你開始萌生暴力的念頭時…

column1: You think better of burning up the woods.
column2: 你改變主意，決定不放火燒毀森林。

column1: She's beyond your reach.
column2: 她超出你的範圍。

column1: You needn't dull your blade playing woodsman.
column2: 你不必因為玩木匠就磨鈍你的刀。

column1: She's rooted to the ground.
column2: 她根在地上。

column1: The stag remains out of your reach.
column2: 那隻雄鹿仍然超出你的範圍。

column1: The Dryad appears to be a woman made of wood and leaves.
column2: 樹精看起來像是一個由木頭和葉子組成的女人。

column1: You cannot look at what you cannot see.
column2: 你無法看到看不見的東西。

column1: The large, gnarled oak seems to draw your attention.
column2: 那棵又大又扭曲的橡樹似乎吸引了你的注意。

column1: There is a rock wall northwest of the large oak tree.
column2: 大橡樹的西北邊有一面石牆。

column1: The majestic white stag is an outstanding example of the beauty of the animal kingdom.
column2: 莊嚴的白鹿是動物王國美麗的傑出典範。

column1: You see no such animal.
column2: 你沒看到這樣的動物。

column1: There is a large, slightly glowing acorn on the ground.
column2: 地上有一個很大的、微微發光的橡樹果。

column1: There are acorns here and there on the ground.
column2: 地上到處都是橡樹果。

column1: The forest becomes so lush and thick that it is difficult to see far.
column2: 森林變得如此茂密，很難看得遠。

column1: The brush and undergrowth has formed an impassable thicket.
column2: 灌木和地被植物形成了不可通過的灌木叢。

column1: You see thick brush, rocks, and heavy woods to the north.  A large oak tree dominates the view.
column2: 你看到北邊有濃密的灌木、岩石和茂密的森林。一棵大橡樹佔據了視野。

column1: You see a clearing back the way you came.
column2: 你看到來時路的方向有個空地。

column1: Most of the bushes around here seem to be bramble and are very thorny.
column2: 這裡周圍的大多數灌木似乎是荊棘，非常有刺。

column1: You follow the stag into this forest corner.  You feel as though the eyes of the forest are watching you.
column2: 你跟著白鹿進入森林的角落。你感覺彷彿整個森林的眼睛都在注視著你。

column1: Again, you feel a sense of closeness with nature.
column2: 你再次感到與自然相近的感覺。

column1: You watch the stag, fascinated with his grace and beauty.  There is something special about this place.
column2: 你看著白鹿,被它的優雅和美麗迷住了。這個地方有什麼特別的。

column1: What a beautiful animal!
column2: 多麼美麗的動物!

column1: Have you brought the seed I requested?
column2: 「你帶來我要求的種子了嗎?」

column1: Well, Hero-to-be!  Are you yet a friend of the woods?
column2: 「好吧,未來的英雄!你是森林的朋友嗎?」

column1: I am the Dryad, keeper of the woods.  Are you one with the woods?
column2: 「我是樹精，森林的守護者。你與森林融為一體嗎？」

column1: You see an acorn fall slowly to the ground.
column2: 你看到一個橡樹果慢慢地掉到地上。

column1: You drop the seed into the Dryad's limbs.
column2: 你把種子掉進樹精的枝幹中。

column1: You tell the Dryad:  "No amount of persuasion could convince the flowers to relinquish their seed easily, so I was forced to hack my way to the seed with my weapon."
column2: 你告訴樹精:「無論多少勸說都無法輕易說服花朵放棄它們的種子,所以我被迫用武器砍出一條路到達種子。」

column1: Now this will boldly grow where none has grown before!
column2: 「現在它將大膽地在無人種植過的地方生長!」

column1: Heed now my words, friend of the forest, and heed them well.
column2: 「現在就要聽我的話,森林的朋友,要聽清楚。」

column1: Friend, you must know that there is an evil in this valley which perverts the ways of nature.  The prophecy says that a hero will bring a young human from out this darkness.
column2: 「朋友，你必須知道這個山谷裡有股邪惡勢力扭曲了大自然的方式。預言說一個英雄會把一個年輕的人類從這黑暗中帶出來。」

column1: If you are the one to accomplish this task, there is a potion to break enchantments which you must have the Healer make.
column2: 「如果你是完成這項任務的人，你需要讓治療師製作一瓶解除詛咒的魔藥。」

column1: You must gather these ingredients:
column2: 「你必須收集這些材料:」

column1: Flowers from Erana's Peace,
column2: 「伊瑞娜的安寧之地的花朵,」

column1: Green Fur,
column2: 「綠色的毛皮,」

column1: Fairy Dust,
column2: 「妖精之塵,」

column1: a Magic Acorn,
column2: 「一個魔法橡實,」

column1: and Flying Water.
column2: 「和飛行之水。」

column1: Farewell, friend.  I must return to my concentration.  May the forest forever surround you.
column2: 「再見了,朋友。我必須回去冥想。願森林永遠環繞著你。」

column1: You pick it up and eat it.
column2: 你拿起它並吃了它。

column1: The acorn appears too ordinary.  You drop it back on the ground.
column2: 那顆橡實看起來太普通了。你把它掉回地上。

column1: You did WHAT???
column2: 你做了什麼？？？

column1: You have destroyed the plant that was to be preserved!
column2: 「你摧毀了本應被保護的植物!」

column1: As you have destroyed, so shall you now become!
column2: 「正如你所摧毀的,你現在也將變成這樣!」

column1: Well, it looks like the Dryad really made you eat dirt.  You're the spitting image of a Spirea plant, and you'll be hanging around for a long, long time.
column2: 嗯,看起來樹精真的讓你吃土了。你簡直就是繡球花的翻版,你會在那邊掛很久很久。

column1: You have attempted to harm a free creature of the forest!
column2: 「你試圖傷害森林的自由生物!」

column1: You shall now become a part of the forest!
column2: 「你現在將成為森林的一部分!」

column1: You have misused your skills, oh Magician!  You have used the Flame Dart on my forest.
column2: 「你濫用了你的技能,哦,法師!你在我的森林上使用了火焰鏢。」

column1: You must be made less dangerous.
column2: 「你必須變得不那麼危險。」

column1: You dare to harm the Dryad of this forest!  You must be made less dangerous.
column2: 「你竟敢傷害這片森林的樹精！你必須被制服。」

column1: You are staggered by the Dryad's spell.  She appears to enjoy having lots of bucks.
column2: 你被樹精的咒語震撼。她好像喜歡有一堆錢。

column1: You sleep comfortably and well in this quiet and protected corner of the woods.
column2: 你在這片寧靜安全的森林角落舒服地睡得很好。

column1: Your spell has no effect.
column2: 你的魔法沒有效果。

column1: The stag seems to be magically protected.
column2: 那隻雄鹿似乎受到魔法保護。

column1: You don't see a stag here.
column2: 你這裡沒看到雄鹿。

column1: The beautiful white stag is foraging for food.
column2: 美麗的白鹿正在覓食。

column1: The white stag takes a mighty leap.
column2: 白鹿縱身一躍。

column1: You seem to have startled the white stag.
column2: 你似乎嚇到了白鹿。

column1: You seem to have caused an Antwerp POPulation EXPLOSION!
column2: 你似乎導致了一場安特衛普人口爆炸!

column1: You see no Antwerps here.
column2: 你這裡沒看到安特衛普。

column1: The bouncing baby Antwerps are all so cute, you can't bring yourself to interfere with their playing.
column2: 跳躍著的小安特衛普都很可愛，你捨不得打擾它們玩耍。

column1: Looking up, you can see a small, blue dot in the sky, getting bigger... and bigger... and BIGGER!
column2: 向上看,你可以看到天空中一個小的藍點,越來越大…還有更大…還有更大!

column1: Too late!
column2: 太遲了！

column1: There's no time for that!
column2: 沒有時間做那個!

column1: Where to? There is no escaping the hurtling blue blob.
column2: 往哪裡？你無法逃脫飛來的藍色物體。

column1: You're obviously in no shape to continue the game.
column2: 你顯然沒有體力繼續遊戲了。

column1: There is a roaring sound off to the east.
column2: 東邊傳來一陣咆哮聲。

column1: The hollow log looks somberly back with it's single giant eye and you know at once that nothing is to be gained by investigating this gaunt relic of a more vertical past.
column2: 那根空心的木頭用它唯一的巨大眼睛沮喪地看著你，你立刻明白調查這個龐大的垂直過去的遺跡沒有任何好處。

column1: You'll have to climb down to do that.
column2: 你得爬下來才能做到。

column1: A river plunges for more than a hundred feet down the face of a cliff.  A doorway has been built into the side of the cliff.
column2: 一條河流從懸崖面上直瀉下超過百英尺。崖邊建了一道門。

column1: The water seems to be trying to fly as it leaps from the mountain above.
column2: 水似乎在從上面的山上躍下時試圖飛起。

column1: You see slick, vertical cliff walls.
column2: 你看到光滑的垂直崖壁。

column1: The water is white as it splashes against the rocks with great force and purpose and disappears into a narrow canyon to the south.
column2: 水流撞擊岩石時衝出白色泡沫，然後消失在南邊的狹窄峽谷中。

column1: The grass is green with the freshness of new spring.
column2: 草地呈現新春的嫩綠。

column1: There is a narrow ledge in front of the wide door.
column2: 寬門前有一個狹窄的岩架。

column1: You don't see a ladder here.
column2: 你在這裡看不到梯子。

column1: The door is built into the face of the mountainside.  You suspect that the person who lives behind it treasures privacy.
column2: 門被建造在山邊的表面。你懷疑住在它後面的人珍視隱私。

column1: It's a large door.  It must open pretty wide.
column2: 這是一扇大門。它一定開得相當寬。

column1: you don't have a flask
column2: 你沒有水瓶

column1: The water comes from melting snow.  This early in the season, it's much too cold to swim.
column2: 水來自融雪。這個季節這麼早，水冷得不能游泳。

column1: The door is securely locked.
column2: 門被牢牢鎖住了。

column1: The door is so securely locked that it defies your abilities to unlock it.
column2: 門被鎖得太牢固，超出了你的開鎖能力。

column1: It's a little hard to knock on the door from where you're standing.
column2: 從你站的地方敲門有點困難。

column1: You're in front of the door already.  You don't need to climb any farther.
column2: 你已經在門前了。不需要再往上爬。

column1: As you prepare your spell...
column2: 當你準備你的魔法…

column1: The only thing you can open here is the door, and you're not skilled enough to do that.
column2: 你唯一能在這裡打開的東西是門,而你沒有足夠的技能來做那個。

column1: Wheeee!
column2: 呼!

column1: Wow!
column2: 哇!

column1: The roar of the waterfall fills your ears, and the cold spray dampens your face as you approach.
column2: 瀑布的轟鳴聲填滿了你的耳朵,當你靠近時,冷的噴霧潮濕了你的臉。

column1: RAP!
column2: 敲擊!

column1: The rock makes a sharp sound as it hits the door.
column2: 岩石撞到門時發出尖銳的聲音。

column1: Missed
column2: 偏了

column1: All right, already!
column2: 「好吧,已經!」

column1: Is someone there?
column2: 「有人嗎?」

column1: You again?  Either come up, or go away.   I really doesn't cares which.
column2: 「又是你?要麼上來,要麼滾開。我真的不在乎哪一個。」

column1: Oh, 'ello.  Come right up.
column2: 「哦,嘿。直接上來。」

column1: Just climb the ladder.
column2: 「就爬梯子吧。」

column1: You take a drink of water from the icy mountain river.  It refreshes you.
column2: 你從冰冷的山河喝了一口水。它使你恢復活力。

column1: You take another drink of water.  You were thirsty!
column2: 你又喝了一口水。你很渴!

column1: BOY!  You must have really been thirsty!
column2: 天哪!你一定真的很渴!

column1: You fill an empty flask with crystal-clear water from the waterfall.
column2: 你用瀑布的清澈的水填滿了一個空的瓶子。

column1: Flat...you feel very, very flat.
column2: 扁了……你感到非常非常扁。

column1: And dead...you feel dead, too.  In your weakened condition, you succumbed to a mild-mannered hermit's propensity for Tarzan imitations.  Back up and play it again, Sam.
column2: 死了……你也感到死了。在你虛弱的狀態下，你屈服於一個溫和隱士的泰山模仿傾向。倒帶重玩，Sam。

column1: That hermit seems to know his "Trigger" spells pretty well.  He sure pulled the trigger on you (not to mention the plug) by teleporting you to the top of the falls without a barrel.
column2: 那隱士看起來很懂「觸發術」。他肯定對你發動了觸發（更別提直接結束了），把你傳送到瀑布頂端卻沒給桶子。

column1: You knock three times.
column2: 你敲了三下。

column1: Just a minute!  Just a minute!
column2: 「等一下！等一下！」

column1: The straw is relatively fresh and looks more comfortable to lie upon than the rocks.
column2: 稻草相對新鮮，看起來比石頭舒服多了。

column1: Yes, what you see is a table.
column2: 是的，你看到的就是桌子。

column1: You see a chair.
column2: 你看到一把椅子。

column1: You see a cot.
column2: 你看到一張簡易床。

column1: A single candle sits upon the table.
column2: 一根蠟燭孤獨地擺在桌上。

column1: You see the shaggiest little man (you think it's a man, anyway) you've ever seen.
column2: 你看到一個毛髮蓬亂的小個子（你想應該是個男人啦）。

column1: Come in, come in.  Good to see you again.   Get's kind o' tiresome sitten here an' thinken by meself all the time.
column2: 「進來進來。很高興又見到你。一個人坐在這裡想東想西有點煩人。」

column1: The cave is cold and clammy and it smells like mildew and wet dog hair in here!
column2: 這個洞穴又冷又濕，聞起來像黴味和濕狗毛！

column1: The walls are covered with phosphorescent fungus.
column2: 牆壁上佈滿磷光菌。

column1: The phosphorescent fungus glows eerily in the cave's dim light.
column2: 磷光菌在洞穴昏暗的光線中詭異地發光。

column1: It's on the table.
column2: 它在桌上。

column1: Me guest bed is the straw in the corner over there. You're welcome to stay the night since you've shared your rations. When you want to sleep, just say so.
column2: 「我的客用床是那邊角落的稻草。既然你分享了口糧，歡迎你留下睡一晚。想睡的時候就說一聲。」

column1: Me guest bed is the straw in the corner over there. You're welcome to come back and sleep tonight since you've shared your rations.
column2: 「我的客人床就是角落那邊的稻草。既然你分享了你的給養，歡迎你今晚回來睡覺。」

column1: You can't share what you don't have.
column2: 你沒有的東西無法分享。

column1: You can't sleep in this old rickety chair.  Get up and sleep on the bed of straw.
column2: 你沒法在這把老舊搖搖晃晃的椅子上睡覺。起來睡稻草床吧。

column1: Get closer to the bed of straw.
column2: 靠近稻草床。

column1: You need to get closer to the chair.
column2: 你需要靠近椅子。

column1: You are already sitting... on your brain, perhaps?
column2: 你已經坐下了……也許坐在你的腦子上？

column1: You're not sitting.
column2: 你沒有坐著。

column1: The 'ermit wouldn't like that.
column2: 那個『隱士』不會喜歡那樣。

column1: You should wait until the hermit is asleep to check his place out.
column2: 你應該等隱士睡著才去查看他的地方。

column1: Despite your intentions, you can't bring yourself to spend any more time playing with the talkative 'Enry.
column2: 儘管你有意願，你無法讓自己再花時間和那個話多的『亨利』玩。

column1: You spend an hour at the game while 'Enry talks.  You've obviously made his day.
column2: 你花了一小時玩遊戲，同時『亨利』說著話。你顯然讓他開心了一整天。

column1: You should sit down first.
column2: 你應該先坐下。

column1: The scroll vanishes even as you read the magical runes upon it.   You now have the knowledge to cast a "Trigger" spell.
column2: 當你讀著上面的魔法符文時，捲軸消失了。你現在掌握了施展「觸發術」的知識。

column1: You see no scroll.
column2: 你沒看到捲軸。

column1: You can detect a faint aura of magic here.
column2: 你能感受到這裡有微弱的魔法光暈。

column1: There is no need to use magic to open anything here.
column2: 這裡沒有什麼需要魔法才能打開的。

column1: It's not polite to steal from your 'airy little 'ost.
column2: 從你『氣派的小』『主人』那裡偷東西不禮貌。

column1: You seem to have made the 'ermit un'appy.
column2: 看來你惹得這隱士很不開心。

column1: Me guest bed is the straw in the corner over there. You're welcome to stay if you share some rations and play some cribbage.
column2: 「我的客用床是那邊角落的稻草。要是你願意分享口糧和我玩幾盤牌戲，歡迎你留下。」

column1: If you be wanting to learn the spell, I'll be pleased to share it to you.  It's just getting molding in me 'iding place.
column2: 「如果你想學習法術，我很樂意分享給你。它只是在我『藏』的地方發黴。」

column1: I think you be 'avin too little magic for it to be any use to you.
column2: 「我想你的魔法太弱，對你沒用。」

column1: Do you want it?
column2: 「你要嗎？」

column1: Oh well, maybe sumone else can use it sumtime.
column2: 「嗯，或許別人以後用得上。」

column1: You already 'ave me only scroll.
column2: 「你已經有我唯一的捲軸了。」

column1: Yes, I still have me magic scroll, but you didn't seem to want it. 'Ave you changed your mind?
column2: 「是啊，我還有我的魔法捲軸，但你似乎不想要。你改主意了嗎？」

column1: Let's see.  'Ow does Erasmus do it?  Oh, yes.
column2: 「讓我想想。伊拉斯謨怎麼做的？喔，對了。」

column1: 'E summons it with a Trigger.  'Ere goes!
column2: 「他用觸發術召喚它。來吧！」

column1: There you 'as it.
column2: 「看吧，就這樣。」

column1: I don't 'ave much, but wot I 'ave, I 'ave, an' t'isn't for sale.
column2: 「我沒『有』多少，但我『有』的就是『有』，不是為了出售。」

column1: Well, see'en as I don't get out much, I don't know much.
column2: 「嗯，既然我不常出門，我也不知道多少。」

column1: If you be ever needen a place to stay the night and you're in the area, I could be willen to put you up for the night for sum rations and a couple o' games o' cribbage.
column2: 「要是你以後需要地方睡覺又在這一帶，我樂意為了幾份口糧和幾盤牌戲讓你睡一晚。」

column1: After having traded a ration, which the 'ermit promptly swallowed, and spending a rather boring hour of cribbage and listening to 'Enry chatter, you hit the hay.
column2: 在交換了給養（『隱士』迅速吞下），並花了相當無聊的一小時玩牌戲和聽『亨利』閒聊後，你倒頭就睡。

column1: Unfortunately, since the fungus on the walls shines at night so the room doesn't get dark, and the fact the hermit talks most of the night, you only get five hours rest.
column2: 很遺憾，由於牆上的真菌在夜間發光讓房間保持明亮，加上隱士幾乎整晚都在講話，你只睡了5小時。

column1: You can't sleep here, you got's no rations to give me.
column2: 「你沒法睡這裡，你沒有口糧給我。」

column1: By golly, It's %s!
column2: 天哪，是 %s！

column1: Now that the big Antwerp has split, this corner of the forest seems strangely quiet.
column2: 現在大安特衛普已經分裂，森林的這個角落變得奇異地安靜。

column1: You look around and see rocks and grass, but no Antwerp.
column2: 你環顧四周，看到岩石和草，但沒有安特衛普。

column1: You see rocks, grass and an Antwerp.
column2: 你看到岩石、草和一隻安特衛普。

column1: The only Antwerp known in these parts has split... into parts.
column2: 這一帶唯一的安特衛普已經分裂…成碎片了。

column1: The Antwerp seems to have flown the coop.
column2: 安特衛普似乎逃脫了。

column1: Antwerps are on the endangered species list.  They are rarely seen.
column2: 安特衛普在瀕危物種名單上。它們很少被看到。

column1: The rocks were left here by some receding glacier.
column2: 這些岩石是被某座退縮的冰河遺留下來的。

column1: There is a narrow cave entrance among the rocks.
column2: 岩石中有一個狹窄的洞穴入口。

column1: You find a keyhole concealed in a crack in the rock.
column2: 你在岩石裂縫中發現了隱藏的鑰孔。

column1: You do not find anything nearby.
column2: 你附近找不到任何東西。

column1: The grass is luscious, just the thing for hungry herbivores.
column2: 草很茂盛，正是饑餓的草食動物喜歡的。

column1: The way is impassable.  Sheer rock cliffs rise to serious heights.
column2: 無法通行。陡峭的岩石懸崖高聳到驚人的高度。

column1: The forest extends to the east and north.
column2: 森林向東和向北延伸。

column1: Antwerps aren't dazzled easily.
column2: 安特衛普不容易被眩目。

column1: You aren't close enough to a lock.
column2: 你離鎖太遠。

column1: You have no weapon with which to fight the Antwerp.
column2: 你沒有武器與安特衛普戰鬥。

column1: However, you bravely attack the bouncing beast with your bare hands.
column2: 然而，你勇敢地用赤手空拳攻擊這個彈跳的野獸。

column1: The only Antwerp known to these parts, split ...into parts.
column2: 這個地方唯一已知的安特衛普，分解成……碎片。

column1: He's on a diet.
column2: 他在節食。

column1: You don't see any locks nearby.
column2: 你附近看不到任何鎖。

column1: The lock in the rock clicks open.
column2: 岩石上的鎖發出喀噠一聲打開了。

column1: You'd have a much easier time of this if you had the key.
column2: 有鑰匙的話會容易得多。

column1: Ah, got it!  The lock in the rock clicks open.
column2: 啊，成功了！岩石上的鎖發出喀噠一聲打開了。

column1: The lock is beyond your present skill.  It might help if you had the key.
column2: 這把鎖超出你現在的能力。有鑰匙的話可能會有幫助。

column1: ... Or at least a better set of tools.
column2: …或至少一套更好的工具。

column1: You are not strong enough yet to open the rock door.
column2: 你還不夠強大無法打開石門。

column1: Despite your mightiest efforts, the rock does not move.
column2: 儘管你盡力了，岩石還是沒有動。

column1: The rock door has already been opened.
column2: 石門已經打開了。

column1: You hear the sound of someone...or something...moving deeper into the cave to let you pass.
column2: 你聽到某個……或某種東西……在洞穴深處移動以讓路的聲音。

column1: Ok, you say Hiden Goseke.
column2: 好的，你說出「海登·格塞克」。

column1: Go ahead. If you dare.
column2: 向前走。如果你敢的話。

column1: That's funny.  It bounced right off.
column2: 真有趣。它直接彈開了。

column1: By golly, it's an Antwerp!
column2: 天哪，是安特衛普！

column1: The Antwerp plays rough.
column2: 安特衛普玩得很兇。

column1: The old ticker just couldn't keep going.  Maybe you shouldn't have missed the annual visit with your local Healer.
column2: 老心臟停了。也許你不應該錯過與本地治療師的年度檢查。

column1: Cockily, you loosen up to fight.
column2: 你傲慢地放鬆身體準備打鬥。

column1: Confident and loose, you approach the Antwerp.
column2: 你自信而放鬆地走近安特衛普。

column1: Holy Mackerel!
column2: 「天哪！」

column1: Your spell has unlocked the lock on the rock, but it is not yet powerful enough to open the rock door.
column2: 你的法術打開了岩石上的鎖，但還不夠強大到打開石門。

column1: You seem to have scared the Antwerp with your behavior.
column2: 你的行為似乎嚇到了安特衛普。

column1: You see your own reflection in the crystal-clear waters.
column2: 你在清澈透明的水中看到自己的倒影。

column1: You see a lovely waterfall in the distance, feeding the lake with ice cold mountain water.
column2: 你看到遠處有一座美麗的瀑布，用冰冷的山泉餵養著湖泊。

column1: You see a flock of geese as it passes overhead.
column2: 你看到一群鵝飛過頭頂。

column1: You see the reflection of a flock of geese as it passes overhead.
column2: 你看到一群鵝飛過頭頂的倒影。

column1: It must be your imagination.  What would a submarine be doing in a fantasy adventure?  Perhaps you'd rather be playing `Code Name: Iceman' -- it has a submarine.
column2: 那一定是你的想像。潛水艇在奇幻冒險中做什麼？也許你寧願玩『絕密代號：冰人』—— 它有潛水艇。

column1: You don't want to do that.  A walking pace is more appropriate for enjoying the majestic beauty of this scene.
column2: 你不想那樣做。漫步的步調更適合欣賞這場景的雄偉之美。

column1: Swimming in the Spiegelsee is a bad idea.  The lake is fed from mountain run-off, and the water is ice cold.
column2: 在史畢格爾湖中游泳是個壞主意。這個湖由山區徑流餵養，水冰冷刺骨。

column1: The lake water tastes pretty good, if a bit flat.
column2: 湖水嚐起來相當不錯，只是有點沒味道。

column1: ... but this is not the water you want.
column2: ……但這不是你要的水。

column1: You fill a flask with the clear lake water.
column2: 你用清澈的湖水裝滿一個瓶子。

column1: You don't have anything in which to carry the water.
column2: 你沒有東西來裝水。

column1: It's the beautiful Spiegelsee (Mirror Lake).  You pause for some peaceful reflection.
column2: 這是美麗的施皮格爾湖（鏡湖）。你停下來進行一些寧靜的冥想。

column1: There is a strong odor of decay and other more unpleasant smells filling the air.  A pile of something unpleasant lies near the center of this cave.
column2: 空氣中充滿了強烈的腐爛味和其他令人不適的氣味。一堆令人不適的東西堆在這個洞穴的中心附近。

column1: You can watch the water ooze down the sides of the stalactites and drip to the ground.
column2: 你可以看著水從鐘乳石的側面滲出，滴到地面。

column1: You can watch the water drop down from the stalactites and ooze down the sides of the stalagmites.
column2: 你可以看著水從鐘乳石滴下，從石筍的側面滲下。

column1: The water has been polluted by various other disgusting liquids.
column2: 水被各種令人厭惡的液體污染了。

column1: It looks as if someone has piled up bones, bits of armor, and things you can't quite make out and probably don't want to know about. It smells disgusting.
column2: 看起來有人堆積了骨頭、盔甲碎片，以及你無法看清楚且可能不想知道的東西。聞起來很噁心。

column1: A mean-looking Troll looms before you, a snarl on his lips.
column2: 一隻看起來兇惡的巨魔聳立在你面前，嘴角帶著咆哮。

column1: The Troll lies dying upon the slimy floor of the cave.
column2: 巨魔躺在洞穴粘滑的地板上奄奄一息。

column1: A dead Troll lies in a pool of blue blood.
column2: 一隻死巨魔躺在藍色血液的水池中。

column1: You see no such creature here.
column2: 你在這裡看不到這樣的生物。

column1: You have lost your sense of direction, as the cave passage twists around.
column2: 當洞穴通道蜿蜒時，你已經失去了方向感。

column1: The rocks look slick and are slimy to the touch.
column2: 岩石看起來光滑且摸起來有粘液。

column1: The light from the fungus is eerie and vaguely unpleasant.
column2: 真菌發出的光線詭異且隱隱令人不快。

column1: The only entrance to this cave is the one you came through.
column2: 這個洞穴的唯一入口就是你進來的那個。

column1: You can't do that.
column2: 你做不了。

column1: You need to get closer to the dead Troll.
column2: 你需要靠近死巨魔。

column1: The password is for the hidden passage, not the troll's home.
column2: 密碼是用於隱藏通道的，不是巨魔的家。

column1: Get closer to the pile.
column2: 靠近那堆東西。

column1: The dead Troll's huge club is much too heavy for you to lift.
column2: 死去的巨魔的巨大棍棒對你來說太重了，抬不起來。

column1: You don't need it.
column2: 你不需要它。

column1: This Troll looks as though he'd rather eat YOU!
column2: 這隻巨魔看起來寧願吃掉你！

column1: You hear the constant "drip... drip..." of water.
column2: 你聽到水的不斷「滴……滴……」聲。

column1: You hear the constant "drip... drip..." of water, and the unmistakeable sound of something breathing nearby.
column2: 你聽到持續的「滴……滴……」水聲，以及附近某物呼吸的清晰聲音。

column1: There is no magic in this cave.
column2: 這個洞穴裡沒有魔法。

column1: There is nothing here to dazzle.
column2: 這裡沒有什麼要眩目的。

column1: There is nothing here to use that spell on.
column2: 這裡沒有東西可以用法術施展。

column1: There is nothing here to calm.
column2: 這裡沒有什麼要平靜的。

column1: There is nothing here to open.
column2: 這裡沒有什麼可以打開的。

column1: That spell is useless here.
column2: 那個法術在這裡沒有用。

column1: The cave troll is dead.
column2: 洞穴巨魔死了。

column1: Before you can notice him, a Troll sneaks up behind you, and the encounter begins.
column2: 你還沒注意到他，一隻巨魔從你身後溜上來，戰鬥開始了。

column1: The Troll's brute force was too much for your skull.
column2: 巨魔的蠻力對你的頭骨來說太強了。

column1: There is nothing of value on the Troll.
column2: 巨魔身上沒有值錢的東西。

column1: You find nothing on the Troll but a little hair.
column2: 你在巨魔身上只找到一點毛髮。

column1: You dig around in the pile until the odor almost makes you sick.
column2: 你在堆裡翻找，直到臭味幾乎讓你生病。

column1: You find nothing more of value.
column2: 你找不到更多值錢的東西。

column1: You find 30 silvers and 5 gold.
column2: 你找到 30 銀幣和 5 金幣。

column1: There is no beard hair left on the Troll.
column2: 巨魔身上沒有留下鬍子毛。

column1: You cut some beard hair and put it away.
column2: 你剪了一些鬍子毛藏起來。
