column1: As your eyes adjust to the darkness, you see by the light of the fungus that this passageway twists its way through the mountain.  It is cold in here, and you can smell stagnant water and faint hints of decay.
column2: 當你的眼睛適應黑暗時，你借著真菌的光線看到這個通道蜿蜒穿過山脈。這裡很冷，你可以聞到停滯的水和衰爛的淡淡氣味。

column1: The water lies in stagnant pools.
column2: 水聚積在停滯的池子裡。

column1: A large Troll looms before you, a snarl on his lips.
column2: 一隻龐大的巨魔出現在你眼前，嘴角露出獰笑。

column1: The large Troll lies dying upon the slimy floor of the cave.
column2: 龐大的巨魔奄奄一息地躺在洞穴的濕滑地面。

column1: A large, dead Troll lies in a pool of blue blood.
column2: 一隻巨大的死巨魔躺在藍色血液的池子裡。

column1: There's no need for the password now.
column2: 現在不需要密碼了。

column1: The large Troll looks as though he'd rather eat YOU!
column2: 那隻龐大的巨魔看起來寧願吃掉你！

column1: There is nothing here to use it on.
column2: 這裡沒有什麼東西可以用它來對付。

column1: Before you can react, the Troll is upon you, and the encounter begins.
column2: 還沒等你反應，巨魔就撲向你，戰鬥開始了。

column1: You find nothing on the Troll.
column2: 你在巨魔身上找不到任何東西。

column1: You find no more beard hair on the dead Troll.
column2: 你在死巨魔身上找不到更多的鬍鬚。

column1: You cut off the Troll's beard and put it away.
column2: 你剪下巨魔的鬍鬚並收了起來。

column1: %s, you're in big trouble!
column2: %s，你陷入大麻煩了！

column1: The cliffs are rocky and riddled with niches that could conceal things.  Brigand archers for instance.  There is a pile of logs off to the north.
column2: 懸崖多石且布滿可能隱藏東西的縫隙。比如山賊弓手。北邊有一堆木頭。

column1: The rock walls look steep.
column2: 岩壁看起來很陡峭。

column1: It is difficult to see them because of the cliff walls, but you think you can make out something or things behind the logs.
column2: 因為懸崖壁，難以看清楚，但你認為可能看到木頭後面有東西。

column1: From what you can see, you realize that you are in a very dangerous situation.
column2: 從你看到的情況，你認識到自己陷入了極端危險的處境。

column1: You see archers perched on the cliffs.
column2: 你看到弓手們站在懸崖上。

column1: You'd better keep your head up.
column2: 你最好抬起頭來。

column1: You see sheer cliff walls.
column2: 你看到陡峭的懸崖壁。

column1: You see a suspicious pile of logs blocking the passage in this canyon.
column2: 你看到有一堆可疑的木頭擋住了峽谷的通道。

column1: You see where the canyon turns north.
column2: 你看到峽谷向北轉向的地方。

column1: The only way to climb is over the logs and you're not in a good place for that.
column2: 唯一的攀爬方式是越過木頭，但你現在位置不佳。

column1: Looks like the archers are running short on arrows.
column2: 看起來弓手的箭快用完了。

column1: A brigand archer!
column2: 一個山賊弓手！

column1: One bad dude!
column2: 一個狠角色！

column1: A brigand ground fighter!
column2: 一個山賊地面戰士！

column1: You have a splintering headache that's doing you in.
column2: 你有一個碎裂的頭痛，正在要了你的命。

column1: This was not the ending you had in mind when you read the manual on "How to be a Hero".  Either you just aren't ready to take on this situation, or there is a better way to accomplish your mission.
column2: 這不是你讀著《如何成為英雄》手冊時心目中的結局。要麼你還沒準備好面對這個處境，要麼有更好的方法完成你的任務。

column1: You have a bad feeling about this place.
column2: 你對這個地方有不好的感覺。

column1: You have an even worse feeling about this place.
column2: 你對這個地方有更糟糕的感覺。

column1: To the Minotaur, you look like a nice meal.
column2: 對牛頭怪來說，你就像一頓美餐。

column1: The Brigands oblige you by making your pleasant rest permanent!
column2: 山賊為了讓你永久地安息而幫了個大忙！

column1: Sheer cliff walls form a narrow box canyon where the brigands have built a fortress.
column2: 陡峭的懸崖壁形成了一個狹窄的箱形峽谷，山賊們在此建造了要塞。

column1: The rocks look to be good hiding places.
column2: 岩石看起來是很好的藏身之處。

column1: The gate appears to be fastened somehow on the inside.
column2: 大門看起來是從內部某種方式固定的。

column1: The cliff seems very hard to climb.
column2: 懸崖似乎很難攀爬。

column1: You can see marks in the ground, leading towards the bush.
column2: 你可以看到地面上的痕跡，指向灌木。

column1: You see a bush that conceals the secret entrance.
column2: 你看到一叢灌木隱蔽著秘密入口。

column1: The bush sits next to the cliff.  There are marks on the ground near the bush.
column2: 灌木坐在懸崖旁邊。灌木附近的地面上有痕跡。

column1: You see that where the steep cliff walls meet the fortress, there is a pile of gravel.  Once away from the secret passage exit, the fortress is entirely obscured by rocks and brush.
column2: 你看到陡峭的懸崖壁與要塞相接處有一堆碎石。一旦遠離秘密通道的出口，要塞就被岩石和灌木完全遮擋了。

column1: The cliff to the west looks too sheer to climb.  There is a bush growing close to the cliff.
column2: 西邊的懸崖看起來太陡峭無法攀爬。懸崖附近有一叢灌木。

column1: You see what must be the log walls and gate of the brigand fortress.
column2: 你看到那必定是山賊要塞的木製壁和大門。

column1: You see where the sheer cliff walls meet the fortress wall with a pile of rocks.
column2: 你看到陡峭的懸崖壁與要塞壁相接，還有一堆岩石。

column1: The walls of the canyon turn to the west.  You can see a large Minotaur patrolling the canyon.
column2: 峽谷的壁向西轉向。你可以看到一隻大牛頭人在峽谷內巡邏。

column1: A warning bell to alert all the brigands in the fortress.
column2: 用來警報要塞中所有山賊的警鐘。

column1: The sign reads "Ring bell".
column2: 標誌上寫著「搖鐘」。

column1: Step over and get a close look.
column2: 踏過去仔細看。

column1: There is no magical aura present.
column2: 偵測不到任何魔法氣息。

column1: You'll have to give yourself room.
column2: 你必須給自己讓出空間。

column1: The logs of the wall are too slick, and there is no place to get a good hold on them.  You'll have to try somewhere else.
column2: 壁上的木頭太光滑了，沒有好的立足點。你得試試別的地方。

column1: There is no good climbing place on the rock cliff to the east.
column2: 東邊的岩壁上沒有好的攀爬位置。

column1: You don't have enough climbing skill to scale the wall.
column2: 你的攀爬技能不足以爬過這面牆。

column1: You can't climb the rock.
column2: 你無法攀爬岩石。

column1: You're not in a good spot for climbing.
column2: 你現在位置不佳，無法攀爬。

column1: You must get closer.
column2: 你必須靠近一點。

column1: The gate appears to be fastened somehow on the inside.  You'll have to force the gate open.
column2: 大門似乎以某種方式從內側被固定。你必須強行打開大門。

column1: The gate is closed.
column2: 大門關閉。

column1: Why knock?  Use the bell.
column2: 為什麼要敲門？用鐘。

column1: Unless you're Rubber Man you'll have to get closer.
column2: 除非你是橡皮人，否則你必須靠近一點。

column1: Bell to alert gate guard.
column2: 用來提醒門衛的鐘。

column1: Sign reading "Ring Bell".
column2: 寫著「按鈴」的標誌。

column1: Gate to fortress.  A very strong gate.
column2: 通往要塞的大門。一道非常堅固的大門。

column1: Bush, a good place to hide.
column2: 灌木，藏身的好地方。

column1: A good place to hide.
column2: 藏身的好地方。

column1: Brigands' fortress.
column2: 山賊要塞。

column1: The Minotaur seems to have very sensitive hearing.  He looks around every time the bush rustles.
column2: 牛頭人的聽力似乎非常敏銳。每當灌木沙沙響起時，它就會四處張望。

column1: As you attempt the search, the sleeping Minotaur awakes, and...
column2: 當你試圖搜索時，沉睡的牛頭人怪醒了，而且……

column1: You've got all the loot you're going to find.
column2: 你已經得到了你會找到的所有戰利品。

column1: You quickly search the dead Minotaur and find nothing.
column2: 你快速搜查死掉的牛頭人，但什麼都沒找著。

column1: Wait a minute.
column2: 等一下。

column1: The Minotaur's flail looks peculiar.  You pick it up and examine it closely.
column2: 牛頭人怪的連枷看起來很奇怪。你把它撿起來仔細檢查。

column1: Cleverly concealed in the flail are 50 silvers.
column2: 連枷中巧妙地隱藏著 50 枚銀幣。

column1: A just reward for defeating such a valiant fighter, you tell yourself as you pocket the silvers.
column2: 「打敗這麼一位勇敢的戰士，真是恰當的報酬，」當你把銀幣放進口袋時對自己說。

column1: That's not a good idea.
column2: 那不是個好主意。

column1: If you've seen one, you've seen them all.
column2: 如果你見過一隻，就見過它們全部。

column1: Good ears but very poor peripheral vision.
column2: 耳朵好，但周邊視野很差。

column1: Why bother?
column2: 何必麻煩？

column1: OK.  Here's your chance.
column2: 好的。這是你的機會。

column1: I've been out here too long.  My imagination is taking over.
column2: 「我在這裡待太久了。我的想像力開始掌控了。」

column1: What was that noise?
column2: 「那是什麼聲音?」

column1: Must be those Brigands playing games.
column2: 「一定是那些山賊在玩把戲。」

column1: I guess I'm just getting jumpy.
column2: 「我想我只是有點緊張罷了。」

column1: Is anyone there?
column2: 「有人在嗎?」

column1: This place is spooky.
column2: 「這地方陰森森的。」

column1: You hear a snick as the hasp on the gate is opened.
column2: 你聽到一聲輕響，大門的門栓被打開了。

column1: You get the feeling that you're not alone.
column2: 你有一種不是獨自一人的感覺。

column1: No spells are active here.
column2: 這裡沒有任何法術生效。

column1: That was a useless gesture.
column2: 那是個無用的動作。

column1: None of the brigands notices your Calm spell.  There is too much negative thought energy here.
column2: 沒有山賊注意到你的鎮定術。這裡的負面思想能量太強了。

column1: That was a useless spell.
column2: 那是個無用的法術。

column1: There is nothing near that you can get over.
column2: 附近沒有你能越過的東西。

column1: The door is open.
column2: 門是開著的。

column1: This is no time for casual conversation.
column2: 這不是閒聊的時候。

column1: You're too busy trying not to become a human pincushion.
column2: 你忙著試圖不要成為人肉刺蝟。

column1: His aim in life is to make shish kebab.  I hope you're not skewered.
column2: 他的人生目標是烤肉串。希望你別被串成烤肉。

column1: Life as an archer is not dull or pointless.
column2: 箭手的生活並不枯燥無意義。

column1: He has an arrow with your name on it.
column2: 他手上有支刻著你名字的箭。

column1: He has an IQ so low he can't spell IQ.
column2: 他的智商低到連「智商」的英文都拼不出來。

column1: Sacks of grain.  Stored for the winter.
column2: 一袋袋穀物。為冬天儲存的。

column1: Empty wine barrels.  The winters here are very cold.
column2: 空酒桶。這裡的冬天非常寒冷。

column1: Rug with message "Step Here".
column2: 上面寫著「踩這裡」的地毯。

column1: These rolled-up rugs are spares for use when the rug at the left is lost.
column2: 這些捲起的地毯是備用品，用於左邊的地毯丟失時。

column1: A rope tautly strung.
column2: 一條繃緊的繩子。

column1: Sturdy looking bridge with one support pole across the chasm.
column2: 跨越裂隙的堅固橋樑，有一根支柱。

column1: Sturdy looking bridge with two support poles across the chasm.  A message reading "Cross Here" is on this bridge.
column2: 跨越裂隙的堅固橋樑，有兩根支柱。這座橋上寫著「在此過橋」的訊息。

column1: Chasm across the room.  The only safe passage is across the bridges.
column2: 房間對面是裂隙。唯一安全的通道是跨過這些橋樑。

column1: Door to some other room.
column2: 通往某個其他房間的門。

column1: Window showing vague outlines and shapes.
column2: 顯示模糊輪廓和形狀的窗戶。

column1: Some kind of cabinet showing a flip top secured by a hasp.
column2: 某種有翻蓋、用掛鉤固定的櫥櫃。

column1: Some kind of cabinet.
column2: 某種櫥櫃。

column1: Fortification for archers to stand behind.
column2: 弓手們站立的防禦工事。

column1: The stuff that dreams are made of.  Left here by some somnambulistic brigand.
column2: 夢想的材料。某個夢遊的山賊留下的。

column1: This was not the ending you had in mind when you read the manual on "How to be a Hero".  Either you just aren't ready to take on this situation, or there's got to be a better way to accomplish your mission.
column2: 這不是你讀著《如何成為英雄》手冊時心目中的結局。要麼你還沒準備好面對這個處境，要麼必定有更好的方法完成你的任務。

column1: You hear a snick as the hasp on the Jack in the Box is released.
column2: 你聽到跳跳盒上的門栓被打開時發出的輕響。

column1: The flying Wallendas have nothing on you.
column2: 飛行的瓦倫達沒什麼比得上你。

column1: No time for that again (although it WAS fun).  Just get the heck outta here.
column2: 現在沒時間再玩了(雖然確實很好玩)。趕快逃出這鬼地方。

column1: You're not in a good spot.
column2: 你的位置不好。

column1: It's too late for that.
column2: 現在太晚了。

column1: He's been drinking Troll's sweat all night.
column2: 他整晚都在喝巨魔汗水酒。

column1: Brigands over all!  Egotistical bunch, aren't they?
column2: 「山賊至上！」自大的傢伙們，對吧？

column1: The rope holds up the chandelier.  It's used to lower the chandelier so that it can be lit.
column2: 繩子吊著吊燈。它被用來放低吊燈好點燃它。

column1: Sturdy-looking device for holding candles.
column2: 看起來很堅固的燭台。

column1: Chandelier, used to hold up the rope.
column2: 吊燈，用來吊著繩子。

column1: You close and bar the door.
column2: 你關上門並用木條封住了。

column1: You're hopelessly outnumbered.  You should have left this room when you had a chance.
column2: 你被絕望地包圍。你應該在還有機會時離開這個房間。

column1: It looks like the talking is over.
column2: 看起來談話結束了。

column1: No more talking, my friend.  Let's have fun!
column2: 「別再囉唆了，我的朋友。我們來玩樂!」

column1: Now??
column2: 現在？？

column1: ME sees you preparing your spell and...
column2: 「我」看到你在準備法術……

column1: You're too busy.
column2: 你太忙了。

column1: ME sees you preparing to throw and...
column2: 「我」看到你準備投擲然後…

column1: Why would you want to throw that?
column2: 你為什麼想丟那個?

column1: You know me?  Then perhaps you have come to help.  Perhaps you are able to take the child from the band.
column2: 「你認識我？那也許你是來幫忙的。也許你能把小孩從這幫人手中救出來。」

column1: Be more specific, my friend.
column2: 「請說得更具體一點，朋友。」

column1: Talking is a speciality of those who know not what they talk about.
column2: 「談話是那些不知道自己在說什麼的人的專長。」

column1: You know Elsa?  Then perhaps you have come to help.  Perhaps you are able to take the child from the band.
column2: 「你認識艾爾莎?那你可能是來幫忙的。也許你有辦法把孩子從山賊手中救出。」

column1: After you have explained what you know about Elsa, Yorick speaks:
column2: 在你解釋了你對艾爾莎的了解後，尤瑞克說話了：

column1: It's about time that someone else helped Elsa before it's too late!  Toro and I are the only friends she's got here.
column2: 「是時候該有人在太晚前幫助艾爾莎了!我和托羅是她在這兒唯一的朋友。」

column1: She's a lot like her dad, though.  She organized these brigands and changed them from mere thugs to major raiders!
column2: 「不過她很像她爸爸。她組織了這些山賊，把他們從小流氓變成大盜匪!」

column1: She plans to move the entire band away from the valley as soon as the snow clears.  If that happens, I know she will never be disenchanted.
column2: 「她計畫一旦積雪融化就帶著整個幫派離開山谷。要是那樣，我就知道她永遠不會被解除詛咒。」

column1: You know about the enchantment?  Then perhaps you have come to help.  Perhaps you are able to take the child from the band.
column2: 「你知道詛咒的事?那你可能是來幫忙的。也許你有辦法把孩子從山賊手中救出。」

column1: Elsa doesn't know who she really is.  That's part of the enchantment.
column2: 「艾爾莎不知道她真正是誰。那是詛咒的一部分。」

column1: When Elsa was left with some brigands ten years ago, all her memories were taken away from her.
column2: 「十年前艾爾莎被留給山賊時，她的所有記憶都被奪走了。」

column1: I borrowed a Magic Mirror from a friend to try to break the spell, but it only works when spells are cast directly at you.
column2: 「我從朋友那兒借了面魔鏡想破解詛咒，不過它只有在法術直接施加在你身上時才有效。」

column1: If you're going to be the big Hero, I hope you brought a Dispel Potion or something.
column2: 「如果你要做大英雄，希望你帶了解除詛咒藥劑之類的。」

column1: Alas, poor Yorick!  I know me well.
column2: 「唉呀，可憐的尤瑞克！我太瞭解我自己了。」

column1: Ten years ago, I swore I would find Elsa no matter what.  It took me two years to find her, here with the brigands, and I've posed as her warlock ever since.
column2: 「十年前，我發誓一定要找到艾爾莎，無論如何。我花了兩年時間才在這裡找到她跟這些山賊在一起，從那時起我就一直偽裝成她的巫師。」

column1: I couldn't break the spell on her, nor could I leave her to grow up into an attractive woman surrounded by rogues and scoundrels.
column2: 「我無法破除她身上的詛咒，也無法任由她長大成為一個被無賴和騙子包圍的美女。」

column1: Someone had to defend her honor!
column2: 「得要有人為她的名譽辯護！」

column1: Can't you see that I am he?  I try to inspire (before they expire) a sense of awe and majesty in those that enter here.
column2: 「你沒看出來我就是他嗎?我試圖在進入這裡的人心中喚起(在他們完蛋前)敬畏和威嚴的感受。」

column1: After all, I am the Brigand Warlock.  One has to keep up one's appearances, or appear to keep up with one's peers.
column2: 「畢竟，我是山賊巫師。一個人必須維持自己的門面，或看起來要跟上自己的同僚。」

column1: Everybody needs to know a few magic tricks to get by these days.  You'd be surprised how effective Sleeping Powder is for taking out the guards of a caravan.
column2: 「現在誰都得知道幾招魔法才能活下去。你會驚訝於催眠粉對付商隊守衛有多有效。」

column1: You snooze, you lose.  And my banana trick has appeal of its own.
column2: 「你打瞌睡就會輸。而且我的香蕉戲法有其獨特的魅力。」

column1: For thirty years I was the Court Jester.  Now I jest enjoy watching other people take the pratfalls.
column2: 「三十年來我都是宮廷弄臣。現在我只是喜歡看別人出洋相。」

column1: I'll protect the Brigand Leader with everything I've got.  That's no laughing matter as I get madder.
column2: 「我會用盡全力保護山賊首領。這不是開玩笑，當我生氣時尤其如此。」

column1: The true Dispel Potion will break an enchantment.  You must splash the potion on Elsa as soon as you can, or she'll skewer you for sure.  She's more than a match for old Swordy Lordy" now!"
column2: 「真正的解除藥水將破除詛咒。你必須盡快將藥水潑在艾爾莎身上，否則她一定會刺穿你。她現在可絕對是『劍人老爺』的對手！」

column1: Of which potion do you speak?
column2: 「你說的是哪種藥水？」

column1: You must know the Blade Braggart".  He used to show off in the castle courtyard all the time."
column2: 「你一定認識『刀劍吹牛者』。他曾經經常在城堡庭院裡炫耀。」

column1: Elsa once asked if Rapier Ruler" would teach her to use the sword, and the "Sword Bore" told her he would not waste his talent on females."
column2: 「艾爾莎曾經問『劍騎統治者』是否願意教她用劍，而『劍癡乏味者』告訴她他不會浪費天賦在女性身上。」

column1: She came to me in tears.  After all, she was only nine at the time.
column2: 「她眼淚汪汪地來找我。畢竟她那時才九歲。」

column1: She can teach old Weapon Windbag" a thing or two now!  Her arm is true, and she's truly disarming."
column2: 「她現在可以教『武器廢話精』幾招了！她的手臂準確，她確實很令人卸下防備。」

column1: There are plenty of doors.  You wouldn't want me to spoil your fun, now!
column2: 「門多著呢。你應該不想我毀了你的樂趣吧!」

column1: Either you'll figure it out in the end, or you'll end it before you're out.
column2: 「你要不最後想通，要不就在逃出去前把自己終結。」

column1: This room is my way of keeping the brigands from bothering the Leader about raises, since it tends to raze the brigands.
column2: 「這個房間是我用來阻止山賊向首領要求加薪的辦法，因為它會把山賊消滅掉。」

column1: I designed it myself!  This place has people rolling in the aisles.
column2: 「我自己設計的!這地方會讓人笑得滾來滾去。」

column1: Ah yes, the Mirror with the Leer", as Erasmus calls it (or was it Fenrus?).  It reflects a spell back on the caster of the spell."
column2: 「啊是的，伊拉斯謨稱之為『譏諷的鏡子』（或是芬魯斯說的？）。它將魔法反射回施咒者。」

column1: The Warlock ruminates:\n"Now where did I put that mirror...\nwas it on the desk in Elsa's office?"
column2:
巫師沉思著：
「我把那面鏡子放在哪裡…
是放在艾爾莎的辦公室桌
上嗎？」

column1: Toro is the minotaur that guards this fortress.  Elsa found him when he was just a calf, and he's kowtowed to her ever since.
column2: 「托羅是守衛這座要塞的牛頭怪。艾爾莎在他還是牛犢時找到他，他從那時起就對她言聽計從。」

column1: He guarantees that the brigands won't give Elsa any bull."
column2: He guarantees that the brigands won't give Elsa any bull."

column1: This fort is the brigand's palace.  It has been well-constructed.
column2: 「這座要塞是山賊的宮殿。蓋得相當不錯。」

column1: Would he be surprised to see his little girl now!
column2: 「要是他現在看到他的小女兒，一定會大吃一驚!」

column1: If you have business to do, then get busy doing your business.
column2: 「如果你有事要做，那就開始做吧。」

column1: Can't you see that's nothing to me?  You'd better flee.
column2: 「你看不出對我來說那算什麼嗎？你最好逃命。」

column1: The brigands will come to spoil your fun.  You'll be undone.
column2: 「山賊會來破壞你的樂趣。你會完蛋。」

column1: The brigands tend to hold a grudge, and they won't begrudge to tend to you.
column2: 「這些山賊往往懷恨在心，他們也不會介意對付你。」

column1: If you have any famous last statements, you'd better say the secret word, or you'll lament your last state.
column2: 「如果你有什麼著名的遺言，你最好說出那個秘密詞語，否則你會為你的最後處境哀嘆。」

column1: It IS!  It's a bowl of fireflies, used as a light source.  What won't they think of next?
column2: 它正是！那是一碗螢火蟲，用作光源。他們下一步還會想到什麼？

column1: It's "ME".
column2: 是「我」。

column1: The arrow points to "ME".
column2: 箭頭指向「我」。

column1: It's an entrance...or is it an exit?
column2: 這是入口…還是出口？

column1: It's an exit...or is it an entrance?
column2: 這是出口…還是入口？

column1: The attic exit?
column2: 閣樓出口？

column1: The arrow points to a door.  Who could've guessed?
column2: 箭頭指向一扇門。誰能猜到？

column1: The arrow points to a door.  How secret is that?
column2: 箭頭指向一扇門。這有多秘密？

column1: A sign points to a door.  The egress?
column2: 標誌指向一扇門。出口？

column1: It's another door!
column2: 又是一扇門！

column1: It looks like a picture of an upside-down door painted on the wall.
column2: 看起來像是牆上畫的倒著的門的圖片。

column1: Engraved on the button are the words: "PUSH ME".
column2: 按鈕上刻著「按我」。

column1: It's a box of some sort...or so the sign says.
column2: 這是某種箱子…至少標誌這麼說。

column1: A pet door?  It's shorter than you are.
column2: 寵物門？它比你還短。

column1: It's a sign, but you'll have to be closer to read it.
column2: 這是一個標誌，但你得靠近才能讀。

column1: Stairs lead up to the platform on which "ME" is located.
column2: 階梯通往「我」所在的平台。

column1: Have a nice day!
column2: 祝你有美好的一天！

column1: It reminds you of a tie you once received as a present on All-Fools Day.
column2: 它讓你想起你曾經在愚人節收到的一條領帶。

column1: There appear to be stairs leading up (?) to nowhere.  There is also what looks like a bowl full of fireflies.
column2: 似乎有些階梯通往(?)無處可去。還有個看起來裝滿螢火蟲的碗。

column1: There is a deep pit with a walkway winding above it.
column2: 有個深坑，上面有條蜿蜒的走道。

column1: In the "upstairs" doorway, there is a chain with a handle attached.
column2: 在「樓上」的門口，有條綁著把手的鍊子。

column1:
DOOR
"SECRET ENTRANCE"
"EXIT"
"ME"
"BOX"
column2:
「門」
「秘密入口」
「出口」
「我」
「箱子」

column1: The arrows point to various orifices.
column2: 箭頭指向各種孔口。

column1: Take yourself there.
column2: 帶著你自己去那裡。

column1: With the Brigands pounding at the door, you decide not to rest until you're safe.
column2: 山賊在門上狂敲，你決定在安全前不休息。

column1: Your magic has no effect.  It must be something about this room.
column2: 你的魔法沒有效果。一定是這房間的關係。

column1: It would serve no purpose.
column2: 那沒有用。

column1: You curl up for a nice little nap.\nThe brigands break down the door.\nYou wake up dead.
column2: 你蜷縮著打了個小盹。山賊破門而入。你醒了，已經死了。

column1: Get closer.
column2: 靠近點。

column1: You can't do that from where you're standing.
column2: 你從現在的位置做不了這件事。

column1: You dare not unbar the door.  You hear hordes of brigands massing beyond it.
column2: 你不敢打開門。你聽到成群山賊在門外集結。

column1: You can't do it.
column2: 你辦不到。

column1: Nothing is locked.
column2: 沒有任何東西被鎖上。

column1: You finally made it through Yorick's room.
column2: 你終於通過了尤瑞克的房間。

column1: The Leader's room is filled with treasures.
column2: 首領的房間裡堆滿了寶藏。

column1: Used to belong to Bogey.
column2: 曾經屬於無名小卒。

column1: You see loot from brigand raids stacked against the walls.
column2: 你看到山賊襲擊掠奪的物品堆放在牆邊。

column1: You see a desk.  On the desk is a book and a mirror.
column2: 你看到一張桌子。桌子上有一本書和一面鏡子。

column1: What a funny guy!
column2: 多滑稽的傢伙！

column1: He's not here.
column2: 他不在這裡。

column1: This looks like a Persian carpet.  It has a license plate that says " Abdulla Doo 222".
column2: 這看起來像是一張波斯地毯。它有一個牌照寫著「阿布杜拉·嘟 222」。

column1: The brigand leader wears a sword, and it looks well used.
column2: 山賊首領配著一把劍，看起來用過很多次。

column1: Elsa looks much better in a dress.
column2: 艾爾莎穿著衣服看起來好多了。

column1: It depends on your taste, though, I suppose.
column2: 不過那取決於你的品味，我想。

column1: Everybody else is gone.
column2: 其他人都走了。

column1: You throw the Dispel Potion on the brigand leader.
column2: 你把驅散術藥水扔在山賊首領身上。

column1: Good idea, but you don't have that potion.
column2: 好主意，但你沒有那個藥水。

column1: You sense a strong magical aura on the brigand leader.
column2: 你感知到山賊首領身上有強大的魔法氣場。

column1: The hand mirror on the desk radiates a powerful magical aura.
column2: 桌子上的手鏡散發強大的魔法氣場。

column1: You sense magical auras from the large carpet and several other objects in the room, but you don't have time to investigate.
column2: 你感知到大地毯和房間內其他幾個物品上有魔法氣場，但你沒時間調查。

column1: There are no rocks here.
column2: 這裡沒有岩石。

column1: That would be foolhardy.  You would let the brigands in.
column2: 那太愚蠢了。你會讓山賊進來。

column1: You pick up the mirror and store it away.
column2: 你拿起鏡子並把它收起來。

column1: You already have the mirror.
column2: 你已經有那面鏡子了。

column1: You're not near the mirror.
column2: 你離那面鏡子太遠了。

column1: You place the two Healing Potions into your pack.
column2: 你把那兩瓶治療藥水放進你的背包。

column1: There are no more potions here.
column2: 這裡沒有更多的藥水了。

column1: You find nothing more.
column2: 你沒有發現更多東西。

column1: You see a mirror.
column2: 你看到一面鏡子。

column1: You find two Healing Potions.
column2: 你找到兩瓶治療藥水。

column1: A quick but thorough search of the desk discloses two Healing Potions and a mirror.
column2: 一次迅速但徹底的搜索發現了桌上放著兩瓶治療藥水和一面鏡子。

column1: You don't have time to take anything else.
column2: 你沒時間拿其他東西。

column1: Take only those things that will serve your quest.  Your time is limited.
column2: 只拿那些對你的任務有幫助的東西。你的時間有限。

column1: You can't imagine how good it feels to know who I am again.  After all these years, to remember my name is Elsa von Spielburg!
column2: 「你無法想像再次知道我是誰的感覺有多好。經歷了這麼多年，重新記起我的名字是艾爾莎·馮·史畢柏格！」

column1: I used to wish I was not a brigand's child but actually an enchanted princess.  Now I know that I really was enchanted, and I don't have to be a brigand any more!
column2: 「我過去希望我不是山賊的小孩，而是一個被施魔法的公主。現在我知道我真的被施了魔法，我不必再做山賊了！」

column1: Thank you so much for freeing me.
column2: 「非常感謝你救了我。」

column1: I've got to hurry home to father and get his guards to capture the rest of the brigands before they manage to escape with the treasure.  It must be returned to the rightful owners.
column2: 「我得趕緊回家去找父親，讓他的衛兵去抓捕其他山賊，免得他們趁機逃走帶走寶藏。寶藏必須歸還給合法的所有人。」

column1: Yorick!  Yorick!  I'm me again!
column2: 「尤瑞克！尤瑞克！我又是我自己了！」

column1: Sorry I took so long.  I got lost.
column2: 「抱歉我花了這麼久。我迷路了。」

column1: Will your Dad be glad that your spell's been repelled!  We need to go before the brigands know or they'll spoil our show.
column2: 「你爸爸會很高興你的詛咒被解除了！我們得在山賊知道之前離開，否則他們會破壞我們的計畫。」

column1: C'mon!  We'd better make our getaway before they get in our way.
column2: 「來吧！我們最好在他們擋住我們之前逃脫。」

column1: Yorick and I can return to the castle with the amulet I wear, but I'm afraid you'll have to get there on your own.
column2: 「尤瑞克和我可以用我戴著的護身符回到城堡，但我恐怕你得自己到那裡去。」

column1: There are two Healing Potions in my desk that you may take with you.
column2: 「我桌子裡有兩瓶治療藥水，你可以帶走。」

column1: You should use this secret passage and escape while they are arguing over the treasure.  If they find you in here, you'll be overwhelmed and killed.
column2: 「你應該用這條密道逃脫，趁他們還在為寶藏爭執。如果他們在這裡發現你，你會被壓倒並殺死。」

column1: Thank you again, and good luck!  I'll make sure you are richly rewarded for your bravery.
column2: 「再次感謝你，祝你好運！我會確保你因你的勇敢而得到豐厚的獎勵。」

column1: If you decide to counter the curser, then mind the mirror over mere minds.  So tip the canoe and toodleloo!  Elsa, if you do the honors, I'll honor your due.
column2: 「如果你決定對抗施詛咒者，那就注意鏡子勝於平常心。所以傾斜獨木舟，再見！艾爾莎，如果你做好了，我會尊重你的正當待遇。」

column1: The brigands are trying to break into the room.
column2: 山賊正在試圖破門而入。

column1: Thinking about all that treasure cost you time and more.  Some folks just don't know when to leave.
column2: 淨想著寶藏讓你浪費時間,甚至更多。有些人就是不懂什麼時候該閃人。

column1: Elsa, my dear.  There's a problem I fear.  To our dismay, that spell's here to stay, for alas you did slay your hero today.
column2: 「艾爾莎，我親愛的。恐怕有個問題。令我們遺憾的是，那個咒語在這裡停留了，因為你今天斬殺了你的英雄。」

column1: The brigand leader is deadly with a sword.  The magic spell that possesses her makes her an invincible warrior.
column2: 山賊頭子劍術致命。附身她的魔法咒語讓她成為不敗戰士。

column1: You do not have any daggers.
column2: 你沒有任何短劍。

column1: You do not have any rocks.
column2: 你沒有任何岩石。

column1: You pick up a few small rocks.
column2: 你撿起了幾塊小石頭。

column1: Suddenly a feeling of peace and tranquility permeates the area.
column2: 突然間，一種和平和寧靜的感覺充滿了整個區域。

column1: Uh oh!  The Kobold doesn't look very calm.
column2: 哎呀！地精看起來一點都不平靜。

column1: The Kobold casts a spell with which you are not familiar.
column2: 地精施展了一個你不熟悉的法術。

column1: Well, you THOUGHT all was calm.  Maybe it was just YOU that got calmed.  Or maybe the Kobold just CALMLY blasted you into oblivion.  Maybe he really doesn't want to talk about it.
column2: 好吧，你以為一切都很平靜。也許只是你被鎮定了。或許地精只是冷靜地把你炸成虛無。也許它真的不想談論這件事。

column1: You lost your concentration when you contemplated changing fighting modes.  The Kobold managed to blast you out of the game.
column2: 當你考慮改變戰鬥模式時失去了專注力。地精設法把你轟出了遊戲。

column1: The power of the Kobold Wizard's magic overcomes you.  The best defense against such power would have been a good strategy.
column2: 地精巫師的魔法力量壓倒了你。對付這種力量最好的防禦應該是一個好策略。

column1: You now have the Kobold's key.  Nice work!
column2: 你現在拿到了地精的鑰匙。幹得漂亮！

column1: As you look across this section of the cave, you see a Kobold resting on a ledge across from you.
column2: 當你看向洞穴的這個區域時，你看到一隻地精躺在對面的岩架上休息。

column1: You go! You no stay here!
column2: You go! You no stay here!

column1: No talk!  You GO!
column2: 「別說話！你滾！」

column1: There is a strong aura of magic around the key you have taken from the Kobold.
column2: 你從地精那裡拿走的鑰匙周圍有強大的魔法氣場。

column1: There is a strong aura of magic around the key hanging from the Kobold's neck.
column2: 地精脖子上掛著的鑰匙周圍有強大的魔法氣場。

column1: There is magic in the key left behind by the Kobold.
column2: 地精留下的鑰匙裡有魔法。

column1: You see the outline of a chest glowing in a corner of the cavern.
column2: 你看到洞穴角落裡一個發光的箱子輪廓。

column1: You can detect faint glimmerings of magic from the remains of the exploded chest.
column2: 你可以從爆炸的箱子殘骸中感知到微弱的魔法閃爍。

column1: There is no magic here any more.
column2: 這裡不再有魔法。

column1: He's not a happy Kobold.
column2: 它不是一個快樂的地精。

column1: Quit sightseeing and watch what you're doing!
column2: 停止觀光，注意你在做什麼！

column1: C'mon!  He's not going to KILL you...\nis he?
column2: 來吧！他不會殺死你…\n他會嗎？

column1: Later!  Later!
column2: 待會！待會！

column1: Of course!
column2: 當然！

column1: In other words, keep fighting!
column2: 換句話說，繼續戰鬥！

column1: You're a little too busy at the moment.
column2: 你現在有點太忙了。

column1: You weren't able to take the full force of the blast from the magically booby-trapped Kobold's chest.
column2: 你無法承受被魔法陷阱的地精箱子爆炸的全部衝擊。

column1: Wow!  The chest must've been booby-trapped.  You can really feel the damage from the blast.
column2: 哇！那個箱子一定被設了陷阱。你確實能感受到爆炸的傷害。

column1: The invisible chest's invisible lid lifts.
column2: 隱形箱子的隱形蓋子打開了。

column1: You add ten gold and sixty silver pieces to your money pouch.
column2: 你向錢袋裡加了十金幣和六十銀幣。

column1: You pry at the sealed lid of the chest, and...
column2: 你撬著封死的寶箱蓋子，然後……

column1: You hack and pry at the chest, but it seems to be stronger than you are.
column2: 你砍著並撬著箱子，但它似乎比你更強壯。

column1: Your fingers shift slightly, and...
column2: 你的手指輕輕移動，然後…

column1: A simple yes or no will suffice.
column2: 一句「是」或「否」就夠了。

column1: Come in and sit down.
column2: 進來坐。

column1: Fenrus, our guest has arrived.
column2: 芬魯斯，客人來了。

column1: Cheese, please!
column2: 起司，謝謝！

column1: Ahhhhhh!
Since you are a practitioner of the magical arts, you might be interested in a little game I have.
column2: 既然你是魔法師，也許會對我這個小遊戲感興趣。

column1: I'll teach you the Dazzle spell if you win.  You do want to play, don't you?
column2: 你要是贏了，我就教你眩目術。你想玩吧？

column1: Have you learned the %s spell yet?
column2: 你學過%s術嗎？

column1: Do you know how to cast the %s spell?
column2: 你知道怎樣施放%s術嗎？

column1: Nice to see you back.  Shall we play a game of Mage's Maze?
column2: 歡迎回來。要玩一把「法師迷宮」嗎？

column1: Wonderful!  Then you can play a game of Mage's Maze with me.
column2: 太好了！那咱們就來玩一把「法師迷宮」。

column1: Oh, well.  It is a good game.  Good practice, too.  Maybe we can play the next time you visit.
column2: 喔，那是個很棒的遊戲。也不錯當練習。下次你來的時候也許咱們可以再玩。

column1: I believe I gave a learning scroll to the Hermit.  You should talk to him about it.
column2: 我記得我給隱者一份學習卷軸。你應該跟他談談。

column1: Too bad.  You really can't play the game without it.  You can buy a learning scroll at Zara's magic shop in town.
column2: 太可惜了。沒有它你沒辦法玩。你可以在鎮上薩拉的魔法店買學習卷軸。

column1: I so enjoy playing the Mage's Maze.
column2: 我超喜歡玩「法師迷宮」。

column1: The "Open" spell lets you open things with a mere gesture.
column2: 「開啟術」可以讓你只需揮一下手就能打開東西。

column1: If you're really good at it, you can open locks.  It's useful when you lose your keys.
column2: 要是你夠厲害，你可以打開鎖。丟鑰匙時就派上用場了。

column1: Your keys are in the study under the skull.
column2: 你的鑰匙在書房骷髏下面。

column1: Why would they be there?
column2: 它們怎麼會在那裡？

column1: They're skeleton keys, aren't they?
column2: 那些是骨骼鑰匙，對吧？

column1: Fetch is a useful spell for catching and retrieving small objects at a distance.
column2: 「取物術」是個很有用的法術，可以在遠處抓取和回收小物件。

column1: It's good for putting things in their place.
column2: 它很適合把東西放回原位。

column1: Like uppity familiars?
column2: 像傲慢的魔法侍者？

column1: I'd put you in your place, but I'm above such things.
column2: 我想把你放回原位，但我不屑於做這種事。

column1: Besides, my place is above your reach.
column2: 而且，我的地位超出你夠不著的範圍。

column1: The "Trigger" spell is used to set off an existing spell, just like pulling the spell's "trigger".
column2: 「觸發術」用來觸發現有的法術，就像拉動法術的「扳機」。

column1: For example, if a chest has a magical trap on it, you can set off the trap by casting a "Trigger" spell.
column2: 例如，寶箱上有魔法陷阱的話，你可以施放「觸發術」來觸發它。

column1: Just make sure you're out of range of the trap.
column2: 只要確保你在陷阱範圍外就行。

column1: I can set up other spells and let someone else "Trigger" them.
column2: 我可以設置其他法術，讓別人去「觸發」它們。

column1: I fixed Henry the Hermit's cave for him so that he can do alot of magic with only one spell.
column2: 我幫隱者亨利修好了他的洞穴，這樣他用一個法術就能做很多魔法。

column1: Now that's a spell with charm!
column2: 那才叫有魅力的法術！

column1: It's true name is Erasmus' Razzle Dazzle, by the way.
column2: 順便說一下，它的真名是「伊拉斯謨的眼花繚亂」。

column1: You cast the spell and anyone looking directly at its corruscating light is blinded for awhile and can't do anything.
column2: 你施放法術，任何直視其閃爍光芒的人都會短時間失明，動彈不得。

column1: Magical traps are a good way to keep thieves out of your chests and other places.
column2: 魔法陷阱是把盜賊趕出寶箱和其他地方的好辦法。

column1: Put a fire trap, for example, on a chest.  When someone tries to open it, the thief will go up in smoke!
column2: 比如在寶箱上放個火焰陷阱。誰試著打開它，那盜賊就會被燒成灰燼！

column1: Tell our hero here what happens when you forget and try to open the chest yourself.
column2: 告訴我們的英雄，當你忘記而試著自己打開寶箱時會發生什麼。

column1: I only got singed the last time.  Nothing major.
column2: 上次我只是被燒焦了。沒什麼大礙。

column1: Never met her.  I only built this vacation home here a century ago.
column2: 從沒見過她。我只是一百年前在這裡蓋了度假別墅。

column1: Erana has a nice little magical meadow in her name northeast of here.  Pretty respectable Spellcaster, I imagine.
column2: 伊瑞娜在東北方有個以她名字命名的小魔法草地。我想她應該是個很值得尊敬的施法者。

column1: The Protection spell she cast over the town is fairly good, but I believe she missed a few places.
column2: 她在城鎮上施放的保護法術相當不錯，但我認為她漏掉了幾個地方。

column1: You're just jealous because the only thing anyone named after you was a soft drink.
column2: 你只是嫉妒，因為唯一以你命名的東西只是個飲料。

column1: I thought that "Erasmus' Rootin' Tootin' Root Beer" was a WONDERFUL name for a product!  It's a shame the company went broke after that.
column2: 我認為「伊拉斯謨的飆逐颶風根啤酒」是個絕佳的產品名稱！遺憾的是公司之後就倒閉了。

column1: Erana's spell prevents the use of excessive violence or hostile magic within Spielburg's walls, with the exception of a couple of places.
column2: 伊瑞娜的法術防止了在史畢柏格城牆內使用過度暴力或敵對魔法，除了幾個例外地點。

column1: It looks to me from up here as if she missed the back part of the alley, and the spell applies only to areas in town above the ground.
column2: 從我這兒看，她好像漏掉了小巷後面的地方，而且法術只適用於鎮上地面以上的區域。

column1: Pretty shoddy work, if you ask me!
column2: 要問我的話，這算得上草率的工作！

column1: Nobody asked you.
column2: 沒人問你。

column1: Zara has a real flair for the theatric.  Her method of greeting customers at her shop is showy but effective.
column2: 薩拉對戲劇效果很有一手。她在店裡迎接顧客的方式很浮誇，但很有效。

column1: It's a pity she has no sense of humor.
column2: 可惜她沒有幽默感。

column1: Just because she never laughs at your jokes doesn't mean that she lacks a sense of humor.
column2: 只是因為她從不笑你的笑話，並不表示她沒有幽默感。

column1: It just means that she has good taste.
column2: 那只說明她品味不錯。

column1: Baba Yaga is good at curses and shape-\nchanging spells.  She has a nasty temper, so it's best to stay on her good side.
column2: 芭芭雅嘎擅長詛咒和變身\n法術。她脾氣很壞，最好別惹她。

column1: You have to watch her.  She cheats at cards.
column2: 你得小心她。她打牌作弊。

column1: So do you!
column2: 你也一樣！

column1: She started it!  I just wanted to give her a taste of her own medicine.
column2: 她先開始的！我只想以其人之道還治其人之身。

column1: It's a shame she still beats you!
column2: 遺憾的是她還是贏了你！

column1: The curse Baba Yaga placed upon the Baron von Spielburg was a fine example of creative cursing.
column2: 芭芭雅嘎對史蒂芬·馮·史畢柏格男爵所施的詛咒是創意詛咒的典範。

column1: Upon von Spielburg and all his clan, this Curse I now demand:   What I will shall come full measure, so shall ye lose all that ye treasure.
column2: 「我對馮·史畢柏格及其族人施加詛咒：我所願之事必如願達成，汝將失去珍貴之物。」

column1: Of course, the problem is that for every curse there is an equal and opposite countercurse.
column2: 當然，問題在於每一個詛咒都有一個等量的反向解除咒語。

column1: A countercurse is the cure for a curse.  It usually works against the one who cast the curse in the first place.
column2: 解除咒語是詛咒的治療方法。它通常對最初施加詛咒的人起作用。

column1: The countercurse for the curse on the Baron goes:
column2: 對男爵詛咒的解除咒語如下：

column1: Come a hero from the East...
column2: 「來自東方的英雄……」

column1: Free the man from in the beast...
column2: 「救人於野獸之中…」

column1: Bring the child from out the band...
column2: 「救孩童脫離匪幫…」

column1: Drive the Curser from the land.
column2: 「驅逐施咒者離此地。」

column1: So, to break the curse, a hero must get rid of Baba Yaga, in addition to all the other things.  It does not reflect well on Baba Yaga.
column2: 所以，要破解詛咒，英雄必須除掉芭芭雅嘎，還有其他一些事。這對芭芭雅嘎沒什麼好處。

column1: Nothing reflects well upon that Ogress.  She has a face that would break all but a Magic Mirror.
column2: 那個食人妖身上沒什麼好談的。她的臉孔連魔法鏡都會破裂。

column1: What am I, chopped liver?  Am I invisible or something that you talk about me behind my front?
column2: 我是什麼，碎肝臟？我是透明的還是什麼，你在我面前還背後說我？

column1: It's hard to take you seriously when you wear that silly hat!
column2: 你戴著那頂蠢帽子時，我很難認真對待你！

column1: Henry is a hermit who lives by the Flying Falls to the south. Great sense of humor.
column2: 亨利是個隱者，住在南邊的飛瀑附近。幽默感很好。

column1: That means he actually laughs at Erasmus' jokes.
column2: 那表示他真的笑伊拉斯謨的笑話。

column1: I think he borrowed something the last time I saw him, but I can't remember exactly what it was.
column2: 我認為他上次見我時借了什麼東西，但我記不清楚是什麼了。

column1: Oh, well, Henry is a great one to talk to.
column2: 喔，亨利是個很好的聊天對象。

column1: But 'e 'ardly ever 'appens to 'ave much to say.
column2: 但他幾乎從來沒什麼話好說。

column1: You need to complete your quest here before you can become initiated as a Wizard.
column2: 你需要完成你在這裡的任務，才能成為正式的法師。

column1: There are many types of magic mirrors.  One of the more common types is the one that has a strange face popping up every so often.
column2: 有很多種魔法鏡。比較常見的一種是不時會有奇怪臉孔彈出來的。

column1: Reminds me of the one in the master bedroom.
column2: 讓我想起主臥室的那面。

column1: That's not a magic mirror.
column2: 那不是魔法鏡。

column1: No, but it does show a strange face.  Have you looked in it lately?
column2: 不，但它確實顯示一張奇怪的臉。你最近看過嗎？

column1: At any rate, I used to have a nice magic hand mirror, but I misplaced it somewhere.  It could be used to reflect a spell back upon the caster.
column2: 總之，我曾經有一面漂亮的魔法手鏡，但我在某個地方把它弄丟了。它可以用來把法術反射回施法者身上。

column1: So, if you happen upon a small golden magic mirror with a dragon on the handle, you'll know it's mine.
column2: 所以，如果你碰到一面把手上有龍的小金魔法鏡，你就知道那是我的。

column1: It was the flagon with the dragon.  The mirror had a leer.
column2: 那是有龍的法瓶。鏡子有個齷齪的樣子。

column1: Oh.  That's right!
column2: 喔。對了！

column1: Well, if you find a magic mirror with a face on it, you'll know it reflects spells.
column2: 如果你找到一面上面有臉的魔法鏡，你就知道它會反射法術。

column1: Basically, anyone who uses magic is a magic user;   anyone who casts spells is a spellcaster.
column2: 基本上，任何使用魔法的人都是魔法師；任何施放法術的人都是施法者。

column1: However, in order to be a wizard, you need to have undergone initiation into the "Wizard's Institute of Technocery" in the South.
column2: 但是，要成為法師，你必須在南方進行「法師技藝學院」的啟蒙儀式。

column1: Magicians and sorcerers are wizards who specialize.
column2: 魔術師和巫師是專精的法師。

column1: For myself, I prefer to master all aspects of Magic unlike the narrow-\nminded specialists.
column2: 就我而言，我更喜歡掌握魔法的\n所有面向，不像那些眼光狹隘的專家。

column1: You wouldn't want to lose your amateur standing, after all.
column2: 畢竟，你不會想失去業餘身分。

column1: I prefer to think of myself as a dilettante.
column2: 我更喜歡把自己想成業餘愛好者。

column1: Does that mean you tease pickles?
column2: 那表示你在欺負醃菜嗎？

column1: He laughs too much when he casts his spells.
column2: 他施放法術時笑得太多了。

column1: That's because his jokes are funnier than yours.
column2: 那是因為他的笑話比你的更有趣。

column1: Isn't that like the vampire who likes to take necks out for a good time?
column2: 這不像那個喜歡帶脖子出去玩樂的吸血鬼嗎？

column1: No, no!  That's a "neck romancer"!
column2: 不，不對！那是個「脖子浪漫家」！

column1: A necromancer is a magic user who has a rather unsavory relationship with corpses.
column2: 死靈法師是個和屍體有些不愉快關係的魔法師。

column1: Does that mean a necromancer has ghoul friends?
column2: 那表示死靈法師有食屍鬼朋友嗎？

column1: It means, my dear Fenrus, that a necromancer is someone who deals with the dead.
column2: 親愛的芬魯斯，那表示死靈法師是個和死者打交道的人。

column1: Canasta with cadavers?
column2: 和屍體一起打克納斯塔紙牌遊戲？

column1: Spades with shades?
column2: 和幽靈一起打黑桃牌？

column1: If Magic be the lute of life, play on!
column2: 「如果魔法是生命的琴弦，那就奏響吧！」

column1: The quote was "Music", not "Magic"!  And it was "food", not "lute"!
column2: 那句話是「音樂」，不是「魔法」！而且是「食物」，不是「魯特琴」！

column1: Oh!
column2: 喔！

column1: How about:  "Magic makes the world go 'round"?
column2: 怎樣：「魔法讓世界轉動」？

column1: MONEY makes the world go 'round!
column2: 「金錢讓世界轉動」！

column1: Very well, then.  To put it in my own words:  "Magic is the essence and the soul of life, and the Wizard is Her poet".
column2: 好吧。用我自己的話說：「魔法是生命的本質和靈魂，法師是她的詩人」。

column1: Actually, it was Merlin who said that first, but he used the term "Magician", not "Wizard".
column2: 實際上，是梅林先說的，但他用的是「魔術師」，不是「法師」。

column1: Fenrus, there are times when you'd look better as a newt!
column2: 芬魯斯，有些時候你看起來像條蠑螈會更好看！

column1: Why is that?
column2: 為什麼？

column1: Because newts can't talk!
column2: 因為蠑螈不會說話！

column1: Neither can most rats.
column2: 大多數老鼠也一樣。

column1: Just a little something to challenge the mind and the magic.  I like to play it every now and then, but Fenrus isn't much of a challenge anymore.
column2: 只是點小東西來挑戰心靈和魔法。我喜歡不時玩一下，但芬魯斯已經不那麼難以對付了。

column1: That's because I always win.
column2: 那是因為我總是贏。

column1: I still think you're cheating, somehow!
column2: 我還是認為你在某種程度上作弊！

column1: Anyway, the game involves casting four spells:   "Flame Dart", "Open", "Fetch", and "Trigger".
column2: 總之，遊戲涉及施放四個法術：「火焰鏢」、「開啟術」、「取物術」和「觸發術」。

column1: And to make it worth your while, if you beat me, I'll teach you a spell called "Dazzle".
column2: 為了讓你值得花時間，如果你贏了我，我就教你叫「眩目術」的法術。

column1: Do you know what you get when a Tyranno-\nsaurus running eastward meets a Tyranno-\nsaurus running westward?
column2: 你知道一條向東跑的暴龍和一\n條向西跑的暴龍相遇會發生什麼嗎？

column1: That's a smashing good joke, I think.
column2: 我認為那是個棒極了的笑話。

column1: Tyranno-\nsaurus wrecks!
column2: 暴龍殘骸！

column1: Is it true that a monster can't hurt you if you carry a torch?
column2: 是真的，只要你拿著火把，怪物就傷不了你嗎？

column1: Actually, it depends on how fast you carry it!
column2: 實際上，那取決於你拿得有多快！

column1: Do you know what time it is when Otto walks through your door?
column2: 你知道奧圖穿過你的門時是幾點嗎？

column1: So you've met Otto already.
column2: 所以你已經見過奧圖了。

column1: Time to get a new door!
column2: 是時候換個新門了！

column1: Do you know the difference between a Cheetaur and a comma?
column2: 你知道獵豹人和逗號的區別嗎？

column1: Spoilsport!
column2: 掃興鬼！

column1: A Cheetaur has claws at the end of its paws, and a comma is a pause at the end of a clause!
column2: 獵豹人在爪子末端有爪子，逗號是語句末尾的暫停！

column1: Do you know what's big, purple, and eats ungefaehr-\nliche dinge?
column2: 你知道什麼東西又大、紫色，\n而且吃 ungefaehr-liche 的東西？

column1: Well...let me figure out something tougher.
column2: 嗯……讓我想點更難的。

column1: Big, purple ungefaehr-\nliche dinge eaters!
column2: 又大、紫色的 ungefaehr-liche\n東西食客！

column1: Large purple ungefaehr-\nliche dinge eaters are all too common in the forest these days.
column2: 大型紫色 ungefaehr-liche 東西食客在森林裡這些天太常見了。

column1: Do you know which has more legs, one cow or no cow?
column2: 你知道哪個腿更多，一條牛還是沒有牛？

column1: Too easy, huh?  Well, I'll just have to think of a better one.
column2: 太簡單了，哈？好吧，我必須想想更好的。

column1: You're right!  NO cow has more than four legs.  These are too easy.
column2: 你說得對！沒有牛超過四條腿。這些太簡單了。

column1: Do you know the difference between a wounded Triceratops and a magic user casting the "Fly" spell?
column2: 你知道受傷的三角龍和施放「飛行術」的魔法師之間的區別嗎？

column1: Darn.  I thought I made that one up myself!
column2: 該死。我還以為那是我自己想到的呢！

column1: One is a raging Saurus and the other is a soaring Magus!
column2: 一隻是狂暴的索魯斯，另一隻是飛行的法師！

column1: I figured one was a dying lizard and the other was a flying wizard.
column2: 我猜一隻是奄奄一息的蜥蜴，另一隻是會飛的巫師。

column1: Sorry, Fenrus, but "He who tells the jokes gets to make up the punch lines".
column2: 抱歉芬魯斯，不過「會說笑話的人才有權決定笑點」。

column1: Did you hear the joke about the witches broom?
column2: 你聽過關於女巫掃帚的笑話嗎？

column1: Oh.  That's a pity!
column2: 哦，真可惜！

column1: That's odd.  It's sweeping the valley!
column2: 奇怪。它正在掃過山谷！

column1: That one usually floors me, but I'm going to brush it aside before I'm swept away with laughter.
column2: 那個笑話通常讓我笑到不行，但在被笑聲淹沒前，我要把它擱一邊。

column1: (Gag!)
column2: （乾嘔！）

column1: You look for a good place to climb.
column2: 你尋找一個好的攀爬地點。

column1: That was almost too easy...almost like climbing a ladder.
column2: 那太簡單了…簡直像爬梯子一樣。

column1: The rocks are too slick from the spray of the waterfall for your limited climbing skill.
column2: 由於瀑布的噴霧,岩石太光滑了,超出了你有限的攀爬技能。

column1: Man, that's a narrow ledge up there!
column2: 天啊,那是一個狹窄的岩架!

column1: Please move away\nfrom the door.
column2: 「請遠離門。」

column1: Oh.  'ello!    Go on in.
column2: 「哦。嘿!進去吧。」

column1: Mpfph..  grumble..  nobody 'ere, I guess.
column2: 「嗯…抱怨…我想這裡沒人。」

column1: 'ello?
The Baron von Spielburg looks like he was once a strong man, but sorrow has aged him greatly.
column2: 馮·史畢柏格男爵看起來曾經是個強壯的男人，但悲傷讓他蒼老了許多。

column1: The Baronet looks his usual haughty self, although his clothes are a little neater than the last time you saw him.
column2: 男爵之子看起來依舊高傲自大，儘管他的衣著比你上次看到他時整潔一些。

column1: The cupboard contains a variety of dishware and eating utensils.
column2: 碗櫃裡裝著各種碗盤和餐具。

column1: The von Spielburg coat of arms:  Twin Saurus guarding, Winged Chevron, on a field Azure.
column2: 史畢柏格家族紋章：雙索魯斯守衛，有翼雪佛龍，藍色盾場。

column1: The fireplace is cold and lifeless, the grate in front now only an ornament.
column2: 壁爐冰冷無生氣，前面的爐柵現在只是裝飾品。

column1: The guards stand stiffly at attention behind the Baron's table.
column2: 衛兵們筆直地站在男爵餐桌後面。

column1: The long tables are covered with dusty tablecloths.
column2: 長桌上蓋著佈滿灰塵的桌布。

column1: Through the door, you can see the hallway through which you entered, and the stairs leading down to the main entrance.
column2: 從門裡可以看到你進來時經過的走廊，以及通往主入口的樓梯。

column1: That would be inappropriate behavior here.
column2: 這樣的舉動在這裡不太得體。

column1: You realize, of course, that such actions will not only have you grabbed by guards, dragged to the dungeon, and brutally tortured, but that you probably won't be able to win this game if you perform them.
column2: 你當然明白，這類舉動不只會讓你被衛兵抓住、拖進地牢，還會被殘忍拷打，而且你可能根本無法贏得這場遊戲。

column1: The floor has not been cleaned in some time.
column2: 這地板已經有一陣子沒打掃了。

column1: The Great Hall of Spielburg Castle is not very impressive.  The room is plain, with scuffed floors and grimy plastered walls.
column2: 史畢柏格城堡的大廳並不令人印象深刻。房間簡陋，地板磨損，牆面沾滿灰塵。

column1: You are less than awed by Baron von Spielburg's Great Hall.  You'd think a Baron could keep his home a little cleaner.  You try to hide your disappointment.
column2: 你對馮·史畢柏格男爵的大廳並不感到讚嘆。你會認為一位男爵應該把家打掃得乾淨一點。你試圖隱藏你的失望。

column1: Words cannot express the gratitude I have towards you.
column2: 「言語無法表達我對你的感謝。」

column1: Not only have you freed the Baronet from enchantment and allowed my son to return to me, but you have renewed my hope that the curse may be lifted.
column2: 「你不僅解除了男爵之子的魔咒，讓我的兒子回到我身邊，還使我重新燃起詛咒被解除的希望。」

column1: My son, Baronet Barnard von Spielburg, also desires to acknowledge his indebtedness.
column2: 「我的兒子，史畢柏格男爵之子巴納德，也想表達他的感謝。」

column1: Barnard.
column2: 「巴納德。」

column1: We, er, that is, I, am appreciative of your efforts upon my behalf.
column2: 「呃，也就是說，我，對你代表我所做的努力表示感謝。」

column1: Furthermore, I understand you have rid our valley of a dangerous Kobold Magic User in order to free the Baronet von Spielburg.
column2: 「此外，我瞭解你消滅了一個危險的地精法師來解救史畢柏格男爵之子。」

column1: You risked your life to singlehandedly defeat the foul Spellcaster.  You are a true hero, and I thank you.
column2: 「你冒著生命危險獨自擊敗了邪惡的咒語使者。你是真正的英雄，我感謝你。」

column1: I'm pleased you managed to kill the Kobold.
column2: 「我很高興你設法殺死了地精。」

column1: I sincerely hope you will continue on your valiant adventures in our land.  If anyone can rid the land of the brigands, it will be you.
column2: 「我真誠地希望你能在我們的地盤繼續你的英勇冒險。如果有人能清除山賊，那就是你。」

column1: Someday in the near future, I can envision holding a ceremony in your honor and bestowing upon you the title, 'Hero of the Land of Spielburg'.
column2: 「在不久的將來，我能想像為你舉辦典禮，並賜予你『史畢柏格之地的英雄』的稱號。」

column1: It would be nice if someone could finally defeat the brigands and claim the title.
column2: 「如果有人能最終擊敗山賊並獲得頭銜就好了。」

column1: As you leave the castle tomorrow, you will receive the reward money I long ago posted for the safe return of my son, as a token of my thanks.
column2: 「當你明天離開城堡時，你將收到我早前為兒子安全歸來而懸賞的獎金，作為我感謝的表證。」

column1: You will, of course, be expected to dine with us and be our guest in the castle tonight.
column2: 「當然，你將被期待與我們共進晚宴，並在城堡內作我們的客人過夜。」

column1: Do you have any questions?
column2: 「你有任何問題嗎?」

column1: Just ask about anything you wish to know.
column2: 「儘管問你想知道的任何事。」

column1: I see that it is getting late.
column2: 「我看天色已晚。」

column1: Some years back, I tried to force the Ogress Baba Yaga to leave our valley, and she put a curse upon me that I should lose everything I held dear.
column2: Some years back, I tried to force the Ogress Baba Yaga to leave our valley, and she put a curse upon me that I should lose everything I held dear.

column1: When I lost my daughter and my son, I lost all interest in living.  My lands have suffered because I suffered.
column2: 「當我失去女兒和兒子時，我對生活失去了所有興趣。我的領地受苦是因為我受苦。」

column1: Now, though, with the return of my son, I trust the prophecy may yet come true, thanks to you.
column2: 「但現在，隨著我兒子的歸來，我相信預言可能會成真，感謝你。」

「預言說，
來自東方的英雄，
解救獸形的人；
救出被擄的孩子；
驅逐詛咒者離開此地。」

column1: You entered here from the East, and you released my son from the form of a bear.  I am certain you can fulfill the rest of it, and remove this dreadful curse.
column2: 「你從東方進來，你把我的兒子從熊的形態中解救出來。我確信你能完成其餘的，並消除這可怕的詛咒。」

column1: I have attempted everything in my power to get her to go away.  I fear it will take some sort of magic to be able to counteract her evil magic.
column2: 「我已經盡力讓她離開。我擔心需要某種魔法才能對抗她邪惡的魔法。」

column1: I know very little of magic, much to my sorrow.  Perhaps you should talk to the wizards -- Zara in the town, or Erasmus on Zauberberg.
column2: 「我對魔法知之甚少，這讓我很遺憾。也許你應該去找法師──城鎮裡的札拉，或是魔法山上的伊拉斯謨。」

column1: Zauberberg, the 'magic mountain', is the home of Erasmus, a kindly, but rather eccentric, wizard.  Zauberberg is directly east of here.
column2: 「魔法山，『魔法之山』，是伊拉斯謨的家，一位友善但相當古怪的法師。魔法山在這裡的正東方。」

column1: My beautiful little daughter was stolen away ten years ago by Baba Yaga's magic.
column2: 「我美麗的小女兒十年前被芭芭雅嘎的魔法奪走了。」

column1: She has never been found, despite all the searching that has been done for her.
column2: 「儘管進行了大量搜尋，她始終沒有被找到。」

column1: I thought she was lost forever, but you have renewed my hope that I will again have her beside me.
column2: 「我以為她永遠丟失了，但你重新點燃了我再次擁有她的希望。」

column1: My wife has been gone for many years.
column2: 「我的妻子已經離開我很多年了。」

column1: She died giving birth to Elsa, my daughter.
column2: 「她在生艾爾莎（我的女兒）時去世了。」

column1: If I had more guards, I would wipe the brigands out myself, now that I have my son at my side.
column2: 「如果我有更多衛兵，現在兒子在我身邊，我自己就能消滅山賊。」

column1: It will take some time before this castle and land have recovered from my sorrow, so I still think it will take a Hero to destroy the brigands.
column2: 「城堡和領地需要一些時間才能從我的悲傷中恢復，所以我仍然認為需要一個英雄來摧毀山賊。」

column1: I know they have some sort of fortress at the far south of the valley.  I have heard rumors they have set up an ambush for all that approach.
column2: 「我知道他們在山谷的南端有某種要塞。我聽說他們為所有靠近的人設置了埋伏。」

column1: It is also rumored that there is another way into their fortress, but I have no further knowledge about it.
column2: 「還有傳言說有另一條進入他們要塞的路，但我沒有更多的資訊。」

column1: Yorick was my court jester, a man of infinite jest.  He went off to find my daughter, but alas, poor Yorick never returned.
column2: 「尤瑞克是我的宮廷弄臣，一位充滿玩笑的人。他出去尋找我的女兒，但可惜，可憐的尤瑞克再也沒有回來。」

column1: I have offered substantial rewards for the return of my son and my daughter, as well as for capturing or killing the leader and warlock of the brigands.
column2: 「我為我的兒子和女兒的歸來，以及捉住或殺死山賊的領袖和術士提供了大筆獎賞。」

column1: There have always been brigands around, but until their new leader and the warlock showed up a few years ago, they were not much of a problem.
column2: 「周圍一直都有山賊，但直到他們的新領袖和術士在幾年前出現，他們才成為問題。」

column1: The leader seems to be a brilliant strategist who manages each raid with little loss.  Once their leader is gone, I am sure the brigands can be easily defeated.
column2: 「這位領袖似乎是位傑出的戰略家，每次襲擊損失都很小。一旦他們的領袖消失，我確信山賊可以輕易被擊敗。」

column1: As if this valley hasn't been plagued with enough evil magic users!  From what I have heard, his magic isn't powerful, but he uses it effectively.
column2: 「彷彿這個山谷還沒被足夠的邪惡魔法使用者困擾過！根據我聽到的，他的魔法並不強大，但他使用得很有效。」

column1: This castle has been in the family for two centuries now.  It was a gift from King Siegfried the Third.
column2: 「這座城堡在家族裡已經有兩百年了。這是西吉弗瑞德三世國王的贈禮。」

column1: I rode off one morning with my men in search of monsters to fight.  I got separated from my men when I came across the entrance to the cave.
column2: 「我一大早騎馬和我的人去尋找怪物戰鬥。當我來到洞穴入口時，我與我的人走散了。」

column1: As I entered the cave, the Kobold ordered ME to get out.
column2: 「當我進入山洞時，地精命令我滾出去。」

column1: I informed it that I was Baronet Barnard von Spielburg, and heir to the lands and all within.  I ordered it to bow before me.
column2: 「我告訴它我是男爵之子巴納德·馮·史畢柏格，這些土地和其中所有東西的繼承人。我命令它在我面前低頭。」

column1: Then it cast a spell upon me, and I remember very little after that.
column2: 「然後它對我施了咒，之後我記得不多。」

column1: I would prefer not to talk about that.
column2: 「我寧願不談這個。」

column1: As you can plainly see, Barnard is back to his old self, thanks to you.
column2: 顯然，巴納德已經恢復了本來的樣子，感謝你。

column1: Now that my son is back, I have much to do.  Kindly restrict your questions to matters of import.
column2: 「現在我的兒子回來了，我有很多事要做。請把你的問題限制在重要的事項上。」

column1: That is of no importance to your quest, and time is short.
column2: 「這對你的任務並不重要，而且時間緊迫。」

column1: A servant is awaiting you outside to show you to your quarters.  We will see you at supper later.
column2: 「有個僕人在外面等著帶你到你的房間。我們晚餐時見。」

column1: Ello.  'Ow are you?  'Ave we met before?
column2: 「嗨。你好嗎？我們見過面嗎？」

column1: I'm 'Enry the 'ermit, that's me.  Me Farther was an 'ermit and me Murther was an 'ermit sos I come by me job rightly.
column2: 「我是隱士亨利，就是我。我的父親是隱士，我的母親也是隱士，所以我算是子承父業。」

column1: Don't 'ave too many visitors.  'Ermits don't, you know.  (Part o' the job description.) I likes to see a new face, though.
column2: 「沒太多訪客。隱士就是不會有很多訪客，你知道。（那是職業的一部分。）但我還是喜歡看到新面孔。」

column1: Good to 'ear anurther's speaking besides meself.  Sos wot can I do for you?
column2: 「很高興除了自己還能聽到別人說話。那麼我能為你做什麼？」

column1: E's me friend, 'e and Fenrus.  Powerful wizard 'e is.
column2: 「他是我的朋友，他和芬魯斯。他是位強大的巫師。」

column1: I 'ere 'is 'ouse is sumthin' to see up north.
column2: 「我聽說他的房子在北邊，值得一看。」

column1: I don't make it out much, and 'e's pretty busy, sos I don't see 'em too often.  But when we do all get together, you can be sure we'll 'ave fun.
column2: 「我出門不多，他也很忙，所以我不常見到他們。但當我們聚在一起時，保證會很開心。」

column1: 'E loves to play 'is games, 'e do.
column2: 「他喜歡玩他的遊戲，真的。」

column1: Fenrus is Erasmus' familiar or is that the other way around?
column2: 「芬魯斯是伊拉斯謨的助手，還是說反過來啊？」

column1: Erasmus' favorite game is the Mage's Maze.  'Ave to be a magic user to play it, though.  Erasmus is awful good at it.
column2: 「伊拉斯謨最喜歡的遊戲是法師迷宮。雖然只有法師才能玩。伊拉斯謨特別擅長。」

column1: Nasty sorts, they is.  I sees them sneaking arund the woods all the times.  Uther then the Warlock, I 'as nothing to do with them
column2: 「他們是壞傢伙。我常常看到他們在森林裡鬼鬼祟祟。除了術士外，我跟他們沒有瓜葛。」

column1: E's not so bad.  Got a good sense o' 'umor, 'e 'as. I get the giggles just to think of 'im.  He heh he heh he.
column2: 「他還不錯。很有幽默感。我一想到他就咯咯笑。哈哈哈哈哈。」

column1: 'E's come by at times to chat.  Borrowed the mirror wot I borrowed from Erasmus 'e did.
column2: 「他有時會來聊天。他借走了我從伊拉斯謨那兒借來的鏡子。」

column1: A magic mirror of reflection, it was.  If you use it, when a nasty spell was cast at you, it was wot sent it back at the one wot cast it.  Do un to others, I sez.  Heh, he, he.
column2: 「那是一面魔法反射鏡。要是有人對你施壞法術，它會把法術反射回施術者。就是以其人之身還治其人之身。哈，呵呵，呵。」

column1: Well, it's dark and damp and the waters roars sos there's not a moment's quiet.  The ceiling drips and the walls are slimy and it's always cold.  But, oh well, sez I, it's 'ome.
column2: 「嗯，這裡黑暗潮濕，水聲轟鳴，片刻都不寧靜。天花板滴水，牆壁黏膩，永遠很冷。但老實說，我就當它是『家』。」

column1: A 'ermit is one wot lives alone, far away from them wot talks too much or asks too many questions.  'Ermits are shy, quiet types wot don't say much.  Me brother 'Arry 'ardly says a word in a year.  Me sister 'Ortense 'asn't spoken since she were six.  I never talks at all.  Yes, we 'ermits nose how to keep a mouth shut.
column2: 「隱士就是住在偏僻地方的人，遠離那些多嘴或愛問問題的人。隱士是害羞、安靜的人，不愛說話。我哥哥亨利一年幾乎說不了一句話。我妹妹霍蒂斯自六歲以來就沒說過話。我從不說話。沒錯，我們隱士知道如何閉嘴。」

column1: Me family lives pretty far apart from each other. We're all 'ermits, you know.
column2: 「我們一家住得很散。我們都是隱士。」

column1: I'm 'enry.  'Enry the eighth I is.  Me farther was an 'Enry and 'is farther was an 'Enry and 'is farther was an 'Enry and 'is farther was an 'Enry and 'is farther was an 'Enry and 'is farther was an 'Enry and 'is farther was an 'Enry. And every one was an 'ermit.
column2: 「我是亨利。我是亨利八世。我的父親是亨利，他的父親是亨利，他的父親是亨利，他的父親是亨利，他的父親是亨利，他的父親是亨利，他的父親是亨利。每一個都是隱士。」

column1: I likes to play the game.  I can show you how to play, if you likes.
column2: 「我喜歡玩遊戲。要是你有興趣，我可以教你怎麼玩。」

column1: It's the spell wot sets off uther spells.
column2: 「那是用來啟動其他法術的法術。」

column1: Like when you wants to see the ladder, you casts a trigger and there you 'ave it, large as life.
column2: 「比如說，你想看梯子時，你就施一個觸發術，嘿，梯子就在眼前。」

column1: Course, you gots to 'ave a spell on the invisible ladder to make it visible first.  But it 'as its uses.
column2: 「當然，你得先對隱形的梯子施法讓它現身。但它確實有用。」

column1: I thinks I 'ave a scroll with the spell, if you be knowing sum magic, that is.
column2: 「我想我有個捲軸裡有法術，假如你懂點魔法的話。」

column1: The spells wot I nose is pretty simple maybe, but I like 'em.
column2: 「我懂的法術或許很簡單，但我喜歡。」

column1: I can make me ladder show up and me door close and even get rid o' pests using the trigger spell, you see.
column2: 「我可以用觸發術讓梯子出現，讓門關上，甚至除掉害蟲，你看。」

column1: I nose sum magic.  Erasmus the wizard tot me sum spells.
column2: 「我懂一些魔法。法師伊拉斯謨教了我一些法術。」

column1: He's wot put the spell on me ladder sos the brigands don't get up.  Them wot climbs th' rocks gets a door opening in their nose.
column2: 「他就是在我的梯子上施法，讓山賊爬不上去。那些爬岩石的傢伙會被門打在鼻子上。」

column1: This 'ere's wot's called the Flying Falls.
column2: 「這個地方叫做飛瀑。」

column1: I've doon sum flying falls arund 'ere meself. That porch gets slippery sumtimes.  He, heh, he.
column2: 「我自己也在這附近飛過瀑布。那個陽台有時會很滑。哈，呵呵，呵。」

column1: Confidently, you cast the Calm spell.
column2: 你自信地施展鎮定術。

column1: Why, how cute!  You cast the Calm spell, and the monster visibly relaxed.  Why, now it's calmly and relaxedly ripping you to shreds and eating you.
column2: 哎呀，多可愛！你施展了鎮定術，怪物明顯放鬆了。現在它冷靜地、悠閒地把你撕成碎片並吃掉你。

column1: It was a tough battle, and you lost.\nNever fear!  All you have to do is restore your game, and...\nWhat do you mean, "Restore WHAT game?"
column2: 這場戰鬥很激烈,你輸了。別害怕!你只需要讀檔,然後……等等,你說什麼「讀什麼檔?」

column1: You're hopelessly surrounded.  You should have stopped those brigands somehow.  Maybe you'll see the light.
column2: 你無可救藥地被包圍了。你應該想辦法阻止那些山賊。也許你會看到光明。

column1: You'd better find a way to prevent the brigands from getting at you from all sides if you want to survive this.
column2: 你想活下來就得想辦法防止山賊四面夾擊。

column1: Oh, a wise guy, eh.
column2: 哦，你是個聰明人，嗯。

column1: Die drei Knochelkopfen grab you off the table.  You should have waited until they were out of reach.
column2: 三個骷髏頭怪搶過去把你從桌上抓走。你應該等到他們夠不著你再說。

column1: You're hopelessly surrounded.  You should have taken quicker action against these Knochelkopfs.  Elevate your thoughts to a higher plane.
column2: 你無可救藥地被包圍了。你應該更快對付這些骷髏頭怪。把你的思想提升到更高的境界。

column1: You move the chair in front of the door.
column2: 你移動椅子到門前。

column1: There are still too many brigands for you to fight in here.  You need to find some way to block one of the doors.
column2: 這裡的山賊還是太多,你打不過。你得想辦法封住其中一扇門。

column1: Wooden tables, benches, and chairs fill the space of this brigand meeting area.  A chandelier lights the room and an unlit candelabra stands in the corner.  A rope hangs from the chandelier.
column2: 木桌、長凳和椅子填滿了這個山賊集會區。一盞吊燈照亮房間，角落裡站著盞未點燃的燭台。繩子從吊燈垂下。

column1: There is a chandelier with a rope attached.
column2: 有一盞吊燈上面綁著繩子。

column1: The floor is strewn with flotsam and jetsam.
column2: 地板上散落著各種廢棄物。

column1: You see a door .
column2: 你看到一扇門。

column1: The door you came through is the only object you see.
column2: 你進來的那扇門是你唯一看到的物體。

column1: Most of the tables are grimy with dried beer and such.  The head table, though, is clean.
column2: 大部分的桌子都粘著乾燥的啤酒漬之類的東西。不過主桌看起來很乾淨。

column1: The chandelier is firmly attached to the rope in order to lower it to light the candles.
column2: 吊燈牢固地連接到繩子上，以便放低它來點燃蠟燭。

column1: The massive candelabra certainly seems to get in the way.
column2: 那個巨大的燭臺確實擋了路。

column1: It says, "Brigands over all" (only in German).
column2: 上面寫著「山賊至上」（只用德文）。

column1: They remind you of a bunch of Knochelkopfs.
column2: 它們讓你想起一群 Knochelkopfs(碰頭怪)。

column1: The rope is heavy and very sturdy.  It is secured to the chandelier.
column2: 繩子很重，非常牢固。它固定在吊燈上。

column1: You don't dare; the brigands are coming.
column2: 你不敢；山賊要來了。

column1: The door is locked from the other side.
column2: 門從另一邊被鎖上了。

column1: You're not near a door.
column2: 你不在門附近。

column1: That wasn't at all helpful.
column2: 那點幫助都沒有。

column1: There are no rocks in here.
column2: 這裡沒有石頭。

column1: The door is closed, but has no lock.
column2: 門關著，但沒有鎖。

column1: The door is locked.
column2: 門是鎖著的。

column1: The door is already blocked.
column2: 門已經被堵住了。

column1: Get closer to the chair.
column2: 靠近椅子。

column1: The door is closed but has no lock.
column2: 門關著，但沒有鎖。

column1: The candelabra won't budge.
column2: 燭臺紋絲不動。

column1: It's much too heavy to carry.  You can barely move it.
column2: 它太重了，拿不動。你幾乎動不了它。

column1: Just keep a cool head and be logical.  You don't need to run or sneak.
column2: 保持冷靜，要有邏輯。你不需要跑或偷偷摸摸。

column1: Welcome!  Welcome, traveler, to the Hero's Tale Inn!  I am Shameen, at your service.  May you find what you seek here.
column2: 「歡迎!歡迎,旅人,來到英雄客棧!我叫沙敏,為你效勞。希望你能在這裡找到你要找的東西。」

column1: I am sorry, but I'm much too broken to talk about such things right now.  I have lost everything!
column2: 「我很抱歉，但我現在太傷心了，無法談論這些事。我失去了一切！」

column1: The merchant is a rather fat man with a small moustache and beard.  He wears a turban.
column2: 商人是個相當肥胖的傢伙,長著小鬍子和鬍鬚。他戴著頭巾。

column1: The merchant consumes your meal in record time.
column2: 商人以創紀錄的速度吃掉了你的餐點。

column1: He then procedes to finish your drink in one gulp.
column2: 他隨後一口氣喝完了你的飲料。

column1: The merchant gratefully accepts your food ration, and consumes it in record time.
column2: 商人感激地接受你的食物配給,並以創紀錄的速度吃完了它。

column1: You can't give what you don't have.
column2: 你不能給你沒有的東西。

column1: The merchant finishes your drink in one gulp.
column2: 商人一口氣喝完了你的飲料。

column1: It is the merchant's hat.
column2: 這是商人的帽子。

column1: It is the merchant's turban.
column2: 這是商人的頭巾。

column1: There were about twelve brigands, including a Minotaur, if you can believe it!  Their leader was wearing a hooded cloak.  I could not see the face, but he had a high-pitched voice.  There was also some sort of warlock who giggled a lot.
column2: 「大約有十二個山賊，包括一隻牛頭人（如果你信的話）！他們的領袖穿著連帽披風。我看不到臉，但他聲音很尖銳。還有某種會咯咯笑的術士。」

column1: Although worn with age, the brigand leader's cloak must have, at one time, been an exquisite garment.  No doubt it was stolen, as were all of my goods!
column2: 「儘管因年代久遠而磨損，山賊領袖的披風曾經一定是件精美的衣服。毫無疑問它是被偷的，就像我所有的貨物一樣！」

column1: Perhaps it was my eyes that deceived me, but I could swear there was Gnome blood in that odd magician.
column2: 「也許是我眼花了，但我可以發誓那個奇怪的魔法師身上有侏儒血統。」

column1: The magician who travels with the brigands is no wizard.
column2: 「與山賊同行的魔法師不是法師。」

column1: It is my hat.
column2: 「這是我的帽子。」

column1: It is my turban.
column2: 「這是我的頭巾。」

column1: I am Abdulla Doo, son of Ali, grandson of Hasan, and formerly Master Merchant of Shapeir.  Now I am but a penniless burden upon my friends.
column2: 「我是阿布杜拉·嘟，阿里之子，哈桑之孫，曾為夏皮爾的商人之王。現在我只是朋友身上的無錢之累。」

column1: Even though they, too, lost a fortune when the brigands stole from me, Shameen and Shema are caring for this frail shadow of a great man.
column2: 「儘管沙敏和席瑪在山賊從我這裡偷走東西時也失去了一筆財富，他們仍在照顧這位偉大男人的脆弱影子。」

column1: Oh, I cannot bear to talk about my family at this sad time!
column2: 「哦，在這悲傷的時刻，我無法忍受談論我的家人！」

column1: You must not have heard of the brigands who prey upon the innocent and unwary.
column2: 「你一定沒聽說過那些掠食無辜者和不謹慎者的山賊。」

column1: All that I once owned is gone, alas!
column2: 「我曾經擁有的一切都消失了，唉！」

column1: Who would have thought that there could be such trouble entering this valley during the off-season?
column2: 「誰會想到在淡季進入這個山谷會遇到這樣的麻煩？」

column1: My good friends Shema and Shameen, owners of this inn, are the finest of all the Katta people that ever graced Shapeir!  But for their kindness, I would have starved long since.
column2: 「我的好朋友席瑪和沙敏，這家客棧的老闆，是曾經在夏皮爾出現過的所有卡塔族人中最好的！要不是他們的善良，我早就餓死了。」

column1: If you have never seen such a beast before, you may count yourself lucky.  It is like a giant of a man with a bull's head.
column2: 「如果你之前沒見過這樣的野獸，你算是幸運的。它像一個有牛頭的巨人。」

column1: Two weeks ago, I was about to become wealthy beyond my dreams.  I, Abdulla Doo, would be the first merchant into the valley this year.  But my life was shattered by those vile brigands!
column2: 「兩週前，我即將變得富有得超越我的夢想。我阿布杜拉·嘟將是今年進入山谷的第一位商人。但我的生活被那些邪惡的山賊粉碎了！」

column1: The band of brigands ambushed us just as we crossed the pass into the valley. They first used some magic which blinded us.  Then they overwhelmed my six guards and my assistants.
column2: 「當我們越過山口進入山谷時，那群山賊伏擊了我們。他們先用魔法使我們失明。然後他們壓倒了我的六個衛兵和助手。」

column1: All of my trade goods were taken from me, right before my very eyes!  Why the brigand leader spared my life, I do not know.  I am now but a beggar, living off the generosity of my friends.
column2: 「我所有的貿易商品都在我眼前被奪走了！山賊領袖為什麼饒了我的命，我不知道。我現在只是個乞丐，靠朋友的慷慨生活。」

column1: Ah, Shapeir!  Beautiful land of golden sands and shining sun! The heart of civilization!  Alas, she is plagued with fierce Djinni and Efreets, who seek to drive all men and Kattas from the land.
column2: 「啊，夏皮爾！金色沙漠和閃耀陽光的美麗之地！文明的心臟！唉，她被兇猛的精靈和火精靈困擾，他們試圖把所有人類和卡塔族人趕出這片土地。」

column1: But I can speak no more of the homeland I shall never see again.  Instead, I will die in this cold, forsaken land, bereft of all I love!
column2: 「但我再也不能談論我再也見不到的故鄉。相反，我將死在這個寒冷、被遺棄的土地上，失去所有我愛的。」

column1: Alas, had I known magic, perhaps I could have turned the tide against those abominable brigands.  Their magic did not seem so strong, but they outnumbered my poor caravan by two to one!
column2: 「唉，要是我懂魔法，也許我能扭轉局面對抗那些可憎的山賊。他們的魔法似乎不那麼強大，但他們的人數是我可憐的商隊的兩倍！」

column1: My guards are all gone, killed or run away like dogs!  I, too, would be lying dead had not the strange brigand leader spared my life, for what reason I know not!
column2: 「我的衛兵全都死了或像狗一樣逃跑了！如果不是那個奇怪的山賊領袖饒了我的命，我也早就死了，但我不知道是什麼原因！」

column1: Your kindness overwhelms me.  I can tell you will someday be a great hero.
column2: 「你的善良讓我不知所措。  我能看出你將來會成為一位偉大的英雄。」

column1: Now you will have a secret.  Among the items they stole from me was a magic rug.  I alone know the words to command it.
column2: 「現在你就擁有了一個秘密。  他們從我這裡偷走的物品中，有一條魔法地毯。  只有我知道驅動它的言語。」

column1: If you help recover my treasure, I will take you, Shameen, and Shema back to our land by way of the flying carpet.  Shapeir needs powerful heros, too!
column2: 「如果你幫我找回寶藏，我會帶你、沙敏和席瑪搭乘飛毯回到我們的家鄉。夏皮爾也需要強大的英雄！」

column1: Oh, it is indeed sad and dangerous times we live in when a man who struggles daily to keep from starving should be robbed of all his earthly possessions!
column2: 「哦，確實這些是悲傷和危險的時代，一個每天都在與挨餓作鬥爭的人被搶劫了他所有的塵世財產！」

column1: Hello again, my friend.
column2: 「又見面了，我的朋友。」

column1: May you dream of all the rewards you deserve.
column2: 「願你夢到所有你應得的獎勵。」

column1: You thank Shameen and pay him 5 silvers for the room.
column2: 你感謝沙敏,為房間付了他 5 枚銀幣。

column1: I am Shema.  Allow me to serve you, Wanderer from Afar.  Do you wish food or drink?
column2: 「我叫席瑪。讓我為你效勞,遠方的流浪者。你要食物還是飲料?」

column1: I bring you that which you ordered.  May it please and satisfy you.
column2: 「我為你帶來了你所點的東西。希望它能取悅並滿足你。」

column1: You thank Shema and pay her.
column2: 你感謝席瑪並付錢給她。

column1: Not until you're through with Shema.
column2: 除非你跟席瑪結束。

column1: You should wait for Shema to bring you what you ordered.
column2: 你應該等席瑪把你點的東西帶過來。

column1: Eat your meal first.
column2: 先吃你的飯菜。

column1: Finish your drink.
column2: 喝完你的飲料。

column1: Beyond the bear, the cave seems to continue.
column2: 過了熊之外，洞穴似乎還在延伸。

column1: The bear is gone.  All that is left is the body of a dead man.
column2: 熊消失了。剩下的只是一具死人的屍體。

column1: The bear is gone.
column2: 熊消失了。

column1: He's dead, %s.
column2: 他死了，%s。

column1: The man is gone.
column2: 那個人消失了。

column1: There is no magic in the cavern.
column2: 洞穴裡沒有魔法。

column1: The body lying there looks a lot like the picture of the Baronet von Spielburg on the poster at the guild hall.  You have a funny feeling that you may have made a slight tactical error.
column2: 躺在那裡的屍體看起來很像公會大廳海報上貴族少爺馮·史畢柏格的圖片。你覺得事情不對勁，好像犯了個小戰術錯誤。

column1: You have a funny feeling that you may have made a slight tactical error.
column2: 你覺得事情不對勁，好像犯了個小戰術錯誤。

column1: You have the honor of meeting the Baronet Barnard von Spielburg.  We are pleased that you broke our enchantment.  Perhaps our father the Baron will reward you should you ever visit our castle.
column2: 「你有幸遇見巴納德·馮·史畢柏格貴族少爺。我們很高興你打破了我們的魔法。也許我們的父親男爵會在你訪問我們的城堡時獎勵你。」

column1: The arrogance of the Baronet astonishes you.  Perchance the kobold Magic User had a reason for changing him into a bear.
column2: 貴族少爺的傲慢令你驚訝。也許那個地精法師有理由把他變成熊。

column1: You see the brigand leader.  From the look in her eyes you've only got seconds to live.
column2: 你看到山賊領袖。從她眼中的神情，你只剩幾秒鐘的生命。

column1: Casting a spell isn't useful now.
column2: 現在施咒沒用。

column1: She's not in a talking mood.
column2: 她沒有心情說話。

column1: Not a good idea.
column2: 不是個好主意。

column1: No time for that.
column2: 沒時間做那個。

column1: You think that you can fight the brigand leader?  You'd best think of some other plan.
column2: 你認為你能和山賊領袖打鬥？你最好想想其他計劃。

column1: I score that about a 3.  Those diving lessons were a complete waste.  Maybe you should have taken up bowling.
column2: 我打分 3 分。那些跳水課真是浪費。也許你該去學保齡球。

column1: You see a sign that reads, "Beware of Porcupine".  That's odd, you don't see a ...\nYou do, however, see many archers leaning over the chasm edge with arrows pointed toward you.  You now have a funny feeling you know who the porcupine is going to be in a few minutes.
column2: 你看到一個標誌，上面寫著「小心豪豬」。奇怪，你看不到…\n不過你看到許多弓箭手靠在峽谷邊緣，弓箭指向你。你現在有種奇怪的感覺，知道誰會變成豪豬。

column1: How embarrassing, to get this far and then fail to see the trip rope.  How did they get that Antwerp up there?
column2: 多尷尬，走到這裡卻沒看到絆繩。他們怎麼把那隻安特衛普弄上去的？

column1: There is a small sign which reads "Booby Trap".  You are now in a pitiful situation as you hear the brigands approach to see the booby they've caught.  You think to yourself, "If it looks like a trap, and it feels like a trap, then maybe it IS a trap".
column2: 有一個小標誌寫著「陷阱」。當你聽到山賊靠近來看他們抓到的獵物時，你陷入了可悲的困境。你心想，「如果看起來像陷阱，感覺也像陷阱，那也許它確實是個陷阱」。

column1: You seem to be having a rugged time of it.  You've had a terrible ca-tapestry.
column2: 你似乎經歷了艱難的時光。你經歷了可怕的掛毯慘劇。

column1: There are two notes.
column2: 有兩張紙條。

column1: There is a sign on the blue carpet which reads, "Step Here".
column2: 藍色地毯上有一個標誌，上面寫著「踩這裡」。

column1: There is a sign on the right bridge which reads, "Cross Here".
column2: 右邊的橋上有一個標誌，上面寫著「過這裡」。

column1: This is not what you expected the brigands' fortress to look like in your imagination.  The barracks with the open half-doors to the east and west are fairly normal looking.  The carpet lying near the gate, however, is a bit odd.  The small chasm in the middle of this yard with the bridge across it seems strange, too.  There are two blockades near the gate and some barriers near the north.
column2: 這不是你想像中山賊要塞的樣子。東西方開著半門的營房看起來相當正常。不過，靠近大門的地毯有點奇怪。中間的小峽谷和跨越它的橋也很奇怪。大門附近有兩個路障，北邊有一些障礙。

column1: That's interesting.  There seems to be a pit trap under the carpet.
column2: 這很有趣。地毯下好像有個陷阱坑。

column1: The carpets look rather new, perhaps recently stolen from the merchant in town.  There is a sign on the blue carpet which reads, "Step Here".
column2: 地毯看起來相當新，也許最近才從城裡的商人那裡偷來的。藍色地毯上有一個標誌，上面寫著「踩這裡」。

column1: The barracks have open half-doors and you can hear noise behind them.
column2: 營房有開著的半門，你可以聽到後面有聲音。

column1: The chasm is about six feet across and ten feet deep.
column2: 峽谷寬約六呎，深約十呎。

column1: There is a dark rope stretched tautly across the path.  It can easily be avoided by stepping over it.
column2: 有條黑繩子繃得很緊，橫跨小徑。你可以輕易地跨過去。

column1: It looks like a plain, simple, harmless box to you.
column2: 在你眼裡，這就是個普通、簡單、人畜無害的盒子。

column1: The bridge looks sturdy resting on its pole across the chasm.
column2: 木橋靠著一根柱子跨越峽谷，看起來很牢固。

column1: The bridge looks sturdy resting on its poles across the chasm.  There is a sign on it which reads, "Cross Here".
column2: 木橋靠著兩根柱子跨越峽谷，看起來\n很牢固。橋上有張告示寫著「在此\n渡過」。

column1: Sturdy looking bridge with two support poles across the chasm.  A sign that reads: "Cross Here" is atop the bridge.
column2: 牢固的木橋有兩根支柱橫跨峽谷。\n橋頂上有張告示寫著「在此渡過」。

column1: There are two bridges which cross the chasm.
column2: 有兩座橋跨越峽谷。

column1: The sacks seem to be full of grain.
column2: 麻袋看起來裝滿了穀物。

column1: The barrels look like empty wine casks.
column2: 木桶看起來像空的葡萄酒桶。

column1: Nothing but cobwebs.
column2: 只有蜘蛛絲。

column1: You see a rope tautly strung about 6 inches off the floor.
column2: 你看到繩子繃得很緊，離地板約 6 吋。

column1: Don't -- you'll get dizzy.
column2: 別──你會頭暈。

column1: The building to the north has a window and a single door.
column2: 北邊的建築有一扇窗和一扇門。

column1: You can see the fortress wall and the open gate.
column2: 你可以看到城牆和敞開的大門。

column1: You can see the fortress wall and the closed gate.
column2: 你可以看到城牆和關閉的大門。

column1: All that can be seen through the window are vague shapes and outlines.
column2: 從窗戶透過去只能看到模糊的身影和輪廓。

column1: You see a group of brigands playing cards.  One looks back at you in surprise and starts yelling.
column2: 你看到一群山賊在打牌。其中一個驚訝地轉身看著你，開始大喊。

column1: You're not near any window
column2: 你離窗口太遠了。

column1: Yep, it's a door all right
column2: 是的，確實是扇門。

column1: An empty cabinet.
column2: 空的櫃子。

column1: You don't have much time to look at toys.
column2: 你沒時間看玩具。

column1: The cabinet seems to have some kind of hasp.
column2: 櫃子似乎有某種門環。

column1: Get close for a good look.
column2: 靠近看得更清楚。

column1: The rugs are too big to put into your backpack and too heavy to carry around.  You do notice a pit trap under the spread-out carpet.
column2: 地毯太大放不進背包，也太重抬\n不動。你注意到攤開的地毯下有個\n陷阱。

column1: The rope is securely fastened and is pretty useless anyway.
column2: 繩子繫得很牢，不過也沒什麼用。

column1: Your life is in grave danger and you want to play with toys?  What kind of hero are you?.
column2: 你生命危在旦夕還想玩玩具？你\n算什麼英雄？

column1: They're too heavy to carry.
column2: 它們太重，搬不動。

column1: It's already open.
column2: 已經打開了。

column1: You're not close enough
column2: 你靠得不夠近。

column1: The door is closed.
column2: 門是關閉的。

column1: What a waste!  No treasure!
column2: 真是浪費！  沒有寶藏！

column1: You find a single silver coin, carefully polish it, and place it in your pouch.  What a way to make a living!
column2: 你找到了一枚銀幣，小心翼翼地擦拭它，然後放進荷包裡。  真是謀生的好辦法！

column1: You find %d silver coins, and carefully place them in your pouch.
column2: 你找到了 %d 枚銀幣，小心翼翼地放進荷包裡。

column1: You thought that the Troll concealed some of its treasure in that thick beard, but you didn't find any there.
column2: 你以為巨魔把寶藏藏在那濃密的鬍子\n裡，但你什麼都沒找到。

column1: You retrieve your daggers.
column2: 你拿回了你的匕首。

column1: You search your opponent.
column2: 你搜查你的對手。

column2: 可用點數

column2: 按 TAB 移動，\n按方向鍵調整數值。

column2: 你確定要重新開始？
