column1: As the sun begins to rise, the Troll runs off to some dark hole.
column2: 當太陽開始升起時，巨魔跑進某個黑暗的洞。

column1: You could not escape.  The battle is on.
column2: 你無法逃脫。戰鬥開始了。

column1: No rest for the weary.  Something's after you!
column2: 疲憊者無寧日。有什麼東西追著你！

column1: All the excitement of the battle has caused you to lose your appetite.
column2: 戰鬥的興奮讓你沒了食欲。

column1: It's dead, of course.
column2: 當然，它已經死了。

column1: It doesn't smell very good, either.
column2: 它的味道也不太好。

column1: You can't help but smell it.
column2: 你不禁聞到了它。

column1: You only think you can smell that.
column2: 你只是以為你聞到那種味道。

column1: Some of the Cheetaur's claws have been broken off or damaged, apparently in battle.  You remove the remaining claws and put them away in your pack.
column2: 獵豹人的一些爪子已被打斷或損壞，顯然是在戰鬥中。  你移除了剩下的爪子並放進背包。

column1: Cheetaur claws are very rare except on Cheetaurs.
column2: 獵豹人的爪子除了在獵豹人身上\n外很少見。

column1: You already have the Troll's beard.
column2: 你已經有巨魔的鬍子了。

column1: You remove as much of the Troll's beard as you can and put it away in your pack.
column2: 你盡可能地去除巨魔的鬍子並放入你的背包。

column1: That's not easy to do, especially since this is not a Troll.
column2: 那不容易做到，尤其是因為這不是\n巨魔。

column1: You look all around, but you can't find any of those.
column2: 你四處看看，但你找不到那種東西。

column1: It's an ugly little Goblin.
column2: 這是一隻醜陋的小哥布林。

column1: Uh oh, this looks like one of the brigands that have been terrorizing the Valley.  Better hope his friends aren't around.
column2: 哎呀，這看起來像是一直在騷擾山谷的山賊中的一個。  最好希望他的朋友不在附近。

column1: Don't worry, it's just a little purple Saurus.  Watch out for those teeth though.
column2: 別擔心，只是一隻小紫色索魯斯。  不過要小心那些牙齒。

column1: Oh, no!  It's a fierce (and hungry) looking Saurus Rex (smarter than your average Saurus).
column2: 哦，天哪！  這是一隻兇猛（且飢餓）的索魯斯雷克斯（比普通索魯斯更聰明）。

column1: It's a Cheetaur, a Cheetah Centaur, one of the deadliest creatures in the land.  Hope you've saved your game recently.
column2: 這是獵豹人，一種獵豹半人馬，陸地上最致命的生物之一。  希望你最近存檔了。

column1: It's an Ogre, big, mean, and ugly.  Ogres are said to eat human flesh.
column2: 這是食人妖，又大又兇又醜。  據說食人妖會吃人肉。

column1: It's a Troll, hard as stone.
column2: 這是巨魔，堅硬如石。

column1: It's a Mantray, a former sea creature that took flight many generations ago and moved to the mountains.  Don't get stung!
column2: 這是魔鬼魟，一種多代前飛上天空並遷移到山脈的海生生物。  小心別被刺中！

column1: It's a Program Bug!
column2: 這是程式漏洞！

column1: There is nothing here to fight.
column2: 這裡沒有可以戰鬥的東西。

column1: Although that spell is not useful here, you at least had a chance to practice it.
column2: 雖然那個咒語在這裡沒有用，但你至少有機會練習它。

column1: You don't see anything closed around here.
column2: 你沒看到周圍有任何關閉的東西。

column1: Maybe that creature is magical.
column2: 也許那生物是魔法造物。

column1: Certainly you don't want to Fetch THAT!
column2: 你當然不想用取物術取那個!

column1: Good practice, but there's nothing worth Fetching here.
column2: 很好的練習，但這裡沒有值得取的東西。

column1: Hero Status
column2: 英雄狀態

column1: Enemy Status
column2: 敵人狀態

column1: You cannot escape this encounter.
column2: 你無法逃脫這次遭遇。

column1: You cannot make the arcane gestures to cast spells while carrying a shield.
column2: 你拿著盾牌時無法做出咒語的奧秘\n手勢。

column1: This might not be the best time for practicing non-combat spells.
column2: 現在可能不是練習非戰鬥法術的最\n好時機。

column1: Go ahead!
column2: 去吧！

column1: You're too close.
column2: 你靠得太近了。

column1: You don't have time for that.
column2: 你沒時間做那事。

column1: Caught in combat with neither sword nor dagger, you are unable to resist the monster's fierce onslaught.  Try to be better equipped in your next life.
column2: 在沒有劍或匕首的戰鬥中,你無法抵抗怪物的猛烈攻擊。下輩子記得帶齊全裝備。

column1: You're too busy!
column2: 你太忙了！

column1: You ARE!
column2: 你確實是！

column1: You're not fighting, you're PRACTICING!
column2: 你不是在戰鬥，你是在練習！

column1: Why would you want to do that?
column2: 你為什麼要那樣做？

column1: Pity.  I'm in the mood for some good combat practice.
column2: 「可惜。我現在想要一場好的戰鬥練習。」

column1: I am a skilled teacher as well as a skilled fighter.
column2: 「我既是高明的教師，也是高明的戰鬥家。」

column1: However, the combat style I teach relies on the subtle alternation of Strike and Parry.  As I observe that you have no skill in the use of Parry, you would receive no benefit from my lessons.
column2: 「不過，我教的戰鬥風格靠的是招架和揮砍的微妙交替。我看得出你在招架上毫無技能，所以上我的課對你沒益處。」

column1: For a mere gold coin, I will be glad to give you some practice and some pointers on how to better your skills.
column2: 「只要一個金幣，我樂意給你一些練習機會，教你怎麼進步。」

column1: Will you pay for a lesson?
column2: 「你願意付費上課嗎？」

column1: You pay the Weapon Master's price and then...
column2: 你付了武器大師的價錢，然後…

column1: That's easy to say, but you don't HAVE a gold coin.  You make your apologies to the Weapon Master.
column2: 這麼說輕鬆，但你根本沒有金幣。你向武器大師道歉。

column1: Do not ramble, my friend
column2: 「別嘰哩呱啦，朋友」

column1: Will you pay?
column2: 「你會付錢嗎？」

column1: Will you fight?
column2: 「你會戰鬥嗎？」

column1: I'm sorry.  Since you have no skill in parrying blows, you would receive no benefit from my instruction.
column2: 「我很遺憾。由於你沒有招架的\n技能，你從我的指導中得不到任何\n好處。」

column1: The man has the muscles of a trained athlete and is apparently quite skilled with the sword.
column2: 這個人有受過訓練的運動員的肌肉，顯然對劍術相當熟練。

column1: The weapon master carries a blunted practice sword, in the belief that students retain more of their learning if they survive the practice sessions.
column2: 武器師傅拿著一把鈍的練習劍，因為\n他相信學生如果在練習中活下來會保\n留更多知識。

column1: Please don't bore me, young adventurer.  Ask me about something interesting to me.
column2: 「請別讓我感到厭倦，年輕的冒險\n家。問我一些我感興趣的事。」

column1: Fighting is both an art and a skill.
column2: 「戰鬥既是藝術也是技能。」

column1: It is a skill in the use of the muscle strength and agility's control.
column2: 「這是肌肉力量和敏捷控制的技能。」

column1: It is an art in the out-witting and out-maneuvering of the opponent.
column2: 「這是以聰慧應對和機動迴避對手\n的藝術。」

column1: I am, of course, a most skilled artist.
column2: 「當然，我是最熟練的藝術家。」

column1: Do you fight?
column2: 「你會戰鬥嗎？」

column1: If you were a swordsman skilled in both attack and parry, we could have had a practice bout.
column2: 「如果你是個攻防兼備的劍客，我們\n就可以來一場練習比賽。」

column1: The sword is the finest of all weapons.  It requires an equal measure of skill and strength.
column2: 「劍是所有武器中最好的。它需要同等的技能和力量。」

column1: I am the Weapon Master.
column2: 「我是武器師傅。」

column1: You will find them very skilled at fighting.  After all, I am the one who trains all of the guards at this castle.
column2: 「你會發現他們戰鬥能力很強。畢竟，\n我就是訓練這座城堡所有衛兵的人。」

column1: The Baron had the wisdom to recognize greatness when I presented myself.  Thus he made me Weapon Master.
column2: 「男爵有遠見認出我的偉大。因此他\n讓我做了武器師傅。」

column1: The Baronet had some minor talent with weapons, but he was too impatient.  If he had but listened and learned what I teach, he would never have been lost.
column2: 「少爺在武器上有些小才能，但他太\n沒耐心了。如果他肯聽課和學習我的\n教誨，就不會迷失。」

column1: I never use a shield or armor under most circumstances.  It only gets in the way of my agility and dodging ability.  It is best used by those who are strong, but not swift or agile.
column2: 「大多數時候我不用盾牌或盔甲。它\n只會妨礙我的敏捷和閃避能力。它最\n適合那些強壯但不敏捷的人用。」

column1: Practice is the way to refine and improve one's skills.
column2: 「練習是磨練和進步技能的途徑。」

column1: If you are addressing me, sir, you must respect me enough to address me to my face!
column2: 「如果你要跟我說話，先生，你得尊重我到面對面說話！」

column1: I salute you!
column2: 「我向你致敬！」

column1: En garde! shouts the Weapon Master...
column2: 「小心！」武器師傅喊著…

column1: And the lesson begins!
column2: 課程開始了！

column1: Enough! cries the Weapon Master.
column2: 「夠了！」武器師傅叫著。

column1: I can see now that my career must end.  Anytime that I, the Weapon Master, can be beaten by a mindless, nasty little twerp such as you, it is time to take an interminable vacation.
column2: 「我現在明白了，我的生涯該結束了。一個像你這樣沒腦子、討厭的小傢伙竟然能打敗武器大師我，是該去放個沒完沒了的假期了。」

column1: By your leave, I hope your sword rusts and your cape fragments in the most awful manner!
column2: 「你瞧著，希望你的劍生銹，你的披風以最難看的方式破裂！」

column1: The lesson wears you down, and you become exhausted.
column2: 這堂課把你磨得筋疲力盡。

column1: You are forced to admit that the Weapon Master has gotten the better of you.
column2: 你被迫承認武器大師擊敗了你。

column1: Even as we fought, I could detect your skills improving through practice.
column2: 「雖然我們在交手，但我看得出你的技能在進步。」

column1: It was most prudent that you should give in to my superior skill.
column2: 「你明智地認輸於我高超的技能，真是聰慧。」

column1: Still, you seem to tire more easily than you should.
column2: 「話說回來，你似乎比應該的還要容易疲勞。」

column1: Be more aware of the angle with which your weapon approaches your opponent.  The thrust bites quick and deep, but the swing will do more damage if it connects.
column2: 「要更留意你的武器朝向對手的角\n度。刺擊又快又深，但揮擊如果擊中\n會造成更多傷害。」

column1: The art of dancing would be a good skill for you to practice, my friend.  The balance of the body over the rapidly moving feet has won many a contest.
column2: 「舞蹈藝術對你是個好技能，朋友。\n身體在快速移動雙腳上的平衡贏得過\n許多比賽。」

column1: You have a tendency to overbalance when you dodge a blow.  This will adversely affect the timing and effectiveness of your counterattack.
column2: 「你躲避攻擊時容易失衡。這會影響你反擊的時機和效果。」

column1: Put your attention to the control of your legs when you duck a swing.  Your springiness makes you seem to bounce, and a good opponent (like myself) will always take advantage of the opening.
column2: 「當你躲開揮擊時，注意腿的控制。\n你的彈性讓你看起來在彈跳，好對手\n（像我一樣）總是會利用空隙。」

column1: If you would like a stretcher, my weak, tired friend...\nYou seem a shadow of the person who began this little lesson.
column2: 「你需要擔架嗎，我虛弱疲憊的朋友…你看起來只是開始上課時那個人的影子了。」

column1: Should we meet again, I would not be adverse to another go-round.  Farewell, friend!
column2: 「改天若再相逢，我樂意再切磋一番。再見，朋友！」

column1: You're mad as heck, and you're not going to take it anymore.  As a matter of fact, you CAN'T take it anymore.  Restore your strength and health and try again.
column2: 你氣炸了,再也受不了。事實上,你真的受不了了。恢復你的力量和生命力再試一次。

column1: You're mad as heck, and you just won't take it anymore.  As a matter of fact, you CAN'T take it anymore.  Start over, and remember to keep up your strength and health.
column2: 你怒火中燒，再也受不了了。事實上，\n你根本受不了了。重新開始，記得保持\n體力和生命力。

column1: NOT NOW!!
column2: 現在不行！！

column1: You're mad as heck, and you just won't take it anymore.  As a matter of fact, you CAN'T take it anymore.  Keep up your strength and health and try again.
column2: 你怒火中燒，再也受不了了。事實上，\n你根本受不了了。保持體力和生命力，\n再試一次。

column1: It's a little sign that reads: "Do not read this sign under any circumstances.
column2: 這是個小告示寫著：「在任何情況下\n都不要讀這張告示。」

column1: You feel disoriented.
column2: 你感到暈眩。

column1: How does he DO this?
column2: 他到底是怎麼做到的?

column1: There's an ECHO IN HERE...Echo In Here...echo in here...
column2: 這裡有迴音…迴音…迴音…

column1: M. C. Escher would LOVE this place.
column2: M. C. 艾舍爾一定會愛上這個地方。

column1: OK, enough fooling around!
column2: 好吧，別亂來了!

column1: This is getting old!
column2: 這傢伙太老了!

column1: A person COULD get used to this.
column2: 人是有可能習慣這個的。

column1: You bar the door behind you.
column2: 你在身後用木條封住門。

column1: You behold a most fascinating place.
column2: 你看到了一個最迷人的地方。

column1: The person labeled "ME" speaks:
column2: 被標籤為「我」的人說話了：

column1: Abandon mope, all ye who enter here!
column2: 「所有進入此地者，都要拋棄悶悶不樂!」

column1: Job hunting, or just sight-seeing?
column2: 「在找工作，還是只是來觀光?」

column1: I foresee a brilliant future for you in the fall.
column2: 「我預見你的輝煌未來…會在秋天到來。」

column1: If you've got something to say, then speak fast or forever hold your pieces.
column2: 「如果你想說什麼，就說快點，要不就永遠閉嘴。」

column1: State your case before I case your estate!
column2: 「在我接收你的遺產之前，先陳述你的理由!」

column1: Elsa is right next door.  I'll go set up the secret exit for your escape.
column2: 「艾爾莎就在隔壁。我去準備秘密出口好讓你逃脫。」

column1: This time the joke fell flat.  That was a truly dirty trick.  Too bad you won't have a chance to get even...or will you?
column2: 這次笑話沒成功。那真是個骯髒的把\n戲。太遺憾了你沒機會報仇…或者你有？

column1: What a miserable experience!
column2: 多麼慘糕的經驗！

column1: Oops!  You thought this door led SOMEWHERE!
column2: 哎呀！你以為這扇門通往某個地方！

column1: The bright smell of fresh herbs mingles with the aroma of wood smoke as you near a hut by the side of the road.
column2: 當你靠近路邊的小屋時，新鮮草藥的\n清香與木煙的香氣混合在一起。

column1: It takes a lot of skill and practice to climb this tree.
column2: 爬這棵樹需要很多技能和練習。

column1: You carefully bend down and reach into the nest.
column2: 你小心地彎下身子，伸手進入巢裡。

column1: In the nest, you find a gold ring.
column2: 在巢裡，你找到了一枚金戒指。

column1: You take the ring and move carefully back along the limb.
column2: 你拿起戒指，小心地沿著樹枝往回走。

column1: Made it!  Now back down the tree.
column2: 成功了！現在下樹吧。

column1: Your bug doesn't have any hands.  This makes throwing anything out of the question.
column2: 你的蟲子沒有手。這使得投擲任何\n東西都是不可能的。

column1: Looks like all you can do is wriggle.
column2: 看起來你只能蠕動。

column1: Better concentrate on the game.
column2: 最好專注於遊戲。

column1: Schwertfisch
column2: 寶劍魚

column1: Ach du lieber
column2: 天哪

column1: Schweinhund
column2: 豬狗

column1: Antwerp
column2: 安特衛普

column1: Rheingold
column2: 萊茵金

column1: Deutschmark
column2: 德國馬克

column1: Purple Saurus
column2: 紫色索魯斯

column1: The brigands were waiting for you to come back.  They expected you to make another try for some of their treasure.
column2: 山賊在等你回來。他們以為你會再試一次搶他們的寶藏。

column1: Grumble Grumble Grumble.  Stupid guard duty, third time this week.  Why always me?  Grumble Grumble Grumble.
column2: 「碎碎念碎碎念碎碎念。該死的衛兵職責，本週第三次。為什麼總是我？碎碎念碎碎念碎碎念。」

column1: I'M BAAD!
column2: 「我是個壞傢伙！」

column1: You find a good purchase on the rocks to climb.
column2: 你在岩石上找到了一個很好的立足點來攀爬。

column1: The dirt is too loose for climbing here.
column2: 這裡的泥土太鬆散了，無法攀爬。

column1: Well I'll huff, and I'll puff, and I'll smash the gate down.
column2: 「好吧，我要深吸氣，鼓起勁，砸開\n大門。」

column1: Boy that feels good.
column2: 「天哪，感覺真好。」

column1: 'DING DING DING DING....' \n"Well, maybe the brigands aren't such inhospitable creatures after all."
column2: 『叮叮叮叮…』\n「也許山賊並不是那麼不好客的生\n物。」

column1: Then again, maybe they are.
column2: 不過話說回來，說不定他們是。

column1: The brigands have an even temperament...all bad!   You seem to have a knack for doing the wrong thing.
column2: 山賊的脾氣很平穩…全都是壞！你似\n乎很會做錯事。

column1: As you reach toward the painting...
column2: 當你伸向畫……

column1: From somewhere upstairs, you hear the Sheriff shout: "Otto!".
column2: 你聽到樓上某處治安官喊著：「奧圖\n！」。

column1: The people who own this house must have some money. Everything looks new and there is not a speck of dust visible.
column2: 擁有這棟房子的人一定有些錢。所有東西看起來都很新，看不到一絲塵埃。

column1: The room smells vaguely of sauerkraut and bratwurst, with just a faint odor of smoke from pinewood. From somewhere in the house you can hear someone snoring.
column2: 房間裡隱隱散發著酸菜和德式香腸的味道，還有一絲松木煙火的淡淡氣味。你可以聽到房子某處傳來打鼾聲。

column1: By lifting the painting, you can see what certainly must be a safe, hidden in the wall.
column2: 藉由抬起畫，你可以看到一定是隱藏在牆裡的保險箱。

column1: You carefully lower the painting into its original position.
column2: 你小心地把畫放回原來的位置。

column1: Naughty, naughty.  The Sheriff and Otto arrive on the scene and arrest you for "blatant power-gaming".  You have to *work* at it to become a *real* hero!
column2: 壞壞。治安官和奧圖現身逮捕你「明目張膽亂遊戲」。你得真的去*努力*才能成為*真正*的英雄！

column1: Obviously, you shouldn't have done that!
column2: 顯然，你不應該這樣做！

column1: Now you've done it!  It's hard to be a hero when you're locked up for breaking and entering (or entering and breaking, as the case may be).  Be a little more intelligent (and a lot quieter) if you ever try something like this again.
column2: 現在你完蛋了!當你因為非法侵入(或侵入後破壞,不管哪種)被鎖起來時,很難成為英雄。下次要是再試什麼花樣,聰明點(也安靜點)。

column1: Gently and stealthily, you lift the lid on the little box.
column2: 你輕輕地、悄悄地打開小盒子的蓋子。

column1: As the little music box begins to play, you hear the Sheriff yell out: "Otto, stop playing with that music box and GO TO BED!"
column2: 當小音樂盒開始演奏時，你聽到治安官大聲叫道：「奧圖，停止玩那個音樂盒，去睡覺！」

column1: Boy, did YOU make a mistake!
column2: 哎呀，你可真犯了個錯誤！

column1: Otto can't find the music box, but he's too dim and sleepy to figure it out, so he heads on back to bed
column2: 奧圖找不到音樂盒，但他太遲鈍又太瞌睡，想不出來，所以他就回床上去。

column1: Otto, even in his sleepy state, winds the music box and closes the lid before he heads back to bed.
column2: 奧圖即使在瞌睡狀態下，也在回床之前把音樂盒上了發條並關上蓋子。

column1: Otto stares sleepily at the closed music box for a moment and heads back to bed.
column2: 奧圖睡眼惺忪地看著關上的音樂盒片刻，然後回床上去。

column1: That was close! The goon must've been so dumb or sleepy or both that he didn't even see you standing there.
column2: 差點就暴露了！那傢伙一定是太笨、太瞌睡，或者兩者都有，才沒看到你站在那裡。

column1: Obviously, getting in the goon's way was not one of your brighter ideas. You've had it now!
column2: 很顯然,擋在手下的路上不是你的聰慧之舉。現在你完蛋了!

column1: It's hard to be a hero when you're sitting in a jail cell.  The Sheriff apologized for your broken arm, but he did warn you (didn't he?) that Otto was only partly trained.  In the future, you'll probably be more careful when you're robbing someone.
column2:
坐在牢房裡很難成為英雄。治安官為
你折斷的手臂道歉，但他確實警告過
你（不是嗎？）奧圖只經過部分訓練。
將來，你搶劫別人時可能會更小心。

column1: When it comes to breaking in, it looks like the only thing broken is your head!  When at last you come to your senses, you find yourself in a jail cell.  By the time you get out of here, you'll be too old to be a hero.
column2: 說到撬鎖,看起來只有你的腦袋被撬破了!當你終於清醒時,你發現自己在牢房裡。等你出獄,你已經老到不能當英雄了。

column1: Uh Oh! As you see a pillow flying toward you, you hear the Sheriff's wife say...
column2: 哎呀！當你看到一個枕頭飛向你時，你聽到治安官妻子說……

column1: SCREEEEEEEEEEEECH!
column2: 「啊啊啊啊啊啊啊啊啊啊啊！」

column1: You never dreamed a feather pillow could be so HARD!  Or was it the floor?  You also didn't realize how hard it is for a thief to be a Hero.  The Thief of Baghdad should have this kind of luck!
column2: 你從沒想到羽毛枕頭會這麼硬！或者\n是地板？你也沒意識到盜賊成為英雄\n有多難。巴格達大盜應該有這樣的運氣！

column1: It looks difficult. You look around for a good place to climb.
column2: 看起來很難。你四處尋找適合爬的地方。

column1: This looks like a good place to you.
column2: 這看起來像個不錯的地方。

column1: You valiantly scrabble against the cliff wall, but it looks like your climbing skill could stand some improvement.
column2: 你勇敢地在懸崖壁上亂抓，但看起來你的攀爬技巧還有待改進。

column1: Perhaps there is some other way to get to your objective.
column2: 也許還有其他方式到達你的目標。

column1: You look for an appropriate place to climb.
column2: 你尋找適合爬的地方。

column1: Ohhhhhhh!
You remain unconscious on the ground for an indeterminate amount of time. As you finally revive, you realize that you've caused yourself a fair amount of damage.
column2: 你昏迷在地上好一陣子。當你終於清醒過來時，你意識到你對自己造成了相當大的傷害。

column1: You put the seed into your pack and climb down.
column2: 你把種子放進背包並爬了下去。

column1: BE STILL!
column2: 「別動！」

column1: Critics!  We can't all be gourmands, I suppose.
column2: 「美食家呢！我們也不是都是美食家。」

column1: Now, Oh-Soon-To-Be-Supper...\nI don't suppose you have a name?"
column2: 嗯，即將成為晚餐…\n我猜你沒名字吧？

column1: You try your best to croak out your name, or at least let the witch know that you do indeed have one.
column2: 你盡力用呱呱聲說出你的名字，至少要讓女巫知道你確實有個名字。

column1: So you're the one who's trying to be a hero around here.  The only good hero's a dead hero, I always say!
column2: 「所以你就是想在這裡逞英雄的傢伙。我常說，好英雄就是死英雄！」

column1: But I do have a need for a brave fool.  Are you brave?
column2: 「但我確實需要一個勇敢的傻瓜。\n你勇敢嗎？」

column1: You make little froggy sounds, trying your best to indicate your agreement.
column2: 你發出小青蛙的叫聲，盡力表示同意。

column1: Wellll...
If you're willing to do a small little teensy favor for me, I might reconsider having you for supper.
column2: 「如果你願意幫我做個小小的小忙，我也許會重新考慮把你當晚餐吃。」

column1: I need the root of a mandrake plant\nthat grows in the graveyard.  Will you be\na sweet and fetch me some?
column2: 「我需要在墓園裡長的曼陀羅根。親愛的，你能去給我拿一些嗎？」

column1: Anything but Frog Legs Fricassee!, you think.   You croak your agreement to the task.
column2: 「什麼都好，就別是炸青蛙腿！」你想著。你用呱呱聲表示同意這任務。

column1: And I had my mouth watering for frog.  Oh well!
column2: 「我本來還滿想吃青蛙呢。算了！」

column1:
Hear what I say
And hear me right:
Mandrake must be pulled
At precisely Midnight!
column2:
「聽我言
聽我說：
曼德拉草必被拔起
午夜時分！」

column1:
This I tell you
And this I say:
Return with the root
Ere the break of next day.
column2:
「這是我告訴你
這是我要說的：
帶著根回來
天亮前！」

column1:
Hear what I say
And know I don't lie:
Bring back the root
Or else you will die!!
column2: 「聽我說，相信我不會說謊：把根帶回來，不然你會死！」

column1: You, Shoo!
Back so soon?
column2: 「這麼快就回來？」

column1:
Spirits of Mist
And Creatures of Bog:
Transform my guest
To the shape of a Frog.
column2: 「迷霧之精與沼澤生物：把我的客人變成青蛙的形狀。」

column1: This I vow:\nStay there now!
column2: 「這是我發誓的：現在待著！」

column1: Once again you're a frog, and once again you can't move.  You find it very exasperating!
column2: 你再次變成了青蛙，再次動不了。你覺得這很惱人！

column1: Yum, Yum.  Froggie Frappe!
column2: 「好吃，好吃。青蛙小冰沙！」

column1: Did you bring me my mandrake like you promised?
column2: 「你有像約好的那樣給我帶曼陀羅來嗎？」

column1: As you make a feeble croaking sound, you try to nod your head.

column1: This seems like a quiet little town.  On the porch ahead of you are two people.  The standing one is large, rather ugly, and playing with a yo-yo.  The seated person smoking a pipe looks like he might be the Sheriff.
column2: 這看起來像是一個安靜的小城鎮。  你前面的門廊上有兩個人。  站著的那個又大又醜，在玩溜溜球。  坐著抽煙鬥的那個人看起來可能是治安官。

column1: Otto and the Sheriff have retired for the evening.
column2: 奧圖和治安官已經晚間退休了。

column1: The Sheriff calls out behind you:  "Just a friendly suggestion, but whatever you do, don't drink the Dragon's Breath.  Not even Otto can drink Dragon's Breath!"
column2: 治安官在你身後喊道：「只是友善的建議，但無論如何都別喝龍息酒。連奧圖都喝不了龍息！」

column1: There's no hint of the Ogre.
column2: 沒有食人妖的蹤跡。

column1: You see no sign of the Sheriff.
column2: 你看不到治安官的蹤影。

column1: The Sheriff's office doubles as a jail for the town and a hangout for the Sheriff and the Goon.
column2: 治安官辦公室兼作城鎮監獄和治安官與食人妖的聚集地。

column1: The Sign of the Scissors above the door, along with the striped pole, indicates that the building contains a Barber Shop.
column2: 門上的剪刀標誌，加上那條紅白相間的竿子，表示這棟建築是理髮廳。

column1: A typical barber's pole.
column2: 一根典型的理髮店竿子。

column1: The shades are drawn, and it appears that the Inn is closed.
column2: 窗簾被拉上了，客棧似乎已經關閉了。

column1: The windows glow from the light inside. The sign reads "Hero's Tale Inn".
column2: 窗戶從裡面的燈光發出光芒。  招牌上寫著「英雄傳奇客棧」。

column1: The town gate is made of sturdy logs.
column2: 城鎮大門是用結實的木頭做的。

column1: It stands open.
column2: 它敞開著。

column1: It is closed, locked, and barred.
column2: 它已關閉、上鎖、並被加鎖。

column1: There's a sign on the door of the barber shop, informing whoever reads it that the barber is out to lunch.  The door is locked.
column2: 理髮廳門上有個標誌，告訴讀者理髮師出去吃午餐了。門被鎖上了。

column1: The barber must keep odd lunch hours.
column2: 理髮師的午餐時間真奇怪。

column1: There are signs over each of the buildings informing you of their purpose.
column2: 每棟建築上都有標誌，告訴你它們的用途。

column1: Searching a law enforcer is a bad idea.
column2: 搜索執法官是個壞主意。

column1: The door appears to be locked, and breaking into the Sheriff's office might evoke the wrath of Otto Von Goon.
column2: 門看起來被鎖住了，硬闖進治安官辦公室可能會引起古恩先生的憤怒。

column1: You'd better not. The Goon would probably try to strangle you with his yo-yo if he thought you were going into the Sheriff's office without permission.
column2: 你最好別這樣。如果古恩先生認為你要未經允許進入治安官辦公室，他可能會試圖用他的溜溜球勒死你。

column1: There's a sign on the door of the barber's shop, informing whoever reads it that the barber is out to lunch. The door is locked.
column2: 理髮廳門上有個標誌，告訴讀者理髮師出去吃午餐了。門被鎖上了。

column1: Turn around.
column2: 轉身。

column1: That's the Sheriff's job.
column2: 那是治安官的工作。

column1: The gate is already closed.
column2: 大門已經關閉了。

column1: The town gate is standing wide open, as it does every day.
column2: 城鎮大門像往常一樣敞開著。

column1: The sturdy town gate is completely locked and barred, as it is every evening.
column2: 結實的城鎮大門像每個晚上一樣完全被鎖住和加鎖。

column1: Only the Sheriff can unlock the gate.
column2: 只有治安官能打開大門。

column1: Spielburg remains colorful, even at night.
column2: 即使在晚上，史畢柏格依然色彩繽紛。

column1: The Barber Shop appears to be closed. There's a note hanging from the doorknob.
column2: 理髮廳似乎關閉了。門把上掛著一張紙條。

column1: The man with the pipe greets you.
column2: 那個抽煙鬥的男人向你問好。

column1: Welcome to our town. You are lucky to have made it down from the mountains before the snow blocked the pass again. It's gotten pretty dangerous outside of town, I understand.
column2: 「歡迎來到我們的城鎮。  你很幸運在雪再次堵住山口之前從山上下來。  據我了解，城鎮外已經變得相當危險。」

column1: Many monsters have been trapped around here with the late snow. Between them and the brigands, we certainly could use a Hero around here.
column2: 「許多怪物隨著遲到的雪被困在這裡。  在他們和山賊之間，我們肯定需要一位英雄。」

column1: I am Sheriff Schultz Meistersson. This is Otto Von Goon, my assistant. What do you call yourself?
column2: 「我是警官舒爾茨·邁斯特松。這位是我的助手奧圖·古恩。你叫什麼名字？」

column1: Unknown Hero
column2: 不知名的英雄

column1: Good luck in your quest, %s.
column2: 祝你在任務中好運，%s。

column1: There's more than a hint of Ogre about this strange and bulky character. He seems cheerful, though.
column2: 這個奇怪且笨重的角色身上有超過一絲食人妖的氣息。  不過他似乎很高興。

column1: Schultz appears to be an affable old coot, content to smoke his pipe. There's a hardness behind his eyes, however, that gives you reason to believe that he's no pushover.
column2: 舒爾茨看起來是個親切的老傢伙，滿足於抽煙鬥。  然而，他的眼睛後面有著堅毅，讓你有理由相信他不是好惹的。

column1: It goes up and down...up and down...up and down...up and down.....
column2: 它上下…上下…上下…上下…

column1: The Sheriff's pipe is a truly impressive hand-carved ivory pipe.
column2: 治安官的菸斗是真正令人印象深刻的手工雕象牙菸斗。

column1: The Sheriff informs you that he's much too busy for idle chit-chat.
column2: 治安官告訴你他太忙，無法閒聊。

column1: Otto only expresses himself physically.
column2: 奧圖只用身體語言表達自己。

column1: Just walk around and find out for yourself.
column2: 「你自己走走看，去發現吧。」

column1: You'll need to talk to the Baron about that. Or go ask Wolfgang down at the Guild Hall, which is just down the street.
column2: 「你需要和男爵談論這個。  或者去公會大廳找沃夫岡，那就在街道下方。」

column1: So maybe you're going to be a hero? Well, we could surely use one around here, what with all the brigands.
column2: 「所以也許你要成為英雄？  好吧，憑藉這些山賊，我們肯定能用上一個。」

column1: There's a whole band of brigands hiding out somewhere in the mountains around us. They robbed a merchant just last week. Got a fair amount of treasure, I hear.
column2: 「有一整支山賊隊伍躲在我們周圍的某個山區。  他們上週剛搶劫了一個商人。  據我聽說，他們得到了不少寶藏。」

column1: I am Sheriff Schultz Meistersson, and this is Otto Von Goon, my assistant.
column2: 「我是警官舒爾茨·邁斯特松，這位是我的助手奧圖·古恩。」

column1: The Sheriff replies, "Why, that's me!"
column2: 治安官回答，「咦，那就是我！」

column1: The one who was robbed? He's staying at the Inn, I believe.
column2: 「被搶劫的那個？  我相信他待在客棧。」

column1: Go talk to the Innkeeper. He will help you there.
column2: 「去和旅館老闆談談。  他會在那裡幫助你。」

column1: You should know. You came down from there.
column2: 「你應該知道。  你是從那裡下來的。」

column1: My friend, the Sheriff says, "this world's filled with dangers. Sometimes I think it might be better to choose your danger, rather than sitting around, waiting for it, as I seem to do."
column2: 「朋友，」治安官說，「這個世界充滿了危險。有時我認為最好是主動選擇你的危險，而不是像我這樣坐等著。」

column1: But enough of this philosophical rambling. I am content enough to be Sheriff of Spielburg.
column2: 「但這種哲學性的漫談已經夠了。  我很滿足於成為史畢柏格的治安官。」

column1: It's simple. When somone breaks the law, and we catch them, we hold them prisoner.
column2: 「很簡單。  當有人違反法律，我們抓住他們時，我們就把他們關起來。」

column1: Obtaining treasure involves putting yourself in danger. If you're brave, foolish, or lucky, you might get some treasure.
column2: 「獲得寶藏涉及冒著生命危險。  如果你勇敢、愚蠢或幸運，你可能會得到一些寶藏。」

column1: The monsters come down from the mountains every winter, but they're usually gone by now. The Baron just doesn't have enough men to handle them.
column2: 「怪物每年冬天都從山上下來，但通常到現在已經消失了。  男爵就是沒有足夠的人手來對付它們。」

column1: Wolfgang is the one to talk to about monsters. He's had a lot of experience with them.
column2: 「沃夫岡是談論怪物的人選。  他與他們打過許多交道。」

column1: Baron Stefan keeps mostly to himself at his castle north of here. We haven't seen him here in town for years.
column2: 「史蒂芬男爵大部分時間待在他在這裡北邊的城堡裡。  我們多年沒有在城鎮裡看到他了。」

column1: Wolfgang, down at the Guild hall, is the one to talk to about monsters. He's had a lot of experience with them.
column2: 「位於公會大廳的沃夫岡是談論怪物的人選。  他與他們打過許多交道。」

column1: Otto is a big help to me, the Sheriff tells you. "He's very smart for a Goon. His prisoners only suffer a few broken bones when he grabs them."
column2: 「奧圖對我很有幫助，」治安官告訴你。「他對於古恩族來說聰明極了。他抓住的囚犯通常只斷幾根骨頭。」

column1: It took a bit of doing to teach him to bring prisoners back alive, but he hardly ever forgets, now.
column2: 「花了一些功夫教他活著帶回囚犯，但他現在幾乎不會忘記。」

column1: The Guild Hall is at the Southwest corner of town. If you're looking for work, that's a good place to go.
column2: 「公會大廳在城鎮的西南角。如果你在找工作，那是個好地方。」

column1: Just next to the Inn is a small Magic Shop. The owner, Zara, will let you in if you have some abilities in that area. She's a strange one, all right!
column2: 「就在客棧旁邊是一個小魔法店。  老闆莎拉會在你在那個領域有一些能力的情況下讓你進去。  她確實是個奇怪的傢伙！」

column1: The Sheriff says, "You think I'm going to tell you everything I know about everything? Go look around and find things out for yourself."
column2: 治安官說，「你以為我會把我知道的一切都告訴你？你自己到處走走、自己去發現。」

column1: However, this was not a good place to sleep.  You're stiff and sore all over and your wallet feels very light.
column2: 不過，這裡不是睡覺的好地方。你全身酸痛，錢包也變輕了不少。

column1: As safe as our streets are, you still should not sleep on them.  It is a good way to get robbed.
column2: 「雖然我們的街道很安全，但你還是不應該睡在街上。那是被搶劫的好方法。」

column1: You suddenly realize that you don't have enough money to purchase what you want.
column2: 你突然意識到你沒有足夠的錢買你想要的東西。

column1: Blustering, you say to Shema:  "Your prices are too high for me!"
column2: 你結巴著對席瑪說：「你的價格對我來說太高了！」

column1: As your face turns scarlet with embarrassment, Shema smiles and says:
column2: 你的臉紅得像番茄，席瑪微笑著說：

column1: We are sorry not to be able to show more generosity, but we, too, are victims of hard and evil times.
column2: 「我們很遺憾無法表現出更多的慷慨，但我們也是困難邪惡時代的受害者。」

column1: You must pay the innkeeper for a room.
column2: 你必須為房間付錢給客棧老闆。

column1: This looks like an alpine inn, complete with dishes on the wall and heavy beams on the ceiling.  It is pleasantly warm, and the aroma of strange spices fills the air.
column2: 這看起來像是一家山區客棧,牆上掛著盤子,天花板上有粗重的樑柱。裡面溫暖宜人,奇異香料的香氣充滿了空間。

column1: You see a small, cat-like creature known as a Katta.  You've heard that Kattas are common in the southern deserts.
column2: 你看到一隻小貓咪似的生物,叫做卡塔族。你聽說過卡塔族人在南方沙漠中很常見。

column1: You see a rather attractive female Katta wearing her native costume.
column2: 你看到一隻長得相當漂亮的母卡塔族人,穿著她的民族服裝。

column1: She's not here.
column2: 她不在這裡。

column1: The tables look like they have been rough-cut from some of the local pines.
column2: 這些桌子看起來像是用當地松木粗糙地切割而成的。

column1: One of the tables must have been made for some tall folks, perhaps Goons, Ogres, or Centaurs.
column2: 其中一張桌子一定是為高個子的人做的，也許是古恩族、食人妖或半人馬。

column1: The chair is finely made, in contrast to the rough-hewn tables.
column2: 椅子製作精良，與粗糙的桌子形成對比。

column1: The door with the window in it must be the door to the kitchen.  The other door goes elsewhere in the Inn.
column2: 有窗戶的那扇門一定是通往廚房的。另一扇門通往客棧的其他地方。

column1: The walls have an ancient, solid look, well-worn and comfortable.
column2: 牆壁看起來古老堅固，飽經風霜但很舒適。

column1: The heavy timbers look substantial.
column2: 沉重的木樑看起來很結實。

column1: The oak planking is well-worn, and the carpet is clean, but becoming threadbare.
column2: 橡木地板飽經磨損，地毯乾淨但變得破舊。

column1: The mantle above the hearth is a solid slab of wood.  There is nothing on it.
column2: 壁爐上方的架子是一塊結實的木板。上面什麼都沒有。

column1: The fireplace tools are placed neatly by the hearth, along with the proprietor.
column2: 壁爐工具和老闆娘整齊地擺放在爐邊。

column1: The beverage is more fragrant than most.
column2: 這種飲料比大多數更香。

column1: You practically licked the inside of the cup.  That was delicious.
column2: 你幾乎舔乾了杯子裡面。太美味了。

column1: You have no cup.
column2: 你沒有杯子。

column1: The food looks unusual, but smells delicious.
column2: 食物看起來很奇怪，但聞起來很美味。

column1: You practically licked the plate clean.  That was delicious.
column2: 你幾乎把盤子舔得乾乾淨淨。那真是美味!

column1: The plates on the wall are strictly for decoration.  Dishes with food on them are much more useful, if you're hungry.
column2: 牆上的盤子純粹是裝飾。如果你餓了，裝有食物的盤子會更有用。

column1: For all that this door appears to be free to swing back and forth, it doesn't budge when you try to open it.
column2: 雖然這扇門看起來可以自由擺動，但你試著打開它時它卻紋絲不動。

column1: The door appears to be stuck fast.
column2: 這扇門似乎卡住了。

column1: For what?
column2: 為了什麼？

column1: The aromas are enticing.
column2: 香氣很誘人。

column1: You're already standing.
column2: 你已經站著了。

column1: Move closer to the chair.
column2: 靠近椅子。

column1: You're already sitting down.
column2: 你已經坐下了。

column1: If you're hungry, order a meal.
column2: 如果肚子餓，點一份飯。

column1: If you're hungry, you should sit down and order a meal.
column2: 如果肚子餓，你應該坐下來點一份飯。

column1: You haven't been served yet.
column2: 還沒有上菜。

column1: Both meal and drink have been consumed.
column2: 餐點和飲料都吃完了。

column1: If you're thirsty, order a drink.
column2: 如果口渴，點一杯飲料。

column1: If you're thirsty, you should sit down and order something to drink.
column2: 如果口渴，你應該坐下來點一杯飲料。

column1: The drink you ordered is all gone.
column2: 你點的飲料全喝完了。

column1: You have already ordered.
column2: 你已經點過了。

column1: You're not hungry.
column2: 你不餓。

column1: Shema will be back soon.
column2: 席瑪很快就回來。

column1: Very good food, very good drink, finest in town.  Sit, rest, you will be served by my Shema.
column2: 「食物很好,飲料也很好,鎮上最好的。坐著,休息一下,我的席瑪會招待你。」

column1: It's too early to go to bed; you don't feel tired enough to sleep.
column2: 現在去睡覺太早了;你還不夠累,睡不著。

column1: You are sure that you would not be able to sleep, because you are so hungry.
column2: 你確信你無法入睡,因為你實在太餓了。

column1: Because your silver is too low, you must find other accomodations.  Perhaps you can find work somewhere.
column2: 因為你的銀幣不夠,你必須尋找其他住處。也許你能在某個地方找到工作。

column1: The tavern is located in the northwest corner of our town.
column2: 「酒館位在我們城鎮的西北角。」

column1: The Guild Hall is just down the street from here.
column2: 「公會廳就在這裡的街道下方。」

column1: Zara's magic shop is next door to my Inn.  You can't miss it.
column2: 「莎拉的魔法店就在我客棧隔壁。你絕對找得到。」

column1: I have run this inn for three years now, with Shema.
column2: 「我和席瑪一起經營這家客棧已經三年了。」

column1: We had thought to return to Shapeir, my homeland, with Abdulla Doo, but Alas, Fate has decreed otherwise.
column2: 「我們本想和阿布杜拉·嘟一起回到故鄉夏皮爾，但命運另有安排。」

column1: Very nice room.  Very soft beds, finest in town.  Only five silvers a night.
column2: 「房間很舒適。床很軟，是城鎮裡最好的。一晚只要五枚銀幣。」

column1: I have little knowledge of these things.  Perhaps if you shop around..."
column2: 我對這些事瞭解不多。也許你可以多逛逛…」

column1: Poor Abdulla sits.  He tries to forget his loss in food and drink. I think he will not be sorry if you join him at his table.
column2: 「可憐的阿布杜拉坐在那邊。他試著用飲食來忘記失落感。我想他見到你去陪他，應該會很高興。」

column1: My friend, Abdulla, is recovering from his robbery last week.  He should be here around suppertime.
column2: 「我的朋友阿布杜拉正在從上週的搶劫中恢復。他應該會在晚餐時間前後到達。」

column1: The world is filled with many kinds of creatures, both good and bad.
column2: 「這世界充滿各種各樣的生物，有好有壞。」

column1: Although in this valley I now have friends, I miss my homeland terribly.
column2: 「雖然我在這山谷裡現在有朋友，但我真的很想家。」

column1: My apologies, Wanderer, but for such drink you must go to the tavern at the other end of town.
column2: 「抱歉，流浪者，但那種飲料你得去城鎮另一頭的酒館。」

column1: Alas, the brigands stole all of Abdulla's wealth and mine own when they robbed his caravan.  Now I am forced to stay in this wet, cold land.
column2: 「唉，當山賊搶劫了阿布杜拉的商隊，他們也盜走了我們的財富。現在我被迫留在這個寒冷潮濕的土地上。」

column1: I hoped to return to my homeland with the profits Abdulla, Shema, and I would make from the caravan.  Now, I fear, we will remain here unless someone defeats the brigands and returns our stolen treasures.
column2: 「我希望能用阿布杜拉、席瑪和我從商隊賺得的利潤回到故鄉。現在，我恐怕除非有人打敗山賊並歸還我們被竊的寶藏，否則我們會一直留在這裡。」

column1: Hot spice.  Hot!  I hope you like!
column2: 「辣香料。很辣！希望你喜歡！」

column1: We live far to the south in the desert lands.  Someday, we may yet return there.
column2: 「我們住在南方很遠的沙漠地帶。也許有一天我們會回到那裡。」

column1: Work of an adventurous sort is available.  You must go to the Hall of the Adventurer's Guild.
column2: 「有冒險性質的工作。你必須去冒險者公會廳。」

column1: I know little about them except that they must be wealthy now.
column2: 「我對他們瞭解不多，只知道他們現在應該很有錢。」

column1: I am Shameen, mate of Shema, innkeeper to the town of Spielburg.
column2: 「我是沙敏，席瑪的丈夫，史畢柏格鎮的客棧老闆。」

column1: Shema is my mate and the light of my life. If you sit down, she will be happy to serve you.
column2: 「席瑪是我的妻子，是我生命中的光。如果你坐下來，她會樂意為你服務。」

column1: We Katta are a race of desert dwellers, wanderers of the endless sands."
column2: 我們卡塔族是沙漠居民，無盡沙漠的流浪者。」

column1: I am but a poor unfortunate, forced to seek my fortune in this cold land, far from my homeland, Shapeir, and my people.
column2: 「我不過是個可憐人，被迫在這寒冷的土地上尋求發展，遠離我的故鄉夏皮爾和我的人民。」

column1: We of the Katta know only low and simple spells.
column2: 「我們卡塔族只懂簡單的低階法術。」

column1: Alas, this humble innkeeper is ignorant of that about which you ask.
column2: 「唉，這個謙卑的客棧老闆對你問的事一無所知。」

column1: A thousand pardons!  I am a fool and have no answer for your question.
column2: 「千般抱歉！我是個傻瓜，無法回答你的問題。」

column1: Forgive me, that I am unable to speak with knowledge on that subject.
column2: 「原諒我，我無法就那個主題提供知識性的回答。」

column1: Shema, mate of Shameen the innkeeper.
column2: 席瑪，客棧老闆沙敏的妻子。

column1: It is my pleasure to serve you.
column2: 「為你效勞是我的榮幸。」

column1: Then you shall order food or drink.
column2: 「那麼你應該點食物或飲料。」

column1: I am so sorry.  I do not understand.
column2: 「非常抱歉。我不懂。」

column1: Then please enjoy our table and our fire.
column2: 「那麼請享受我們的餐桌和爐火。」

column1: We have very good stew of newborn lamb along with honey chicken for a mere 3 silvers.
column2: 「我們有非常好吃的新生羊肉燉菜和蜜汁雞，只需 3 枚銀幣。」

column1: We have rich black coffee, freshly ground and brewed as only my people do.  Such coffee will bring you to your full alertness.
column2: 「我們有濃郁的黑咖啡，用我的人民獨特的方式現磨現泡。這樣的咖啡能讓你完全清醒。」

column1: We also have a tea of the rarest herbs, which will quench the thirst of the desert.  Each is only one silver.
column2: 「我們也有用最稀有香草泡的茶，可以解決沙漠的渴。每一杯只要一枚銀幣。」

column1: For that sort of beverage you must go to the tavern.
column2: 「那種飲料你得去酒館。」

column1: I am called Shema, mate of Shameen, your humble servant.
column2: 「我叫席瑪，是沙敏的妻子，你的謙卑僕人。」

column1: Shameen is of the oldest and finest ancestry of the Katta people.
column2: 「沙敏是卡塔族最古老最優秀的血統。」

column1: Shapeir is a land of desert sands, flowers, fruit, and beauty.  How I wish to return.
column2: 「夏皮爾是一片沙漠、鮮花、果實和美麗的土地。我多希望能回到那裡。」

column1: She merely blushes, as if embarrassed.  She appears to be very shy.
column2: 她只是害羞地臉紅。她看起來非常害羞。

column1: You just did.
column2: 你剛才做過了。

column1: Please pay for your order after I bring it.
column2: 「請在我送來之後付款。」

column1: With great pleasure will I serve you a fine meal.
column2: 「我很樂意為你端上美味的餐點。」

column1: I will fetch that which will satisfy your thirst.
column2: 「我會拿來能解你渴的東西。」

column1: I am sorry that our inn has not what you desire.
column2: 「我很遺憾我們的客棧沒有你想要的東西。」

column1: The food tastes surprisingly good.
column2: 食物嚐起來出乎意料地好吃。

column1: The beverage goes down smoothly and well.
column2: 飲料順利爽快地下了肚。

column1: The odor of cooking food wafts through the kitchen door.
column2: 烹飪食物的香味飄進廚房門。

column1: Collection of designer plates loaned to the Hero's Inn by Mary Meister.
column2: 由瑪麗·麥斯特先生借給英雄客棧的設計師盤子合集。

column1: The warmth of the fire feels good on such a chilly day.  The proprietor of the inn apparently thinks so, too.  Cats always seem to gravitate to a warm hearth.
column2: 在這樣寒冷的日子裡,火的溫暖令人感到舒服。客棧老闆顯然也這麼認為。貓咪似乎總是喜歡靠近溫暖的爐火。

column1: This table must have been made for some tall folks, perhaps Goons, Ogres, or Centaurs.
column2: 這張桌子肯定是為高大的人設計的，也許是古恩、食人妖或半人馬。

column1: A door to some other room.  Perhaps the proprietor can tell you what's in there.
column2: 通往其他房間的門。也許客棧老闆能告訴你裡面有什麼。

column1: The door isn't locked.  Just turn around.
column2: 門沒鎖。轉身就行了。

column1: Picking locks looked a lot easier in the instruction book.
column2: 開鎖在教科書裡看起來容易多了。

column1: The owners seem to have been made nervous by the recent rash of house burglaries.  The door is barred from the inside.  But you do get some valuable lock-picking practice.
column2: 業主似乎被最近頻繁的入室盜竊嚇得神經兮兮。門從裡面被擋住了。但你確實獲得了寶貴的開鎖練習。

column1: You hear a "Snick!".  The lock is open!
column2: 你聽到了「卡！」一聲。鎖打開了！

column1: She seems to have retired...or maybe she's at the tavern.
column2: 她似乎退休了…也許她在酒館。

column1: This (door?) doesn't have a visible lock.
column2: 這（門？）沒有明顯的鎖。

column1: Not the sturdiest you've ever seen.
column2: 不是你見過最結實的。

column1: Turn around and go on in.
column2: 轉身進去。

column1: The door appears to be locked.  It also appears that the occupant is taking her nap at the bottom of the stairs right now.
column2: 門似乎上著鎖。看起來住户現在在樓梯底部打盹。

column1: No one seems to be home, and the door is securely locked.
column2: 似乎沒人在家，門也鎖得很牢。

column1: The door appears to be locked.
column2: 門似乎是鎖著的。

column1: You have come to the end of the main street. The town wall is to the south. You have an eerie feeling that someone is watching you.
column2: 你來到了主街的盡頭。城牆在南邊。你有種被誰盯著的怪異感覺。

column1: Not only does this building look weird, but it feels weird to be near it.
column2: 這棟建築物不只看起來奇怪，靠近它也感覺很奇怪。

column1: The house looks closed up.  There is no sign of the Little Old Lady.
column2: 房子看起來關門了。看不到那位老奶奶。

column1: The Guild Hall building appears to be dark and closed up.
column2: 冒險者公會的建築看起來很暗，而且關門了。

column1: You can just see a thin thread of light under the door, so there is probably someone inside.
column2: 你隱約看到門下傳來一絲光線，所以可能裡面有人。

column1: The fence around the Little Old Lady's house looks like it's made of wrought iron.
column2: 圍繞老婦人房子的柵欄看起來像是用熟鐵製成的。

column1: There's no getting around it...a shop with its eye on you makes you nervous.
column2: 無法繞過…被店盯著讓你緊張。

column1: It would seem that the strangely decorated building is involved in the magic trade.
column2: 看起來這個裝飾奇怪的建築物涉及魔法生意。

column1: Judging from what the sign says, the building at the end of the street is the Guild Hall.
column2: 根據標誌上的內容判斷，街道盡頭的建築就是冒險者公會。

column1: Isn't it hard to tear your eyes away?\n"PYROMANIA."
column2: 很難把目光移開吧？\n「縱火狂。」

column1: Flames? What flames?  You don't see any flames.
column2: 火焰？什麼火焰？你看不到任何火焰。

column1: She's deeply asleep in her rocker.
column2: 她在搖椅裡深深睡著。

column1: In it sits the Little Old Lady, asleep.
column2: 裡面坐著老婦人，在睡覺。

column1: She's really sleeping soundly and doesn't notice your presence.
column2: 她睡得真沉，沒注意到你的存在。

column1: ZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZ!
column2: 「呼呼呼呼呼呼呼呼呼呼呼呼呼呼呼呼呼呼呼呼呼」！

column1: Grmpf..snrt..hmphspft!
column2: 「嗯…呸…唔唔」！

column1: This looks like a nice, neat little house. There is a grandmotherly little old lady rocking on the front lawn.
column2: 這看起來像個漂亮、整潔的小房子。有個祖母般的老婦人在前草坪上搖晃。

column1: Get closer for a good look.
column2: 靠近看仔細。

column1: The remaining pages are blank, awaiting further tales of adventure.
column2: 剩下的頁面是空白的，等待更多冒險故事。

column1: The writing on this page looks very recent.  It says "I, %s, have come to Spielburg to become a Hero."
column2: 這頁上的字跡看起來很新鮮。寫著「我、%s 來到史畢柏格要成為英雄。」

column1: The %s entry was made several years ago.  It says: "Baronet Barnard von Spielburg killed a Troll near the Flying Falls on this 23 day of Octember".
column2: %s 的紀錄是幾年前寫的。寫著「少爺巴納德·馮·史畢柏格在飛瀑附近殺死了一隻巨魔，十月伯月二十三日」。

column1: The other pages record the exploits of the great adventurers of Spielburg's past.  The names Wolfgang Abenteuer and Baron Stefan von Spielburg appear most often.
column2: 其他頁面記錄了史畢柏格過去偉大冒險者的事蹟。沃夫岡·阿本特爾和史蒂芬·馮·史畢柏格男爵的名字最常出現。

column1: It looks like this old Guild Master doesn't do too much adventuring any more.  Still, he looks like he was plenty tough in his day, and he probably has many a tale to tell.
column2: 看起來這位年邁的公會主人不再進行太多冒險了。不過，他看起來在年輕時很強硬，他可能有很多故事要講。

column1: The fire blazing cheerily in the fireplace helps warm the Guild Master's old bones.
column2: 壁爐裡歡快燃燒的火焰幫助溫暖了公會主人的老骨頭。

column1: The plaque under the Moose says: "Courtesy of Sierra Online Prop Dept."
column2: 麋鹿下面的牌子上寫著「蒙Sierra Online 道具部贈送」。

column1: You never saw a purple Saurus before you came to Spielburg, but it looks like a really stupid monster.  The plaque reads:  "Saurus slain by Hans Halfwitten".
column2: 在你來到史畢柏格之前，你從未見過紫色索魯斯，但它看起來像個很愚蠢的怪物。牌子上寫著「索魯斯，被漢斯·哈夫威滕殺死」。

column1: It looks like it must have been a particularly nasty Troll.  You wouldn't want to meet him in a dark forest.  The plaque reads:  "Troll slain by Wolfgang Abenteuer".
column2: 它看起來肯定是一隻特別凶惡的巨魔。你不會想在黑暗森林裡碰到它。牌子上寫著「巨魔，被沃夫岡·阿本圖爾殺死」。

column1: This crossbreed of eagle and lion could have torn a man apart when it was alive.  The plaque reads: "Gryphon slain by Wolfgang Abenteuer".
column2: 這隻鷹和獅子的混種在活著時能撕裂一個人。牌子上寫著「獅鷲，被沃夫岡·阿本圖爾殺死」。

column1: Even in death, this monster remains awesome.  The plaque reads: "Dragon slain by Baron Stefan von Spielburg".
column2: 即使死了，這隻怪物仍然令人畏懼。牌子上寫著「龍，被史蒂芬·馮·史畢柏格男爵殺死」。

column1: The head is like a panther's, but with a strong human-like quality.  It is still rather frightening.  The plaque reads:  "Cheetaur slain by Wolfgang Abenteuer".
column2: 頭部像豹子，但有強烈的人類特徵。它仍然相當恐怖。牌子上寫著「獵豹人，被沃夫岡·阿本圖爾殺死」。

column1: This is certainly a weird one! You've never seen anything quite like it.  The plaque reads: "Antwerp slain by Two Guys From Andromeda".
column2: 這確實是個奇怪的東西！你從未見過類似的。牌子上寫著「安特衛普，被仙女座的兩位傢伙殺死」。

column1: This Adventurer's Guild Hall reminds you of the one in your home town.  The traditional Moose head and other stuffed monsters (Saurus, Troll, Gryphon, Dragon, Cheetaur, and the terrible Antwerp) adorn the walls.
column2: 這個冒險者公會大廳讓你想起你家鄉的那個。傳統的駝鹿頭和其他標本怪物（索魯斯、巨魔、獅鷲、龍、獵豹人和可怕的安特衛普）裝飾著牆壁。

column1: You see the registration book on the desk and the bulletin board full of job listings.  The man seated by the fire must be the Guild Master.  He is snoring.
column2: 你看到桌子上的註冊簿和貼滿工作啟事的公告欄。坐在火邊的男人肯定是公會主人。他在打鼾。

column1: There is nothing here that you need, and the Guild Master may be more aware than he seems.
column2: 這裡沒有你需要的東西，公會主人可能比看起來更清醒。

column1: The desk drawers are empty.
column2: 桌子的抽屜是空的。

column1: There is no way to close the desk.
column2: 沒有辦法關上桌子。

column1: On the desk are an old but ornate leather-bound book and a quill pen in the ink bottle.
column2: 桌上放著一本舊但精裝的皮製書籍和插在墨瓶裡的羽毛筆。

column1: You see an ordinary pen and ink.
column2: 你看到一支普通的筆和墨水。

column1: On the walls hang dusty, moth-eaten monster heads.  You recognize the purple Saurus, the Troll, the Gryphon, and the Dragon.
column2: 牆上掛著佈滿灰塵、被蟲蛀的怪物頭顱。你認出了紫色的索魯斯、巨魔、獅鷲和龍。

column1: You figure that the black head must belong to the Cheetaur, and you have no idea what the four-eyed creature might be.  You are also not quite sure why the Moose head is considered traditional, but every Guild Hall seems to have one.
column2: 你推測黑色頭顱肯定屬於獵豹人，你不知道四眼生物可能是什麼。你也不太確定為什麼麋鹿頭被認為是傳統的，但每個公會廳似乎都有一個。

column1: The desk holds a pen, an inkwell, and the Adventurer's Guild Registration and Log Book.
column2: 桌上放著筆、墨水瓶和冒險者公會註冊和日誌簿。

column1: But you've already done that!
column2: 但你已經做過了！

column1: You sign your name into the Adventurer's Log Book with a flourish.
column2: 你在冒險者日誌中簽下你的名字，做得很瀟灑。

column1: He already is.
column2: 他已經在了。

column1: Oh, my young friend!  What shall we talk about?  I have so many stories.  Just ask me about something.
column2: 「噢，我的年輕朋友！我們應該談什麼？我有很多故事。你問我什麼都行。」

column1: Ja.  What with the baron losing his son and daughter, all the monsters, Baba Yaga, and now the brigands, it has been one thing after another.
column2: 「是啊。加上男爵失去了他的兒子和女兒，所有的怪物、芭芭雅嘎，現在還有山賊，可說是禍不單行。」

column1: Baron von Spielburg was once a brave protector of our valley.  We used to adventure together when we were younger.  No brigand or monster would dare show his face here if the Baron had not angered Baba Yaga.  Now it is said that he goes nowhere and sees no one.
column2: 「史畢柏格男爵曾經是我們山谷的勇敢保護者。我們年輕時常一起冒險。如果男爵沒有激怒芭芭雅嘎，就不會有山賊或怪物敢在這裡出現。現在據說他足不出戶，也不見任何人。」

column1: He was a hero and worthy of the name Barnard von Spielburg.  He rode off to hunt one morning five years ago, and his horse returned with large and deep claw marks.  No sign of the Baronet's body was ever found.
column2: 「他是個英雄，配得上巴納德·馮·史畢柏格的名號。五年前的某個早上他騎馬去狩獵，馬回來了，身上有大大深深的爪痕。從未發現少爺的屍體。」

column1: Elsa was a beautiful eight-year-old child with blonde hair and sky-blue eyes.  She was the Baron's pride and joy.
column2: 「艾爾莎是一個漂亮的八歲女孩，金色頭髮、天藍色眼睛。她是男爵的驕傲和喜悅。」

column1: Ten years ago she was carried off by something which came over the wall and flew off with her.  The search for her lasted many years, but at last everyone gave up.
column2: 「十年前，有什麼東西飛過牆，帶走了她。尋找她持續了很多年，但最後大家都放棄了。」

column1: Everyone, that is, except for the Baron's Jester, Yorick.
column2: 「除了男爵的弄臣尤瑞克，所有人都放棄了。」

column1: Yorick was a funny little man, but brave.  He swore he would spend his life searching for the Baron's Elsa.
column2: 「尤瑞克是個有趣的小個子，但很勇敢。他發誓要用一生去尋找男爵的艾爾莎。」

column1: She is the center of our problems, I think.  Baba Yaga is a powerful Ogress.  Baron von Spielburg tried to force her to leave this valley, but she cursed him.
column2: 「我認為她是我們所有問題的中心。芭芭雅嘎是個強大的食人妖女。史畢柏格男爵試圖強迫她離開山谷，但她詛咒了他。」

column1: Now the Baron has lost everything but his land, and I don't know how long he will keep that.  What our valley needs is a Hero.
column2: 「現在男爵失去了一切，只剩下他的土地，我不知道他能保留多久。我們的山谷需要的是一位英雄。」

column1: Did I ever tell you about the days when Schultz and I rid this valley of Antwerps?  Ja, we were real adventurers then, and this was a true Guild Hall.  Now we are just old men, and this is just a place to tell old stories.
column2: 「我有沒有告訴過你，我和舒爾茨清除這山谷安特衛普的日子？是啊，那時我們是真正的冒險家，這也是真正的公會廳。現在我們只是老人，這只是個講舊故事的地方。」

column1: Schultz and I have been friends for a long time.  He too has been a real adventurer.  Now, he is just the Sheriff of the town.
column2: 「我和舒爾茨是很久的朋友。他也曾是真正的冒險家。現在他只是鎮上的治安官。」

column1: This is, of course, where an adventurer can find out where there is a need for someone brave and courageous.  There are jobs on the bulletin board over there.
column2: 「當然，這是冒險家能找到有哪裡需要勇敢和膽識人士的地方。公告欄裡有工作。」

column1: It is also a good place to talk about adventures on a cold afternoon.  We used to play cards here once a week, as well, but there are too few adventurers in Spielburg anymore.  They all died at the hands of monsters or brigands, or they just became too old.
column2: 「這也是在寒冷下午談論冒險的好地方。我們也曾經每週在這裡打一次牌，但史畢柏格的冒險家已經不多了。他們都死在怪物或山賊手中，或者他們變老了。」

column1: You can see some of the types of monsters that live around here if you look at our walls.
column2: 「如果你看看我們的牆，你可以看到住在這附近的一些怪物類型。」

column1: There is a reward for anyone who can stop the brigands by capturing or killing their leaders.  The information is on the bulletin board.
column2: 「任何能夠抓住或殺死山賊首領來阻止山賊的人都有賞金。詳情在公告欄。」

column1: Oh, those are nothing.  Anyone can kill a Saurus.
column2: 「哦，那些不算什麼。任何人都能殺死索魯斯。」

column1: It's been a while since Schultz and I killed the one on the wall.  It is fortunate that few Trolls remain; they're deadly.
column2: 「我和舒爾茨殺死牆上那隻已經很久了。幸運的是剩下的巨魔很少，它們很危險。」

column1: Watch out for Cheetaurs.  I bear the scars of my last fight with one to this day.
column2: 「小心獵豹人。我至今還帶著和一隻獵豹人的最後一戰的傷疤。」

column1: I remember the day I killed one.  It was the biggest one I had ever seen.  It put up a tough fight, but I was tougher.  You can see the result on the wall.
column2: 「我還記得我殺死一隻的那天。它是我見過最大的。它打得很兇悍，但我更強。你可以看到牆上的結果。」

column1: One day, years ago, a pair of dragons tried to take over our valley.  We adventurers rode out to meet them.
column2: 「多年前的某一天，一對龍試圖佔據我們的山谷。我們冒險家騎馬出去迎戰他們。」

column1: I can still see Stefan von Spielburg charging forward on his black horse.  He slew the one whose head adorns the wall above our fireplace.  The other one flew off.
column2: 「我還能看到史蒂芬·馮·史畢柏格騎著黑馬衝鋒。他殺死了頭顱掛在我們壁爐上方的那隻。另一隻逃走了。」

column1: People say that they can sometimes see that other dragon flying high overhead, but it has never dared attack us again.
column2: 「人們說他們有時能看到那另一隻龍飛過頭頂，但它從未敢再襲擊我們。」

column1: One year, this valley was overrun by those odd and terrible monsters.  Schultz and I fought long and hard to eliminate them completely.  We might have failed even so, had it not been for those two peculiar tourists who came to our aid.
column2: 「某一年，這個山谷被那些奇怪可怕的怪物佔滿。舒爾茨和我費力完全消滅了它們。若不是那兩個古怪的遊客來幫忙，我們可能會失敗。」

column1: That was the most vicious moose I ever ran into.  Nearly bit my nose off!
column2: 「那是我遇到過最兇惡的麋鹿。差點咬掉我的鼻子！」

column1: They certainly were strange.
column2: 「他們確實很奇怪。」

column1: That's where the tourists said they came from.  Must be some place up North -- I sure never heard of it.
column2: 「那就是遊客說他們來自的地方。肯定是北方某個地方——我肯定從沒聽過。」

column1: Not much of an adventurer, but he did kill one Saurus before he made the mistake of tangling with an Ogre.
column2: 「不是什麼了不起的冒險家，但他在犯了和食人妖糾纏的錯誤之前確實殺過一隻索魯斯。」

column1: Ogres are a lot like Goons, but even meaner.  Not as bright, though.
column2: 「食人妖類似古恩，但更兇殘。不過沒那麼聰明。」

column1: A Goon is a lot like an Ogre, not as tough, but a little smarter.  We have two Goons in town, Otto and Crusher.
column2: 「古恩很像食人妖，但不那麼兇悍，聰明一點。我們鎮上有兩個古恩，奧圖和克魯什。」

column1: I am Wolfgang Abenteuer, Guild Master of this hall.
column2: 「我是沃夫岡·阿本圖爾，這個大廳的公會主人。」

column1: A real hero is someone who did not start out strong or powerful, but who uses his courage, brains, and skills to become the best he can be.
column2: 「真正的英雄不是一開始就強大有力，而是靠勇氣、智慧和技能成為最好的自己。」

column1: I'm sorry.  I only know what's written on the poster.
column2: 「我很抱歉。我只知道海報上寫的。」

column1: The castle is just north of the Healer's house.
column2: 「城堡就在治療師房子的北邊。」

column1: The Healer's house is just north of the crossroads outside of town.
column2: 「治療師的房子就在城鎮外十字路口的北邊。」

column1: You know, that reminds me very little of the time old Schultz and I.......
column2: 「你知道，那不太讓我想起當年舒爾茨和我……」

column1: Wait a minute. It'll come to me.
column2: 「等等。我會想起來。」

column1: ZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZ.
column2: 「呼呼呼呼呼呼呼呼。」

column1: Ach! I was so busy I didn't notice you come in. Welcome, welcome! It is so seldom that we have new adventurers here. Most people think this valley is cursed!
column2: 「哎呀！我太忙了，沒注意到你進來。歡迎，歡迎！我們這裡很少有新冒險者。大多數人都認為這個山谷被詛咒了！」

column1: The smell of lavender and dust fills your nose as you walk in.  This reminds you of a great aunt's house you once visited.  The couch looks every bit as uncomfortable as your aunt's.
column2: 薰衣草和塵埃的香氣撲鼻而來。這讓你想起曾經拜訪過的遠房阿姨家。沙發看起來和你阿姨家的一樣不舒服。

column1: There is a covered birdcage near the stairs, and a knitting basket beside the couch.
column2: 樓梯附近有一個蓋著的鳥籠，沙發旁邊有一個編織籃。

column1: Just wait. He'll catch up to you
column2: 等等。他會趕上你。

column1: In the purse, you find 20 silvers and some soiled hankies. You take the silver.
column2: 在錢包裡，你找到 20 個銀幣和一些髒手帕。你拿走銀幣。

column1: A string of pearls seems to have fallen into the bag among the knitting. You take the pearls, of course.
column2: 一條珍珠項鍊似乎掉進了編織物中的袋子裡。你當然拿了珍珠。

column1: You find nothing else of value in the desk.
column2: 你在桌子裡沒找到其他有價值的東西。

column1: You find nothing else by searching the couch.
column2: 你在沙發上搜索沒找到其他東西。

column1: You find 3 silvers that have fallen down into the cushions.
column2: 你發現 3 枚銀幣掉進了靠墊裡。

column1: Nothing but dirt and leaves there.
column2: 那兒只有灰塵和樹葉。

column1: You take the silver candlesticks and stow them in your pack.
column2: 你拿起銀燭臺並把它們放進背包。

column1: Call Mayflower!
column2: 呼叫五月花號！

column1: Nothing up there.
column2: 上面沒有任何東西。

column1: The rug is blue, and the floor has recently been painted red.
column2: 地毯是藍色的，地板最近被漆成紅色。

column1: The house is tidy, but there is a layer of dust over everything.
column2: 房子很整潔，但所有東西上都覆蓋了一層灰塵。

column1: The oval rug is blue to match the walls.
column2: 橢圓形地毯是藍色的，與牆壁相搭。

column1: The stairs lead to the door to an upstairs room.
column2: 樓梯通向樓上房間的門。

column1: The door to the upstairs room.
column2: 通往樓上房間的門。

column1: The walls are a deep shade of blue.
column2: 牆壁是深藍色的。

column1: The fire is out, but the heat from the hearth is still felt in the room.
column2: 火滅了，但壁爐的熱氣仍然能在房間裡感受到。

column1: The Little Old Lady left her purse on the couch.  Mighty careless.
column2: 老太太把錢包丟在沙發上。真夠不小心的。

column1: On the mantle sits an ordinary hurricane lamp which provides the only light in the room.
column2: 爐台上放著一盞普通的颶風燈，它是房間唯一的光源。

column1: The candlesticks are heavy, ornate and made of sterling silver.
column2: 燭臺很沉，裝飾華麗，由純銀製成。

column1: There is a lace doily on the wooden table.
column2: 木桌上有一條蕾絲墊布。

column1: There is a lace doily in the center of the wooden table and a pair of silver candlesticks on either side of it.
column2: 木桌中央有一條蕾絲墊布，它的兩側各放著一對銀製燭台。

column1: The doily is lacy and shows fine workmanship. It is slightly yellow with age.
column2: 墊布做工精細，帶有蕾絲紋樣。因年月已久而略呈黃色。

column1: The birdcage is on a tall brass stand. There is a cover draped over it to keep the bird warm and quiet.
column2: 鳥籠放在高的黃銅架上。上面蓋著布以保持鳥溫暖和安靜。

column1: Cute but NOISY!.
column2: 可愛，但吵死人了！

column1: The cage is covered, and you can't see the bird.
column2: 鳥籠被蓋住了，你看不到鳥。

column1: The little house cat seems harmless, but strangely restless.
column2: 小家貓似乎無害，但詭異地不安。

column1: Against the wall, there is a small wooden desk with a potted plant on it. The desk has one drawer.
column2: 靠牆有一張小木桌，上面放著一盆盆栽。桌子有一個抽屜。

column1: It's a rubber plant.
column2: 那是一棵橡皮樹。

column1: The Coleus plant has brightly colored leaves.
column2: 一葉蘭的葉子色彩豔麗。

column1: On the desk there is a plant which looks like some kind of violet. In the corner is a taller, leafy house plant.
column2: 桌上有一株看起來像某種紫羅蘭的植物。角落裡還有一株較高的、枝葉茂盛的室內植物。

column1: There are no drugs here.
column2: 這裡沒有毒品。

column1: It's part of the stairs.
column2: 那是樓梯的一部分。

column1: The knitting bag is decorated on the outside with knitted figures. The Little Old Lady must do knitting when she's not asleep.
column2: 編織袋外面裝飾著編織人物。小老太太沒睡覺時定在織東西。

column1: The little cat is wearing a cheap rhinestone collar.
column2: 小貓戴著一條廉價的水鑽項圈。

column1: CHIRP! CHIRP!
column2: 唧唧！唧唧！

column1: Kitty, you'd better not be bothering the little birdie again!
column2: 基蒂，你最好別再騷擾小鳥了！

column1: Who would think a little birdie could be so noisy!
column2: 誰能想到一隻小鳥能這麼吵！

column1: You quickly cover the cage and hope the bird shuts up.
column2: 你迅速蓋住籠子，希望鳥能閉嘴。

column1: You find 1 silver in the desk drawer. You find nothing else of any value to you.
column2: 你在抽屜裡找到 1 枚銀幣。沒有其他有價值的東西。

column1: You have a bad feeling about the very deep, low growl emanating from the cat.
column2: 你對貓發出的低沉、深重咆哮有種不祥的感覺。

column1: The cat seems to want something.
column2: 貓似乎想要什麼。

column1: The cat seems more insistent than before.
column2: 貓看起來比之前更執著了。

column1: You pet the nice kitty.
column2: 你撫摸可愛的小貓。

column1: The cat really likes being petted.
column2: 貓真的喜歡被撫摸。

column1: This is a very insistent cat.
column2: 這是隻很堅持的貓。

column1: You feed the nice kitty a crumb.
column2: 你給可愛的小貓一點麵包屑。

column1: The cat really likes being fed. You give it a leftover morsel.
column2: 貓真的喜歡被餵食。你給它一點剩菜。

column1: This is a very insistent cat. You give it one of your rations.
column2: 這是隻很堅持的貓。你給它一份你的口糧。

column1: You have nothing substantial to feed the cat.
column2: 你沒有什麼實質的東西可以餵貓。

column1: Purrrrrrrr!
column2: 呼嚕嚕嚕！

column1: The Little Old Lady's cries finally bring the Sheriff and Otto to her rescue.  They find you pinned to the floor, unconscious, dreaming deliriously of death by sandpaper.
column2: 小老太太的尖叫聲終於把治安官和奧圖吸引來救她。他們發現你被釘在地板上、昏迷、做著被砂紙磨死的譫妄夢。

column1: When the Little Old Lady awakes to see what's going on, you have to concede to her (through lips that are as raw as hamburger) that you've been licked!  She summons the Sheriff and his goon, Otto.
column2: 老太太醒過來想看發生了什麼時，你只好向她認錯（舔到嘴唇像漢堡一樣生紅）說你被貓舔了！她召喚了治安官和他的手下奧圖。

column1: SQUEEEEAK!
column2: 吱呀呀呀呀！

column1: CREEEEAAK!
column2: 吱嘎呀呀呀！

column1: SCRAAAAWK!
column2: 尖叫聲！

column1: The owner of the house awakens...
column2: 房子的主人醒了……

column1: Help! Burglars! Sheriff! Help!\nKitty! Kitty!
column2: 救命！盜賊！治安官！\n救我！基蒂！基蒂！

column1: KITTY!!!
column2: 基蒂！！！

column1: The powers of magic are reserved for those with the potential to use them.  I sense no such potential in you.
column2: 「魔法的力量是為那些有潛力使用它們的人保留的。我在你身上感不到這樣的潛力。」

column1: May this aid you on your Quest!
column2: 「願這能幫助你完成你的任務！」

column1: As you read the spell scroll, the spell is ingrained in your mind.
column2: 當你讀著法術卷軸時，法術刻入了你的心靈。

column1: Zara says, "I detect that you have not the money to purchase that item.  Do not toy with me!"
column2: 薩拉說，「我發現你沒有足夠的錢購買那件物品。別耍我！」

column1: Tall shelves with books and odds and ends suited to magic line the walls.  In front of you is a counter with a pentagram embossed on the front.  The ceiling and the far wall are mysterious indeed.  There is a strange symbol on the floor.
column2: 牆壁上排滿了放著書籍和各式適合魔法的雜物的高大書架。你面前是一張櫃台，正面浮雕著五芒星。天花板和遠處的牆壁確實神秘莫測。地板上有一個奇異的符號。

column1: You see a wide variety of arcane objects.  For example:
column2: 你看到形形色色的秘術物品。舉例來說：

column1: A replacement part for a magical face-lift.
column2: 魔法拉皮手術的替換零件。

column1: Books dealing with sorcery, necromancy and other occult subjects.
column2: 講述魔法、死靈術和其他神秘學科的書籍。

column1: Bottles of mephitic potions.
column2: 充滿惡臭的藥水瓶。

column1: Tinsel and chaff, very good for avoiding radar.
column2: 金屬絲和糠秕，非常擅長躲避雷達。

column1: A hooded robe for when it rains in here.
column2: 下雨時穿的帽篷斗篷。

column1: Crystal balls, useful only to those initiated into the ways of witches.
column2: 水晶球，只有通曉巫術之道的人才用得上。

column1: A toaster oven, very rarely used (electricity not invented yet).
column2: 一台吐司機，很少使用（電力還沒被發明）。

column1: A frog.
column2: 一隻青蛙。

column1: Dried up Mantray (Manta jerky).
column2: 曬乾的蝠鱝肉乾。

column1: The ceiling is shrouded with clouds of smoke.
column2: 天花板被烈烈升起的煙霧籠罩。

column1: The floor is almost completely covered by a strange symbol.
column2: 地板幾乎完全被一個奇異的符號覆蓋。

column1: You don't see any monsters here.
column2: 你看不到任何怪物。

column1: You don't see any living creatures.
column2: 你看不到任何活物。

column1: This living creature must be some sort of familiar.  There is an uncanny look in its eyes, and you don't want to look at it too closely.  It definitely has magic.
column2: 這隻活物一定是某種法器。它眼神詭異莫測，你不想仔細看它。它肯定帶著魔力。

column1: The walls are covered with shelves full of interesting and very unusual objects.  The back wall behind the counter seems to be not a wall at all, but perhaps an extension of some unearthly place.
column2: 牆壁上滿是放著有趣且非常不尋常物品的書架。櫃台後面的牆壁看起來不像是真正的牆，而是可能延伸到某個異世界的地方。

column1: The titles are totally unfamiliar to you.
column2: 這些書名對你來說完全陌生。

column1: Aside from the pentagram on the front, there is nothing unusual about the counter.
column2: 除了正面的五芒星，櫃台沒有什麼不尋常的地方。

column1: Someone is watching you.
column2: 有人在看著你。

column1: There is no one here to sell you anything.
column2: 這裡沒有人給你賣東西。

column1: My name is Zara.  This is my shop.
column2: 「我叫薩拉。這是我的店。」

column1: I am both human and Faery Folk.  I draw my Power from both.
column2: 「我既是人類，也是妖精。我從兩者身上汲取力量。」

column1: We are a people of Power and Magic, and live in the forests far beyond the mountains to the west.
column2: 「我們是力量與魔法之民，住在西邊山脈遠方的森林裡。」

column1: Monsters stay out of town, and I stay in.  They are of no concern to me.
column2: 「怪物都在城外，我在城裡。它們對我無關緊要。」

column1: Damiano is my familiar and my companion.  We share our lives and our magic.
column2: 「達米亞諾是我的法器和夥伴。我們共享生命和魔法。」

column1: When you have mastered nine spells, have the power to cast them all, and proved yourself worthy by accomplishing a great deed, then you must undergo initiation to become a full Wizard.
column2: 「當你精通了九個法術，有能力施展它們，並通過完成偉大事跡證明自己值得，那麼你必須接受啟蒙儀式才能成為真正的法師。」

column1: There is a place in the distant south where you must journey, but first you must become a hero here.
column2: 「在遠方的南邊有一個你必須前往的地方，但首先你必須在這裡成為英雄。」

column1: A Wizard is one who both shapes Magic and is shaped by it.  Erasmus and I are the only Wizards in this valley.
column2: 「法師是既形成魔法，也被魔法形成的人。伊拉斯謨和我是這個山谷裡唯一的法師。」

column1: There is much Magic in this world for those who know how to use it.  There is Magic in this little town, and a good deal of Magic in this valley.
column2: 「對於懂得如何使用魔法的人，這個世界有許多魔法。這個小鎮有魔法，這個山谷也有相當多的魔法。」

column1: What I sell here are merely tools to help those who have power.  You may purchase restorative potions and scrolls from which you may learn spells if you have the potential.
column2: 「我在這裡賣的只是幫助有力量的人的工具。如果你有潛質，你可以購買恢復藥水和能學到法術的卷軸。」

column1: There is an Aura protecting this town from danger.  Within most of the town's walls, there can be no act of violence or cruel Magic.  Even so, it is prudent to avoid dark places.
column2: 「有一道靈光保護著這個城鎮免受危害。在城鎮的大部分範圍內，不能有任何暴力或殘忍的魔法行為。儘管如此，謹慎地避開黑暗的地方還是明智的。」

column1: An Aura is a Spell of protection surrounding something.  The town is still protected by the Aura cast by the great Spellcaster, Erana.
column2: 「靈光是圍繞著某物的保護法術。城鎮仍然受到偉大魔法師伊瑞娜施放的靈光保護。」

column1: There is much Power in this valley, and it attracts those who use Magic.  I am here in Spielburg, Erasmus has his house on Zauberberg, and even Baba Yaga has her hut cooped up somewhere around here.
column2: 「這個山谷充滿了力量，吸引著許多使用魔法的人。我在史畢柏格，伊拉斯謨在魔法山有他的房子，連芭芭雅嘎都在這附近某處關著她的小屋。」

column1: She is a powerful and wicked hag.  You would be wise to avoid her.  She cursed the Baron von Spielburg when he tried to drive her away.
column2: 「她是一位強大且邪惡的女巫。你應該避開她。當史蒂芬·馮·史畢柏格男爵試圖驅趕她時，她詛咒了他。」

column1: This is Baba Yaga's curse, cast upon the Baron years ago:
column2: 「這是芭芭雅嘎在多年前對男爵施下的詛咒：」

column1: Upon von Spielburg and all his clan, this Curse I now demand:  What I will shall come full measure, so shall ye lose all that ye treasure.
column2: 「我對馮·史畢柏格及其全族施以詛咒：我的意志將十倍奉還，你們將失去所有珍愛之物。」

column1: There is always a way to break a curse; possibly Erasmus knows more about this one.
column2: 「詛咒總有辦法破解的；伊拉斯謨可能對這個詛咒知道得更多。」

column1: Baba Yaga's hut is Magical.  It stands on chicken legs, and you must know the rhyme to enter.
column2: 「芭芭雅嘎的小屋是有魔力的。它站在雞腿上，你必須知道秘語才能進入。」

column1: I have no interest in entering Baba Yaga's hut and therefore do not know the rhyme.
column2: 「我對進入芭芭雅嘎的小屋沒有興趣，因此不知道咒語。」

column1: He was once the great leader of this land until he was foolish enough to anger Baba Yaga.
column2: 「他曾經是這片土地的偉大領導者，直到他愚蠢到激怒了芭芭雅嘎。」

column1: Erana was a powerful Spellcaster who lived long ago.  She brought peace to this valley.  Even now her spell protects this town from violence or foul Magic.
column2: 「伊瑞娜是一位強大的魔法師，她生活在很久以前。她為這個山谷帶來了和平。即使到現在，她的法術仍然保護著這個城鎮免受暴力和邪惡魔法的侵害。」

column1: Her final resting place is due north of town, and it is a place of both safety and healing.  It is known as `Erana's Peace'
column2: 「她最後的安息之地在城鎮正北方，那是一個既安全又能治癒的地方。它被稱為`伊瑞娜的安寧之地`」

column1: Erasmus is a Wizard and a Spellcaster who knows much about this area.  He lives in a tower eastward of town.  He can be very helpful if approached properly, but he has a strange sense of humor.
column2: 「伊拉斯謨是一位法師和魔法師，對這一帶了解很多。他住在城鎮東方的一座塔裡。如果方法得當會很有幫助，但他的幽默感很怪。」

column1: It is a steep mountain to the northeast.
column2: 「那是東北方的一座陡峭的山。」

column1: That is the Spell that lets you cast a Magical Flame upon your enemy.  The Spell Scroll will cost you 60 silver.
column2: 「那是讓你對敵人施放魔法火焰的法術。法術卷軸要價 60 銀幣。」

column1: That is the Spell that lets you place magical energy upon a weapon.  The energy will be released as magical damage when you hit your enemy.
column2: 「那是讓你把魔法能量附加在武器上的咒語。當你擊中敵人時，能量會以魔法傷害的形式釋放出去。」

column1: It is a Spell that allows you at first to unfasten simple locks and knots.  As you gain in skill, then you may open doors with it.  This Spell Scroll will cost you 30 silver.
column2: 「這是一個起初只能解開簡單鎖和繩結的法術。隨著你技能的提升，你就能用它打開門。這個法術卷軸要價 30 銀幣。」

column1: The Fetch Spell allows you to lift or lower a lightweight object from a distance.  The Spell Scroll will cost you 40 silver.
column2: 「取物術允許你從遠距離舉起或放下輕質物體。法術卷軸要價 40 銀幣。」

column1: We sell several Magical Spells on study scrolls.  You may purchase Flame Dart for 60 silver, Fetch for 40 silver, or the Open spell for 30 silver.
column2: 「我們販賣幾種魔法法術卷軸供學習。你可以購買火焰鏢（60 銀幣）、取物術（40 銀幣）或開啟術（30 銀幣）。」

column1: I also know that you can find a wonderful Spell if you learn the secret of Erana's Peace.
column2: 「我也知道如果你發現伊瑞娜的安寧之地的秘密，你就能找到一個奇妙的法術。」

column1: Secrets are made to be kept.
column2: 「秘密是用來保守的。」

column1: We can sell you spell learning scrolls and enchanted potions.
column2: 「我們可以賣給你法術學習卷軸和魔法藥水。」

column1: The Scrolls we sell are Magical.  You have but to read the Spell, and you will learn it.
column2: 「我們販賣的卷軸都是魔法的。你只需要讀法術，就能學到它。」

column1: The Healing Potion will cost you 50 silver.  It is made by the Healer outside of town.
column2: 「治療藥水要價 50 銀幣。它是由城外的治療師製作的。」

column1: Vigor Potions restore your Stamina after you have exerted yourself.  They cost 25 silver each.
column2: 「活力藥水在你耗盡耐力後能恢復你的耐力。每瓶要價 25 銀幣。」

column1: Power is the essence of magic, that which the wizard shapes and is shaped by.
column2: 「力量是魔法的本質，是魔法師塑造並被其塑造的東西。」

column1: The Power Potion restores your magical energy, and costs 75 silver.
column2: 「力量藥劑恢復你的魔法能量，價格是 75 銀幣。」

column1: We have Healing, Vigor, and Power Potions.
column2: 「我們有治療、活力和法力藥水。」

column1: Master the Arts Arcane, use those skills to vanquish the evil curse, and you will become a true Hero!
column2: 「精通秘術藝術，用那些技能消滅邪惡的詛咒，你就會成為真正的英雄！」

column1: Just look at the shelves.
column2: 看一下書架就知道了。

column1: We are entities of Magic and Power.  Matters such as that are of little importance.
column2: 「我們是魔法和力量的實體。那類事情對我們無關緊要。」

column1: We choose only to answer questions which interest us.  Do not persist with uninteresting questions.
column2: 「我們只回答令我們感興趣的問題。別糾纏在令人無趣的問題上。」

column1: We shall not waste our time further.
column2: 「我們不會再浪費時間。」

column1: You may choose from the Fetch, Open, or Flame Dart Spells.
column2: 「你可以選擇取物術、開啟術或火焰鏢。」

column1: You may choose from the Healing, Vigor, or Power Potions.
column2: 「你可以選擇治療、活力或法力藥水。」

column1: I do not sell such things to such as you.
column2: 「我不向你這樣的人出售這些東西。」

column1: So, you return to my shop.  Ask of me what you will, but keep to the point.
column2: 「所以，你又回到我的店裡了。你想問什麼就問，但要切中要點。」

column1: I am Zara, and my companion is Damiano.
column2: 「我是薩拉，我的同伴是達米亞諾。」

column1: The items in this shop are designed for those skilled in the use of magic.  We have very little for ones such as you who have not been initiated, but what we have could prove useful.
column2: 「本店的物品是為懂得使用魔法的人設計的。對於像你這樣未被啟蒙的人，我們提供的很少，但我們擁有的或許能派上用場。」

column1: It's a frog.
column2: 那是一隻青蛙。

column1: A pentagram, used in incantations to conjure spirits.
column2: 五芒星，用來在咒語中召喚靈魂。

column1: Impressive design, but merely decorative.
column2: 設計得很令人印象深刻，但只是裝飾品。

column1: Paraphernalia and apparatus.
column2: 各式各樣的器材和設備。

column1: Accoutrements and equipment.
column2: 裝飾品和器材。

column1: Darned if I know!
column2: 天曉得！

column1: Odds and ends.
column2: 零零碎碎的東西。

column1: Ends and odds.
column2: 五花八門的玩意。

column1: Things and stuff.
column2: 各種各樣的東西。

column1: Jeff's jimcracks.
column2: 傑夫的小玩意。

column1: Magical junk and mystical garbage.
column2: 魔法垃圾和神祕廢物。

column1: Reward for return of lost ring.\nInquire at the Healer's.
column2: 失物招領：戒指。\n詢問治療師。

column1:
This poster is rather dusty and faded.
The picture is of a small child with braids.

"Reward of 50 gold coins for the safe return of
Elsa von Spielburg.
Inquire at Spielburg Castle gates."
column2: 這張海報相當塵埃落定且褪色。圖片是一個梳著辮子的小女孩。「艾爾莎·馮·史畢柏格安全歸來獎勵 50 金幣。詢問史畢柏格城堡門衛。」

column1:
Reward of 30 gold coins for the
Capture or Death
of the Brigand Warlock.
Description:  short, ugly, and
wears brightly colored robes.
Has habit of laughing continually.

Inquire at Spielburg Castle gates.
column2:
懸賞 30 金幣，獎勵捕獲或
殺死山賊巫師。
特徵：身矮、面貌醜陋、
穿著鮮豔的魔法袍、
習慣不停地大笑。

詢問史畢柏格城堡衛士。

column1:
Wanted:  Brigand Leader.
Description:  Unknown appearance, wears a cloak.
Must provide proof of leader's identity.

Reward of 60 gold coins and title of Hero of the Realm.
Inquire at Spielburg Castle gates.
column2:
通緝：山賊首領。
容貌不詳，穿著斗篷。
需提供身份證明。

獎勵 60 金幣及英雄勳爵。
詢問史畢柏格城堡衛士。

column1: Notice:  Spell components needed.\nCash or trade for potions.\nInquire at the Healer's.
column2: 啟事：收購法術成分。\n現金付款或以藥水交換。\n詢問治療師。

column1:
This poster seems to have been here a while.
It has a picture of a handsome, but
arrogant young man.

"Reward of 50 gold coins for information
leading to the return of
Baronet Barnard von Spielburg.
Inquire at Spielburg Castle gates."
column2: 這張海報似乎貼了一段時間。上面有一個英俊但傲慢的年輕男性的圖片。「巴納德·馮·史畢柏格小爵爺失蹤。獎賞：50 金幣。詢問史畢柏格城堡門衛。」

column1: You reach for your money but come up empty-handed.  Unable to pay for your goods, you put the merchandise back.
column2: 你摸向荷包卻一無所獲。因為付不起錢，你把商品放回去。

column1: This house is the same color as the Sheriff's office around the corner.
column2: 這棟房子的顏色和附近的治安官辦公室一樣。

column1: A simple but prosperous-looking edifice it is.  It looks like it might provide some staples that would prove useful to a traveler.
column2: 這是一座簡樸但繁榮的建築。看起來能提供一些對旅客有用的必需品。

column1: It is but a small stall, used for the daily sale of whatever local produce has not been confiscated by the brigands.
column2: 這只是個小攤位，用來販售每天沒被山賊沒收的當地農產品。

column1: There is no response.  The store must be closed for the evening.
column2: 沒有人應門。店鋪可能在晚上關閉了。

column1: There is no one around.  The markets are closed for the night.
column2: 附近沒人。市場已經打烊。

column1: All of the produce has been taken in for the night.
column2: 所有農產品都已經收進來過夜。

column1: The creature in charge of the Farmer's Mart is a rather lovely and shy-looking young centaur.
column2: 農業市集的負責人是一位相當可愛且害羞的年輕半人馬。

column1: The selection is meagre and the appearance is not of the best, but the aromas are strangely enticing.  A barrel contains some small apples, and there are several types of vegetables on the counter.
column2: 選擇不多，外觀也不怎樣，但香氣卻奇異地誘人。一個桶裡裝著一些小蘋果，櫃檯上還有幾種蔬菜。

column1: This appears to be a Market street.  In the corner is a fruit and vegetable stand, or Farmer's Mart.  Next to it is a dry goods store, and next to that is a house.  Across the street you see the back of the barber's shop and the Sheriff's office.
column2: 這看起來像一條市集街道。角落裡有一個水果和蔬菜攤位，或農民市集。它旁邊是一間乾貨店，再旁邊是一間房子。街對面你看到理髮店和治安官辦公室的背面。

column1: You see the backs of buildings.
column2: 你看到建築物的背面。

column1: Unlike an Ogre, YOU can read!
column2: 不像食人妖，你是看得懂字的！

column1: The lock appears to be an extra heavy-duty model.
column2: 這個鎖看起來是特別堅固的型號。

column1: The door appears to be locked, but the lock is of a simple type.
column2: 門看起來是鎖著的，但鎖是簡單的類型。

column1: I will be happy to take your silver.  Please pick out what you want.
column2: 「我很樂意接收你的銀幣。請選出你想要的。」

column1: As you wish, sir.
column2: 「如你所願，先生。」

column1: I am Hilde, daughter of Heinrich Pferdefedern, the farmer.
column2: 「我是希爾德，海因里希·費德費德恩的女兒，他是農夫。」

column1: My father and I are the only centaurs in this valley.
column2: 「我父親和我是這個山谷裡唯一的半人馬。」

column1: We have many kinds of fresh vegetables for sale today.  There are lettuce, carrots, potatoes, and turnips right from the ground at five for a silver.  They are very good for you!
column2: 「我們今天有各式新鮮蔬菜出售。有生菜、胡蘿蔔、馬鈴薯和蕪菁，直接從地裡挖出來，五個一銀幣。對你很有好處！」

column1: It is much too early in the season for those.  Sorry.
column2: 「現在季節太早了，沒有那種東西。抱歉。」

column1: I'm sorry, but the cherry harvest is not yet in.  But we will have very nice cherries two months from now.
column2: 「抱歉，櫻桃季節還沒到。但我們在兩個月後會有非常好的櫻桃。」

column1: There are still some apples left from last season in the barrel.  You may buy ten for a silver, since they are so small.  We will have fresh cherries in a couple of months.
column2: 「桶裡還有去年剩下的蘋果。因為它們很小，你可以十個一銀幣買。我們在幾個月後會有新鮮櫻桃。」

column1: They have twice robbed my Father of his money, and they have tried to steal our food this winter.  That is because we farm outside of town.  They would not dare to try that in town.  The Sheriff would stop them fast!
column2: 「他們搶了我父親兩次的錢，今年冬天還試圖偷我們的食物。那是因為我們在城外務農。他們不敢在城裡這樣做。治安官會立即阻止他們！」

column1: Many robbers have run up to Father as he trotted back to town.  My Father tried to fight, but they hurt him badly.  They always ran away to the west.
column2: 「許多山賊在我父親騎馬回城時出現。我父親試圖反擊，但他們傷了他。他們總是向西逃跑。」

column1: We have some land to the north of town.  It isn't very big, but we grow many things.  You should be here during the harvest time.  Then you would see many fruits and vegetables.
column2: 「我們在城北有一塊地。面積不是很大，但我們種很多東西。你應該在收穫季節來。那時你會看到很多水果和蔬菜。」

column1: You know...in the autumn!  But then, perhaps you have never been a farmer.
column2: 「你知道…在秋天！但也許你從沒當過農民。」

column1: The Healer lives in a nice little cottage on the North road, near my Father's fields.  She is a very nice woman.
column2: 「治療師住在北面道路上一間漂亮的小屋裡，靠近我父親的田地。她是個很好的女人。」

column1: Thank you, but my Father thinks I am too young.  He would not permit it.  But perhaps next year...
column2: 「謝謝你，但我父親認為我太年輕了。他不會同意。也許明年……」

column1: My Father is Heinrich Pferdefedern, who works our farm just north of town.
column2: 「我父親是海因裡希·普費德費德恩，他經營城北我們的農場。」

column1: I am sorry.  I do not get about very much, and I do not really know about many things.
column2: 「抱歉。我不常外出，對很多事情也不太了解。」

column1: If you want to buy something, go over to the vendor.
column2: 如果你想買東西，去找小販。

column1: You pick out the freshest-looking vegetables you can find, and pay the lady a silver piece.
column2: 你挑出看起來最新鮮的蔬菜，付給那位女士一枚銀幣。

column1: You carefully select ten of the best apples from the barrel, and pay the lady a silver piece.
column2: 你仔細地從桶裡挑出十個最好的蘋果，付給女士一枚銀幣。

column1: As I told you, sir, it is too early in the season.  You would not want to buy any.
column2: 「如我告訴你的，先生，現在季節太早了。你不會想買的。」

column1: I'm afraid I can't help you with that, sir.
column2: 「恐怕我幫不了你，先生。」

column1: Just pick out what you would like to purchase, then you can pay me.
column2: 「只要挑出你想要的，然後付錢給我。」

column1: The creature in charge of the Farmer's Mart is a rather lovely and shy-looking young Centaur.
column2: 負責農民市集的生物是一位相當可愛且看起來羞澀的年輕半人馬。

column1: The barrel contains potent looking potatoes more suited to throwing than eating.
column2: 桶裡的馬鈴薯看起來很有力量，更適合拿來丟而不是吃。

column1: The small apples give off a sweet but pungent odor.
column2: 小蘋果散發出甜蜜但刺激的氣味。

column1: Carrots are good for night vision and lower one's cholesterol.
column2: 胡蘿蔔對夜間視力有益，能降低膽固醇。

column1: The turnips seem to blush with pride.  You wonder if the farmer has been using Alar.
column2: 蕪菁似乎因驕傲而臉紅。你納悶農民是否施用過艾拉。

column1: Lettuce and other leafy plants.
column2: 生菜和其他葉類植物。

column1: You can smell apples as you approach this corner.
column2: 當你接近這個角落時，你可以聞到蘋果的香氣。

column1: The pretty young centaur looks at you and says, "Good day, and welcome to Spielburg!  Do you wish to buy some nice fresh fruits or vegetables?"
column2: 漂亮年輕的半人馬看著你說，「早安，歡迎來到史畢柏格！你想要買些新鮮水果或蔬菜嗎？」

column1: However, this was not a good place to sleep.  You're stiff and sore all over and your wallet feels very light.  You give the Hilde the centaur a suspicious look, but you are too sore to pursue the matter.
column2: 不過，這不是個好睡覺的地方。你全身僵硬酸痛，錢包輕了不少。你用狐疑的目光看著半人馬希爾德，但你太痛了無法追究。

column1: I'm sorry, but I had to move you out of my stall!  It is very dangerous to sleep in the street at night.  I fear you have been robbed.
column2: 「抱歉，但我必須把你從我的攤位裡搬出來！在街上睡覺很危險。恐怕你被搶了。」

column1: In the desk drawers, you find an assortment of mostly worthless objects, but you find three silvers, which you take.
column2: 在書桌抽屜裡，你找到一堆多半沒用的東西，但你找到三枚銀幣，就拿走了。

column1: You've looked in there already.
column2: 你已經看過裡面了。

column1: The room is dark and still.
column2: 房間昏暗而寂靜。

column1: You see items which may be interesting and probably valuable.
column2: 你看到可能有趣且可能貴重的物品。

column1: The candelabra looks like it is made of gold.
column2: 枝形燭台看起來是用金製成的。

column1: The vase on the mantle might be worth taking.
column2: 爐台上的花瓶可能值得拿走。

column1: There is also an odd little box on the table.
column2: 桌上還有一個奇怪的小盒子。

column1: How anyone manages to dust a CEILING is beyond you.
column2: 你想不出誰有辦法擦天花板。

column1: The floor is covered with expensive wall-to-wall carpeting not common in this time period.
column2: 地板上舖滿名貴的全室地毯，這種東西在這時代罕見。

column1: The walls look like they've been recently painted.
column2: 牆壁看起來剛粉刷過。

column1: Looks like solid oak.
column2: 看起來像是實心橡木。

column1: Somebody has a green thumb.
column2: 某人頗有園藝天分。

column1: There isn't any.
column2: 沒有。

column1: The glowing embers from the fire cast a dim light in the room.
column2: 火焰中發光的餘燼在房間裡投下昏暗的光線。

column1: There once was a music box on the table, but you took it.
column2: 桌子上原本有個八音盒，但你拿走了。

column1: There is an ornately carved box sitting atop the oak table.
column2: 在橡木桌上放著一個雕花的盒子。

column1: The mantle is a single heavy timber.
column2: 壁爐台由一根沉重的木樑組成。

column1: A vase sits on the mantle.
column2: 一個花瓶擺在壁爐台上。

column1: Over the safe is a charming portrait of the Sheriff and his wife.
column2: 保險箱上方是一幅迷人的治安官和他妻子的肖像。

column1: Over the mantle is a charming portrait of the Sheriff and his wife.
column2: 壁爐台上方掛著治安官和他妻子的迷人肖像。

column1: You took it.  Remember?
column2: 你拿走了。記得嗎？
