column1: The vase is carved from alabaster and could be worth some money, although it would take up a lot of space in your pack.
column2: 這個花瓶是由雪花石膏雕成，可能值一些錢，不過放在背包裡會佔很多位置。

column1: It is a small, beautifully decorated metal box with a hinged lid.
column2: 這是一個小的、精美裝飾的金屬盒子，有活動蓋。

column1: The candelabra looks like it is made of solid gold.
column2: 燭台看起來像是用純金做的。

column1: Most silvers look alike, and they are all the same weight.
column2: 大多銀幣看起來都一樣，重量也都相同。

column1: Safe? You don't see a safe.
column2: 保險箱？你看不到保險箱。

column1: It looks very sturdy, indeed.
column2: 看起來確實很牢固。

column1: Get closer if you want to look into the safe.
column2: 靠近一點就能看進保險箱裡。

column1: There is an empty coin bag in the safe.
column2: 保險箱裡有一個空錢袋。

column1: You see a bag of coins.
column2: 你看到一個錢袋。

column1: You have already done that.
column2: 你已經做過了。

column1: You'd better shut the safe, first.
column2: 你最好先關好保險箱。

column1: There is no need to do that.
column2: 沒有必要這樣做。

column1: It's already in your pack.
column2: 它已經在背包裡了。

column1: You take the vase carefully from the mantle and place it gently on the floor.
column2: 你小心地從壁爐架上拿起花瓶，輕輕地放在地板上。

column1: The vase is on the mantle.
column2: 花瓶在壁爐台上。

column1: You place the vase back on the mantle.
column2: 你把花瓶放回壁爐台上。

column1: You place the vase carefully in your pack beneath your cape.
column2: 你小心地把花瓶放進背包裡，藏在你的披風下面。

column1: You place the candelabra carefully in your pack beneath your cape.
column2: 你小心地把燭台放進背包裡，藏在你的披風下面。

column1: You quickly close the box and toss it into your pack.
column2: 你迅速關上盒子，丟進背包裡。

column1: You quickly toss the box into your pack.
column2: 你迅速地把盒子扔進背包。

column1: Even though it's a 50-year-old Sego palm, it's too big to fit in your pack.
column2: 儘管這是棵 50 年的絲葦棕櫚，也太大了放不進背包。

column1: Nothing like that is visible to you.
column2: 你看不到那種東西。

column1: There might be something like that inside the wall safe.
column2: 牆上的保險箱裡可能有這樣的東西。

column1: You take the fifty silvers, and put the empty bag back in the safe.
column2: 你拿走 50 枚銀幣，把空袋子放回保險箱裡。

column1: The safe is already open.
column2: 保險箱已經打開了。

column1: The safe is securely locked.
column2: 保險箱上鎖得很牢。

column1: Where?
column2: 在哪裡？

column1: What for? It's open!
column2: 幹嘛？它已經打開了！

column1: Ah, got it!
column2: 啊，搞定了！

column1: Cracking safes looked a lot easier in the instruction book.
column2: 撬保險箱在說明書裡看起來簡單多了。

column1: It's not open.
column2: 它沒有打開。

column1: The little box is safely tucked away in your pack. You can open it up later.
column2: 小盒子安全地放在背包裡。你稍後可以打開它。

column1: Oh, no! You won't do THAT again!
column2: 哎呀！你不會再做那樣的事了！

column1: The little box is safely tucked away in your pack. It is already closed.
column2: 小盒子安全地放在背包裡。它已經關上了。

column1: You quickly shut the lid on the music box!
column2: 你迅速關上八音盒的蓋子！

column1: It's already closed.
column2: 它已經關上了。

column1: There is some fairly loud snoring going on in this room. You hear:\n"Brckawwwww...zup-zup-zup-zup-zzzzz."
column2:
這房間裡傳來
相當大聲的鼾聲。
你聽到：「布克—啊—啊—
啊…啾—啾—啾—啾—茲—茲—
茲—茲。」

column1: Very faintly, you hear snoring:\n"Prrrrrt...prrrrrt...prrrrrt."
column2: 你隱隱約約聽到\n鼾聲：「普呃—普呃—\n普呃—普呃—普呃。」

column1: Someone (or someTHING) is snoring up a storm. You hear: "Nkaawww..rspft...honk... buh-buh-buh-buh-buh-buh-phweep.
column2: 某個東西正打著響亮的鼾聲。你聽到：「嗯卡—啊—呃—呃…布—布—布—布—布—布—弗—弗。」

column1: Faintly, you hear: "Nkaawww...prrrrrt... honk...zup-zup-zup-buh-buh-buh-phweep.
column2: 你隱隱約約聽到：「嗯卡—啊—啊—普呃—普呃—嗯—啾—啾—啾—布—布—布—弗—弗。」

column1: If you were a person who liked that sort of food, you'd love this smell!
column2: 如果你喜歡那種食物，你會愛上這個味道！

column1: It would be more pleasant if it wasn't mixed with the odor of sauerkraut.
column2: 如果不是混著酸菜的氣味就會更舒服了。

column1: What a mixture!
column2: 真是一股奇怪的混合氣味！

column1: It's a potted plant.
column2: 這是一盆盆栽。

column1: An uncomfortable looking chair.
column2: 看起來很不舒服的椅子。

column1: An overstuffed couch.
column2: 一張塞得滿滿的沙發。

column1: A solid looking table.
column2: 看起來很實心的桌子。

column1: An overstuffed chair.
column2: 一張塞得滿滿的椅子。

column1: You won't be needing your old leather armor anymore.  I shall give you a 50 silver trade-in discount.
column2: 「你不再需要你的舊皮甲了。我給你 50 銀幣的折舊折扣。」

column1: Here you are; thank you for your patronage.
column2: 「給你；感謝你的光顧。」

column1: As you count your money, you realize that you don't have enough to purchase this particular item.
column2: 你數著你的錢時，才發現你沒有足夠的金錢來買這件東西。

column1: It would not be a good idea to drop anything here.
column2: 在這裡丟棄任何東西都不是個好主意。

column1: This looks like a dry goods store, but it smells like a musty library.  The stove feels nice on such a crisp day.  Behind the counter and on the shelves, there are many and various items for sale.  The shopkeeper appears to ignore you while he reads a book.
column2: 這看起來像個雜貨店，但聞起來像發霉的圖書館。暖爐在這樣爽朗的日子裡感覺很舒服。櫃檯後面和架子上擺著各式各樣的商品。店主似乎邊讀書邊忽視你。

column1: The stove is burning merrily away, making the shop seem almost too warm.
column2: 爐子熱烈地燒著，讓店裡幾乎熱得不得了。

column1: The shopkeeper is a small, balding man who wears glasses.  He looks as if he can barely lift the book he is reading.  Judging from his clothes, he is fairly well-to-do.
column2: 店主是個矮小禿頭、戴著眼鏡的男人。他看起來好像連他正在讀的書都勉強拿得起。從他的衣著看，他過得相當不錯。

column1: The barrels appear to be filled with something and look rather heavy.
column2: 這些桶子看起來裝了東西，看起來相當沉重。

column1: The jars of beets and sauerkraut look like they were canned locally.
column2: 這些甜菜和酸菜罐子看起來像是在本地罐裝的。

column1: Behind the counter are weapons, armor, and such equipment as you would more often find in an "Adventurer's Shop".
column2: 櫃檯後面有武器、盔甲和你更常在「冒險者商店」找到的裝備。

column1: You're not close enough to the counter to see behind it.
column2: 你離櫃檯不夠近，看不到後面。

column1: On the counter and on the shelves are assortments of canned goods, honey jars, sewing items, and odds and ends that people in a small town would need.
column2: 櫃檯上和架子上有罐裝食品、蜂蜜罐、縫製物品，和小鎮居民需要的各式雜物。

column1: The leather armor is more like barding, designed to be used under clothing.  The chain mail is much heavier, and looks very strong.
column2: 皮甲更像是鎧甲襯裡，設計來穿在衣服下面。鎖子甲重得多，看起來非常堅固。

column1: There are barrels of flour and salt next to the counter.  There seem to be jars of pickled pigs' feet, linen cloth, and other things in which you have very little interest.
column2: 櫃檯旁邊擺著裝麵粉和鹽的桶子。似乎還有醃豬腳的罐子、亞麻布和其他你興趣不大的東西。

column1: There are a variety of items of the sort an adventurer might need.
column2: 這裡有冒險者可能需要的各式各樣物品。

column1: The shopkeeper doesn't seem to hear you.
column2: 店主似乎沒聽到你。

column1: Either the shopkeeper can't hear you or he's ignoring you.  Try striding right up to the counter.
column2: 要麼店主聽不到你，要麼他在忽視你。試著直接走到櫃檯。

column1: Go ahead. Talk to him.
column2: 去吧。和他說話。

column1: Unfortunately, I have only standard weapons and equipment,  the shopkeeper tells you.  "I carry daggers and chain armor.  Maybe someday I'll be able to carry magic ones, though."
column2: 「不幸的是，我只有標準的武器和裝備，」店主告訴你。「我販售匕首和鎖子甲。也許有一天我能販售魔法的。」

column1: I can also sell you food or empty flasks for carrying liquids or powders.
column2: 「我也可以賣你食物或空瓶子來裝液體或粉末。」

column1: You can get really good protection from my chain mail armor.  It's very heavy though, and I would have to charge you 500 silvers for it.
column2: 「我的鎖甲能為你提供非常好的保護。不過它很重，我得向你收 500 銀幣。」

column1: Ah!  The use of the dagger is a most skillful art.  Actually, this particular weapon is longer than most, but still easily concealable.  A bargain at 20 silvers.
column2: 「啊！匕首的使用是一門高超的藝術。實際上，這把武器比大多數都長，但仍然容易隱藏。只要 20 銀幣，划算得很。」

column1: Adventuring rations aren't the tastiest food in the world, but they will keep you healthy and alert as you go along.  A pack of 5 rations will cost you just 5 silvers.
column2: 「冒險糧食不是世上最好吃的食物，但它們能讓你在旅途中保持健康和清醒。5 份糧食只要 5 銀幣。」

column1: Oh, no, I don't carry that sort of thing here.  If you want to get drunk, try the tavern.
column2: 「哎呀，我這裡不販售那種東西。如果你想喝醉，去酒館試試。」

column1: It's a very good idea to carry an empty flask or two, in case you want to pick up a liquid or something else that needs a container.  Our flasks are a great bargain at 2 silvers each.
column2: 「隨身帶一兩個空瓶子是個不錯的主意，以防你想裝液體或其他需要容器的東西。我們的瓶子便宜得很，一個只要 2 銀幣。」

column1: Oh, this book?  It's about an adventurer who is trying to become a Hero.  The title is 'A Hero's Death'.
column2: 「哦，這本書？是講一個試圖成為英雄的冒險者。書名是《英雄之死》。」

column1: Oh, you don't want those.  They're for the people who live here in town.  Adventurers just need adventuring equipment.
column2: 「哦，你不會想要那些。那些是給住在鎮上的人的。冒險者只需要冒險裝備。」

column1: The Sheriff and Otto protect this town.  The Sheriff used to be a real adventurer, you know!
column2: 「治安官和奧圖保護這個鎮。治安官以前是真正的冒險家呢！」

column1: Why, that's me!  Kaspar!
column2: 「怎麼，那就是我！卡斯帕！」

column1: Hilde sells fine produce for a reasonable price.
column2: 「希爾德以合理的價格出售優質農產品。」

column1: I don't like to talk about other people behind their backs.
column2: 「我不喜歡在背後談論別人。」

column1: I have pigs feet, brooms, rakes, handles, pots, teapots, eggs, linen, flour, etc.."
column2: 我有豬腳、掃帚、耙子、把手、鍋子、茶壺、雞蛋、亞麻布、麵粉等等。」

column1: I'm sorry, I don't have any of those for sale.
column2: 「抱歉，我沒有那些東西要賣。」

column1: I carry weapons, armor, daggers, rations, ale flasks, and various food stuffs.
column2: 「我販售武器、盔甲、匕首、糧食、啤酒瓶和各式食品。」

column1: Aw, you wouldn't want to buy that.
column2: 「噢，你不會想買那個。」

column1: Oh, I'm sorry! the shopkeeper blurts out.  "I didn't notice you coming in."
column2: 「哦，我很抱歉！」店主脫口而出。「我沒注意到你進來。」

column1: Well, my adventurer friend!  Would you like to make another purchase?
column2: 「好啊，我的冒險家朋友！你想再買點東西嗎？」

column1: Well, my adventurer friend!  Would you like to make a purchase this time?
column2: 「好啊，我的冒險家朋友！這次你想要買東西嗎？」

column1: My, you look like an adventurer!  We don't see too many around here.  The brigands tend to get rid of most adventurers before they make it to town.
column2: 「天哪，你看起來像個冒險家！我們這裡不常見到。山賊通常會在冒險家到達鎮上之前就解決掉他們。」

column1: You may be in luck.  I carry a modest amount of adventurer's equipment behind the counter.  Would you like to make a purchase?
column2: 「你運氣可能不錯。我在櫃檯後面放了些冒險裝備。你想要買東西嗎？」

column1: Oh, good! I do try to stock some things adventurers can use.  I actually would rather be an adventurer than a shopkeeper, you see.
column2: 「喔，太好了！我確實試著準備冒險者能用的東西。其實我寧可當冒險家也不想當店主，你看。」

column1: My items are pretty ordinary, though.  You probably already have most of them if you've done any adventuring at all.
column2: 「不過我的商品相當普通。如果你做過任何冒險活動，你可能已經有大部分了。」

column1: Now what was that you wanted again?
column2: 「那你到底想要什麼？」

column1: Well, look around anyway.  You may find something you could use."
column2: 好吧，隨便看看也不錯。你也許能找到什麼有用的東西。」

column1: You see broom and rake handles in this barrel.
column2: 你在這個桶子裡看到掃帚和耙子的把手。

column1: Flasks, very useful for storing liquids.
column2: 瓶子，很適合用來裝液體。

column1: An unornamented but exceptionally sturdy shield.
column2: 一面樸素但特別堅固的盾牌。

column1: The proprietor's teapot.
column2: 老闆的茶壺。

column1: Pickled Saurus eggs.
column2: 醃制的索魯斯蛋。

column1: Linen coarsely woven from the fibers of spore spitting Spirea plants.
column2: 用能噴孢子的繡線菊纖維粗織而成的亞麻布。

column1: Bags of flour.
column2: 麵粉袋。

column1: Pickled pigs feet.
column2: 醃豬腳。

column1: Jars of beets and sauerkraut.
column2: 甜菜罐和酸菜罐。

column1: Tea imported all the way from Shapeir.
column2: 從夏皮爾運來的茶葉。

column1: Salt from the desert east of the mountains.
column2: 山東邊沙漠的鹽。

column1: Sugar from dried figs.
column2: 曬乾無花果榨出的糖。

column1: Salted, dried Saurus.
column2: 醃漬乾索魯斯。

column1: Locally grown pepper.
column2: 本地種植的胡椒。

column1: Wild honey from the forest.
column2: 森林裡採的野蜜。

column1: There is a sign on the door which says: "Gone Fishing".
column2: 門上有個牌子寫著：「去釣魚了」。

column1: You haven't been practicing very much.
column2: 你練習得不夠多。

column1: That was the easiest lock you've ever encountered.  It's now unlocked.
column2: 那是你遇到過最容易的鎖。現在它已經開了。

column1: There are several signs above the various establishments on this street, indicating the nature of their business.
column2: 街道上各個建築物上方有幾個招牌，表明它們的經營性質。

column1: You are in the northwest corner of town. There are a Butcher's Shop and a Bakery on the north side of the street. Across from them are the backs of buildings on the next street over.
column2: 你在鎮的西北角。街道北邊有一家肉舖和一家麵包店。對面是隔壁街的建築物背面。

column1: The Tavern in the corner looks rather rundown, and the alley beside it is dark. On the other side of the tavern is a rather practical-looking building, like a workshop.
column2: 角落的酒館看起來相當破舊，旁邊的小巷很黑。酒館的另一邊是一棟看起來很實用的建築，像個工坊。

column1: The bakery seems to be closed, although there are some dried-up-looking cupcakes in the window.
column2: 麵包店似乎關著，不過窗戶裡有一些看起來乾掉的杯子蛋糕。

column1: The Butcher's shop seems to be closed.
column2: 肉舖似乎關著。

column1: Which shop do you mean?
column2: 你指的是哪家店？

column1: The building looks old, dark, and a little seedy.
column2: 這棟建築看起來又舊又黑，有點破舊。

column1: The workshop is securely locked.
column2: 工作室被牢牢鎖著。

column1: You can hear sounds of hammering from inside.  The door is securely locked, and no one responds to your knock.
column2: 你能聽到裡面傳來敲打聲。門上了牢牢的鎖，沒人回應你的敲門。

column1: Looks like the door to a Tavern.
column2: 看起來像酒館的門。

column1: Looks like this door has been closed for a long time.
column2: 看起來這扇門已經很久沒打開過了。

column1: There are signs affixed to the door knobs.
column2: 門把上釘著招牌。

column1: It's very dark, but you can see something shining on the ground at the far end of the alley.
column2: 很黑，但你可以看到小巷遠處地上有東西在閃閃發光。

column1: It's dark, but there seems to be someone in there.
column2: 很黑，但裡面似乎有人。

column1: Other than the sorry-looking goods in the window, it is too dark to make out much of anything.
column2: 除了窗戶裡那些看起來可憐的商品，太黑了什麼也看不清。

column1: The Butcher's shop seems to be closed. Through the window, you see what appears to be a layer of dust over most of the interior.
column2: 肉舖似乎關著。透過窗戶，你看到裡面大部分被灰塵覆蓋。

column1: You can't see into any windows from where you're standing.
column2: 你站的地方看不進任何窗戶。

column1: You regain consciousness.
column2: 你恢復了意識。

column1: After dusting yourself off, you check to make sure you're still in one piece.
column2: 拍掉塵埃後，你檢查自己是否還完好無損。

column1: Unfortunately, you discover that while you were "out", someone managed to relieve you of all of your money.
column2: 不幸的是，你發現在你「昏迷」期間，有人把你的所有錢都偷走了。

column1: Now you'll have to find a way to get some money. The street is not the most comfortable place to sleep, and you have to eat, sooner or later.
column2: 現在你得想辦法弄些錢。街道不是最舒服的睡覺地方，而且遲早你得吃飯。

column1: You can hear sounds of hammering from inside.  It must be some sort of workshop.
column2: 你能聽到裡面傳來敲打聲。一定是某種工坊。

column1: The Tavern in the corner looks rather rundown.
column2: 角落的酒館看起來相當破舊。

column1: There's nothing there.
column2: 那裡沒有東西。

column1: You pick up and read the note.
column2: 你拿起紙條並讀了它。

column1: You don't have anything like that to look at.
column2: 你沒有那樣的東西好看。

column1: The bartender frowns on customers who spit in his establishment.
column2: 酒保不悅於在他的店裡吐痰的客人。

column1: You're not close enough to a barstool.
column2: 你離吧檯凳不夠近。

column1: Looks like the only place to sit is at the bar.
column2: 看來唯一的坐處就是在吧台了。

column1: Separate yourself from that stool first.
column2: 先離開那個凳子。

column1: Wait for the drink you just ordered.
column2: 等你剛點的飲料。

column1: Go ahead and drink up, first.  You paid for it!
column2: 先喝掉吧。你已經付錢了！

column1: But you're not sitting down!
column2: 但你沒有坐下！

column1: You'd better talk to Crusher.
column2: 「你最好和克拉舍談談。」

column1: You'll have to get the bartender's attention first.
column2: 你得先引起酒保的注意。

column1: The bartender bellows, "Sit down first!"
column2: 酒保大聲吼道：「先坐下！」

column1: OK. What'll you have?
column2: 「好的。你要喝什麼？」

column1: Drink up before you order anything else!
column2: 先喝完再點別的！

column1: The bartender says, "One well-aged house brew, coming up!"
column2: 酒保說：「一杯陳年自釀啤酒，馬上來！」

column1: You'll need the bartender's help on that one.
column2: 那個你得靠酒保幫忙。

column1: The bartender says, "One mug of my fine Troll's Sweat, coming up!"
column2: 酒保說：「一杯我的上好巨魔汗水，馬上來！」

column1: Sounds potent, but you'd better go see the bartender if you want some.
column2: 聽起來很烈，不過你如果想喝的話最好去找酒保。

column1: The bartender is emphatic as he tells you, "If you want a mug of Dragon's Breath, house rules say it'll have to be cash up front!"
column2: 酒保肯定地告訴你：「如果你要一杯龍息，店規規定得先付現！」

column1: You cough up the cash.
column2: 你掏出了現金。

column1: Thanks, buddy!
column2: 「謝謝，老兄！」

column1: Hey, Crusher! Our friend here wants Dragon's Breath!
column2: 「嘿，克拉舍！我們的朋友想要龍息！」

column1: You tell the bartender that you don't have enough working capital at this point in time.
column2: 你跟酒保說你現在沒有足夠的資金。

column1: He says, "Too bad, buddy. You're missing a real treat, for sure!"
column2: 他說：「真遺憾，老兄。你肯定錯過了一道真正的佳餚！」

column1: Well then!  Belly up to the bar and get some Dragon's Breath!
column2: 好啊！靠在吧台邊來點龍息！

column1: The bartender can't reach your money. You're not close enough.
column2: 酒保夠不到你的錢。你離得不夠近。

column1: You'll need to buy one.
column2: 你得買一杯。

column1: That ain't beer!
column2: 那不是啤酒！

column1: That isn't Troll's Sweat!
column2: 那不是巨魔汗水！

column1: That isn't Dragon's Breath!
column2: 那不是龍息！

column1: You smooth out the piece of paper and read:
column2: 你把紙片展平後讀了起來：

column1: B. - Meet me at the old archery range south of town at noon -- urgent! - B.
column2: 「B. — 中午在鎮南邊的舊射箭場見我 — 急 — B.」

column1: B. - That new adventurer is getting too nosy.  Deal with him. - B.
column2: 「B. — 那個新冒險家太多管閒事了。處理掉他。— B.」

column1: Oh, isn't that nice!  Sounds like you're going to be invited to a card game.
column2: 啊，多好呢！聽起來你要被邀去玩牌了。

column1: The bartender looks tough from his crewcut to his boots. The only thing soft about him is his tattoo, which says "MAMA".
column2: 酒保從平頭到靴子都看起來很凶悍。唯一柔軟的東西是他的刺青，上面寫著「媽媽」。

column1: Crusher, escort this squealer off the premises!
column2: 「克拉舍，把這個告密者趕出去！」

column1: Yes...what?
column2: 「是…什麼？」

column1: No...what?
column2: 「不…什麼？」

column1: The bartender shows little interest in small talk.
column2: 酒保對閒聊沒什麼興趣。

column1: The drunken barber at the end of the bar isn't in any shape to talk, at least not coherently.
column2: 酒吧盡頭醉酒的理髮師沒有心情說話，至少說得不清楚。

column1: Hey! This is an honest establishment!  You\nwant me to ask Crusher?
column2: 「嘿！這是間誠實的場所！你要我去問克魯舍嗎？」

column1: We've got some of the finest ale\nin the valley, only 1 silver.
column2: 「我們有山谷裡最好的麥酒，只要 1 銀幣。」

column1: Our world-famous Troll's Sweat is\nalways fresh and it's only 5 silvers.
column2: 「我們世界有名的巨魔之汗總是新鮮的，只要 5 銀幣。」

column1: The Dragon's Breath is our specialty,\nand it's Crusher's personal favorite.  It's very hard to\ncome by, so it'll cost you 25 silver, cash up front.
column2: 「龍息是我們的特色，也是克魯舍的最愛。很難弄到，所以要 25 銀幣，要先付錢。」

column1: Look buddy...don't get personal!
column2: 聽著，老兄…別多嘴！

column1: He don't like his personal affairs discussed. My advice is not to do anything that'll get him upset.
column2: 「他不喜歡別人討論他的私人事務。我的建議是不要做任何讓他生氣的事。」

column1: This is a bar. I serve drinks. You want a drink, order one. You want answers to stupid questions, GET OUT!
column2: 「這是酒館。我只服務飲料。你要喝酒就點一杯。你要問蠢問題就滾出去！」

column1: The baker says, "There's a lake to the south which has a huge fish, but its pretty dangerous."
column2: 麵包師傅說：「南邊有個湖，裡面有條巨大的魚，但很危險。」

column1: The butcher says, "There's a river to the south which has some good-sized trout.
column2: 屠夫說：「南邊有條河，裡面有一些很大的鱒魚。」

column1: They don't seem to be paying any attention to you.
column2: 他們好像根本沒注意你。

column1: These guys are so intent on their game that they don't want to open their mouths on any other subject.
column2: 這些傢伙專心玩遊戲，對其他話題絕口不提。

column1: It looks like these guys aren't playing with a full deck.
column2: 看起來這些傢伙智商不是很高。

column1: Got any %d's?
column2: 「有 %d 個嗎？」

column1: Go FISH!
column2: 「去釣魚！」

column1: The bartender calls to Crusher:  "Hey, he's not one of us!  Take care of him!"
column2: 酒保對 Crusher 叫道：「嘿，他不是我們這兒的！去收拾他！」

column1: Oh yeah?  What's the password?
column2: 「是嗎？密碼是什麼？」

column1: Uh oh!  It looks like you got Crusher upset!
column2: 糟糕！看來你惹怒粉碎者了！

column1: Oh yeah?  So what is it?
column2: 「是嗎？那是什麼？」

column1: Good idea, but you're in the wrong movie.
column2: 好主意，但你搞錯電影了。

column1: The goon seems to be ignoring you.
column2: 打手似乎在無視你。

column1: As you ask him questions, the goon's eyes darken.
column2: 當你問他問題時，打手的眼神變得陰沉。

column1: Uh oh! It looks like you got Crusher upset!
column2: 糟糕！看來你惹怒粉碎者了！

column1: You thank the bartender and pay him for your beverage.
column2: 你感謝酒保並為飲料付錢。

column1: You inform the bartender that you seem to be temporarily strapped for funds.
column2: 你告訴酒保你現在好像臨時沒錢。

column1: He says, "Hey Crusher! Get this deadbeat bum outta here!"
column2: 他說：「嘿，Crusher！把這個窮光蛋趕出去！」

column1: Whaddaya want?
column2: 「你要什麼？」

column1: There ya go!
column2: 「給你！」

column1: You see a crumpled piece of paper under the stool.
column2: 你看到凳子下有張皺巴巴的紙片。

column1: The man on the west side of the table is wearing a blood-stained apron, like a butcher.
column2: 桌子西邊的男人穿著沾血的圍裙，像個屠夫。

column1: The man on the east has on a baker's hat.
column2: 東邊的男人戴著麵包師的帽子。

column1: This goon looks really tough and mean. He's not someone to pick a fight with.
column2: 這個打手看起來很凶狠。不是個好惹的。

column1: Looks like some kind of card game.
column2: 看起來像某種牌戲。

column1: There appears to be a trapdoor beneath the goon, but he's blocking it very effectively.
column2: 打手下面好像有個活板門，但他擋得很有效。

column1: Someone has carved into the bottom of the table\n" G. MEISTER slept here".
column2: 有人在桌子下面刻著\n「G. MEISTER 在此睡過」。

column1: So this is where the barber goes to lunch! The guy's had so much liquid diet, he looks embalmed.
column2: 所以理髮師就在這裡吃午飯！這傢伙喝了那麼多，看起來都已經防腐了。

column1: Yup, You're sitting on a stool.
column2: 沒錯，你正坐在凳子上。

column1: Not a pleasant sight!
column2: 真是不堪入目！

column1: The world-famous Troll's Sweat is always fresh.
column2: 名聞遐邇的 Troll's Sweat 總是很新鮮。

column1: Dragon's Breath is the house specialty, and it's Crusher's personal favorite.
column2: Dragon's Breath 是這裡的招牌飲品，也是 Crusher 的最愛。

column1: Some of the finest ale in the valley.
column2: 山谷裡最好喝的麥酒之一。

column1: Nothing but a dirty floor.
column2: 只不過是髒兮兮的地板。

column1: This dark, seedy place is less than you expected of a Thieves' Guild.
column2: 這個陰暗破舊的地方還不如你對盜賊公會的想像。

column1: The man seated by the table greets you with a scowl;  Crusher is his usual, amiable self;  and you can't quite make out what lurks behind that massive door.
column2: 坐在桌邊的男人用皺眉臉迎接你；Crusher 依舊親切如常；而那扇大門後面究竟藏著什麼，你看不太出來。

column1: You feel uneasy about the sinister mood here.
column2: 你對這裡的不祥氣氛感到不安。

column1: I don't have time to talk to unlicensed scum.  Pay your guild license fee, and then we can talk.
column2: 「我沒時間跟沒執照的廢物說話。先繳公會會費，我們再談。」

column1: What do you think this is, your home?  Crusher, deal with this deadbeat.
column2: 「你把這兒當你家啊？克拉什，去對付這個賴皮的。」

column1: The door is locked, and Boris won't let you in.
column2: 門上了鎖，柏瑞斯不讓你進去。

column1: Picking locks in the Thieves' Guild is a singularly stupid idea.
column2: 在盜賊公會裡開鎖根本是個蠢到不行的主意。

column1: Crusher ignores you.
column2: 克拉什不理你。

column1: A Thief's license will cost you 25 silvers.  You will need a license to use any of our services.
column2: 「盜賊執照要 25 銀幣。使用我們的任何服務都需要執照。」

column1: Perhaps I could sell you a good lockpick for fifteen silvers, or a wonderful Acme Thief Tool Kit for one hundred silvers.
column2: 「也許我可以賣你一把好開鎖工具 15 銀幣，或一套棒的 Acme 盜賊工具組 100 銀幣。」

column1: I have no time for idle talk.  Why don't you do something constructive like stealing Otto's yo-yo?
column2: 「我沒時間閒聊。你為什麼不做點有建設性的事，比如偷奧圖的溜溜球？」

column1: I can't hear you.  Please come a little closer to the window.
column2: 「我聽不清。請靠近窗戶一些。」

column1: From his expensive clothes to his carefully trimmed mustache, there is an air of sophistication to this villain.
column2: 從他的昂貴衣著到精心修剪的鬍子，這個壞蛋身上散發著品味。

column1: Crusher is his usual, amiable self (every bit the Goon).
column2: Crusher 依舊親切如常（徹頭徹尾的打手）。

column1: The opening in the door is covered with a wire grate, but there is space below it where objects can be passed through.  The mysterious-looking man on the other side is Boris, the cashier and fence for the Guild.
column2: 門上的開口用鐵絲網蓋著，下方有個空隙可以傳東西。那邊神祕的男人是 Boris，公會的出納兼贓物販子。

column1: The ladder up to the tavern is very sturdy.  It has to be to support Crusher's weight.
column2: 通往酒吧的梯子很牢固。要支撐 Crusher 的體重可得夠結實。

column1: It looks like an oversized, well-used dartboard.
column2: 看起來像個超大號、用舊了的飛鏢靶。

column1: There sure are a lot of daggers in the board.  You wonder where the Chief Thief keeps them all.
column2: 板上真的有很多匕首。你納悶盜賊首領把它們全放在哪兒。

column1: The shadowy figure behind the door is Boris, the cashier and fence for the Guild.
column2: 門後的身影是 Boris，公會的出納兼贓物販子。

column1: The floor is surprisingly clean, compared to that of the bar upstairs.
column2: 和樓上酒吧相比，這裡的地板出人意料地乾淨。

column1: The plaster is chipping in several places.
column2: 石膏在好幾個地方剝落。

column1: You can tell where the bar is by the seep marks of spilled beer.
column2: 你可以從潑灑啤酒的痕跡看出吧台在哪裡。

column1: The daggers are nicely made, and apparently designed for throwing.  Besides the Chief Thief's seemingly endless collection, there are two matched sets with differently-colored hilts for playing Dag-Nab-It.
column2: 這些匕首製作精良，顯然是為了投擲而設計。除了首領盜賊似乎無窮無盡的收藏外，還有兩套配對的匕首，柄部顏色不同，用來玩「匕首擲準遊戲」。

column1: Even thieves get thirsty.
column2: 連盜賊都會口渴。

column1: You're probably wondering if there is any honor among thieves.
column2: 你可能在想盜賊之間是否有榮譽。

column1: Stealing from a dagger-throwing thief?\nThat's a dangerous (and stupid) idea!
column2: 搶飛鏢盜賊的東西？\n那是個危險（和愚蠢）的想法！

column1: Go up to the window, please.
column2: 「請到窗口去。」

column1: Don't bother.  You've already paid, stupid.
column2: 「別費事了。你早就付過了，傻瓜。」

column1: Welcome to the Thieves' Guild Union, Local 1313.
column2: 「歡迎來到盜賊公會工會，第 1313 分會。」

column1: I'm sorry, but you don't seem to have enough silver for your license.  Come back when you qualify.
column2: 「抱歉，你的銀幣似乎不夠付會費。等你湊夠了再來。」

column1: Our very special items are reserved for our guild members.  No scabs allowed.
column2: 「我們最特殊的物品只保留給公會成員。不收臨時工。」

column1: There you are.
column2: 「你來了。」

column1: We only deal in cash here.
column2: 「我們這裡只收現金。」

column1: We only sell lockpicks and Thief Toolkits here.
column2: 「我們這裡只賣開鎖工具和盜賊工具包。」

column1: We buy only from our members.
column2: 「我們只跟成員收購。」

column1: You can't fence something you don't have.
column2: 你不能賣你沒有的東西。

column1: We have no interest in those.
column2: 「我們對那些沒興趣。」

column1: Let's see now... deducting the Guild cut, and allowing for resale markup, that comes to...
column2: 「讓我算算…扣掉公會抽成，加上轉售利潤，那就是…」

column1: %d silvers.  Here you are.
column2: 「%d 銀幣。拿著。」

column1: You now have %d gold and %d silver coins.
column2: 你現在有 %d 個金幣和 %d 個銀幣。

column1: A dagger is a thief's best friend, and throwing from a distance is the safest and best way to use it.  If you think you are good enough, perhaps we could play a little game.
column2: 「匕首是盜賊最好的朋友，遠距離投擲是最安全、最好的用法。如果你覺得自己夠本事，我們可以玩個遊戲。」

column1: We use the specially designed Dag-Nab-It board you can see there on the wall.
column2: 「我們用那個牆上的特製 Dag-Nab-It 靶子。」

column1: We could start small, perhaps five silvers a game, perhaps even less.  Of course, we can wager more if you think you can take me.
column2: 「我們可以從小來，一盤五個銀幣，甚至更少。當然，如果你覺得能贏我，可以賭更多。」

column1: There are only a few members of our Guild still around here.  With the snow and the brigands keeping away the tourists, it's hard to earn a decent living.
column2: 「我們公會現在只剩幾個人在這裡。雪和山賊把遊客都趕跑了，很難好好賺生活費。」

column1: Where else but here can one mingle freely with others of our chosen profession, sharing tips and secrets?  Where else can you safely fence stolen items?
column2: 「還有哪裡能跟同行自在地混在一起，分享竅門和祕密？還有哪裡能安全地賣贓物？」

column1: Where else can you ask questions like these and not get thrown into jail or attacked?  The Guild is truly your home away from home.
column2: 「還有哪裡能這樣問問題而不被扔進監牢或攻擊？公會真的是你第二個家。」

column1: Those who do not pay their union dues are the lowest form of life.
column2: 「不交工會費的傢伙是最低等的生物。」

column1: My advice is, don't get caught.
column2: 「我的建議是，別被抓到。」

column1: Now that would be telling.
column2: 「那就別問了。」

column1: Just give your stolen items to Boris and you will be suitably recompensed.  Or, in terms more suited to your apparent intellect, you get cash for your loot.
column2: 「把贓物給 Boris，他會給你適當的報酬。或者，用你理解的方式說，你用贓物換現金。」

column1: Boris is our accountant.
column2: 「柏瑞斯是我們的會計。」

column1: Crusher is our personnel manager.
column2: 「克拉什是我們的人事經理。」

column1: Otto is the Sheriff's goon.  He shouldn't give you any trouble unless you happen to stand in his way.
column2: 「奧圖是治安官的打手。只要你不擋他路，他不會給你麻煩。」

column1: The Sheriff provides a useful service in this town.  He arrests anyone too stupid or unlucky to be a good thief.
column2: 「治安官對這個鎮提供了有用的服務。他逮捕任何太蠢或太倒楣而不是好盜賊的人。」

column1: I am known as 'The Chief'.  But you can call me 'Chief'.
column2: 「我叫『首領』。不過你可以叫我『首領』。」

column1: These non-union cretins are giving thieves a bad name.  I'm sure they have a spy planted in our organization, but I don't know who it is.
column2: 「這些不是工會的白癡在破壞盜賊的名聲。我肯定他們在我們組織裡有內鬼，但我不知道是誰。」

column1: There is certainly a traitor in our midst.
column2: 「我們中間肯定有叛徒。」

column1: There are a few places around town where a clever and skilled thief can make some silver.  Perhaps even you could steal something.
column2: 「城鎮裡有幾個地方，聰明又靈巧的盜賊可以撈到銀幣。也許連你都能偷到東西。」

column1: I have had beginners before, but this Guild reached a new low when you joined.  Child, you are a thief.  Stealing is how you make a living.  So go out there and steal something and hopefully you'll get, umm, that is, you *won't* get caught.
column2: 「我前面也帶過菜鳥，但自從你加入，這個公會降到新低點。小子，你是盜賊。偷東西就是你的謀生之道。所以滾出去偷點東西，希望你，嗯，也就是說，你『不會』被抓到。」

column1: We will gladly sell you a lock pick for a modest fee.  However, for the true professional, nothing will do but the Guild-Approved Acme Thief Tool Kit.  This kit will allow you to pick anything with a little practice, and is a real steal at only one hundred silvers.  Just talk to Boris.
column2: 「我們很樂意用合理的價格賣你開鎖工具。不過，真正的專家非『公會認可的 Acme 盜賊工具包』不可。這套工具只要多練習幾次就能開任何鎖，只要一百銀幣，絕對划算。去找 Boris 就行。」

column1: Now that has to be one of the stupidest things anyone has ever asked me.
column2: 「這絕對是我聽過最蠢的問題。」

column1: Beginners!  Why do they keep giving me beginners?
column2: 「新手！為什麼老是給我配上新手？」

column1: I tell you, Crusher, that a thief of my stature and abilities should be stuck in a podunk town besieged by brigands who aren't even Union Members is the height of injustice!
column2: 「我告訴你，Crusher，像我這樣身份和能力的盜賊居然被困在這個受山賊圍困的小鎮，而那些山賊還不是工會成員，這真是極不公平！」

column1: It will cost you 25 silvers to work in town.  Just pay Boris at the door.  If you don't have the cash, go steal something.
column2: 「在這個鎮工作要花你 25 銀幣。去門口找 Boris 付錢。沒錢的話就去偷點東西。」

column1: A word of warning to you -- don't drink the Dragon's Breath!
column2: 「給你個忠告──別喝龍息酒！」

column1: Alms?  Alms for the poor?
column2: 「施捨？誰能施捨一點給窮人？」

column1: About time I got back to work.  Alms for the poor.  Alms!
column2: 「該回去工作了。施捨給窮人。施捨!」

column1: My name is Sam.  I've lived here for five years now, and I think I'll be heading on once the pass clears of snow.  There's supposed to be some good begging towns to the south.
column2: 「我叫 Sam。我在這裡住了五年了，一旦雪融化我就準備離開。聽說南邊有些討飯的好地方。」

column1: A beggar gets cash only when others get cash, and nobody's got much money here right now.
column2: 「乞丐只有在別人有錢時才能要到錢，現在這裡沒人有錢。」

column1: There's honest work up at the Baron's castle, I hear.  But begging's my business.
column2: 「聽說男爵城堡裡有誠實的工作。但要飯是我的行當。」

column1: I remember when people used to stroll around town at night.  Now, the only people out late are the thieves.  And outside of town, man, you don't dare go out there after dark.  The Night Gaunts'll get you for sure.
column2: 「我還記得以前人們會在夜間在鎮上漫步。現在，夜間出沒的只有盜賊。而且城外啦，天黑後你根本不敢出去。Night Gaunts 肯定會抓你。」

column1: The most dangerous monsters come out at night.  I'd stay inside then if I were you.
column2: 「最危險的怪物都在夜間出沒。要是我的話我就呆在屋裡。」

column1: Nobody's ever lived to tell what the Night Gaunts are.  I sure don't want to find out.
column2: 「沒人活著說出 Night Gaunts 是什麼。我肯定不想去發現。」

column1: Thieves prowl the streets at night.  They don't bother me because I have nothing to steal.  The brigands are worse.  They sure hurt business; no one's getting rich but them.
column2: 「盜賊在夜間遊蕩街道。他們不會騷擾我因為我沒什麼好偷。山賊更糟。他們真的傷生意；除了他們沒人發財。」

column1: You can get a job cleaning the Baron's stable, I understand.  It's good work if you want to build up your muscles or have a place to spend the night, but you won't get rich.  I'd rather beg, myself.
column2: 「據我所知你可以去男爵的馬廄做清潔工。如果你想增強肌肉或找個地方睡覺，這工作不錯，但賺不到大錢。我寧可乞討。」

column1: Stealing from a beggar is not a heroic action.
column2: 從乞丐手裡偷東西不是英雄的舉動。

column1: Some sharp object appears to have been thrown at these walls several times.
column2: 看起來有些尖銳的物體被多次投擲到這些牆上。

column1: You can't make them out from here.
column2: 你從這裡看不清楚他們。

column1: This grubby place must be an alley.  You feel a strange, cold feeling here.  You see some discarded barrels, and a beggar holds out a bowl towards you.
column2: 這個航髒的地方一定是條巷子。你感到一種奇異的寒冷。你看到幾個廢棄的木桶，還有個乞丐向你伸出碗。

column1: A thin man with very patched clothing holds out a bowl containing only your kind contribution.
column2: 一個衣衫襤褸的瘦男人伸出碗，裡面只有你慈悲的施捨。

column1: A thin man with very patched clothing holds out an empty bowl.
column2: 一個穿著破爛衣服的瘦男人拿著空碗。

column1: The beggar's bowl contains your donation.
column2: 乞丐的碗裡裝著你的施捨。

column1: The beggar's bowl looks sadly empty.
column2: 乞丐的碗看起來悲哀地空著。

column1: Nice place.  How does this guy expect to make a living begging in this alley.
column2: 好地方。這傢伙指望在這條巷子裡乞討維生？

column1: It's a vessel made of staves, headings and hoops. \nMore commonly known as a barrel.
column2: 這是由木板、桶頭和箍組成的容器。\n更常見的叫法是木桶。

column1: This barrel reeks of Troll's Sweat.
column2: 這個木桶聞起來像 Troll's Sweat。

column1: The walls seem pretty normal, but there are some curious marks at the north ends of the buildings.
column2: 牆看起來很正常，但建築物北端有些奇異的痕跡。

column1: The barrels are empty, and smell of sour beer.
column2: 這些木桶都是空的，聞起來像酸啤酒。

column1: Although a beggar, the man in the alley appears to be sober.
column2: 雖然是個乞丐，巷子裡的男人看起來很清醒。

column1: The barrels are too heavy to move.
column2: 木桶太重了，搬不動。

column1: You can't.  The beggar is holding onto it with an iron grip.
column2: 不行。乞丐牢牢抓著。

column1: Thanks.  You know, it's really tough trying to make a living begging in this town since the brigands started scaring all the trade away.
column2: 「謝謝。你知道，自從山賊把生意都嚇跑了，在這城鎮靠要飯維生就變得非常困難。」

column1: This used to be such a nice place to live.  My advice to you is not to take up begging.  It just doesn't pay.  And don't go out at night.
column2: 「這地方以前很不錯。我給你的建議是別從事要飯行業。根本賺不到錢。而且別在晚上出門。」

column1: You reach in your pocket, and suddenly realize that you have no money to give.
column2: 你伸進口袋，突然意識到你沒錢可給。

column1: Silver for the poor.
column2: 「施捨銀幣給窮人。」

column1: This place seems sinister at night. You see a strangely glowing object at the far end of the alley.
column2: 這個地方在夜間看起來很陰險。你在巷子的遠端看到一個發著奇異光芒的物體。

column1: These two men may be smiling at you, but they don't seem very friendly.
column2: 這兩個男人可能在對你微笑，但他們看起來不太友善。

column1: The dagger looks sharp and very deadly.
column2: 匕首看起來很鋒利，非常致命。

column1: It's a glowing golden coin.
column2: 這是一枚發光的金幣。

column1: You just see a glint from here.
column2: 你從這裡只能看到一道閃光。

column1: He may be short, but the dagger makes him look 7 feet tall.
column2: 他可能很矮，但那把匕首讓他看起來像 7 英尺高。

column1: The florid face tells you that this thief is a regular at the "Aces and Eights" tavern.
column2: 那張紅潤的臉告訴你這個盜賊是「Aces and Eights」酒館的常客。

column1: The whole place is in need of repair.
column2: 整個地方都需要修繕。

column1: Looks like this barrel once contained Dragon's Breath.  The bottom's eaten out.
column2: 看起來這個桶子曾經盛過龍息。底部已經被腐蝕穿了。

column1: A whiff of Troll's Sweat indicates that this barrel is from the "Aces and Eights" tavern.
column2: Troll's Sweat 的氣味表明這個木桶來自「Aces and Eights」酒館。

column1: You're too far away from the back wall.
column2: 你離後牆太遠了。

column1: Aw, now look what ya done.  You made poor Slink run out of patience.
column2: 「噢，你幹了什麼好事。你讓可憐的 Slink 失去耐心了。」

column1: Aw, ya forgot to pay.  What a shame.
column2: 「啊，你忘記付錢了。太可惜了。」

column1: That's a pretty tall wall.
column2: 這是一堵相當高的牆。

column1: The thieves seem to be ignoring you.
column2: 盜賊們似乎在忽視你。

column1: Yeah, yeah, we know, you're in the Guild too.  How's a poor independent businessman supposed to make a living around here with all this competition?
column2: 「是是是，我們知道你也在公會裡。一個可憐的獨立商人怎麼樣才能在這裡的這麼多競爭中謀生呢?」

column1: Now go before I forget you made the sign and I make you pay anyway.
column2: 「現在滾蛋，趁我還沒忘記你做出那個手勢，不然我讓你付出代價。」

column1: What's that supposed to be?  You trying to pretend you're a thief or something?  Har, har.
column2: 「那是什麼東西?你在假裝你是小偷什麼的?哈，哈。」

column1: You mean to say that the first customer we've had in months is a fellow thief?  And here I thought we were going to make some money.
column2: 「你是說我們好幾個月以來的第一個顧客是個同行盜賊？我還以為我們要發財了。」

column1: You had better check in at the Thieves' Guild before you get in trouble for practicing without a license.  Find Crusher in the tavern, and give him the password.
column2: 「你最好先去盜賊公會登記，免得因為無執照行動而惹麻煩。在酒館裡找到 Crusher，告訴他密碼。」

column1: The password is '%s'.
column2: 「密碼是『%s』。」

column1: That's all right; we already have all your money.  But it was certainly nice of you to offer.
column2: 「沒關係，我們已經拿了你所有的錢。不過你主動獻殷勤真是太好了。」

column1: Hey!  You don't have enough money to be worth our while.  How do you expect us poor independent businessmen to make a living?
column2: 「嘿!你的錢不夠我們費心。你怎麼指望我們這些可憐的獨立商人謀生呢?」

column1: Slink: "Thanks, it's been a pleasure doing business with you.  You can find your way out, I'm sure.  But better hurry before my dagger starts to slip."
column2: Slink：「謝謝，和你做生意很愉快。我相信你能找到出口。但最好快點，免得我的匕首『滑掉』。」

column1: Slink:  "My finger is starting to get a bit twitchy, so forget about questions, just pay the man and get out alive."
column2: Slink：「我的手指開始有點痠癢，所以別問問題了，直接付錢滾出去活著。」

column1: As you start to draw your weapon, the thieves jump you.
column2: 當你開始拔武器時，盜賊們撲向你。

column1: Before you can begin to use any magic, the thieves decide that you're up to no good.
column2: 你還沒開始施魔法，盜賊就決定你沒好事。

column1: Slink says, "That coin light spell gets them every time.  See that dagger?  The next one is centered on your back.  Give Sneak there your cash and you walk out of here.  It's a simple trade, your life for your money."
column2: Slink 說：「那個錢幣光譜咒語每次都有效。看到那把匕首了嗎？下一個就要送進你的後背。把錢給 Sneak，你就能活著走出去。就這麼簡單，用你的命換你的錢。」

column1: Slink:  "Hey, Sneak, this guy's hard of hearing or something.  I told him to get a license last time he was here, and he didn't listen.  Let's teach him a little lesson about listening to his betters."
column2: Slink：「嘿，Sneak，這傢伙是不是耳聾或怎樣。我上次跟他說要去辦執照，他沒聽。咱們給他點教訓，讓他學會聽話。」

column1: Slink:  "Why, how nice to see you again.  I do hope (for your sake) you brought us some nice shiny new silver."
column2: Slink：「哎呀，又見面了。我真希望（為了你好）你帶來了漂漂亮亮的新銀幣。」

column1: Perhaps you shouldn't explore dark alleys at night unless you are looking for trouble.  Or perhaps you should look in your manual to learn how to deal with thieves.
column2: 也許你不該在夜間探索黑暗的小巷，除非你在找麻煩。或者也許你應該查看手冊，學習如何應對盜賊。

column1: This tastes as sour as it smells, and it burns your throat as you swallow it. Still, it isn't the worst beer you've ever drunk.
column2: 這嚐起來和聞起來一樣酸，吞下去時灼傷你的喉嚨。不過，這不是你喝過最難喝的啤酒。

column1: You know, that actually tasted fine! This really isn't such a bad place, after all, and the bartender reminds you of an old friend you used to know.
column2: 嘿，這個味道還不錯啊！看來這裡也不是那麼糟，而且酒保讓你想起了一個老朋友。

column1: Suddenly, you don't feel so good...
column2: 突然間，你感覺不太對勁…

column1: Smells like Troll's Sweat. Tastes like Troll's Sweat. By golly, it IS Troll's Sweat....
column2: 聞起來像 Troll's Sweat。嚐起來像 Troll's Sweat。天啊，這就是 Troll's Sweat…

column1: You've never tasted anything like it before.
column2: 你以前沒嚐過這樣的東西。

column1: Too much beer.
column2: 啤酒喝太多了。

column1: ...and one Troll's Sweat is too many.
column2: …而一杯 Troll's Sweat 也是太多。

column1: Ohhhhhhhhh woooowwwwwww!!!
Maybe you really shouldn't have tried the Dragon's Breath!  Better luck next time, and we hope you saved your game.
column2: 也許你本來不應該嘗試龍息！下次好運，我們希望你存檔了。

column1: From the size of the ancient oak kegs, you surmise that the drinking of large quantities of beverages is the main pastime in this establishment.
column2: 從這些古老橡木桶的大小來看，你推測在這間酒館裡大量飲酒是主要消遣。

column1: As dirt goes, it's pretty dirty.
column2: 論髒汙程度，這相當髒。

column1: It's in the kegs.
column2: 在桶子裡。

column1: Mighty frothy. Looks OK.
column2: 泡沫很多。看起來還行。

column1: Looks lean, mean, and green.
column2: 看起來精幹、惡毒，還有點發綠。

column1: The fumes hurt your eyes.
column2: 煙霧傷害你的眼睛。

column1: It doesn't look like the bartender ever wipes up spills, and the glasses are truly filthy.
column2: 看起來酒保從不擦拭潑灑的東西，杯子真的是髒透了。

column1: The man on the west side of the table is wearing a blood-stained apron, like a butcher. The man on the east has on a baker's hat. They seem to be deeply involved in their game.
column2: 桌子西邊的男人穿著沾血的圍裙，像個屠夫。東邊的男人戴著麵包師的帽子。他們似乎全力投入遊戲。

column1: You can't see the game very well from where you are.
column2: 從你的位置看不太清楚遊戲。

column1: They seem to be using an "Old Maid" deck.
column2: 他們似乎在用「老處女」牌組。

column1: There are several.
column2: 有好幾個。

column1: That's a mighty unusual keg, there.
column2: 那是個非常不尋常的酒桶呢。

column1: That looks mighty nasty!
column2: 看起來很凶狠！

column1: There are three bar stools at the bar.
column2: 吧台上有三個吧檯高腳凳。

column1: It looks like something you'd expect to see in a tavern, but a lot dirtier.
column2: 看起來像你在酒館會看到的東西，但髒得多。

column1: The grimy window lets little light into this tavern.  It smells like stale ale and other more unpleasant things.  The floor is covered with dirt, and the bar with sticky beer.
column2: 髒污的窗戶讓很少的光進入這間酒館。聞起來像陳舊的麥酒和其他更難聞的東西。地板覆著污垢，吧台黏著啤酒。

column1: Smoke appears to be rising from the center cask behind the bar.  To your right, two gamblers are playing cards.
column2: 煙霧似乎從吧台後面的中央酒桶升起。你右邊，兩個賭徒在玩牌。

column1: The bartender glares at you as you enter, and so does the ugly goon on the left. You get the impression that you are not welcome.
column2: 你進去時酒保瞪了你，左邊那個醜陋的打手也是。你有種不受歡迎的感覺。

column1: There seems to be something slimy spilled on top of the stool, and it is eating into the wood.  You think perhaps you should sit on the next stool over.
column2: 高腳凳頂上好像溢出了某種黏稠的東西，正在腐蝕木頭。你想也許你應該坐隔壁的凳子。

column1: Let's finish this game first.
column2: 「我們先玩完這盤。」

column1: If you don't bet, you can't play.
column2: 「如果你不下注，就不能玩。」

column1: Now, let's not get carried away.
column2: 「現在，別太過分了。」

column1: Hey!  This is cash on the table.  You can't bet what you don't have.
column2: 「嘿!這是現金。你不能賭你沒有的東西。」

column1: So, you think you're pretty hot stuff, eh?  Well, we'll see about that.
column2: 「所以，你認為你很了不起，嗯?好吧，我們就看看吧。」

column1: You got me that time.  Say, you're pretty tough!
column2: 「你這次贏了我。說，你很強啊!」

column1: Looks like I won that one.  Must have gotten lucky.
column2: 「看起來我贏了那一局。肯定是運氣好。」

column1: It's a tie.  Let's double the wager.
column2: 「平手。我們加倍賭注吧。」

column1: Hey, nice game.  It's a tie.
column2: 「嘿，好遊戲。平手。」

column1: Shall we play again?
column2: 要再玩一盤嗎？

column1: Dag-Nab-It  [score %d of 500]
column2: Dag-Nab-It  [得分 %d / 500]

column1: The game is called Dag-Nab-It.  You take turns with the Chief Thief throwing daggers at the board.  Each hero gets three turns, and throws three daggers in each turn.
column2: 這個遊戲叫 Dag-Nab-It。你和盜賊首領輪流向靶子投擲匕首。每個英雄有三個回合，每回合投三把匕首。

column1: More points are scored the closer each dagger hits to the center of the board.  Highest score wins the bet.
column2: 匕首越接近棋盤中心，得分就越高。最高分贏得賭注。

column1: Adjust force and angle of throw with the arrow keys or mouse, according to your hand position.  Press Enter or click on the small dag-nab-it board to throw.
column2: 根據你的手部位置，用方向鍵或滑鼠調整投擲的力度和角度。按 Enter 或點擊小的 dag-nab-it 棋盤來投擲。

column1: You killed a goblin.
column2: 你殺了一個哥布林。

column1: You've taken out the brigand, but... oh, no!  Here comes another one!
column2: 你解決了一個山賊，但…天啊！又來一個！

column1: So You've Become A Hero!  [score %d of 500]
column2: 所以你成了英雄!  [得分 %d 分 500]

column1:
Thus the hero from the East
Freed the man from form of beast
Saved beauty from the brigand's band
And forced the Ogress to flee the land.
column2: 因此,來自東方的英雄 解放了獸形的人 拯救美人脫離山賊魔爪 並迫使食人妖逃離這片土地。

column1: You have defeated the brigands\nAnd become a true Hero of Spielburg
column2: 你擊敗了山賊\n成為了真正的史畢柏格英雄

column1: The brigand band has been dispersed\nTheir treasure has been reimbursed.
column2: 山賊團被驅散\n他們的寶藏被歸還了。

column1: So with the Kattas and the Merchant\nYou board a magic carpet to the land of Shapeir.
column2: 和卡塔族和商人在一起\n你登上魔法地毯前往夏皮爾之地。

column1: And so with Kattas and Abdulla Doo\nYou bid the valley a fond adieu.
column2: 和卡塔族和阿布杜拉·嘟在一起\n你向山谷告別。

column1: Unfortunately, since the Baron is still\ncursed, and Baba Yaga remains to work her evil deeds, terror will continue to rule the land.
column2: 不幸的是,因為男爵仍被詛咒,芭芭雅嘎仍在進行邪惡勾當,恐怖將繼續統治這片土地。

Promotional Considerations:

Erasmus' Rootin' Tootin' Root Beer
Spielburg Chamber of Commerce
Shapeir Magic Carpet Tours
Famous Adventurer's Correspondence
School for Heroes
column2: 宣傳考慮:伊拉斯謨的根汀圖汀根啤酒 史畢柏格商會 沙皮爾魔毯旅遊 著名冒險者通信英雄學校

column1: a:hq1_hero.sav
column2: a:hq1_hero.sav

column1: CONGRATULATIONS!!  You have successfully completed "Hero's Quest I:  So You Want To Be A Hero" with the maximum possible score, 500 of 500!!  We welcome you to the ranks of the few, the proud, the True Heroes!
column2: 恭喜！你成功以滿分 500 完成了「英雄傳奇 I：你想成為英雄嗎」！我們歡迎你加入稀有、驕傲的真英雄行列！

column1: Congratulations!  You have successfully completed "Hero's Quest I:  So You Want To Be A Hero."  Your final score was %d of 500 possible Puzzle Points.
column2: 恭喜！你成功完成了「英雄傳奇 I：你想成為英雄嗎」。你的最終得分是 %d / 500。

column1: If you have not already done so, we encourage you to play "Hero's Quest I" again with the other two character types; many of the puzzles are different, or have alternate solutions.
column2: 如果還沒試過，我們建議你用其他兩種職業再玩一次「英雄傳奇I」；很多謎題有不同解法或替代方案。

column1: In the meantime, you are already a winner!  Please insert a writeable disk in your floppy drive to save your winning Hero for use in\n"Hero's Quest II:  Trial By Fire."
column2: 眼下你已經是勝利者了！請在軟碟機放入可寫入\n的軟碟，以便儲存你的得勝英雄，用於\n「英雄傳奇II：烈火之考驗」。

column1: Could not create file -- %s.
column2: 無法建立檔案——%s。

column1: The save character file has been created.  Save this disk for use with "Hero's Quest II:  Trial By Fire" from Sierra On-Line.
column2: 角色存檔已建立。請保留這張軟碟，以便搭配\nSierra On-Line 的「英雄傳奇II：烈火之考驗」使用。

column1: Please answer "y" or "n".
column2: 請回答「y」或「n」。

column1: Thank you for playing "Hero's Quest I:  So You Want To Be A Hero," and congratulations again on winning.  We'll see you again soon in\n"Hero's Quest II:  Trial By Fire."
column2: 感謝遊玩「英雄傳奇I：你想成為英雄嗎」。\n再次恭喜你獲勝。我們很快會在\n「英雄傳奇II：烈火之考驗」再見到你。

column1: Wow!  You're Really A Hero!  [score %d of 500]
column2: 哇！你真的成為英雄了！[分數 %d / 500]

column1: Your spells seem to have no effect here.
column2: 你的法術在這裡似乎沒有效果。

column1: You really can't sleep here.
column2: 你真的不能在這裡睡覺。

column1: That would be a very bad idea.  Someone would hear you playing the Sheriff's music box, and you would probably be arrested.
column2: 那是個非常糟糕的主意。有人會聽到你在播放治安官的音樂盒，你可能會被逮捕。

column1: Despite your intentions, you feel a calmness and a sense of peace descend upon you as you even consider thoughts of violence.
column2: 儘管你有意圖,當你甚至考慮暴力思想時,你會感到平靜和和平降臨。

column1: You are in a clearing at the edge of the forest.  You are faced with impassably steep, rocky escarpments on two sides.
column2: 你在森林邊緣的空地上。兩側面對著難以通行的陡峭岩壁。

column1: The late frosts of winter give way to to the greenery of spring.  There is the crisp smell of cedar in the air.  This forest seems to be unusually still and quiet.
column2: 冬季的晚霜讓位於春天的蒼翠。空氣中有雪松的清新香氣。這片森林似乎異常寧靜。

column1: Pine needles, moss, and early spring grasses cover the forest floor.
column2: 松針、青苔和初春的青草覆蓋著森林地面。

column1: The evening is clear and the stars are bright.  Dark clouds pass over the moon.
column2: 夜晚天朗氣清，星光璀璨。烏雲飄過月亮。

column1: A tumbled pattern of rocks and boulders leads upwards to the steep cliffs and peaks of the mountain range surrounding this small valley.
column2: 雜亂的岩石與巨礫向上排列，形成通往\n環繞這個小山谷的山脈陡峭懸崖與山峰。

column1: The valley floor is covered with fine, moist soil with few rocks.
column2: 谷底被細膩濕潤的土壤覆蓋，岩石稀少。

column1: Looking through the surrounding woods, you occasionally are able to catch a glimpse of the snow-capped mountain peaks.
column2: 向周圍樹林望去，你偶爾能瞥見\n白雪皚皚的山峰。

column1: Let's see how much fun you have pulling splinters from your lips.
column2: 讓我看看你從嘴唇上拔出木刺有多「好玩」。

column1: The late frosts of winter give way to to the greenery of spring. There is the crisp smell of cedar in the air. This forest seems to be unusually still and quiet.
column2: 冬季的晚霜讓位於春天的蒼翠。空氣中有雪松的清新香氣。這片森林似乎異常寧靜。

column1: It IS a ghost...a transparent, decaying, writhing, slimy, undead fragment of undulating ectoplasm!
column2: 那真的是鬼魂……是個透明、腐爛、扭動、\n黏稠的不死幽魂碎片！

column1: You might be near a graveyard!
column2: 你可能靠近墓地了！

column1: There are no ghosts here to look at.
column2: 這裡沒有鬼魂可看。

column1: Pine needles, moss and early spring grasses cover the forest floor.
column2: 松針、青苔和初春的青草覆蓋著森林地面。

column1: The low-lying bushes form tight tangles between the trees.
column2: 低矮的灌木在樹間形成緊密的糾纏。

column1: The valley floor is covered by fine, moist soil with few large rocks.
column2: 山谷地面被細膩、潮濕的土壤覆蓋，大岩石很少。

column1: There aren't any.
column2: 沒有。

column1: That isn't drinkable -- it's an oil.
column2: 那不能喝——是油。

column1: The great house of Baron Stefan von Spielburg rises on a low hillock over the courtyard and castle walls.
column2: 史蒂芬·馮·史畢柏格男爵的大宅座落在\n低丘上，俯瞰著庭院和城堡的牆垣。

column1: There are guard towers at regular intervals around the castle wall.
column2: 城堡牆周圍定時設有守衛塔樓。

column1: It's a gate that raises and lowers.
column2: 這是一扇升降的大門。

column1: The castle walls are thick and massive.  There is a walkway around the rampart for the guards to patrol.
column2: 城堡的牆壁厚實而宏大。城牆上有一條\n為守衛巡邏而設的走廊。

column1: The main courtyard is a broad, grassy area with a large octagonal area of flagstone paving in the center.
column2: 主庭院是一片寬闊的草地，中央有一片\n大型的八邊形石板鋪面。

column1: The flagstones are from a rock quarry in eastern Germany.
column2: 石板取自德國東部的岩石採石場。

column1: Granite from our very own moutains?
column2: 我們自己山裡出產的花崗岩？

column1: You've already opened all that can be opened in here.
column2: 你已經打開了所有可以打開的東西。

column1: You're too busy right now!
column2: 你現在太忙了！

column1: There are no spells left to trigger.
column2: 沒有可觸發的法術了。

column1: The Kobold is still blind from your last Dazzle spell.  Act quickly!
column2: 地精仍然被你上次的眩目術致盲。動作快！

column1: Ding, dong, the Kobold's dead.  Why bother?
column2: 叮噹，地精死了。何必麻煩？

column1: One thing at a time, hero!
column2: 一次一件事，英雄！

column1: Just walk over and get what you want.
column2: 直接走過去拿你要的。

column1: The Kobold's magic blocks your spell.
column2: 地精的魔法阻擋了你的法術。

column1: Shhhh!  You'll probably just wake up the Kobold.
column2: 噓！你可能只會吵醒地精。

column1: It'd just make the Kobold madder.
column2: 這只會讓地精更生氣。

column1: Perhaps something more potent than a rock is in order.
column2: 也許需要比石頭更有威力的東西。

column1: You have no weapon.
column2: 你沒有武器。

column1: There is no one to fight here anymore.
column2: 這裡已經沒有人可以打了。

column1: You're already fighting!
column2: 你已經在戰鬥了！

column1: Now's not a good time to attempt to communicate.
column2: 現在不是溝通的好時機。

column1: Too late for that now!
column2: 現在太晚了！

column1: Your footsteps echo loudly in this cave.  You'd better practice some more before you'll be able to sneak in here.
column2: 你的腳步在洞窟裡發出響亮的回聲。\n你應該多練習，才能在這裡潛行。

column1: This part of the cavern is spacious and multi-leveled.
column2: 洞窟的這部分寬敞且分為多層。

column1: Resting on a rocky platform above the cave floor is a Kobold, one of the race of ugly, miserly magicians.
column2: 一隻地精棲息在洞穴地面上方的岩石平台上，\n牠是醜陋且守財的魔法師種族之一。

column1: There seems to be a crude table at the base of the platform.
column2: 平台底部似乎有一張粗糙的桌子。

column1: There is a lot of activity at present.
column2: 目前有很多活動。

column1: The Kobold seems agitated.
column2: 地精似乎很不安。

column1: Looking back the way you came, you see a faint reflection of the distant sunlight from the cave entrance.
column2: 往回看你來的路，你看到從洞穴入口照進的\n遠方陽光的微弱反射。

column1: Something is lying in a pile on the table.  Mushrooms?  Toadstools??
column2: 某些東西堆放在桌子上。蘑菇？毒蕈？？

column1: They look almost edible... if you're a Kobold.
column2: 它們看起來幾乎可以吃……如果你是地精的話。

column1: There are numerous gold and silver pieces in the chest.
column2: 箱子裡有許多金幣和銀幣。

column1: Where the chest was, you see many pieces of silver and gold.
column2: 原來箱子的地方，你看到許多銀幣和金幣。

column1: The chest is invisible.
column2: 箱子是隱形的。

column1: You don't see anything like that.
column2: 你看不到那樣的東西。

column1: You once had a key.
column2: 你曾經有一把鑰匙。

column1: The key around the creature's neck seems to emit light.  You strongly suspect that it is magical in nature.
column2: 生物脖子上的鑰匙似乎發出光芒。\n你強烈懷疑它具有魔法性質。

column1: The key lies on the stone where you last saw the Kobold.
column2: 鑰匙躺在你上次看到地精的石頭上。

column1: The key is no longer there!   Perhaps you should have gotten it while you had the chance.
column2: 鑰匙不在那裡了！也許你應該在有機會時\n把它拿走。

column1: The strange creature is wearing ragged clothes.
column2: 奇怪的生物穿著破爛的衣服。

column1: There is what looks like a key hanging around its neck.
column2: 它脖子上掛著看起來像鑰匙的東西。

column1: The creature does not seem pleased to see you.
column2: 生物看起來不高興見到你。

column1: This creature is DANGEROUS!
column2: 這生物很危險！

column1: He's gone.
column2: 他走了。

column1: The key doesn't open the chest. It must be for something else.
column2: 鑰匙打不開箱子。它一定是用來開其他東西的。

column1: What is the purpose?
column2: 目的是什麼？

column1: How?
column2: 怎麼樣？

column1: You attempt to smash your way into the invisible chest!
column2: 你試圖砸破隱形的箱子！

column1: You can see no lock to pick.
column2: 你看不到可撬的鎖。

column1: You have no way of doing that.
column2: 你沒辦法做那件事。

column1: You attempt to pick the lock on the invisible chest.  This'll be tricky!
column2: 你試圖撬開隱形箱子上的鎖。\n這會很棘手！

column1: You retrieve the key you dropped.
column2: 你撿起掉落的鑰匙。

column1: The key is no longer here.
column2: 鑰匙不在這裡了。

column1: VERY carefully, you remove the key from around the Kobold's neck.
column2: 你非常小心地把鑰匙從地精的脖子上取下來。

column1: You'll have to be careful if you want to get the key!
column2: 如果你想得到鑰匙，得小心！

column1: You'll have to kill the Kobold first.
column2: 你得先殺死地精。

column1: You take the key.
column2: 你拿走了鑰匙。

column1: You pick up several of them and put them away.
column2: 你撿起其中幾個並放入背包。

column1: It's too heavy.
column2: 太重了。

column1: How are you going to do that?
column2: 你要怎麼做那件事？

column1: There is no treasure to take.
column2: 沒有寶藏可拿。

column1: You're too awake to sleep now.
column2: 你現在太清醒了,無法入睡。

column1: This doesn't look like a good place to curl up.
column2: 這看起來不是個好的蜷縮地點。

column1: Yep, that's a door, all right.
column2: 是啊，那確實是扇門。

column1: The shades are drawn.
column2: 百葉窗拉上了。

column1: The windows appear to be locked.  You certainly don't want to break them and attract attention.
column2: 窗戶似乎被鎖上了。你當然不想\n打破它們吸引注意力。

column1: In broad daylight?  You've got to be kidding!  You'd be arrested for certain.
column2: 在光天化日下?你在開玩笑吧!你肯定會被逮捕。

column1: You don't know how to pick locks.
column2: 你不知道怎麼開鎖。

column1: You can't pick a lock without a lockpick.
column2: 沒有開鎖工具就撬不開鎖。

column1: Uh oh.  You made too much noise.  The Sheriff and Otto arrive on the scene and arrest you for "criminal carelessness".  Work a little faster next time!
column2: 天哪。你太吵了。治安官和奧圖現身逮捕你「魯莽犯罪」。下次快一點！

column1: The door is securely barred from the inside, but you do get some practice.
column2: 門從內側被牢牢棒住,但你確實得到了一些練習。

column1: The town gate's open.  There's no need to climb over.  Besides, you'd make people nervous.
column2: 鎮門開著。不必爬過去。而且，\n你會讓人緊張。

column1: You'd better not try to climb the wall any more right now.  You've been at this long enough that someone is likely to notice.
column2: 你最好現在別再試著爬牆了。  你已經花了很久時間，可能有人會注意到。

column1: There's no good place to climb the wall here.
column2: 這裡沒有好地方爬牆。

column1: You climb over the town wall.
column2: 你爬過了鎮牆。

column1: The wall is too smooth to climb with your level of skill, but keep practicing.
column2: 以你目前的技能等級，牆面太光滑了，無法攀爬。  但繼續練習吧。

column1: There is an absence of the usual litter associated with town streets. This hamlet is kept very clean.
column2: 小鎮街道上沒有通常的垃圾。\n這個小村落被保持得非常乾淨。

column1: The town wall is made of the same rock as the surrounding mountains.It appears to be very sturdy.
column2: 鎮牆由與周圍山脈相同的岩石製成。\n它看起來非常堅固。

column1: It keeps the occupants of the building dry when it rains.
column2: 下雨時，它讓建築物的住戶保持乾燥。

column1: If you look past the town wall, you can see the mountains through which you journeyed to arrive here.
column2: 越過鎮牆往外看，你可以看到你為了\n來到這裡而穿越的山脈。

column1: You curl up on the hard ground and try to get some rest.
column2: 你在硬地上蜷縮起來,試圖休息。

column1: %s needs an 'at:' method.
column2: %s 需要一個「at:」方法。

column1: %ssg.dir
column2: %ssg.dir

column1: New save-game directory:
column2: 新的存檔目錄：

column1: %s\nis not a valid directory
column2: %s\n不是有效的目錄

column1: You must type a description for the game.
column2: 你必須為遊戲輸入描述。

column1: File: %s
column2: 檔案：%s

column1: Your save game disk is full. You must either use another disk or save over an existing saved game.
column2: 你的存檔磁片已滿。你必須使用另一張磁片或覆蓋現有的存檔。

column1: That game was saved under a different interpreter. It cannot be restored.
column2: 該遊戲是用不同的直譯器存檔的。無法還原。

column1: I don't understand "%s".
column2: 我不懂「%s」。

column1: That doesn't appear to be a proper sentence.
column2: 這似乎不是適當的句子。

column1: That sentence doesn't make sense.
column2: 那句話沒有意義。

column1: You've left me responseless.
column2: 你讓我無言以對。

column1: Please insert your %s disk in drive %s.
column2: 請在 %s 磁碟機中插入你的 %s 軟碟。

column1:
DURING THE GAME:
Click at the top of the screen or press ESC to use the menus. Additional shortcuts are shown there.
Click Right or Shift-Click Left Mouse
Button on an object to look at it.

     IN TYPING WINDOWS:
Arrows, Home and End move the cursor, or click anywhere with the mouse. Ctrl-C clears the line.

     IN DIALOG WINDOWS:
Select the outlined item with <Enter> and
use Tab and Shift-Tab to move between 
choices. 
Or click with the mouse to select an item.

ESC always cancels.
column2:
遊戲進行中：
點擊螢幕頂部或按 ESC 使用菜單。
其他快速鍵會在那裡顯示。
用滑鼠右鍵或 Shift+左鍵
點擊一個物件來查看它。

　　　　　　打字窗口中：
方向鍵、Home 和 End 移動遊標，或
用滑鼠點擊任何地方。Ctrl-C 清除該行。

　　　　　　對話窗口中：
用 <Enter> 鍵選擇突出顯示的項目，
用 Tab 和 Shift-Tab 在各選項間移動。
或用滑鼠點擊選擇項目。

ESC 總是取消。

column1: \nYou can change your mind\n   if you have one.
column2: \n你可以改變主意\n　　如果你有的話。

column1: Every hero needs a break now and then.  Have a nice nap.
column2: 每位英雄都需要時不時休息一下。\n好好睡一覺吧。

column1: Sorry, there's not enough room to see that here.
column2: 抱歉，這裡沒有足夠的空間看到那個。

column1: %s\n[Collection of size %d]
column2: %s [集合大小 %d]

column1: %s\n[List of size %d]
column2: %s [列表大小 %d]

column1: %s\n[Set of size %d]
column2: %s [集合大小 %d]
